meta.note: 短文精读：170-220 词短文 + 4 道理解题 + 长难句拆解 + 全文翻译。

items.title: The Origins of Human Language
items.passage:
How language originated remains one of the biggest mysteries in science. For centuries, thinkers have tried to explain why humans, alone among animals, developed such a complex vocal system for communication. Some researchers believe that spoken language did not appear suddenly but evolved gradually from simpler forms of vocal signaling used by early humans.

Scientists studying this question must proceed carefully, because it is easy to exaggerate the significance of early evidence. A single fossil or an unusual pattern in animal calls is not enough to prove how language began. When two research teams announce simultaneous discoveries that seem to support different theories, the public can quickly grow impatient for a final answer, even though scientists themselves remain far from certain. Some critics even allege that certain widely cited studies were designed to attract media attention rather than to test a theory rigorously.

Genetic research has added a new dimension to the debate. By comparing the DNA of humans with that of closely related species, scientists have identified specific genes linked to speech and language ability. From this evidence, researchers can deduce that the roots of language may lie deep in our evolutionary past, long before anatomically modern humans appeared. Still, no single piece of evidence can fully explain such a remarkable ability, and the search for answers continues today.
items.translation:
语言究竟是如何起源的，一直是科学界最大的谜题之一。几个世纪以来，思想家们一直试图解释，为什么唯独人类在动物界中发展出了如此复杂的有声交流系统。一些研究者认为，口语并非突然出现，而是从我们祖先所使用的更简单的发声信号形式逐渐演化而来。

研究这一问题的科学家必须格外谨慎，因为很容易夸大早期证据的意义。一块化石或动物叫声中某种不寻常的模式，都不足以证明语言是如何产生的。当两个研究团队同时宣布支持不同理论的发现时，公众往往会很快变得不耐烦，急于得到最终答案，尽管科学家自己对此远未确定。一些批评者甚至宣称，某些被广泛引用的研究，其设计初衷是为了吸引媒体关注，而非严谨地检验理论。

基因研究为这场争论增添了新的维度。通过比较人类与近缘物种的DNA，科学家已经识别出与言语和语言能力相关的特定基因。基于这些证据，研究者可以推断，语言的根源可能深植于我们进化历史的远古时期，早于解剖学意义上的现代人类出现之前。不过，没有任何单一证据能够完全解释这种非凡的能力，对答案的探索仍在继续。

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Scientists have finally solved the mystery of how language began.
The origin of human language is still uncertain and requires careful, evidence-based research.
Genetic research proves that language ability is entirely inherited.
The media has exaggerated every scientific discovery about language.
questions.explain: 第一段提出语言起源是未解之谜，第二段强调科学家需谨慎对待证据，第三段说明基因证据虽有帮助但无法完全解释，全文主旨是语言起源仍不确定、需要审慎的实证研究，答案为B。依据第一、二段。

questions.q: According to the passage, what do some critics allege about certain widely cited studies on language origin?
options: They were funded by unreliable sources.
They were designed mainly to attract media attention rather than test theories rigorously.
They used outdated genetic technology.
They ignored fossil evidence completely.
questions.explain: 依据第二段最后一句，一些批评者宣称，某些被广泛引用的研究其设计初衷是吸引媒体关注而非严谨检验理论，对应选项B。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the passage that genetic research on language origin __.
options: has completely settled the debate about how language began
shows that language ability appeared only after modern humans emerged
suggests language may have roots that go back further than modern humans
has been rejected by most scientists studying language
questions.explain: 第三段指出，基因证据可推断语言根源可能早于解剖学意义上的现代人类出现，对应C；同段末句说明单一证据不能完全解释该能力，故A错误；B、D原文均未提及或矛盾。依据第三段。

questions.q: In the second paragraph, the word "simultaneous" most probably means __.
options: happening at the same time
completely false
extremely rare
officially confirmed
questions.explain: 根据第二段语境，两个研究团队宣布的simultaneous discoveries指几乎同时公布的发现，与“同时发生的”含义吻合，故选A。依据第二段。

longSentences.sentence: Some researchers believe that spoken language did not appear suddenly but evolved gradually from simpler forms of vocal signaling used by early humans.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Some researchers believe that...（主语 Some researchers + 谓语 believe + that 引导的宾语从句）
longSentences.modifiers: that 宾语从句中包含 not...but... 并列谓语（did not appear suddenly but evolved gradually）；过去分词短语 used by early humans 作后置定语，修饰 vocal signaling
longSentences.translation: 一些研究者认为，口语并非突然出现，而是从早期人类所使用的更简单的发声信号形式逐渐演化而来。
longSentences.tip: 先抓主干“researchers believe that...”，再看 that 从句内部的 not...but 并列结构，分词短语作定语可先跳过再回读。

longSentences.sentence: When two research teams announce simultaneous discoveries that seem to support different theories, the public can quickly grow impatient for a final answer, even though scientists themselves remain far from certain.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：the public can quickly grow impatient for a final answer（主语 the public + 系动词性谓语 grow + 表语 impatient）
longSentences.modifiers: When 引导时间状语从句，从句中 that 引导定语从句修饰 discoveries；even though 引导让步状语从句，置于句末补充说明
longSentences.translation: 当两个研究团队宣布看似支持不同理论的同时发现时，公众往往会很快变得急于得到最终答案，尽管科学家自己对此远未确定。
longSentences.tip: 长句先跳过 When...和 even though...两个状语从句，锁定中间的主干“the public can grow impatient”，再分别理解两端状语的作用。

longSentences.sentence: From this evidence, researchers can deduce that the roots of language may lie deep in our evolutionary past, long before anatomically modern humans appeared.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：researchers can deduce that...（主语 researchers + 谓语 can deduce + that 引导的宾语从句）
longSentences.modifiers: that 从句中 long before anatomically modern humans appeared 为时间状语，修饰整个从句，说明语言根源出现的时间早于现代人类
longSentences.translation: 基于这一证据，研究者可以推断，语言的根源可能深植于我们进化历史的远古时期，早于解剖学意义上的现代人类出现之前。
longSentences.tip: 抓住“researchers can deduce that + 从句”这一主干，句末 long before...是时间状语，补充说明从句内容发生的时间点。

items.title: Reforesting a Thirsty Land
items.passage:
A well-known environmental foundation recently launched a large reforestation project in a region that has grown increasingly thirsty after years of low rainfall. Rising population density in the area has placed demanding pressure on limited water resources, forcing local communities to search for new solutions.

According to project organizers, the main driver behind the initiative is not simply a shortage of rain but decades of poor land management. Large areas of farmland have become worthless because the soil can no longer hold water or support crops. The foundation hopes that planting trees native to the region will spur natural recovery of the soil and gradually restore the local ecosystem.

Scientists involved in the project argue that finding a lasting replacement for outdated farming methods is just as important as planting new trees. Community leaders have welcomed the plan, though some remain cautious, noting that similar projects elsewhere failed simply because local residents were not properly consulted. Even so, organizers remain optimistic that the project can make the region genuinely sustainable within a generation.
items.translation:
一家知名环保基金会近日启动了一项大规模的植树造林项目，项目所在地区因多年降雨稀少而变得日益干旱。当地人口密度的上升给有限的水资源带来了苛刻的压力，迫使当地社区寻找新的解决办法。

据项目组织者介绍，推动这一举措的主要因素并不仅仅是降雨短缺，而是数十年来土地管理不善的结果。大片农田因土壤已无法保水或支持作物生长而变得毫无价值。基金会希望，种植本地原生树种能够刺激土壤的自然恢复，并逐步修复当地生态系统。

参与该项目的科学家认为，为过时的耕作方式找到持久的替代方案，与种植新树同样重要。社区领袖对该计划表示欢迎，但也有人保持谨慎，指出其他地方的类似项目仅仅因为没有充分征求当地居民意见而以失败告终。尽管如此，组织者仍然相信，该项目能在一代人的时间内让这一地区真正实现可持续发展。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: An environmental foundation's plan to move local farmers to a new region
A reforestation project launched to fight land degradation and restore a drought-affected area
A scientific debate over whether climate change is real
A merger between two environmental organizations
questions.explain: 第一段说明一家环保基金会启动了大规模植树造林项目，应对该地区因干旱和土地退化带来的问题，第二、三段进一步说明原因和目标，主旨为B。依据第一段。

questions.q: According to the passage, what does the foundation believe is the main cause of the land problems in the region?
options: A sudden and severe drought this year
Decades of poor land management rather than simply low rainfall
Overuse of chemical fertilizers by farmers
A recent merger of local farms into large corporations
questions.explain: 第二段指出，推动这一举措的主要因素并非仅仅是降雨短缺，而是数十年来的土地管理不善，对应B。依据第二段。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the last paragraph that some earlier environmental projects failed mainly because __.
options: they did not have enough funding
local residents were not properly consulted
the trees they planted were not native species
the government refused to support them
questions.explain: 第三段提到，其他地方的类似项目因未能充分征求当地居民意见而失败，对应B，A、C、D原文均未提及。依据第三段。

questions.q: In the second paragraph, the word "worthless" is closest in meaning to __.
options: extremely expensive
having no value
very fertile
legally protected
questions.explain: 根据上下文，farmland have become worthless because the soil can no longer hold water or support crops，说明土地因无法保水或支持作物而失去价值，故 worthless 意为“没有价值的”，对应B。依据第二段。

longSentences.sentence: A well-known environmental foundation recently launched a large reforestation project in a region that has grown increasingly thirsty after years of low rainfall.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：A well-known environmental foundation launched a large reforestation project（主语+谓语+宾语）
longSentences.modifiers: that 引导定语从句修饰 region；after 引导时间状语，说明干旱加剧的原因
longSentences.translation: 一家知名环保基金会近日在一个因多年降雨稀少而日益干旱的地区，启动了一项大规模的植树造林项目。
longSentences.tip: 先找主谓宾“foundation launched project”，再看 in a region that...是地点状语加定语从句，逐层剥离修饰成分。

longSentences.sentence: Community leaders have welcomed the plan, though some remain cautious, noting that similar projects elsewhere failed simply because local residents were not properly consulted.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Community leaders have welcomed the plan（主语+谓语+宾语）
longSentences.modifiers: though 引导让步状语从句；noting that...为现在分词短语作伴随状语，其中 that 从句作 noting 的宾语，because 引导原因状语从句
longSentences.translation: 社区领袖对该计划表示欢迎，但也有人持谨慎态度，他们指出，其他地方的类似项目仅仅因为没有充分征求当地居民的意见而失败。
longSentences.tip: 主干很短“leaders have welcomed the plan”，后面 though...和 noting that...都是附加说明，可先理解主干再逐一补充从句信息。

items.title: A Family Day at the Ice Rink
items.passage:
Today, taking the whole family skating has become a popular way for parents in Chinese cities to spend the weekend together. At a busy ice rink in Beijing, a rental clerk hands out skates to a line of excited customers every Saturday morning, and children can be heard laughing and screaming with joy as soon as they step onto the ice.

Doctors are increasingly aware of both the benefits and the risks of this popular family activity. Skating is amazing exercise that gets people moving together instead of sitting separately in front of their own phones. However, instructors often ask beginners to repeat the same simple drill again and again before they can keep their balance, and the cold air inside the rink can leave a sore throat if people are not careful. Some parents even disagree about whether young children are ready for the sport.

To keep visitors safe, most rinks now take extra measures, such as requiring helmets for children and limiting how many skaters can be on the ice at once. Rink managers insist this is not an abuse of the rules but a sensible way to prevent accidents. As long as families follow the rules, an afternoon on the ice is rarely interrupted by anything more than the announcement that it is time to go home.
items.translation:
如今，携全家一起去滑冰已成为中国城市里父母们共度周末的一种流行方式。在北京一家繁忙的溜冰场，每逢周六上午，一名租赁柜台的店员都会为排队的顾客一一分发冰鞋，孩子们一踏上冰面便笑着尖叫，兴奋不已。

医生们越来越意识到这项热门家庭活动的益处与风险。滑冰是一项极好的运动，能让一家人一起活动，而不是各自分开盯着自己的手机。然而，教练常常要求初学者反复练习同一个简单动作，才能学会保持平衡；溜冰场内的冷空气如果不加注意，也可能让人嗓子发痛。一些家长甚至对孩子是否已经适合这项运动意见不一。

为了保障游客安全，大多数溜冰场如今都采取了额外措施，比如要求儿童佩戴头盔，并限制同一时间在冰面上的滑冰人数。溜冰场管理人员坚称，这并非滥用规则，而是预防事故的合理做法。只要各家庭遵守规定，一个下午的滑冰活动通常不会被什么严重的事情打断，最多也就是被“该回家了”的广播打断而已。

questions.q: What is this passage mainly about?
options: The history of ice skating in China
Family skating as a popular weekend activity, along with its benefits and safety concerns
A famous ice rink in Beijing
Why children should not go skating
questions.explain: 第一段介绍全家滑冰成为周末流行活动，第二段讲益处与风险，第三段讲安全措施，全文围绕这一活动展开，答案为B。依据全文。

questions.q: According to the passage, what do instructors often ask beginners to do?
options: Watch other skaters before trying
Repeat the same simple movement again and again
Wear a helmet at all times
Skate only on Saturday mornings
questions.explain: 第二段提到，教练常常要求初学者反复练习同一个简单动作，才能学会保持平衡，对应B。依据第二段。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the rinks' safety measures?
options: They are meant to prevent accidents, not to limit people's fun.
They were created because of a serious accident last year.
They are only followed by professional skaters.
They make the rinks too expensive for most families.
questions.explain: 第三段提到，管理人员坚称这些措施并非滥用规则，而是预防事故的合理做法，可推断措施的目的是保障安全而非限制乐趣，对应A；B、C、D原文均未提及。依据第三段。

questions.q: In the second paragraph, the phrase "sitting separately" most likely means __.
options: sitting together as a group
sitting apart from one another
sitting outside in the cold
sitting for a long time
questions.explain: 根据上下文，gets people moving together instead of sitting separately in front of their own phones，意为滑冰让家人一起活动而不是各自分开各看手机，separately 意为“分开地”，对应B。依据第二段。

longSentences.sentence: At a busy ice rink in Beijing, a rental clerk hands out skates to a line of excited customers every Saturday morning, and children can be heard laughing and screaming with joy as soon as they step onto the ice.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：并列句，前半 a rental clerk hands out skates...，后半 children can be heard laughing and screaming...（and 连接两个并列分句）
longSentences.modifiers: as soon as 引导时间状语从句，说明孩子尖叫欢笑发生的时间；laughing and screaming with joy 为现在分词短语作补语，修饰 children can be heard doing
longSentences.translation: 在北京一家繁忙的溜冰场，每逢周六上午，一名租赁柜台的店员都会为排队等候的顾客们分发冰鞋，孩子们一踏上冰面便能听到他们笑着尖叫，满心欢喜。
longSentences.tip: 句子由 and 连接两个并列分句，先分别找出各自的主谓宾，再看 as soon as 引导的时间状语从句作补充说明。

longSentences.sentence: However, instructors often ask beginners to repeat the same simple drill again and again before they can keep their balance, and the cold air inside the rink can leave a sore throat if people are not careful.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：并列句，前半 instructors ask beginners to repeat the same simple drill（主语+谓语+宾语+宾语补足语），后半 the cold air can leave a sore throat（主语+谓语+宾语）
longSentences.modifiers: before 引导时间状语从句；if 引导条件状语从句
longSentences.translation: 然而，教练常常要求初学者反复练习同一个简单动作，才能学会保持平衡；溜冰场内的冷空气如果不加注意，也可能让人嗓子发痛。
longSentences.tip: 先用 and 把句子切成两个并列分句，再分别找主谓宾；before 和 if 引导的从句是次要信息，可放在最后处理。

items.title: Volunteering for Sustainable Livelihoods
items.passage:
Every summer, thousands of college students across China volunteer to spend several weeks in remote villages, helping farmers find more sustainable ways to earn a living. Many of these youngsters have never lived without electricity or reliable internet, so the experience often comes as a shock. Living conditions in the villages are normally fairly simple, with basic food and no access to the modern conveniences that city students take for granted.

Contrary to popular belief, volunteering in the countryside is rarely a glorious adventure. Much of the work involves repetitive, unglamorous tasks such as repairing irrigation channels or teaching basic farming techniques that can help villagers build a more sustainable livelihood. Still, volunteers say the experience teaches them patience and humility in ways that no classroom ever could.

Organizers stress that the goal is not to offer symbolic gestures of goodwill but to create lasting change. A single summer trip cannot solve deep-rooted problems of poverty, but when combined with follow-up support, it can help a village develop skills that outlast the volunteers themselves. For many youngsters, the trip becomes the starting point of a lifelong commitment to community service.
items.translation:
每年夏天，中国各地成千上万的大学生自愿前往偏远乡村，住上几周，帮助农民寻找更可持续的谋生方式。这些年轻人中许多人从未经历过没有电或没有稳定网络的生活，因此这段经历常常给他们带来不小的冲击。乡村的生活条件通常相当简朴，只有基本的食物，也无法享受城市学生视为理所当然的现代便利设施。

与人们普遍的想象相反，在乡村做志愿者很少是一场光荣的冒险。大部分工作都是重复而毫不光鲜的活计，比如修理灌溉渠道，或者教授基础的耕作技术，从而帮助村民建立起更可持续的生计。尽管如此，志愿者们表示，这段经历教会了他们耐心与谦逊，这是任何课堂都无法给予的。

组织者强调，此举的目标并非提供象征性的善意姿态，而是要创造持久的改变。一次暑期之行无法解决根深蒂固的贫困问题，但如果配合后续的持续支持，它就能帮助一个村庄培养出即便志愿者离开后依然能够延续下去的技能。对许多年轻人而言，这次旅程成为他们终身投身社区服务的起点。

questions.q: What is the main purpose of the passage?
options: To criticize college students for volunteering in remote villages
To describe how youth volunteers help rural communities build more sustainable livelihoods and what the experience teaches them
To explain why village life is easier than city life
To promote a particular university's volunteer program
questions.explain: 全文讲述大学生志愿者赴乡村帮助村民建立可持续生计，以及这段经历带给他们的成长与思考，主旨对应B。依据全文，尤其第一、二段。

questions.q: According to the second paragraph, what does most of the volunteer work actually involve?
options: Teaching villagers how to use computers
Repetitive and unglamorous tasks such as repairing irrigation channels and teaching farming techniques
Organizing large public celebrations
Building new schools from scratch
questions.explain: 第二段明确指出，大部分工作是重复而不光鲜的活计，如修理灌溉渠道、教授基础耕作技术，对应B。依据第二段。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the passage that a single summer volunteer trip __.
options: is enough to completely eliminate rural poverty
is meaningless without follow-up support
can be the beginning of a long-term commitment to service, but cannot solve deep problems alone
is mainly organized to give students a holiday
questions.explain: 第三段指出，一次暑期之行无法解决根深蒂固的贫困问题，但配合后续支持能带来持续影响，且对许多年轻人而言这只是终身社区服务的起点，对应C；A与原文矛盾；B表述过于绝对；D原文未提及。依据第三段。

questions.q: In the second paragraph, the word "unglamorous" most probably describes work that is __.
options: dangerous and risky
exciting and rewarding
plain and not impressive
highly paid
questions.explain: 根据上下文，repetitive, unglamorous tasks such as repairing irrigation channels...描述的是平凡、不光鲜的劳动，与令人兴奋相对，故 unglamorous 意为“不光鲜的，平淡无奇的”，对应C。依据第二段。

longSentences.sentence: Many of these youngsters have never lived without electricity or reliable internet, so the experience often comes as a shock.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：并列句，前半 Many of these youngsters have never lived without electricity or reliable internet，后半由 so 引导结果状语 the experience often comes as a shock
longSentences.modifiers: so 引导结果状语从句，说明前面情况导致的结果
longSentences.translation: 这些年轻人中许多人从未经历过没有电或没有稳定网络的生活，因此这段经历常常给他们带来不小的冲击。
longSentences.tip: 抓住 so 前后两个分句各自的主谓宾，so 表示因果关系，前因后果依次理解即可。

longSentences.sentence: A single summer trip cannot solve deep-rooted problems of poverty, but when combined with follow-up support, it can help a village develop skills that outlast the volunteers themselves.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：并列转折句，前半 A single summer trip cannot solve deep-rooted problems of poverty，后半 it can help a village develop skills（由 but 连接）
longSentences.modifiers: when combined with follow-up support 为省略主语和 be 动词的时间状语从句（即 when it is combined with...）；that outlast the volunteers themselves 为定语从句，修饰 skills
longSentences.translation: 一次暑期之行无法解决根深蒂固的贫困问题，但如果配合后续的持续支持，它就能帮助一个村庄培养出即便志愿者离开后依然能够延续下去的技能。
longSentences.tip: 先用 but 把句子分成两个对比分句，再注意 when combined with...是省略了主语和 be 动词的状语从句，that 从句修饰 skills 说明技能的持续性。

items.title: A Milestone Moment on Screen
items.passage:
Last spring, a singing competition that has run on national television for over a decade quietly made history. Among the dozens of hopefuls competing for a recording contract, one pair stood out: two contestants who performed a duet as a married lesbian couple, openly holding hands on stage. For a mainstream broadcast, this was still an unusual occurrence, and producers later admitted they had debated for weeks whether to include the segment at all.

The hesitation, it turned out, was not simply about public reaction. Every song performed on the show must clear copyright clearance before broadcast, and the couple had chosen an obscure ballad whose ownership proved surprisingly difficult to trace. Only after lawyers confirmed the rights could the performance go ahead. When it finally aired, viewers reacted with warmth rather than controversy, and critics described the episode as a milestone in how ordinary families are portrayed on screen.

Industry analysts note that such moments reflect a broader shift in television. Programs once built around a narrow, predictable formula are increasingly embracing diversity, both in the contestants they cast and the stories they choose to tell. Whether this trend continues will depend on audience response, but for now, the show has proven that authentic representation and popular entertainment are not mutually exclusive.
items.translation:
去年春天，一档已经在全国电视台播出十多年的选秀节目悄然创造了历史。在数十名争夺唱片合约的选手中，有一对格外引人注目：两位以已婚女同性恋伴侣身份演唱二重唱、并在台上公开牵手的参赛者。对于一档主流广播节目来说，这仍然是一件不寻常的事，制作方后来也承认，他们曾为是否播出这段表演争论了数周。

事后看来，他们的犹豫并不仅仅出于对公众反应的担忧。节目中演唱的每一首歌都必须在播出前完成版权审核，而这对情侣选中的是一首鲜为人知的情歌，其版权归属竟出人意料地难以查清。直到律师确认了版权，这段表演才得以播出。节目最终播出后，观众的反应是温暖而非争议，评论人士称这一集是电视如何呈现普通家庭方式上的一个里程碑。

业内分析人士指出，这类时刻反映出电视行业更广泛的转变。曾经围绕单一、可预测模式打造的节目，如今正日益拥抱多元化，无论是在选角的参赛者身上，还是在他们所选择讲述的故事上。这一趋势能否延续将取决于观众的反应，但就目前而言，该节目已经证明真实的呈现与大众娱乐并非彼此排斥。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A televised singing competition changed its judging rules.
A same-sex couple's performance on a talent show became a symbol of change in television.
A copyright lawsuit delayed a music competition's broadcast.
Producers cancelled a show due to public protest.
questions.explain: 全文主旨是选秀节目中一对已婚女同性恋伴侣的表演成为电视媒体变化的象征，第二、三段说明其被称为“里程碑”并反映行业转变，故选B；A、C、D均为对细节的误读或原文未支持的说法。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, why did producers hesitate before airing the performance?
options: They worried the song's copyright ownership was hard to confirm.
They doubted the couple's singing ability.
They lacked funding for the broadcast.
The couple refused to perform live.
questions.explain: 第二段提到节目歌曲必须获得版权许可，而这对情侣所选歌曲的版权归属难以查证，直到律师确认后才得以播出，故选A。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the show's producers from paragraph 2?
options: They ignored copyright rules to save time.
They were indifferent to how the segment would be received.
They were cautious partly for legal, not only social, reasons.
They planned the segment purely for controversy.
questions.explain: 第二段指出他们的犹豫“并不仅仅出于对公众反应的担忧”，还与版权确认有关，说明制作方的谨慎兼有法律与社会两方面原因，故选C；A与事实相反（他们确认了版权而非忽视），B、D原文未提及或与文意矛盾。

questions.q: The word "occurrence" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: mistake
event
rule
argument
questions.explain: 语境中occurrence指该表演在主流电视节目中仍属少见的“事件、现象”，最接近event，其余选项与文意不符，依据见第一段。

longSentences.sentence: Among the dozens of hopefuls competing for a recording contract, one pair stood out: two contestants who performed a duet as a married lesbian couple, openly holding hands on stage.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“one pair stood out”（有一对选手格外引人注目）
longSentences.modifiers: “Among the dozens of hopefuls competing for a recording contract”为地点状语，其中competing为现在分词短语修饰hopefuls；冒号后“two contestants who performed a duet as a married lesbian couple”为同位语，内含who引导的定语从句修饰contestants；“openly holding hands on stage”为现在分词短语作伴随状语。
longSentences.translation: 在数十名争夺唱片合约的选手中，有一对格外引人注目：两位以已婚女同性恋伴侣身份演唱二重唱、并在台上公开牵手的参赛者。
longSentences.tip: 先抓主干“one pair stood out”，再依次剥离分词短语和定语从句，不要被冒号后的同位语打断阅读节奏。

longSentences.sentence: Every song performed on the show must clear copyright clearance before broadcast, and the couple had chosen an obscure ballad whose ownership proved surprisingly difficult to trace.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列句：“Every song...must clear copyright clearance” + “the couple had chosen an obscure ballad”
longSentences.modifiers: “performed on the show”为过去分词短语修饰song；“whose ownership proved surprisingly difficult to trace”为whose引导的定语从句修饰ballad。
longSentences.translation: 节目中演唱的每一首歌都必须在播出前完成版权审核，而这对情侣选中的是一首鲜为人知的情歌，其版权归属竟出人意料地难以查清。
longSentences.tip: 遇到whose从句时，把whose所指代的名词代入从句内部理解，即可理清“归属难查”的逻辑主语。

longSentences.sentence: Whether this trend continues will depend on audience response, but for now, the show has proven that authentic representation and popular entertainment are not mutually exclusive.
longSentences.skeleton: “Whether...continues will depend on audience response”与“the show has proven that...”为并列的两个分句
longSentences.modifiers: “Whether this trend continues”为主语从句，作第一个分句的主语；“that authentic representation and popular entertainment are not mutually exclusive”为that引导的宾语从句。
longSentences.translation: 这一趋势能否延续将取决于观众的反应，但就目前而言，该节目已经证明真实的呈现与大众娱乐并非彼此排斥。
longSentences.tip: 识别whether引导的主语从句和that引导的宾语从句，分别译成“……是否……”和“……这一点”。

items.title: The Risk Behind a High-Speed Crash
items.passage:
At last month's national cycling championship, thousands of onlookers lining the final descent watched a high-speed crash that instantly became the day's most talked-about moment. A young racer, chasing his first major title, misjudged a turn just as his machine reached nearly seventy kilometers an hour. Even the sturdiest frame, built to absorb such punishing conditions, could not prevent the fall from ending his race early.

Rushed to the hospital in his torn racing kit, the cyclist was soon changed into a hospital gown and diagnosed with a strained shoulder muscle and mild concussion. Doctors imposed an immediate two-week rest, warning that returning too soon could turn a minor strain into a lasting injury. Yet the athlete initially denied that anything was wrong, insisting he felt fine, and his refusal to accept the diagnosis worried both his coach and his family.

Sports physicians say such reactions are common: the stimulus of competition can make riders ignore pain that would otherwise be obvious. Constant pressure to conform to a punishing training calendar, combined with the drain of back-to-back races, leaves little room for recovery. At high speed, even a tiny lapse in concentration can magnify into a serious accident, which is why more teams are now questioning whether excessive scheduling puts riders at unnecessary risk.
items.translation:
在上个月的全国自行车锦标赛上，数千名沿最后下坡赛段观赛的观众，目睹了一场瞬间成为当天热议焦点的高速撞车事故。一名正在争夺个人首个重要冠军头衔的年轻车手，就在他的赛车时速接近七十公里之际判断失误，摔出了赛道。即便是为承受如此严苛条件而制造的最坚固车架，也未能阻止这次摔车提前终结他的比赛。

这名车手仍穿着破损的比赛装备就被送往医院，随后换上了病号服，被诊断为肩部肌肉拉伤并伴有轻微脑震荡。医生要求他立即休息两周，并警告说过早复出可能会让轻微的拉伤变成难以痊愈的伤病。然而这名运动员起初却否认自己有任何问题，坚称自己感觉良好，他拒绝接受诊断的态度让教练和家人都感到担忧。

运动医学专家指出，这种反应很常见：比赛带来的刺激会让选手忽视原本明显能察觉到的疼痛。持续面临遵从高强度训练计划的压力，加上连场比赛带来的体力消耗，几乎不给身体留下恢复的空间。在高速状态下，哪怕是注意力上极小的疏忽，也可能被放大成一场严重事故，这正是越来越多车队开始质疑高强度赛程安排是否会给车手带来不必要风险的原因。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A cyclist's crash reveals risks linked to overtraining and pressure to push through pain.
A new bicycle design improves rider safety at high speed.
A championship race was cancelled after an accident.
Doctors developed a new treatment for concussions.
questions.explain: 全文围绕一名自行车选手在高速比赛中受伤展开，进而讨论运动员否认伤情、过度训练带来的风险，故A为主旨；B、C、D均非文章重点或原文未提及。

questions.q: What did doctors do after diagnosing the cyclist's injury?
options: They allowed him to keep racing immediately.
They ordered two weeks of rest.
They performed surgery on his shoulder.
They banned him from the sport permanently.
questions.explain: 第二段提到“Doctors imposed an immediate two-week rest”，即医生要求他立即休息两周，故选B；其余选项原文均未提及。

questions.q: Why does the passage mention that the cyclist \"initially denied that anything was wrong\"?
options: To show that he fully agreed with his doctors.
To illustrate how competitive pressure can make athletes underestimate injury.
To prove the diagnosis was incorrect.
To explain why the race was rescheduled.
questions.explain: 第三段解释比赛带来的刺激会让选手忽视本应明显的疼痛，呼应第二段车手否认伤情的细节，说明比赛压力可能导致运动员低估伤势，故选B。

questions.q: In paragraph 3, "magnify" most likely means:
options: reduce
enlarge
ignore
repair
questions.explain: 原句“even a tiny lapse in concentration can magnify into a serious accident”意为微小的疏忽会被放大成严重事故，故magnify意为“放大、扩大”，选B，依据见第三段。

longSentences.sentence: A young racer, chasing his first major title, misjudged a turn just as his machine reached nearly seventy kilometers an hour.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“A young racer misjudged a turn”（一名年轻车手判断失误）
longSentences.modifiers: “chasing his first major title”为插入的现在分词短语，作伴随状语说明背景；“just as his machine reached nearly seventy kilometers an hour”为just as引导的时间状语从句。
longSentences.translation: 一名正在争夺个人首个重要冠军头衔的年轻车手，就在他的赛车时速接近七十公里之际判断失误，摔出了赛道。
longSentences.tip: 插入语可以先跳过，抓住主谓宾“racer misjudged a turn”，再回头补充分词状语交代的背景信息。

longSentences.sentence: Doctors imposed an immediate two-week rest, warning that returning too soon could turn a minor strain into a lasting injury.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“Doctors imposed an immediate two-week rest”（医生要求立即休息两周）
longSentences.modifiers: “warning that returning too soon could turn a minor strain into a lasting injury”为现在分词短语作伴随状语，内嵌that引导的宾语从句，从句主语为动名词returning。
longSentences.translation: 医生要求他立即休息两周，并警告说过早复出可能会让轻微的拉伤变成难以痊愈的伤病。
longSentences.tip: warning that...是分词短语作状语表伴随结果，that从句的主语是动名词短语returning too soon，要把它当作名词性主语来理解。

longSentences.sentence: Sports physicians say such reactions are common: the stimulus of competition can make riders ignore pain that would otherwise be obvious.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“Sports physicians say such reactions are common”，冒号后为解释性并列分句“the stimulus of competition can make riders ignore pain”
longSentences.modifiers: “that would otherwise be obvious”为定语从句修饰pain，otherwise表示“若非如此（本会）”的隐含对比。
longSentences.translation: 运动医学专家指出，这种反应很常见：比赛带来的刺激会让选手忽视原本明显能察觉到的疼痛。
longSentences.tip: otherwise在从句中表示“（若不是因为比赛的刺激）本来会……”，翻译时要补出这种隐含的对比条件。

items.title: Laughter, Meals, and Teenage Wellbeing
items.passage:
Researchers at a university recently spent a school year following a group of students through early adolescence to study a simple question: does laughter at mealtimes affect how young people feel about school? Each day, the students recorded how much time they spent laughing while they consumed breakfast, lunch, and dinner with family or friends.

The results, published last month, showed a clear pattern. Teenagers who laughed often during meals reported more stable moods and fewer conflicts at home, whatever the weather outside, be it sunshine or wind. Frequent laughter seemed to be a characteristic shared by students who also expressed a stronger intention to stay in school rather than resign themselves to dropping out when grades slipped. Even small moments, like friends waving to each other before class, seemed to add to this sense of belonging.

The researchers admit that laughter alone cannot explain every difference between students, and maybe other factors, such as family income or sleep, play a larger role. Still, there is a real chance that something as ordinary as sharing a laugh over a meal helps teenagers handle the pressures of growing up. Educators have saluted the study for offering a low-cost way to support student wellbeing without adding to already busy school schedules.
items.translation:
某大学的研究人员最近用了一整个学年的时间追踪一组处于青春期早期的学生，以研究一个简单的问题：用餐时的笑声是否会影响年轻人对学校的感受？每天，这些学生都要记录自己与家人或朋友一起吃早餐、午餐和晚餐时大笑的时间有多长。

上个月公布的研究结果显示出一个清晰的规律。经常在用餐时大笑的青少年报告说，无论室外天气是晴是风，他们的情绪都更稳定，家中的冲突也更少。经常大笑似乎是学生们共有的一个特点，这些学生也更倾向于表现出继续留校的意愿，而不是在成绩下滑时就自甘辍学。哪怕是像朋友之间上课前互相打招呼这样的小小举动，似乎也增添了这种归属感。

研究人员承认，笑声本身无法解释学生之间的所有差异，也许家庭收入、睡眠等其他因素也起着更大的作用。尽管如此，像与家人共餐时开怀一笑这样平常的事，确实有可能帮助青少年更好地应对成长过程中的压力。教育工作者称赞这项研究，因为它提供了一种在不增加本已繁忙的课程安排负担的情况下，支持学生身心健康的低成本方法。

keyWords.zh: n. 特征 a. 特有的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A study suggests that laughter during family meals may support teenagers' emotional stability.
A school introduced mandatory family dinners for all students.
Researchers proved that laughter cures teenage depression.
Teenagers who eat alone perform better academically.
questions.explain: 全文介绍一项研究发现，青少年在用餐时的笑声与情绪稳定及留校意愿相关，故A为主旨；B、C、D均非文章内容，或表述过于绝对，原文未作此类结论。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, teenagers who laughed often during meals were more likely to
options: change schools frequently
report stable moods and intend to stay in school
skip meals with their families
argue with their parents more
questions.explain: 第二段指出常在用餐时大笑的青少年情绪更稳定，且更倾向于继续留在学校而非中途放弃，故选B。

questions.q: What can be inferred from paragraph 3?
options: The researchers believe laughter is the only factor affecting teenagers' wellbeing.
The researchers acknowledge that other factors may also influence the results.
The study found no chance that laughter helps teenagers.
Educators criticized the study for being too costly.
questions.explain: 第三段提到研究者承认笑声不能解释学生间所有差异，家庭收入、睡眠等因素也可能起作用，说明他们承认其他因素的影响，故选B；A表述绝对化，C与原文相反，D原文说educators“saluted”（称赞）而非批评。

questions.q: The word "resign" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: fight
give up
apply
celebrate
questions.explain: “resign themselves to dropping out”意为“听任自己辍学、自甘放弃”，resign（oneself to）表示“顺从、放弃抵抗”，故最接近give up，依据见第二段。

longSentences.sentence: Researchers at a university recently spent a school year following a group of students through early adolescence to study a simple question: does laughter at mealtimes affect how young people feel about school?
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“Researchers spent a school year following a group of students...to study a simple question”
longSentences.modifiers: “following a group of students through early adolescence”为现在分词短语作伴随状语；“to study a simple question”为不定式表目的；冒号后“does laughter at mealtimes affect how young people feel about school”是对question内容的具体说明，其中how引导宾语从句嵌套在affect之后。
longSentences.translation: 某大学的研究人员最近用了一整个学年的时间追踪一组处于青春期早期的学生，以研究一个简单的问题：用餐时的笑声是否会影响年轻人对学校的感受？
longSentences.tip: 冒号后的问句是对前面“a simple question”的具体说明，可以把冒号理解为“即”来断句阅读。

longSentences.sentence: Teenagers who laughed often during meals reported more stable moods and fewer conflicts at home, whatever the weather outside, be it sunshine or wind.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“Teenagers reported more stable moods and fewer conflicts at home”
longSentences.modifiers: “who laughed often during meals”为定语从句修饰Teenagers；“whatever the weather outside, be it sunshine or wind”为让步状语，其中be it...or...为虚拟语气让步结构。
longSentences.translation: 经常在用餐时大笑的青少年报告说，无论室外天气是晴是风，他们的情绪都更稳定，家中的冲突也更少。
longSentences.tip: be it A or B是让步状语中的虚拟语气固定结构，相当于whether it be A or B，可译为“无论是A还是B”。

longSentences.sentence: Frequent laughter seemed to be a characteristic shared by students who also expressed a stronger intention to stay in school rather than resign themselves to dropping out when grades slipped.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“Frequent laughter seemed to be a characteristic”
longSentences.modifiers: “shared by students”为过去分词短语修饰characteristic；“who also expressed a stronger intention to stay in school rather than resign themselves to dropping out”为定语从句修饰students，内含rather than并列结构；“when grades slipped”为时间状语从句。
longSentences.translation: 经常大笑似乎是学生们共有的一个特点，这些学生也更倾向于表现出继续留校的意愿，而不是在成绩下滑时就自甘辍学。
longSentences.tip: rather than连接两个并列的动词短语，表示“宁愿……而不是……”，注意区分intention to stay和resign themselves to dropping out这两个对比的行为。

items.title: Rethinking How Students Earn Degrees
items.passage:
Universities around the world are quietly rebuilding the mechanism by which students earn a degree. Instead of requiring four continuous years on campus, a growing number of institutions now let learners complete a bundle of short online modules whenever it suits them, then combine the credits into a full qualification later.

Supporters argue that this flexible approach makes institutional learning more accessible, easing the financial and time pressures that once forced many capable students to give up their studies. A single parent working night shifts, for example, no longer has to sit idle at home wondering whether a traditional timetable will ever fit; with a schedule built around her own life, she can qualify for the same degree as a full-time student, only at her own pace. In the following years, administrators expect even more workers to take this route.

Critics, however, warn that scattered online modules cannot fully replace the discipline built through sustained classroom study, and that some employers still hesitate to treat such qualifications as equal in value. Whether this new mechanism eventually reshapes higher education, or simply adds one more option alongside the old model, will likely depend on how well institutions can prove that flexible learning produces graduates just as capable as their campus-based peers.
items.translation:
世界各地的大学正在悄然重塑学生获得学位的机制。越来越多的院校不再要求学生连续在校学习四年，而是让学习者按照自己的时间随时完成一系列简短的在线模块课程，之后再将学分合并成一个完整的学位资格。

支持者认为，这种灵活的方式让制度化学习变得更容易获得，从而缓解了曾经迫使许多有能力的学生被迫放弃学业的经济和时间压力。例如，一位上夜班的单亲家长，不必再闲坐家中，担心传统的课程表是否永远也无法适应自己的生活；按照围绕自己生活安排的时间表学习，她可以像全日制学生一样取得同样的学位资格，只是节奏由自己掌控。在接下来的几年里，管理者预计会有更多在职人员选择这条路径。

然而，批评者警告说，零散的在线学习模块无法完全取代通过持续课堂学习所培养的自律，而且一些雇主仍然不愿将这类资格视为同等价值。这种新机制最终是会重塑高等教育，还是仅仅在旧模式之外多提供一种选择，很可能取决于院校能在多大程度上证明，灵活学习培养出的毕业生和校园学习的同龄人一样能力出众。

keyWords.zh: a. 闲置的，无所事事的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: Flexible, module-based learning is changing how students earn degrees, though debate continues over its value.
All universities have abandoned traditional classroom teaching.
Employers now refuse to hire graduates of online programs.
Governments have banned short online courses.
questions.explain: 全文介绍高校用模块化、灵活的机制取代传统四年制学习，并提及支持与质疑两方观点，故A概括了主旨；B、C、D表述绝对或原文未提及。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, how does the flexible mechanism help a single parent working night shifts?
options: It excuses her from earning any qualification.
It lets her follow a schedule built around her own life to qualify for a degree at her own pace.
It requires her to attend classes only at night.
It removes all costs of higher education.
questions.explain: 第二段举例说明，上夜班的单亲家长可以按照围绕自己生活安排的时间表学习，最终以自己的节奏取得与全日制学生相同的学位资格，故选B。

questions.q: What is the critics' main concern mentioned in paragraph 3?
options: Online modules are more expensive than campus study.
Scattered online study may not build the same discipline as sustained classroom study, and some employers may not value it equally.
Universities are losing too much money from online programs.
Students are no longer interested in earning degrees.
questions.explain: 第三段指出批评者担心零散的在线学习无法培养持续课堂学习所锻炼的自律，且部分雇主仍不愿平等看待这类资格，故选B。

questions.q: In paragraph 2, the word "idle" most likely describes someone who is:
options: busy working
not active or occupied
angry and impatient
traveling frequently
questions.explain: “no longer has to sit idle at home”意为不必闲坐在家中无所事事，idle意为“闲置的、无所事事的”，故选B，依据见第二段。

longSentences.sentence: Instead of requiring four continuous years on campus, a growing number of institutions now let learners complete a bundle of short online modules whenever it suits them, then combine the credits into a full qualification later.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“a growing number of institutions...let learners complete a bundle of short online modules...then combine the credits into a full qualification later”
longSentences.modifiers: “Instead of requiring four continuous years on campus”为介词短语作状语，表对比；“whenever it suits them”为时间状语从句；let后接两个并列的动词原形complete和combine，共享主语institutions。
longSentences.translation: 越来越多的院校不再要求学生连续在校学习四年，而是让学习者按照自己的时间随时完成一系列简短的在线模块课程，之后再将学分合并成一个完整的学位资格。
longSentences.tip: let后面接了两个并列的动词原形complete和combine，翻译时要看清是institutions先后允许学习者做这两件事。

longSentences.sentence: Supporters argue that this flexible approach makes institutional learning more accessible, easing the financial and time pressures that once forced many capable students to give up their studies.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“Supporters argue that this flexible approach makes institutional learning more accessible”
longSentences.modifiers: “easing the financial and time pressures...”为现在分词短语作伴随状语，说明结果；“that once forced many capable students to give up their studies”为定语从句修饰pressures。
longSentences.translation: 支持者认为，这种灵活的方式让制度化学习变得更容易获得，从而缓解了曾经迫使许多有能力的学生被迫放弃学业的经济和时间压力。
longSentences.tip: easing...是分词短语表结果，可译为“从而……”；随后的that从句修饰的是pressures，注意按就近原则判断先行词。

longSentences.sentence: Critics, however, warn that scattered online modules cannot fully replace the discipline built through sustained classroom study, and that some employers still hesitate to treat such qualifications as equal in value.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“Critics warn that...and that...”
longSentences.modifiers: warn后接两个由and连接的并列that宾语从句；第一个从句中“built through sustained classroom study”为过去分词短语修饰discipline。
longSentences.translation: 然而，批评者警告说，零散的在线学习模块无法完全取代通过持续课堂学习所培养的自律，而且一些雇主仍然不愿将这类资格视为同等价值。
longSentences.tip: warn跟了两个并列的that从句，分别对应两个不同角度的担忧，翻译时可拆成两句分别处理。

items.title: A City Policy to Rescue Food
items.passage:
Every year, tons of fresh vegetables are thrown away on farms simply because they look imperfect or arrive at market too late. To fight this waste, many city governments have launched a food-rescue policy. Instead of letting good food end up in the trash, workers collect it and send it to food banks, which now run branches across the city.

The process is not easy. Refrigerated trucks visit nearby farms each morning, and drivers check a small recorder that tracks the inside temperature, since a hot afternoon can let bacteria grow fast on fresh produce. Volunteers wearing masks sort abundant crops such as green beans and tomatoes, cutting large ones into a slice or two so every family gets a fair share. It is hard work, but to reward farmers who donate, the government offers a tax credit.

Not everyone supports the policy. Some officials argue that public money should not fund what they call a political project instead of a real cure for poverty. Even so, public trust keeps growing, because families who once had nowhere to turn for a decent meal can now visit a nearby branch every week and receive real help.
items.translation:
每年，大量新鲜蔬菜仅仅因为外观不够完美，或送达市场的时间稍晚，就在农场里被白白扔掉。为了应对这种浪费，许多城市政府启动了一项“食物救援”公共政策。这些工作人员不再让优质食物进垃圾桶，而是把它们收集起来，送往食物银行，如今食物银行已在全市设立了多个分支机构。

这一过程并不轻松。冷藏卡车每天早上都要前往附近的农场，司机会查看车上一个小小的记录仪，以追踪车厢内的温度，因为哪怕一个炎热的下午，也可能让细菌在新鲜农产品上迅速滋生。戴着口罩的志愿者们徒手将丰富的农产品（如菜豆、西红柿）分拣归类，把大块蔬菜切成一片片，好让每个家庭都能分到公平的一份。这是艰苦的工作，但为了奖励捐赠食物的农户，政府会给予他们税收抵免。

并不是所有人都支持这项政策。一些官员认为，公共资金不应该用来资助他们眼中的“政治项目”，而不是真正解决贫困问题的办法。尽管如此，公众对该项目的信任却在不断增长，因为曾经无处可去、吃不上一顿像样饭菜的家庭，如今每周都能去附近的分支机构获得实实在在的帮助。

keyWords.zh: n. 信贷；信用；（税收）抵免
ad. 什么地方都没有，无处

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: A new law that bans the sale of imperfect vegetables.
A city policy that collects surplus farm food and gives it to those in need.
A study on how bacteria spreads in refrigerated trucks.
A guide for farmers on how to grow more beans.
questions.explain: 全文（尤其第一段）主旨是介绍一项收集农场剩余食物并分发给有需要家庭的城市政策，故选B；A、C、D均为局部细节或原文未提及的内容。

questions.q: According to the passage, why do drivers check the recorder in the truck?
options: To calculate how many vegetables have been collected.
To make sure the truck arrives at the farm on time.
To monitor the temperature and prevent bacteria growth.
To record how many volunteers are wearing masks.
questions.explain: 第二段提到司机查看记录仪追踪车厢温度，因为温度过高会导致细菌滋生，故选C，依据在第二段。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the passage that some officials oppose the policy because ______.
options: they believe donated food is not safe to eat.
they think it is more of a political move than a real solution to poverty.
they worry that farmers will lose money.
they doubt that food banks can operate branches efficiently.
questions.explain: 第三段提到部分官员认为该政策更像“政治项目”而非真正解决贫困的办法，故选B，依据在第三段。

questions.q: In the sentence "families who once had nowhere to turn for a decent meal," the word "nowhere" is closest in meaning to ______.
options: no place
no time
no reason
no money
questions.explain: nowhere意为“没有地方”，与no place对应，此处指这些家庭曾经找不到地方获得像样的饭菜，依据在第三段。

longSentences.sentence: Refrigerated trucks visit nearby farms each morning, and drivers check a small recorder that tracks the inside temperature, since a hot afternoon can let bacteria grow fast on fresh produce.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列句主干：“冷藏卡车每天早上前往附近农场，司机们检查一个小记录仪。”
longSentences.modifiers: “that tracks the inside temperature”是定语从句，修饰recorder；“since a hot afternoon can let bacteria grow fast on fresh produce”是原因状语从句，说明检查记录仪的原因。
longSentences.translation: 冷藏卡车每天早上前往附近的农场，司机们会查看一个用来追踪车厢内温度的小记录仪，因为哪怕一个炎热的下午也可能让细菌在新鲜农产品上迅速滋生。
longSentences.tip: 遇到that/since引导的从句，先抓住主句的主谓宾，再分别处理定语从句和原因状语从句。

longSentences.sentence: Volunteers wearing masks sort abundant crops such as green beans and tomatoes, cutting large ones into a slice or two so every family gets a fair share.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：“志愿者们分拣丰富的农产品。”
longSentences.modifiers: “wearing masks”是现在分词短语作后置定语，修饰主语Volunteers；“cutting large ones into a slice or two”是现在分词短语作伴随状语；“so every family gets a fair share”是结果状语从句。
longSentences.translation: 戴着口罩的志愿者们对丰富的农产品（如菜豆和西红柿）进行分拣，并把大块的蔬菜切成一片片，好让每个家庭都能分到公平的一份。
longSentences.tip: 句中多个现在分词短语（wearing…、cutting…）不是谓语，只是修饰或伴随状语，翻译时可转换成“……的”或“并……”的动作。

longSentences.sentence: Some officials argue that public money should not fund what they call a political project instead of a real cure for poverty.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：“一些官员认为……。”（Some officials argue that...）
longSentences.modifiers: “that public money should not fund...”是argue的宾语从句；“what they call a political project”是fund的宾语，由what引导的名词性从句；“instead of a real cure for poverty”表比较对象。
longSentences.translation: 一些官员认为，公共资金不应该用来资助他们所谓的“政治项目”，而不是真正治愈贫困的办法。
longSentences.tip: 遇到双层从句时先找主句谓语argue，再依次剥离that从句和what从句，逐层翻译。

items.title: Restoring Old Clothes, Building a Business
items.passage:
Five years ago, Mia Chen made what many of her friends called a rash decision: she quit a stable job in finance to open a small shop that restores old clothing. At the time, few people believed a single wardrobe of forgotten garments could signify the start of a real business. Her transition from spreadsheets to sewing machines seemed to disregard everything she had been taught about a safe career, yet she was determined to prove them wrong.

Instead of following fast-fashion trends, Mia searches flea markets and family closets for garments made of exotic fabrics that carry a story. She restores each piece by hand, and every finished item feels unique enough to arouse curiosity. This approach stands out even more today, since many nearby malls have lost store after store; empty windows have turned into a kind of cemetery for brands that failed to adapt and finally collapsed.

Mia's small shop now attracts individual customers who want clothing with a personal history rather than something mass-produced. Her story has also begun to enlighten a new generation of serial entrepreneurs who see restoration, not invention, as a path worth trying. For Mia herself, the shop has become more than a job; it feels like a lifetime commitment to giving old things a second chance.
items.translation:
五年前，米娅·陈做出了一个被许多朋友称为“轻率”的决定：她辞去了金融行业的稳定工作，开了一家专门修复旧衣物的小店。当时，几乎没人相信，一柜被遗忘的旧衣服能预示着一门真正生意的开端。她从做电子表格转向踩缝纫机的这次转型，看起来完全无视了她曾接受过的“稳妥职业”教育，但她仍然下定决心要证明大家错了。

米娅没有追随快时尚潮流，而是在跳蚤市场和各家的衣柜里寻找那些用异域面料制成、带着故事的衣物。她亲手修复每一件衣服，成品都独一无二，足以唤起人们的好奇心。如今这种做法显得格外突出，因为附近的商场接连失去一家又一家门店；空荡荡的橱窗仿佛变成了一座“墓地”，埋葬着那些未能及时转型、最终倒闭的品牌。

米娅的小店如今吸引了众多渴望拥有带个人历史的服装、而非流水线产品的顾客。她的故事也开始启发新一代连续创业者，他们开始把“修复”而非“发明”视为一条值得尝试的路。对米娅本人而言，这家店已不仅仅是一份工作，更像是一份用一生去践行的承诺——让旧物获得第二次生命。

keyWords.zh: a. 外来的，异国情调的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The history of fast-fashion brands in shopping malls.
How an entrepreneur built a business around restoring old clothing.
Reasons why shopping malls are closing down across the country.
A guide to sewing techniques for beginners.
questions.explain: 全文围绕米娅辞职创业、修复旧衣服并获得成功的故事展开，故选B；A、C为背景细节，D原文未提及。

questions.q: According to the passage, where does Mia find the clothes she restores?
options: Only from designer boutiques.
From flea markets and family closets.
From shopping malls that have closed.
From a factory that makes exotic fabrics.
questions.explain: 第二段明确提到米娅在跳蚤市场和家庭衣柜中寻找衣物，故选B，依据在第二段。

questions.q: It can be inferred that Mia's friends originally thought her decision to quit her job was ______.
options: wise and well-planned
unwise and hasty
required by her employer
related to a family emergency
questions.explain: 第一段提到朋友们认为这是一个“rash decision”（轻率的决定），可推知他们认为此举不明智、过于草率，故选B，依据在第一段。

questions.q: In the sentence "empty windows have turned into a kind of cemetery for brands that failed to adapt and finally collapsed," the word "cemetery" is used to suggest that these brands ______.
options: moved to a better location
became extremely profitable
are figuratively 'dead' as businesses
donated their old stock to charity
questions.explain: cemetery本义为“墓地”，此处比喻那些倒闭品牌如同被埋葬，喻指其作为企业已经“死亡”，故选C，依据在第二段。

longSentences.sentence: Five years ago, Mia Chen made what many of her friends called a rash decision: she quit a stable job in finance to open a small shop that restores old clothing.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：“米娅·陈做出了一个决定。”（Mia Chen made a decision）
longSentences.modifiers: “what many of her friends called a rash decision”是what引导的名词性从句，作made的宾语，说明这个决定被朋友们称为“轻率的”；冒号后内容是对这个决定的具体解释；“that restores old clothing”是定语从句修饰shop。
longSentences.translation: 五年前，米娅·陈做出了一个被许多朋友称为“轻率”的决定：她辞去了金融行业的稳定工作，开了一家专门修复旧衣物的小店。
longSentences.tip: 遇到what引导的名词性从句作宾语时，先把从句整体当作一个名词短语来理解，再看冒号后的具体解释内容。

longSentences.sentence: Instead of following fast-fashion trends, Mia searches flea markets and family closets for garments made of exotic fabrics that carry a story.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：“米娅在跳蚤市场和家庭衣柜中寻找衣物。”
longSentences.modifiers: “Instead of following fast-fashion trends”是介词短语作方式状语，表示“不追随快时尚”；“made of exotic fabrics”是过去分词短语修饰garments，说明衣物的材质；“that carry a story”是定语从句修饰fabrics，说明面料承载着故事。
longSentences.translation: 米娅没有追随快时尚潮流，而是在跳蚤市场和各家衣柜里寻找那些用带着故事的异域面料制成的衣物。
longSentences.tip: 分辨过去分词短语（made of…）与定语从句（that carry…）分别修饰的对象，逐层剥离再翻译。

longSentences.sentence: Her story has also begun to enlighten a new generation of serial entrepreneurs who see restoration, not invention, as a path worth trying.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：“她的故事开始启发新一代连续创业者。”
longSentences.modifiers: “who see restoration, not invention, as a path worth trying”是定语从句修饰entrepreneurs；从句中“not invention”是插入的对比成分，强调是“修复”而非“发明”；“worth trying”是后置形容词短语修饰path。
longSentences.translation: 她的故事也开始启发新一代连续创业者，他们把“修复”而非“发明”视为一条值得尝试的路。
longSentences.tip: 插入语（如not invention）在阅读时可以先跳过抓主干，再回头理解其对比或补充作用。

items.title: An Old House Draws Tourists
items.passage:
At the edge of a small coastal town stands a two-hundred-year-old house known locally as the Grey House. Its peculiar roofline and a wide, conspicuous porch that wraps around three sides of the building make it one of the most photographed spots in the region. For decades the house sat empty, but ten years ago the town council decided to restore it and open it to visitors.

Turning the old house into a museum was not simple. The council held a public contest to choose the best restoration design, and the winning team worked carefully to enforce strict rules that would protect the original wooden floors and windows. Builders also had to plan around adverse weather, since heavy coastal storms could damage unfinished work overnight. When the first restoration architect retired, a successor was chosen to complete the final stage of the project.

Today, visitors often murmur in surprise when they step through the front door and see how much of the house remains unchanged. Local guides claim that the museum has become one of the most significant reasons tourists choose to stop in the town rather than simply pass through. What began as a single old house has quietly grown into a symbol the whole community is proud of.
items.translation:
在一个小海滨小镇的边缘，矗立着一栋有着两百年历史的房子，当地人称之为“灰宅”。它那奇特的屋顶轮廓，以及环绕建筑三面、十分醒目的门廊，使这里成为该地区被拍照次数最多的地点之一。这栋房子曾空置了几十年，但十年前，镇议会决定将其修复并对外开放。

把这栋老房子改造成博物馆并不简单。镇议会举办了一场公开竞赛，评选出最佳修复方案，中标团队小心翼翼地严格执行各项规定，以保护原有的木地板和窗户。建筑工人还必须应对不利的天气，因为沿海地区的暴风雨可能在一夜之间破坏尚未完工的部分。当第一位修复建筑师退休后，接任者被选出，负责完成项目的最后阶段。

如今，游客们走进大门，看到房子里有这么多地方保持原貌时，常常会惊讶地低声议论。当地导游称，这座博物馆已成为游客选择在小镇停留、而不是匆匆路过的最重要原因之一。这栋曾经只是一间普通老房子的建筑，如今已悄然成长为整个社区引以为豪的象征。

keyWords.zh: a. 显著的，显而易见的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The daily life of people living in a coastal town.
How an old house was restored and became an important attraction.
A competition to design a new museum building from scratch.
The reasons coastal storms damage old buildings.
questions.explain: 全文讲述了灰宅从空置到被修复、最终成为吸引游客重要景点的过程，故选B；A、D为局部或无关信息，C与原文不符（原文是修复而非新建）。

questions.q: According to the passage, why did builders have to plan around adverse weather?
options: Because tourists refused to visit in the rain.
Because heavy coastal storms could damage the unfinished restoration work.
Because the council required work to stop during storms.
Because the original windows could not withstand wind at all.
questions.explain: 第二段提到建筑工人须应对不利天气，因为沿海暴风雨可能在一夜之间破坏尚未完工的部分，故选B，依据在第二段。

questions.q: It can be inferred that the town council held a public contest mainly in order to ______.
options: entertain local residents during the summer
select the best plan for restoring the house
raise money to buy the old house
find a new site for a museum
questions.explain: 第二段提到镇议会举办公开竞赛以“选出最佳修复方案”，可推知竞赛目的是挑选最优修复设计，故选B，依据在第二段。

questions.q: In the sentence "visitors often murmur in surprise when they step through the front door," the word "murmur" most likely means ______.
options: shout loudly
speak in a low voice
remain completely silent
argue with each other
questions.explain: murmur指“低声说话”，与speak in a low voice意思相近，指游客们看到房屋保存完好时的低声惊叹，故选B，依据在第三段。

longSentences.sentence: Its peculiar roofline and a wide, conspicuous porch that wraps around three sides of the building make it one of the most photographed spots in the region.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：“它的屋顶轮廓和门廊使这里成为该地区拍照最多的地点之一。”
longSentences.modifiers: “peculiar”和“wide, conspicuous”分别修饰并列主语roofline和porch；“that wraps around three sides of the building”是定语从句，修饰porch，说明门廊环绕建筑三面。
longSentences.translation: 它那奇特的屋顶轮廓，以及那环绕建筑三面、十分醒目的宽阔门廊，使这里成为该地区被拍照次数最多的地点之一。
longSentences.tip: 遇到并列主语加定语从句的长句，先找出两个并列主语和共同谓语，再单独处理定语从句的修饰内容。

longSentences.sentence: The council held a public contest to choose the best restoration design, and the winning team worked carefully to enforce strict rules that would protect the original wooden floors and windows.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：“镇议会举办了一场公开竞赛，中标团队努力执行严格的规定。”
longSentences.modifiers: “to choose the best restoration design”是不定式短语作目的状语，说明举办竞赛的目的；“to enforce strict rules”是不定式短语作目的状语；“that would protect the original wooden floors and windows”是定语从句修饰rules，说明规定的作用。
longSentences.translation: 镇议会举办了一场公开竞赛，以挑选出最佳修复方案，中标团队则小心翼翼地执行严格的规定，以保护原有的木地板和窗户。
longSentences.tip: 两个并列分句各自带有不定式目的状语，翻译时可分别译成“为了……”，使逻辑更清晰。

longSentences.sentence: What began as a single old house has quietly grown into a symbol the whole community is proud of.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：“最初只是一栋老房子的东西，如今已悄然成长为一个象征。”
longSentences.modifiers: “What began as a single old house”是what引导的主语从句，作整句主语；“the whole community is proud of”是省略了关系代词的定语从句，修饰symbol，还原为“a symbol that the whole community is proud of”。
longSentences.translation: 这栋曾经只是一间普通老房子的建筑，如今已悄然成长为整个社区引以为豪的象征。
longSentences.tip: 注意定语从句中省略关系代词的情况（symbol后可补出that/which），先补全再翻译更清楚。

items.title: 3D Printers Aid Rural Clinics
items.passage:
A small team of engineers in a rural workshop has built a low-cost 3D printer that is already changing how remote clinics get medical tools. Instead of waiting weeks for parts to arrive by slow transportation, doctors can now print what they need on site. The printer's simplicity is its biggest selling point: a single technician can set it up in under an hour, with almost no training required.

The machine works by pushing a thick paste through a heated nozzle in a process similar to injection, building an object layer by layer. Early versions produced only crude, rough shapes, but later models became precise enough to print splints and simple surgical tools. The idea first came from an unexpected event: after a bus crash left wreckage on a mountain road, local doctors lacked a replacement part for broken equipment and had to wait two days for a new one.

That delay gave the team its first real hint that speed could save lives, and it pushed them to keep improving the design. A deputy from the regional health department recently visited the workshop and praised the project publicly, calling it a model for the whole province. The team hopes that within a few years, every rural clinic will have its own printer ready for the next emergency.
items.translation:
在一个乡村工作坊里，一支小型工程师团队制造出了一台低成本的3D打印机，它正在改变偏远诊所获取医疗器械的方式。医生们不必再花上数周时间等待零件通过缓慢的运输方式送达，如今可以现场打印所需的东西。这台打印机最大的卖点就是操作简单：一名技术员在不到一小时内就能完成安装，几乎不需要任何培训。

这台机器的工作原理是将黏稠的膏状材料通过一个加热喷嘴挤出，其过程类似于注射成型，逐层堆叠形成物体。早期版本只能制作出粗糙、简陋的形状，但后来的型号已经能够精确地打印出夹板等简单的手术器械。这个想法最初源于一场意外事件：一次大巴事故在山路上留下了残骸，当地医生缺少替换零件来修复损坏的设备，不得不等待两天才拿到新零件。

这次延误给了团队第一个真正的启示——速度可以挽救生命，也促使他们不断改进设计。地区卫生部门的一位副部长最近参观了这家工作坊，并公开称赞该项目，称其为全省的典范。团队希望在几年之内，每一家乡村诊所都能拥有一台属于自己的打印机，随时应对下一次紧急情况。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A team using a low-cost 3D printer to solve medical supply problems in rural clinics.
The history of injection molding technology in factories.
A government policy that bans slow transportation in rural areas.
A bus accident that damaged medical equipment permanently.
questions.explain: 全文讲述工程师团队研发低成本3D打印机，解决偏远诊所医疗器械短缺问题，故选A；B、C、D均为文中局部细节或原文未提及的内容。

questions.q: According to the passage, what first gave the team the idea to develop the printer?
options: A request from the regional health department.
A delay in getting a replacement part after a bus crash.
A shortage of trained technicians in rural clinics.
A competition organized by a technology company.
questions.explain: 第二段提到该想法源于一场大巴事故后当地医生等待两天才拿到替换零件的经历，故选B，依据在第二段。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the passage that the printer's simplicity is important mainly because ______.
options: it allows the printer to be sold at a very high price.
it lets people with little training operate the printer quickly.
it makes the printer too complicated for technicians to use.
it prevents the printer from being used in emergencies.
questions.explain: 第一段提到该打印机操作简单，一名技术员不到一小时即可完成安装且几乎不需要培训，可推知简单易操作是其重要优势，故选B，依据在第一段。

questions.q: In the sentence "Early versions produced only crude, rough shapes," the word "crude" is closest in meaning to ______.
options: expensive
roughly made
brightly colored
extremely fast
questions.explain: crude意为“粗糙的，未加工的”，与rough同义，此处指早期打印机做出的形状粗糙简陋，故选B，依据在第二段。

longSentences.sentence: A small team of engineers in a rural workshop has built a low-cost 3D printer that is already changing how remote clinics get medical tools.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：“一支工程师团队制造出了一台低成本3D打印机。”
longSentences.modifiers: “in a rural workshop”是介词短语作地点状语，修饰team；“that is already changing how remote clinics get medical tools”是定语从句修饰printer，从句谓语changing后又跟了一个how引导的宾语从句。
longSentences.translation: 在一个乡村工作坊里，一支小型工程师团队制造出了一台低成本的3D打印机，它正在改变偏远诊所获取医疗器械的方式。
longSentences.tip: 定语从句中嵌套宾语从句时，先确定定语从句的主干（that is changing…），再处理内部的how从句。

longSentences.sentence: The idea first came from an unexpected event: after a bus crash left wreckage on a mountain road, local doctors lacked a replacement part for broken equipment and had to wait two days for a new one.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：“这个想法最初源于一场意外事件。”冒号后具体说明事件内容：“当地医生缺少替换零件，不得不等待两天。”
longSentences.modifiers: “after a bus crash left wreckage on a mountain road”是时间状语从句，交代事件发生的背景；“lacked a replacement part for broken equipment”和“had to wait two days for a new one”是并列谓语，共同陈述医生遇到的困境。
longSentences.translation: 这个想法最初源于一场意外事件：一次大巴事故在山路上留下了残骸，当地医生缺少替换零件来修复损坏的设备，不得不等待两天才拿到新零件。
longSentences.tip: 冒号后往往是对前文的具体解释，可先读懂主句大意，再通过冒号内容补充细节。

longSentences.sentence: The team hopes that within a few years, every rural clinic will have its own printer ready for the next emergency.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：“团队希望……。”（The team hopes that...）
longSentences.modifiers: “within a few years”是时间状语，插入在从句主谓之间；“ready for the next emergency”是形容词短语作宾语补足语，修饰printer，说明打印机应处于随时可用的状态。
longSentences.translation: 团队希望在几年之内，每一家乡村诊所都能拥有一台属于自己的、随时应对下一次紧急情况的打印机。
longSentences.tip: 宾语从句中如有插入的时间状语，可先跳过，抓住“每家诊所将拥有打印机”的主干，再补充其余修饰成分。

items.title: The Real Challenges of Remote Work
items.passage:
When companies first allowed employees to work from home, many managers showed strong resistance, fearing that productivity would drop once staff left the office. Surveys conducted over the past few years, however, have found the opposite: workers who telecommute often report longer, more focused hours, provided they are given the right tools and support.

Still, remote work poses genuine challenges. Employees who spend most of the day alone can feel as if they are operating in a vacuum, cut off from the casual conversations and peer feedback that once shaped their thinking. Strong self-discipline is required to keep a project moving without a supervisor looking over one's shoulder, and companies have had to unify scattered messaging apps, shared drives, and video platforms into a single system so that teams do not waste hours searching for information.

To address these gaps, many organizations are revising their management practices. Deadlines for the submission of reports are now paired with regular video check-ins, and mentors are encouraged to reach out to junior colleagues proactively rather than waiting to be approached. Far from blocking careers, thoughtful remote policies can even speed advancement, since employees are increasingly judged by the quality of their output rather than by the hours they spend visible at a desk.
items.translation:
当公司最初允许员工居家办公时，许多管理者表现出强烈的抵触情绪，担心一旦员工离开办公室，工作效率就会下降。然而，过去几年进行的调查却发现了相反的结果：远程办公的员工往往报告说自己工作时间更长、更加专注，前提是他们获得了合适的工具和支持。

不过，远程办公也带来了真实的挑战。大部分时间独自一人的员工可能会感觉自己仿佛置身于真空之中，被隔绝在曾经塑造他们思维的日常闲谈和同事反馈之外。在没有主管在旁监督的情况下推进项目，需要很强的自律；公司也不得不将分散的即时通讯软件、共享云盘和视频平台统一整合为一个系统，以免团队浪费大量时间去查找信息。

为了弥补这些不足，许多机构正在修订其管理方式。报告提交的截止日期现在都配有定期的视频沟通环节，导师们也被鼓励主动联系初级同事，而不是坐等对方求助。深思熟虑的远程办公政策非但不会阻碍职业发展，反而可能加快晋升的步伐，因为员工越来越多地是凭借产出质量而非在办公桌前露面的时长来评判的。

keyWords.zh: n. 自我，自身（self-discipline 自律）

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The reasons companies banned remote work initially
The technical tools used for video conferencing
The benefits and challenges of remote work and how companies are adapting
A comparison between telecommuting and traditional commuting costs
questions.explain: 全文围绕远程办公展开：第一段讲态度转变，第二段讲挑战，第三段讲应对措施，故主旨为C（第1-3段）。

questions.q: According to paragraph 1, what did surveys find about employees who work from home?
options: They tend to report shorter working hours
They often report longer and more focused working hours
They are less productive than expected
They rarely use the tools provided by companies
questions.explain: 依据第一段最后一句，调查发现居家办公员工往往报告工作时间更长、更专注，答案为B（第1段）。

questions.q: It can be inferred from paragraph 2 that working "in a vacuum" mainly refers to __.
options: working in a place without air conditioning
being physically isolated from colleagues and missing everyday interaction
using outdated communication software
working overtime without breaks
questions.explain: 结合上下文“cut off from the casual conversations and peer feedback”，可推断“a vacuum”指与同事隔绝、缺乏日常互动的状态，选B（第2段）。

questions.q: The word "unify" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to __.
options: separate
combine into one
delete
duplicate
questions.explain: 根据上下文“unify scattered messaging apps...into a single system”，可知unify意为把分散的整合为一体，故选B（第2段）。

longSentences.sentence: Employees who spend most of the day alone can feel as if they are operating in a vacuum, cut off from the casual conversations and peer feedback that once shaped their thinking.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Employees can feel as if they are operating in a vacuum
longSentences.modifiers: who spend most of the day alone 为定语从句修饰Employees；cut off from...为过去分词短语作伴随状语，补充说明置身真空的状态；that once shaped their thinking为定语从句修饰conversations and peer feedback
longSentences.translation: 大部分时间独自一人的员工可能会感觉自己仿佛置身于真空之中，被隔绝在曾经塑造他们思维的日常闲谈和同事反馈之外。
longSentences.tip: 先抓主谓宾“员工感觉像是在真空中”，再逐层剥离定语从句和分词状语。

longSentences.sentence: Far from blocking careers, thoughtful remote policies can even speed advancement, since employees are increasingly judged by the quality of their output rather than by the hours they spend visible at a desk.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：thoughtful remote policies can even speed advancement
longSentences.modifiers: Far from blocking careers为介词短语作状语，表让步转折；since引导原因状语从句；从句中rather than by...为比较结构，they spend visible at a desk为省略关系代词的定语从句修饰hours
longSentences.translation: 深思熟虑的远程办公政策非但不会阻碍职业发展，反而可能加快晋升的步伐，因为员工越来越多地是凭借产出质量而非在办公桌前露面的时长来评判的。
longSentences.tip: 注意“Far from doing”表示“远非，非但不”的让步转折，不要理解成字面“远离”。

items.title: Supporting Refugees Through Public Policy
items.passage:
Across the world, millions of people are forced to leave their homes each year, often because a repressive government has made ordinary life unbearable. Wars, persecution, and economic collapse in their country of origin push many refugees toward safer nations, where public policy must decide how newcomers will be received.

One of the most well-known forms of support is free legal aid. Volunteer attorneys spend hundreds of hours preparing asylum cases, since a single missing document can determine whether a family is allowed to stay. This assistance is particularly valuable for refugees who arrive without money, contacts, or knowledge of the local language, and who would otherwise struggle to even locate the correct government office, let alone identify the original source of a legal requirement.

Resettlement, however, is only the first step. Studies tracking refugee households over a decade consistently show that early earnings are low, as qualifications from the country of origin are frequently not recognized abroad. Yet with training programs and community support, most families see their circumstances grow steadily better, and their children often go on to make lasting contributions to their new communities.
items.translation:
世界各地每年都有数百万人被迫离开家园，往往是因为压迫性的政府使得平常的生活变得难以忍受。战争、迫害以及原籍国的经济崩溃，将许多难民推向更安全的国家，而这些国家的公共政策必须决定该如何接纳这些新来者。

其中最著名的一种支持形式是免费法律援助。志愿律师花费数百个小时来准备庇护申请案件，因为哪怕缺少一份文件，都可能决定一个家庭能否获准留下。这种援助对那些身无分文、举目无亲、又不懂当地语言的难民尤其宝贵，否则他们甚至很难找到正确的政府部门，更不用说弄清楚某项法律要求最初的出处了。

然而，安置只是第一步。追踪难民家庭长达十年的研究一致显示，难民初期的收入普遍偏低，因为他们在原籍国获得的资质往往得不到当地承认。但借助培训项目和社区支持，大多数家庭的境况会逐步改善，他们的子女也常常能为新的社区做出持久的贡献。

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Refugees always struggle to find legal representation
The causes, support systems, and long-term outcomes of refugee resettlement
Why repressive governments cause economic collapse
How to become a volunteer attorney
questions.explain: 第一段讲难民产生的原因，第二段讲法律援助，第三段讲安置后的长期结果，三段共同支持主旨B（第1-3段）。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, why is legal aid particularly valuable to refugees?
options: Because refugees prefer not to pay for lawyers
Because refugees often lack money, contacts, and knowledge of the local language
Because volunteer attorneys work faster than paid ones
Because refugees are not allowed to apply for asylum alone
questions.explain: 依据第二段“particularly valuable for refugees who arrive without money, contacts, or knowledge of the local language”，答案为B（第2段）。

questions.q: It can be inferred from paragraph 3 that refugees' early earnings are low mainly because __.
options: they are unwilling to work
their qualifications from their home country are often not recognized
local laws forbid refugees from being employed
training programs are too expensive to attend
questions.explain: 依据第三段“as qualifications from the country of origin are frequently not recognized abroad”，可推知答案为B（第3段）。

questions.q: In paragraph 1, the word "repressive" most nearly means __.
options: generous and open
harsh and controlling
financially unstable
newly established
questions.explain: 结合上下文“a repressive government has made ordinary life unbearable”，可知repressive指政府专制压迫、使生活难以忍受，故选B（第1段）。

longSentences.sentence: This assistance is particularly valuable for refugees who arrive without money, contacts, or knowledge of the local language, and who would otherwise struggle to even locate the correct government office, let alone identify the original source of a legal requirement.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：This assistance is particularly valuable for refugees
longSentences.modifiers: 两个由who引导的并列定语从句修饰refugees：who arrive without money...和who would otherwise struggle to...；let alone...表示“更不用说”，引出程度更深的情况
longSentences.translation: 这种援助对那些身无分文、举目无亲、又不懂当地语言的难民尤其宝贵，否则他们甚至很难找到正确的政府部门，更不用说弄清楚某项法律要求最初的出处了。
longSentences.tip: 遇到并列的两个who从句，先分别理清各自修饰的内容，再看let alone引出的递进关系。

longSentences.sentence: Studies tracking refugee households over a decade consistently show that early earnings are low, as qualifications from the country of origin are frequently not recognized abroad.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Studies show that early earnings are low
longSentences.modifiers: tracking refugee households over a decade为现在分词短语作后置定语修饰Studies；that early earnings are low为show的宾语从句；as引导原因状语从句
longSentences.translation: 追踪难民家庭长达十年的研究一致显示，难民初期的收入普遍偏低，因为他们在原籍国获得的资质往往得不到当地承认。
longSentences.tip: 先找准句子主干“研究显示……”，分词短语和as原因状语从句都是补充说明，不影响主干判断。

items.title: Aging Towns and Vanishing Trades
items.passage:
In many small towns, population aging is no longer a distant statistic; it is now apparent in daily life. Walk down a quiet street and you may pass a bakery where an elderly merchant still sells warm loaves each morning, or a workshop where an aging carpenter repairs furniture exactly as he did forty years ago. Both would happily train a successor, but almost no young person applies.

The problem reaches far beyond a single shop. As the working-age population shrinks, the labor market struggles to attract enough new workers to replace those retiring, and in some regions entire trades risk dying out within a generation. Local leaders have begun testing tentative solutions, such as offering scholarships to students willing to learn traditional skills instead of moving to big cities for office jobs.

Whether such programs will raise the number of young apprentices to earlier standards remains uncertain. Supporters argue that even modest success is worthwhile, since a town that loses its bakers and carpenters loses more than convenience: it loses a visible link to its own history and identity.
items.translation:
在许多小城镇，人口老龄化已不再是一个遥远的统计数字，如今它已在日常生活中显现出来。走在一条安静的街道上，你可能会经过一家面包店，店里一位年迈的商贩每天早上仍在出售热乎乎的面包；也可能经过一间作坊，一位上了年纪的木匠仍像四十年前一样在修理家具。两人都很乐意培养接班人，但几乎没有年轻人前来应聘。

这个问题远不止关乎一家小店。随着劳动年龄人口的萎缩，劳动力市场难以吸引足够多的新工人来接替即将退休的人，一些地区的整个行业都面临在一代人之内消失的风险。当地领导人已开始试行一些试验性的解决办法，比如向愿意学习传统技艺、而不是搬去大城市从事办公室工作的学生提供奖学金。

这些项目能否将年轻学徒的人数提升到以往的水平，目前仍不确定。支持者认为，即使只取得有限的成效也是值得的，因为一个失去面包师和木匠的城镇失去的不仅仅是生活的便利，它还失去了一条通向自身历史与身份认同的可见纽带。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The decline of traditional bakeries in big cities
How population aging threatens the survival of traditional trades in small towns
A new scholarship program for city students
The rising cost of furniture repair
questions.explain: 全文围绕小城镇因人口老龄化导致面包店、木匠铺等传统行业后继无人展开，故B为主旨（第1-3段）。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, what have local leaders begun to do?
options: Close down failing shops immediately
Offer scholarships to encourage students to learn traditional skills
Move traditional workshops to big cities
Ban young people from working in offices
questions.explain: 依据第二段“offering scholarships to students willing to learn traditional skills instead of moving to big cities”，答案为B（第2段）。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the merchant and the carpenter mentioned in paragraph 1?
options: They are planning to retire without training anyone
They are eager to find a successor but cannot find young applicants
They have already found young apprentices
They are moving their businesses to another town
questions.explain: 依据第一段“Both would happily train a successor, but almost no young person applies.”，可推知答案为B（第1段）。

questions.q: In paragraph 3, the phrase "a visible link to its own history and identity" most likely means __.
options: a modern road connecting old and new districts
a museum that displays historical documents
a tangible connection that keeps a town's traditions and identity alive
a legal requirement to preserve old buildings
questions.explain: 结合全文语境，面包店、木匠铺等传统行业的存续被视为城镇历史与身份认同的具体体现，故指看得见的、维系传统与身份的纽带，选C（第3段）。

longSentences.sentence: As the working-age population shrinks, the labor market struggles to attract enough new workers to replace those retiring, and in some regions entire trades risk dying out within a generation.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：the labor market struggles to attract enough new workers...，and entire trades risk dying out...（并列句）
longSentences.modifiers: As the working-age population shrinks为时间/原因状语从句；to replace those retiring为不定式作目的状语，retiring为现在分词作定语修饰those；within a generation为时间状语
longSentences.translation: 随着劳动年龄人口的萎缩，劳动力市场难以吸引足够多的新工人来接替即将退休的人，一些地区的整个行业都面临在一代人之内消失的风险。
longSentences.tip: 句子由and连接两个并列分句，先分别找出各自的主谓宾，再处理前面的as状语从句。

longSentences.sentence: Local leaders have begun testing tentative solutions, such as offering scholarships to students willing to learn traditional skills instead of moving to big cities for office jobs.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Local leaders have begun testing tentative solutions
longSentences.modifiers: such as offering scholarships...为举例状语，动名词短语作such as的宾语；willing to learn traditional skills为形容词短语作students的后置定语；instead of moving to big cities for office jobs为介词短语表对比选择
longSentences.translation: 当地领导人已开始试行一些试验性的解决办法，比如向愿意学习传统技艺、而不是搬去大城市从事办公室工作的学生提供奖学金。
longSentences.tip: such as后面跟的动名词短语是解决办法的具体例子，可以先跳过例子抓住主干，再回头理解举例内容。

items.title: Epidemics Demand Patience, Not Quick Fixes
items.passage:
When an epidemic spreads quickly through a population, health officials often focus on one goal above all others: flattening the curve so hospitals are not overwhelmed by patients arriving all at once. Restrictions on large public assembly, once considered an extreme measure, quickly become common practice, and citizens are obliged to adjust daily habits that took decades to form.

Behind the scenes, pharmaceutical giants race to develop treatments. Experimental drugs move through trials at unusual speed, though scientists caution that no single medicine works like a magical cure; a meaningful reduction in deaths usually comes from combining several imperfect tools rather than waiting for one perfect one. Even a promising vaccine can be derailed by manufacturing delays or public doubts about its usage.

Perhaps the most important question is how durable any protection turns out to be. Immunity that fades within months forces health systems to plan for repeated rounds of vaccination, while longer-lasting protection allows resources to shift toward other urgent needs. Either way, researchers agree that patience, rather than a single dramatic breakthrough, remains the most reliable path forward.
items.translation:
当一种流行病在人群中迅速蔓延时，卫生官员往往把首要目标锁定在压平曲线上，以免医院一下子被蜂拥而至的患者压垮。对大规模公众集会的限制曾一度被视为极端措施，如今却迅速成为普遍做法，公民们也被迫调整那些历经数十年才养成的日常习惯。

与此同时，制药巨头们竞相研发治疗方法。实验性药物以异乎寻常的速度推进临床试验，但科学家们提醒人们，没有哪一种药物像有魔力般药到病除；死亡人数的显著下降，通常来自多种并不完美的手段的综合运用，而不是等待某一种十全十美的方案出现。即便是一款前景看好的疫苗，也可能因为生产延误或公众对其使用方式的疑虑而受挫。

也许最重要的问题在于，任何一种保护效果究竟能持续多久。如果免疫力在数月内就会消退，卫生系统就不得不为一轮又一轮的重复接种做准备；而如果保护效果能持续更久，资源就可以转向其他紧迫的需求。无论如何，研究人员都认为，真正可靠的前进之路在于耐心，而不是某一次戏剧性的突破。

keyWords.zh: n. 流行病；a. 流行的
a. 有魔力的，不可思议的

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Vaccines are always ineffective against epidemics
Public assemblies should be permanently banned
Controlling an epidemic requires combined efforts and patience rather than a single quick fix
Pharmaceutical companies only care about profit during epidemics
questions.explain: 全文强调流行病应对需要多种手段综合运用并保持耐心，而非单一速效方案，第二、三段均支持此观点，故C为主旨（第2-3段）。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, what mainly causes the reduction in deaths during an epidemic?
options: A single perfect medicine discovered quickly
Combining several imperfect tools rather than relying on one perfect solution
Banning all public assemblies immediately
Increasing the price of pharmaceutical products
questions.explain: 依据第二段“a meaningful reduction in deaths usually comes from combining several imperfect tools rather than waiting for one perfect one”，答案为B（第2段）。

questions.q: It can be inferred from paragraph 3 that if immunity fades quickly, health systems will need to __.
options: stop all vaccination programs
plan for repeated rounds of vaccination
shift all resources to other needs immediately
ignore the epidemic completely
questions.explain: 依据第三段“Immunity that fades within months forces health systems to plan for repeated rounds of vaccination”，答案为B（第3段）。

questions.q: In paragraph 2, the word "derailed" most nearly means __.
options: accelerated
disrupted or prevented from proceeding smoothly
financially rewarded
officially approved
questions.explain: 结合上下文“a promising vaccine can be derailed by manufacturing delays or public doubts”，可知derailed指因延误或疑虑而受阻、偏离正轨，故选B（第2段）。

longSentences.sentence: Restrictions on large public assembly, once considered an extreme measure, quickly become common practice, and citizens are obliged to adjust daily habits that took decades to form.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Restrictions...quickly become common practice, and citizens are obliged to adjust daily habits...（并列句）
longSentences.modifiers: once considered an extreme measure为过去分词短语作插入语，补充说明Restrictions曾经的定性；that took decades to form为定语从句修饰daily habits
longSentences.translation: 对大规模公众集会的限制曾一度被视为极端措施，如今却迅速成为普遍做法，公民们也被迫调整那些历经数十年才养成的日常习惯。
longSentences.tip: 插入语用逗号隔开，先跳过它抓主干，再回头理解其补充说明的内容。

longSentences.sentence: Experimental drugs move through trials at unusual speed, though scientists caution that no single medicine works like a magical cure; a meaningful reduction in deaths usually comes from combining several imperfect tools rather than waiting for one perfect one.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：由分号连接的两个独立分句——Experimental drugs move through trials at unusual speed；a meaningful reduction in deaths usually comes from combining several imperfect tools
longSentences.modifiers: though引导让步状语从句，其中内嵌caution的宾语从句that no single medicine works like a magical cure；rather than waiting for one perfect one表比较对照
longSentences.translation: 实验性药物以异乎寻常的速度推进临床试验，但科学家们提醒人们，没有哪一种药物像有魔力般药到病除；死亡人数的显著下降，通常来自多种并不完美的手段的综合运用，而不是等待某一种十全十美的方案出现。
longSentences.tip: 分号连接两个独立分句，可分别处理；though引导的让步状语从句中还嵌套了一个that宾语从句，需要逐层拆解。

items.title: A Food Safety Scare at the Festival
items.passage:
Every autumn, the small town of Millbrook holds a harvest festival, an occasion when local farmers sell fresh produce to thousands of visitors. Last year the celebration took an unexpected turn: a batch of leafy greens sold at one stall had become infected with a harmful strain of bacteria, and within two days a sizable number of visitors reported stomach illness. A cheerful gathering quickly turned into a public health scare.

Local health officials assembled a panel of food-safety specialists to investigate. Laboratory tests revealed that the vegetables also carried pesticide residues slightly above the legal tolerance level, though officials stressed that this alone was unlikely to have caused the outbreak. Investigators noted that vegetables harvested past their ideal maturity are more prone to bacterial growth, since ripening skin tends to crack and let microbes in. The affected batch was then treated with an approved compound designed to neutralize the remaining toxins.

News of the outbreak proved sensational, spreading quickly online after a well-known celebrity chef who had attended fell ill and posted about it. Rather than letting public concern simply vanish, town officials responded with an impressive set of new measures: mandatory bacterial testing, stricter storage rules, and public reports on every stall. Most residents feel the costly reforms were worth the price of restored confidence in local food.
items.translation:
每年秋天，小镇米尔布鲁克都会举办丰收节，这是当地农民向数千名游客出售新鲜农产品的一个场合。然而去年，这场庆祝活动出现了意外转折。一个摊位出售的一批绿叶蔬菜不知何故感染了一种有害细菌菌株，两天之内相当多的游客报告出现肠胃不适。原本欢乐的聚会很快演变成了一场公共卫生恐慌。

当地卫生部门组织了一个食品安全专家小组进行调查。实验室检测显示，这些蔬菜所含的农药残留也略高于法定容许限度，不过官员强调，仅凭这一点不太可能是造成疫情暴发的原因。调查人员指出，采摘时间超过理想成熟期的蔬菜更容易滋生细菌，因为成熟的表皮容易开裂，让微生物趁虚而入。为减少已经存在的毒素，问题批次的蔬菜在丢弃前接受了一种经批准的中和有害残留的处理。

这起疫情事件很快变得轰动起来，在一位参加过丰收节、后来因此患病并在网上发帖的知名大厨的传播下迅速扩散。镇政府没有让公众的担忧就此消散，而是采取了一系列令人印象深刻的新举措：强制细菌检测、更严格的储存规定，以及每个摊位的公开报告。大多数居民认为，这些改革虽然代价不小，但为重建人们对本地食品的信心是合理的付出。

keyWords.zh: a. 轰动的，耸人听闻的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A town's harvest festival traditions
A food safety incident and the response to it
The dangers of pesticide use in farming
How celebrity endorsements affect food sales
questions.explain: 文章讲述丰收节上蔬菜被细菌污染引发食品安全事件，以及随后的调查与整改措施，主旨为B，依据全文（第一至三段）。

questions.q: According to the passage, what did investigators find in addition to the bacteria?
options: The greens contained pesticide residue above the legal tolerance level
The greens had been stored at the wrong temperature
The stall owner had sold expired produce intentionally
