The festival organizers had ignored earlier warnings
questions.explain: 第二段提到检测发现蔬菜农药残留略高于法定容许限度（tolerance level），依据第二段。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the second paragraph that vegetables harvested too late __.
options: taste less sweet than those picked early
are more likely to attract insects
are more vulnerable to bacterial contamination
lose most of their nutritional value
questions.explain: 第二段指出超过理想成熟期（past their ideal maturity）的蔬菜表皮容易开裂，更容易滋生细菌，故答案为C，依据第二段。

questions.q: The word "vanish" in the third paragraph is closest in meaning to __.
options: increase gradually
disappear completely
become official
spread quickly
questions.explain: vanish意为“消失”，结合语境“Rather than letting public concern simply vanish”，指不能让公众的关切轻易“消失”，故选B，依据第三段。

longSentences.sentence: Investigators noted that vegetables harvested past their ideal maturity are more prone to bacterial growth, since ripening skin tends to crack and let microbes in.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是Investigators noted that ...（that引导宾语从句）。
longSentences.modifiers: “harvested past their ideal maturity”是过去分词短语作定语修饰vegetables；since引导原因状语从句。
longSentences.translation: 调查人员指出，采摘时间超过理想成熟期的蔬菜更容易滋生细菌，因为成熟的表皮容易开裂，让微生物趁虚而入。
longSentences.tip: 先抓住noted that后面宾语从句的主干，再处理定语和since状语从句。

longSentences.sentence: Rather than letting public concern simply vanish, town officials responded with an impressive set of new measures: mandatory bacterial testing, stricter storage rules, and public reports on every stall.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是town officials responded with an impressive set of new measures。
longSentences.modifiers: “Rather than letting public concern simply vanish”是让步/对比状语；冒号后三项是new measures的具体列举。
longSentences.translation: 镇政府没有让公众的担忧就此消散，而是采取了一系列令人印象深刻的新举措：强制细菌检测、更严格的储存规定，以及每个摊位的公开报告。
longSentences.tip: rather than开头的部分不是主句，要先跳过找后面真正的主谓结构。

items.title: Saving a Historic House from Demolition
items.passage:
Cooper House, a two-century-old structure at the center of town, has always attracted curious visitors. According to local legend, the building once sheltered a young martyr during a colonial-era uprising, hiding him in a small room behind the kitchen. Over the following decades the house passed through many hands and slowly fell into disrepair. Last year its landlord, hoping to build modern apartments, announced plans to demolish a portion of the original building.

The announcement quickly sparked a dispute between the landlord and a local heritage group, who argued that the house's singular architectural style made it worth protecting rather than tearing down. Pointing to legislative rules that shield structures older than 150 years, city officials intervened and halted the demolition, ordering a full restoration instead.

Restoration workers carefully repaired the original wooden beams, each marked with a distinctive knot pattern left by the carpenters who built the house. They also completed the installation of a modern fire-safety system while keeping traditional organic materials, such as lime plaster, on the walls. When the house reopens next spring, visitors will no longer need to depart with only half the story - the full history of Cooper House will finally be on display.
items.translation:
库珀之屋是一座位于镇中心、有着两百年历史的建筑，一直吸引着好奇的游客。据当地传说，这栋建筑曾在殖民地时期的一次起义中庇护过一名年轻的烈士，将他藏在厨房后面的一个小房间里。此后的几十年里，这栋房子几经转手，逐渐年久失修。去年，房东为了建造现代公寓，宣布计划拆除这栋原建筑的一部分。

这一消息很快引发了房东与当地一个遗产保护团体之间的争议，后者认为这栋房子独特的建筑风格值得保护而非拆除。市政官员援引保护150年以上建筑的立法规定进行干预，叫停了拆除计划，转而下令进行全面修复。

修复工人们仔细修补了原有的木梁，每根梁上都留有建造者所刻的独特木节纹样。他们还完成了现代消防系统的安装，同时在墙壁上保留了石灰灰泥等传统天然材料。当这栋房子明年春天重新开放时，游客们将不再需要带着残缺的故事离开——库珀之屋的完整历史终将得以展示。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The daily life of a colonial-era martyr
A dispute over the fate of a historic building and its eventual restoration
How landlords in a small town make money from apartments
The rules for protecting all buildings older than 100 years
questions.explain: 全文围绕库珀之屋是否拆除产生争议、最终被保护并修复展开，主旨为B，依据全文。

questions.q: Why did the landlord originally want to demolish part of Cooper House?
options: The building was too dangerous to enter
He wanted to build modern apartments
The heritage group asked him to
The government ordered the demolition
questions.explain: 第一段提到房东为了建造现代公寓而计划拆除部分建筑，依据第一段。

questions.q: It can be inferred that the legislative rules mentioned in paragraph two __.
options: apply only to buildings connected to famous battles
were created specifically for Cooper House
protect structures over a certain age from demolition
require all landlords to open their buildings to the public
questions.explain: 第二段提到官员援引保护150年以上建筑的立法规定阻止了拆除，说明这类法规保护达到一定年限的建筑，依据第二段。

questions.q: The word "singular" in paragraph two is closest in meaning to __.
options: lonely
distinctive
simple
temporary
questions.explain: singular在此语境中意为“独特的，与众不同的”，指该建筑独特的建筑风格值得保护，故选B，依据第二段。

longSentences.sentence: According to local legend, the building once sheltered a young martyr during a colonial-era uprising, hiding him in a small room behind the kitchen.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是the building once sheltered a young martyr。
longSentences.modifiers: “According to local legend”是插入语，交代信息来源；“during a colonial-era uprising”是时间状语；“hiding him in a small room behind the kitchen”是现在分词短语作伴随状语。
longSentences.translation: 据当地传说，这栋建筑曾在殖民地时期的一次起义中庇护过一名年轻的烈士，将他藏在厨房后面的一个小房间里。
longSentences.tip: 逗号前的插入语先放一边，抓住“the building sheltered a young martyr”这条主干。

longSentences.sentence: The announcement quickly sparked a dispute between the landlord and a local heritage group, who argued that the house's singular architectural style made it worth protecting rather than tearing down.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是The announcement sparked a dispute between A and B。
longSentences.modifiers: “who argued that...”是非限制性定语从句修饰heritage group，从句中又嵌套了一个that宾语从句。
longSentences.translation: 这一消息很快引发了房东与当地一个遗产保护团体之间的争议，后者认为这栋房子独特的建筑风格值得保护而非拆除。
longSentences.tip: who引导的非限制性定语从句里还套了一个that宾语从句，要一层层剥开来看。

longSentences.sentence: They also completed the installation of a modern fire-safety system while keeping traditional organic materials, such as lime plaster, on the walls.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是They completed the installation of a modern fire-safety system。
longSentences.modifiers: “while keeping...on the walls”是伴随状语；“such as lime plaster”是插入语，举例说明organic materials。
longSentences.translation: 他们还完成了现代消防系统的安装，同时在墙壁上保留了石灰灰泥等传统天然材料。
longSentences.tip: while+doing结构表示伴随的动作，可以直接翻译成“同时……”。

items.title: Life and Training at a Landscaping Firm
items.passage:
GreenScape, a landscaping company that maintains lawns and gardens for office campuses, has grown quickly in recent years. Because keeping large properties healthy requires knowledgeable staff, the company holds a two-day workshop for every new employee before they are allowed to operate machinery on their own.

During training, workers learn to sharpen mower blades correctly, since a dull blade tears grass instead of cutting it cleanly and can leave a lawn looking patchy. They are also taught to check whether soil is too dry or too moist by using small wireless sensors buried in the ground, which send readings straight to a phone app. When a client's vegetable garden is raided by rabbits or insects, staff collect a plant specimen and place it in a sealed glass vessel for testing at the company lab.

To make the most of long summer daylight, crews often extend their working hours rather than rushing through a job. Employees rotate between sites each week so that no one grows bored maintaining the same garden. Equipment such as sensors and specialized blades can be costly, but managers say quality tools save money over time. After a long shift, most workers gather at the small cafe next to the office to relax before heading home.
items.translation:
绿景园艺公司专为写字楼园区维护草坪和花园，近年来发展迅速。由于维护大型场地需要经验丰富的员工，公司会在每位新员工被允许独立操作机械之前，为他们举办为期两天的培训班。

在培训中，工人们要学习正确地打磨割草机刀片，因为不锋利的刀片会撕扯草坪而非整齐地切割，可能让草坪看起来斑驳不齐。他们还被教导如何借助埋在地下的小型无线传感器来检查土壤是否过干或过湿，这些传感器会把读数直接传送到手机应用程序。当客户的菜园遭到兔子或昆虫侵袭时，工作人员会采集一份植物样本，装入密封的玻璃容器中，送往公司实验室检测。

为充分利用夏季较长的日照，工作团队常常延长工作时间，而不是匆忙赶工。员工每周在不同场地之间轮换，这样就不会有人因长期维护同一个花园而感到厌倦。传感器和专用刀片等设备可能价格不菲，但管理者表示优质工具从长远看能省钱。长时间工作结束后，大多数员工会到公司旁边的小咖啡馆放松一下再回家。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: How to grow vegetables without pesticides
The training and daily work of employees at a landscaping company
The history of lawn-mowing equipment
Why office workers should spend more time outdoors
questions.explain: 全文围绕绿景园艺公司如何培训并安排员工完成草坪与花园维护工作展开，主旨为B，依据全文。

questions.q: According to the passage, why do new employees attend a two-day workshop?
options: To learn how to negotiate with clients
To be allowed to operate machinery on their own afterward
To receive a company uniform and badge
To meet other employees from different branches
questions.explain: 第一段提到新员工须完成两天培训后才被允许独立操作机械，依据第一段。

questions.q: It can be inferred that the wireless sensors mentioned in paragraph two are mainly used to __.
options: record how many visitors enter a garden
track employees' working hours
monitor whether the soil is too dry or too moist
warn staff about approaching rabbits
questions.explain: 第二段说明无线传感器埋在土里，用来检测土壤是否过干或过湿，并把数据传到手机应用，依据第二段。

questions.q: The word "extend" in paragraph three is closest in meaning to __.
options: shorten
lengthen
postpone
record
questions.explain: extend在此处意为“延长”，指工人在夏季利用较长的日照延长工作时间，故选B，依据第三段。

longSentences.sentence: Because keeping large properties healthy requires knowledgeable staff, the company holds a two-day workshop for every new employee before they are allowed to operate machinery on their own.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是the company holds a two-day workshop for every new employee。
longSentences.modifiers: “Because...”是原因状语从句；“before they are allowed...”是时间状语从句。
longSentences.translation: 由于维护大型场地需要经验丰富的员工，公司会在每位新员工被允许独立操作机械之前，为他们举办为期两天的培训班。
longSentences.tip: 句首Because引导原因状语从句，先跳过，找后面主句的主谓宾。

longSentences.sentence: They are also taught to check whether soil is too dry or too moist by using small wireless sensors buried in the ground, which send readings straight to a phone app.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是They are also taught to check whether...。
longSentences.modifiers: “by using small wireless sensors...”是方式状语；“buried in the ground”是过去分词短语作后置定语修饰sensors；“which send readings...”是非限制性定语从句。
longSentences.translation: 他们还被教导如何借助埋在地下的小型无线传感器来检查土壤是否过干或过湿，这些传感器会把读数直接传送到手机应用程序。
longSentences.tip: which引导的非限制性定语从句紧跟在最近的名词sensors后面，用来补充说明。

longSentences.sentence: When a client's vegetable garden is raided by rabbits or insects, staff collect a plant specimen and place it in a sealed glass vessel for testing at the company lab.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是staff collect a plant specimen and place it in a sealed glass vessel。
longSentences.modifiers: “When...”是时间状语从句；“for testing at the company lab”是目的状语。
longSentences.translation: 当客户的菜园遭到兔子或昆虫侵袭时，工作人员会采集一份植物样本，装入密封的玻璃容器中，送往公司实验室检测。
longSentences.tip: When引导的时间状语从句放在句首，主句是并列谓语collect...and place...。

items.title: Rethinking Popular Claims About Sleep
items.passage:
Bold headlines about sleep appear almost daily, promising that a single trick will fix anyone's rest. Such claims can mislead readers, since good sleep habits vary a great deal from person to person. A handful of ardent supporters of extremely early rising insist that waking before dawn is the only path to productivity, yet little solid evidence supports such a narrow rule.

Researchers who study sleep from an outsider's perspective, unaffiliated with any commercial sleep-aid brand, often question the validity of small studies used to sell mattresses or supplements. One team compared city dwellers with residents of remote rural villages who have no electric lighting. They found that natural bedtimes there were far more variable than modern guidelines stipulate, shifting gradually with the seasons instead of following a fixed clock.

Even so, most experts agree that a reasonably orderly routine helps: going to bed and waking at similar times lets the body's internal clock work efficiently. Bad habits, such as checking a phone underneath the blanket, can creep into a routine almost without notice and quietly undo months of progress. Ultimately, there is no single right viewpoint on sleep, only guidance that must be adjusted to fit each individual.
items.translation:
关于睡眠的耸动标题几乎每天都会出现，宣称某个单一技巧就能解决所有人的睡眠问题。这类说法可能会误导读者，因为良好的睡眠习惯因人而异，差异很大。一小群热衷于极早起床的支持者坚称，黎明前起床是提高效率的唯一途径，但几乎没有确凿证据支持这一狭隘的说法。

那些以局外人视角研究睡眠、不隶属于任何商业睡眠产品品牌的研究者，常常质疑那些被用来推销床垫或补充剂的小型研究的可靠性。一个研究团队将城市居民与没有电力供应的偏远乡村居民进行了比较，发现那里的自然就寝时间比现代作息指南所规定的更加多变，会随季节逐渐变化，而不是遵循固定的时钟。

尽管如此，大多数专家仍认为，相对有规律的作息是有帮助的：在相近的时间上床和起床，能让人体内部的生物钟高效运转。一些不良习惯，比如在被子底下看手机，几乎在不知不觉中渗入日常作息，悄悄抵消掉数月的努力成果。归根结底，关于睡眠并没有一个绝对正确的观点，只有需要根据每个人情况调整的指导原则。

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Sleep needs are the same for everyone and should follow a strict schedule
Popular claims about sleep are often oversimplified, and good sleep habits vary from person to person
Rural residents sleep better than city residents because they have no electricity
Sleep-aid products are the most reliable way to improve sleep quality
questions.explain: 全文批评耸人听闻的睡眠说法过于简单化，强调睡眠习惯因人而异，主旨为B，依据全文。

questions.q: According to paragraph two, what did the researchers find when comparing city dwellers with rural villagers?
options: Rural villagers slept far fewer hours than city dwellers
Natural bedtimes in rural villages were more variable and shifted with the seasons
City dwellers had healthier sleep habits overall
Rural villagers refused to follow any sleep schedule
questions.explain: 第二段提到研究发现农村居民的自然就寝时间比现代作息指南所规定的更加多变，并随季节逐渐变化，依据第二段。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the passage that the "outsider" researchers mentioned in paragraph two __.
options: have no interest in sleep research at all
are more likely to give an unbiased assessment of sleep studies
disagree with every claim made by sleep-aid companies
have never studied rural communities
questions.explain: 第二段指出这些研究者不隶属于任何商业睡眠产品品牌，暗示他们能给出更客观公正的评价，依据第二段。

questions.q: The word "creep" in paragraph three is closest in meaning to __.
options: disappear suddenly
enter gradually and almost unnoticed
improve steadily
become forbidden
questions.explain: creep在此语境中意为“悄悄地、逐渐地进入”，指不良习惯不知不觉渗入日常作息，故选B，依据第三段。

longSentences.sentence: A handful of ardent supporters of extremely early rising insist that waking before dawn is the only path to productivity, yet little solid evidence supports such a narrow rule.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是A handful of ... supporters insist that...，yet little evidence supports...。
longSentences.modifiers: “of extremely early rising”修饰supporters；that引导宾语从句；yet连接前后转折的并列分句。
longSentences.translation: 一小群热衷于极早起床的支持者坚称，黎明前起床是提高效率的唯一途径，但几乎没有确凿证据支持这一狭隘的说法。
longSentences.tip: yet连接两个并列分句，注意前后语义的转折对比。

longSentences.sentence: Researchers who study sleep from an outsider's perspective, unaffiliated with any commercial sleep-aid brand, often question the validity of small studies used to sell mattresses or supplements.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是Researchers often question the validity of small studies。
longSentences.modifiers: “who study sleep from an outsider's perspective”是定语从句修饰Researchers；“unaffiliated with any commercial sleep-aid brand”是插入的形容词短语补充说明；“used to sell mattresses or supplements”是过去分词短语修饰studies。
longSentences.translation: 那些以局外人视角研究睡眠、不隶属于任何商业睡眠产品品牌的研究者，常常质疑那些被用来推销床垫或补充剂的小型研究的可靠性。
longSentences.tip: 句子里有定语从句和分词短语两处修饰成分，先抓Researchers...question...validity这条主干。

longSentences.sentence: Bad habits, such as checking a phone underneath the blanket, can creep into a routine almost without notice and quietly undo months of progress.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是Bad habits can creep into a routine and quietly undo months of progress。
longSentences.modifiers: “such as checking a phone underneath the blanket”是插入语，举例说明Bad habits；“almost without notice”是方式状语。
longSentences.translation: 一些不良习惯，比如在被子底下看手机，几乎在不知不觉中渗入日常作息，悄悄抵消掉数月的努力成果。
longSentences.tip: 主语Bad habits和谓语can creep之间插入了such as举例说明的部分，读的时候可先跳过插入语抓主干。

items.title: AI's Growing Role in Daily Life
items.passage:
Artificial intelligence (AI) no longer belongs only to science-fiction films; it has quietly become part of daily life. Today an AI program can act as a weather analyst, a forest ranger's assistant, or even a personal secretary, handling tasks that once required a room full of specialists.

Consider weather forecasting. By examining data along several dimensions—temperature, humidity, and wind speed—AI systems can now produce increasingly accurate predictions, often warning of a storm before the first thunder is even heard. This improvement has been gradual rather than sudden, partly because forecasters have trained their models on records gathered from thousands of stations, including remote posts deep inside a forest. A national weather association recently reported that AI-assisted forecasts deliver warnings nearly two hours earlier than traditional methods.

AI has proved equally useful in everyday matters. Many busy professionals now rely on a virtual secretary to manage their calendars: it can appoint meeting times, remind them of an important dinner with a client, and even draft a polite reply to a stranger requesting information. Such assistants act swiftly, especially when the schedule is tightly packed. Still, most experts agree that human judgment cannot yet be fully replaced by a machine.
items.translation:
人工智能（AI）早已不再局限于科幻电影之中，它已经悄然融入日常生活。如今，一套AI程序既能充当气象分析师、森林护林员的助手，也能扮演私人秘书的角色，承担起过去需要满满一屋子专业人员才能完成的工作。

以天气预报为例。通过分析气温、湿度、风速等多个维度的数据，AI系统如今能够做出越来越精确的预测，常常在第一声雷响之前就发出风暴预警。这种进步是逐步积累而非一蹴而就的，部分原因在于预报人员多年来一直在用来自数千个观测站（包括深藏在森林里的偏远站点）的记录训练模型。一家全国性气象协会近日报告称，AI辅助的预报比传统方法提前近两个小时发出预警。

AI在日常事务中同样大有用处。许多繁忙的职场人士如今依靠虚拟秘书来管理日程：它可以安排会面时间、提醒他们与客户的重要晚餐，甚至替他们给索取信息的陌生人起草礼貌的回复。这类助手行动迅速，尤其是在日程排得很满的时候。不过，大多数专家仍然认为，人类的判断力目前还无法被机器完全取代。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The history of artificial intelligence development
How AI has become part of many everyday tasks
Why AI cannot replace human judgment
The dangers of relying too much on technology
questions.explain: 文章第一段指出AI已悄然融入日常生活，随后两段分别以天气预报和个人助理为例展开说明，全文主旨为B。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, what advantage do AI-assisted weather forecasts have, as reported by a national weather association?
options: They cost significantly less money to produce
They can predict earthquakes as well as storms
They deliver storm warnings almost two hours earlier
They completely replace weather stations in forests
questions.explain: 依据第二段末句“AI-assisted forecasts deliver warnings nearly two hours earlier than traditional methods”，答案为C。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the last paragraph that
options: virtual secretaries will soon completely replace human secretaries
human judgment is still considered valuable in certain situations
AI assistants often make scheduling mistakes
most professionals refuse to use AI assistants
questions.explain: 末段末句提到“most experts agree that human judgment...cannot yet be fully replaced by a machine”，可推知人类判断仍有其不可替代的价值，答案为B。

questions.q: The word “dimensions” in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to
options: measurements or aspects of data
physical shapes of clouds
time periods of a day
locations of weather stations
questions.explain: 根据上下文“examining data along several dimensions—temperature, humidity, and wind speed”，dimensions指数据的不同方面/维度，答案为A。

longSentences.sentence: By examining data along several dimensions—temperature, humidity, and wind speed—AI systems can now produce increasingly accurate predictions, often warning of a storm before the first thunder is even heard.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：AI systems can now produce increasingly accurate predictions（AI系统如今能做出越来越精确的预测）
longSentences.modifiers: By examining...wind speed 为方式状语，说明预测所依据的数据维度（破折号内为具体举例）；often warning of a storm before the first thunder is even heard 为现在分词短语作伴随状语，说明预测带来的效果。
longSentences.translation: 通过分析气温、湿度、风速等多个维度的数据，AI系统如今能够做出越来越精确的预测，常常在第一声雷响之前就发出风暴预警。
longSentences.tip: 遇到破折号插入的举例内容，可先跳过快速抓主干，再回头把插入部分当作补充信息理解。

longSentences.sentence: Many busy professionals now rely on a virtual secretary to manage their calendars: it can appoint meeting times, remind them of an important dinner with a client, and even draft a polite reply to a stranger requesting information.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Many busy professionals now rely on a virtual secretary to manage their calendars（许多职场人士依靠虚拟秘书管理日程）
longSentences.modifiers: 冒号后由三个并列谓语（appoint...、remind...、and draft...）构成，具体说明秘书完成的任务；requesting information 为现在分词作后置定语修饰stranger。
longSentences.translation: 许多繁忙的职场人士如今依靠虚拟秘书来管理日程：它可以安排会面时间、提醒他们与客户的重要晚餐，甚至替他们给索取信息的陌生人起草礼貌的回复。
longSentences.tip: 冒号后的并列结构是对前面内容的具体展开，抓住三个并列动词即可理清层次。

items.title: Social Media, Identity and Teen Health
items.passage:
Social media has quietly changed the way many young people build their identity. Posting a carefully chosen self-portrait, checking how many likes it receives, and comparing one's popularity with that of friends have become a normal mode of daily life for a whole generation. A single photo, capturing just one flattering angle, can shape how thousands of strangers imagine someone's entire personality.

Yet this new mode of living carries a hidden cost. Recent statistics reveal a steady rise in obesity among teenagers who spend hours scrolling instead of moving, and doctors warn that excess fat can damage organs such as the liver. The human body, after all, is a single organism whose parts constantly affect one another, so doctors now treat weight as just one metric among many when judging overall health. A study on this topic once managed to stir public debate.

Take one ordinary case: a fourteen-year-old lad who used to spend most evenings online eventually gave up the habit and joined a local football club instead. Within months he felt more competent, both physically and socially, and described the change as something that finally liberated him from constant comparison. His story suggests that stepping back from a screen may be the healthier mode of growing up.
items.translation:
社交媒体正悄然改变着许多年轻人构建自我身份的方式。精心挑选一张自拍肖像发布出去，查看它收获了多少点赞，再拿自己的受欢迎程度与朋友比较，这已成为整整一代人日常生活的常态。一张只捕捉到最上镜角度的照片，就能左右成千上万陌生人对某个人整体性格的想象。

然而，这种新的生活方式也带来了隐藏的代价。近期的统计数据显示，那些整日刷手机而不是运动的青少年中，肥胖率正稳步上升，医生警告说，过多的脂肪会损害肝脏等器官。毕竟，人体是一个各部分相互影响的完整有机体，因此医生如今把体重视为评估整体健康时的众多指标之一。一项关于这一话题的研究曾一度引发了公众热议。

以一个普通的例子来说：一个十四岁的男孩曾经大部分晚上都泡在网上，后来他放弃了这个习惯，加入了当地的一家足球俱乐部。几个月之后，他感到自己在体能和社交上都更加胜任，并把这种改变形容为终于把自己从无休止的比较中解放了出来。他的经历说明，从屏幕前抽身而出，或许才是更健康的成长方式。

questions.q: The passage mainly discusses
options: the benefits of spending time on social media
how social media shapes identity and can affect health
a biography of a famous football player
techniques for taking better self-portraits
questions.explain: 第一段说明社交媒体如何塑造年轻人的身份，第二段进一步指出其对健康（肥胖、肝脏）的影响，主旨为B。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, doctors are concerned that spending long hours on social media may lead to
options: improved sleep quality
higher levels of physical competence
health problems such as obesity and liver damage
increased popularity among friends
questions.explain: 第二段提到“a steady rise in obesity”以及“excess fat can damage organs such as the liver”，答案为C。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the lad mentioned in paragraph 3?
options: He became less socially confident after leaving social media
He found real-life activity more fulfilling than being online
He was forced by his parents to quit social media
He continued to spend most evenings online
questions.explain: 第三段描述他放弃长时间上网、加入足球俱乐部后感到更加胜任并被解放，可推知线下活动带来了更多满足感，答案为B。

questions.q: In paragraph 3, the word “liberated” is closest in meaning to
options: confused
set free
exhausted
embarrassed
questions.explain: liberate意为“解放，使自由”，此处指他摆脱了不断比较的束缚，答案为B。

longSentences.sentence: Recent statistics reveal a steady rise in obesity among teenagers who spend hours scrolling instead of moving, and doctors warn that excess fat can damage organs such as the liver.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：两个由and连接的并列句——Recent statistics reveal a steady rise in obesity...，and doctors warn that excess fat can damage organs...
longSentences.modifiers: among teenagers who spend hours scrolling instead of moving 为定语从句修饰teenagers；that excess fat can damage organs such as the liver 为宾语从句作warn的宾语，其中such as the liver举例说明organs。
longSentences.translation: 近期的统计数据显示，那些整日刷手机而不是运动的青少年中，肥胖率正稳步上升，医生警告说，过多的脂肪会损害肝脏等器官。
longSentences.tip: 长句中出现and连接两个完整分句时，可拆成两个独立句子分别理解再合并记忆。

longSentences.sentence: Within months he felt more competent, both physically and socially, and described the change as something that finally liberated him from constant comparison.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：he felt more competent...and described the change as something...（并列谓语felt / described）
longSentences.modifiers: Within months 为时间状语；both physically and socially 为插入语，说明competent涉及的两个方面；that finally liberated him from constant comparison 为定语从句修饰something。
longSentences.translation: 几个月之后，他感到自己在体能和社交上都更加胜任，并把这种改变形容为终于把自己从无休止的比较中解放了出来。
longSentences.tip: 抓住and连接的两个并列谓语（felt / described），插入语和定语从句都是补充说明，可先略过理清主干再回补细节。

items.title: The Hidden Psychology of Embarrassment
items.passage:
Few things embarrass adults more than an awkward public accident: spilling a glass of wine across a white tablecloth, walking out of a public bathroom with a strip of paper stuck to one's shoe, forgetting a colleague's name while handing out an award, or knocking a fragile antique off a shelf in a small shop. Each scene seems almost cruel, replaying itself in memory for years.

Psychologists argue that embarrassment is not simply bad luck; it works like a bridge connecting our private sense of self with how a troop of colleagues nearby might judge us. The sudden blush that appears, as if by magic, is in fact a biological symbol of social awareness: it signals that we recognize the mistake and still care about others' opinion. In this sense, the feeling is less a weakness of the soul than proof of close attention to those around us.

Learning to live with embarrassment, rather than fighting it, is therefore worth the effort. Experts admire people who can laugh at their own mistakes instead of hiding from them, since this small extension of self-acceptance often turns shame into a shared joke. Building a richer emotional vocabulary allows people to name what they feel more accurately and, eventually, to move past it with less pain.
items.translation:
没有什么比一次尴尬的公开意外更让成年人难堪的了：在雪白的桌布上打翻一杯葡萄酒，从公共卫生间出来时鞋上还粘着一条卫生纸，在颁奖时忘记同事的名字，或是在小店里不小心把一件易碎的古董碰下架子。多年之后，每一个场景仍会在记忆里反复重演，显得近乎残酷。

心理学家认为，尴尬并非仅仅是运气不好；它更像是一座桥梁，连接着我们私下的自我认知与身边一群同事可能做出的评判。那突然涌上的脸红，仿佛变魔术一般，其实是一种社会意识的生物性象征：它表明我们已经意识到自己的失误，依然在意他人的看法。从这个意义上说，这种感受与其说是灵魂的软弱，不如说是一个人对周围人保持关注的证明。

学会与尴尬共处，而不是与之对抗，因此是值得付出的努力。专家们欣赏那些能笑对自己失误、而不是躲躲藏藏的人，因为这种自我接纳的小小延伸，常常能把羞愧变成一个共同的笑谈。培养更丰富的情感词汇量，能让人们更准确地说出自己的感受，并最终以更少的痛苦走出这种情绪。

questions.q: The passage is mainly concerned with
options: listing examples of rude behavior in public
explaining what embarrassment is and how to cope with it
describing traditional wine-tasting customs
comparing embarrassment in different cultures
questions.explain: 第一段列举尴尬场景引出话题，第二段分析其心理机制，第三段谈应对方式，全文主旨为B。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, blushing when embarrassed mainly signals that a person
options: is lying to cover up a mistake
does not care what others think
is aware of the mistake and still values others' opinions
wants to leave the situation immediately
questions.explain: 第二段指出“it signals that we recognize the mistake and still care about others' opinion”，答案为C。

questions.q: It can be inferred from paragraph 3 that people who can laugh at their own embarrassing mistakes
options: are usually less intelligent than others
tend to gain more respect and cope better emotionally
never feel embarrassed again
are avoided by their coworkers
questions.explain: 第三段提到专家欣赏这类人，且这种态度能帮助人们“move past it with less pain”，可推知他们更受认可、情绪应对更好，答案为B。

questions.q: The phrase “this small extension of self-acceptance” in paragraph 3 most likely refers to
options: a longer vacation taken to relax
an added, slightly larger degree of accepting oneself
a new rule set by psychologists
an official award given to brave people
questions.explain: extension意为“延伸，扩展”，此处指自我接纳程度的进一步扩大，答案为B。

longSentences.sentence: Few things embarrass adults more than an awkward public accident: spilling a glass of wine across a white tablecloth, walking out of a public bathroom with a strip of paper stuck to one's shoe, forgetting a colleague's name while handing out an award, or knocking a fragile antique off a shelf in a small shop.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Few things embarrass adults more than an awkward public accident（没有什么比一次尴尬的公开意外更让成年人难堪）
longSentences.modifiers: 冒号后四个并列的动名词短语（spilling...、walking...、forgetting...、or knocking...）作同位语，具体列举“尴尬意外”的例子；stuck to one's shoe为过去分词作后置定语修饰paper。
longSentences.translation: 没有什么比一次尴尬的公开意外更让成年人难堪的了：在雪白的桌布上打翻一杯葡萄酒，从公共卫生间出来时鞋上还粘着一条卫生纸，在颁奖时忘记同事的名字，或是在小店里不小心把一件易碎的古董碰下架子。
longSentences.tip: 冒号后若干并列的动名词短语常用来举例说明前面的抽象名词，逐个对应即可理清结构。

longSentences.sentence: Psychologists argue that embarrassment is not simply bad luck; it works like a bridge connecting our private sense of self with how a troop of colleagues nearby might judge us.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：分号连接的两个并列句——Psychologists argue that...；it works like a bridge...
longSentences.modifiers: that embarrassment is not simply bad luck 为宾语从句作argue的宾语；connecting our private sense of self with how a troop of colleagues nearby might judge us 为现在分词短语作后置定语修饰bridge，其中包含从句how...might judge us。
longSentences.translation: 心理学家认为，尴尬并非仅仅是运气不好；它更像是一座桥梁，连接着我们私下的自我认知与身边一群同事可能做出的评判。
longSentences.tip: 分号连接的两个分句地位平等，可分别抓主干，再理解比喻关系（it works like a bridge）。

items.title: The Ancient Custom of Silent Trade
items.passage:
Long before modern currency or written contracts existed, some ancient trading communities relied on a custom historians call the so-called 'silent trade,' or dumb barter. As early as the fifth century BC, the Greek historian Herodotus described how Carthaginian sailors exchanged goods with a tribe in North Africa without a single word being spoken. Centuries before European imperialism reshaped global trade routes, this quiet method let two peoples with no shared language trade safely.

The process followed a simple loop. One side would arrive at midday, lay out a selection of goods on the shore, then hail the other party by beating a drum, and withdraw. The second group would inspect the offer and leave a fair amount of gold in exchange, without approaching too closely. Neither side sent a delegate, and no one showed any haste, since rushing could be read as an insult or even an illicit attempt to cheat.

Historians remain speculative about how such trust developed between strangers, but the system clearly worked, lasting for generations without conflict turning explosive. As trade networks grew and the promotion of shared languages made direct bargaining possible, the silent method slowly disappeared. Yet it remains a reminder that cooperation does not always require speech — sometimes patience and mutual respect speak louder than words.
items.translation:
早在现代货币或书面契约出现之前，一些古代贸易社群就依靠一种历史学家如今称之为“无言贸易”或“哑巴以物易物”的习俗进行交易。早在公元前五世纪，希腊历史学家希罗多德就记载过，迦太基水手与北非一个部落之间进行交易时不发一言。在欧洲帝国主义重塑全球贸易路线的数百年之前，这种无声的方式就已经让语言不通的两个民族能够安全地做生意。

整个过程遵循一套简单的循环。一方会在正午时分到达，把一批精选的货物摆放在岸边，然后击鼓向对方打招呼，随即离开。另一方则会查看这些货物，留下一定数量、他们认为公道的黄金作为交换，同样不会靠得太近。双方都不派代表出面协商，也没有人表现出丝毫急躁，因为匆忙行事很容易被视为一种冒犯，甚至是一种非法的欺骗企图。

关于这种陌生人之间的信任究竟是如何建立起来的，历史学家至今仍只能推测，但这一制度确实行之有效，曾持续数代之久，却极少演变成激烈的冲突。随着贸易网络的扩大以及共通语言的推广使直接议价成为可能，这种无声的交易方式便逐渐消失了。然而，它仍然提醒着人们：合作并不总是需要言语——有时候，耐心与相互尊重胜过千言万语。

questions.q: What is the main topic of the passage?
options: A modern method of international shipping
An ancient trading custom carried out without speaking
The military conquests of Carthage
The invention of written contracts
questions.explain: 全文围绕历史上“无言贸易（silent trade / dumb barter）”这一习俗展开介绍，答案为B。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, why did neither side hurry during the exchange?
options: Because haste might be seen as disrespectful or as an attempt to cheat
Because the goods were too heavy to move quickly
Because local law forbade fast trading
Because both sides could not see each other clearly
questions.explain: 第二段指出“no one showed any haste, since rushing could be read as an insult or even an illicit attempt to cheat”，答案为A。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the passage that the silent trade eventually disappeared mainly because
options: gold became worthless over time
growing communication made direct bargaining possible
Carthaginian sailors stopped exploring new regions
local tribes rejected all foreign goods
questions.explain: 第三段提到“the promotion of shared languages made direct bargaining possible, the silent method slowly disappeared”，答案为B。

questions.q: The word “speculative” in paragraph 3 is closest in meaning to
options: based on solid proof
based on guesswork rather than certain facts
officially recorded in law
widely rejected by scholars
questions.explain: speculative意为“推测的”，指历史学家对信任如何建立仍只能推测而非确证，答案为B。

longSentences.sentence: As early as the fifth century BC, the Greek historian Herodotus described how Carthaginian sailors exchanged goods with a tribe in North Africa without a single word being spoken.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：the Greek historian Herodotus described how Carthaginian sailors exchanged goods with a tribe in North Africa（希腊历史学家希罗多德记载了迦太基水手与北非部落交易的情形）
longSentences.modifiers: As early as the fifth century BC 为时间状语，置于句首；without a single word being spoken 为介词without+动名词被动式构成的伴随状语，说明交易时不发一言。
longSentences.translation: 早在公元前五世纪，希腊历史学家希罗多德就记载过，迦太基水手与北非一个部落之间进行交易时不发一言。
longSentences.tip: without doing / without sth being done 结构常作伴随状语，表示“在没有……的情况下”，翻译时可处理成“不……地”。

longSentences.sentence: Neither side sent a delegate, and no one showed any haste, since rushing could be read as an insult or even an illicit attempt to cheat.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Neither side sent a delegate, and no one showed any haste（并列句）
longSentences.modifiers: since rushing could be read as an insult or even an illicit attempt to cheat 为since引导的原因状语从句，说明双方为何都不急躁。
longSentences.translation: 双方都不派代表出面协商，也没有人表现出丝毫急躁，因为匆忙行事很容易被视为一种冒犯，甚至是一种非法的欺骗企图。
longSentences.tip: since在此表示“因为”，引导原因状语从句，注意与表示“自从”的since区分，可根据句意判断。

items.title: A Marathon Boom on the Mainland
items.passage:
Marathon running has become an awesome social trend across the Chinese mainland. In big cities, tens of thousands of runners now register for a race months before it starts, hoping to secure one of the limited places. What used to be a quiet hobby for a small group of fitness fans has turned into a public affair that draws crowds from all walks of life.

Getting ready for 42 kilometers is not easy. Many runners devote at least three months to serious training, running further and further each week to raise the quality of their performance. On long training days, their legs may tremble with tiredness, but most keep going with a bunch of friends who share the same goal, encouraging one another along the muddy or hilly roads.

On race day, streets are closed to cars so that runners can move safely through the city. When exhausted athletes finally cross the finish line, they usually break into a wide smile, forgetting the pain of the last few kilometers. Afterward, many stop for a quick bite with friends before heading home. City officials say the event also promotes public welfare, since it encourages healthier habits and brings the community closer together.
items.translation:
马拉松跑步已经成为中国大陆一种令人惊叹的社会风尚。在各大城市，数以万计的跑者会在比赛开始前数月就报名参赛，希望能争取到为数不多的名额。这项曾经只是一小群健身爱好者的安静爱好，如今已变成吸引各行各业人群参与的公共活动。

备战42公里的赛程并不容易。许多跑者会将至少三个月的时间投入到系统训练中，每周不断增加跑量，以提高自己的训练质量。在漫长的训练日里，他们的双腿可能会因疲惫而颤抖，但大多数人仍与一群志同道合的朋友一起坚持，在泥泞或起伏的道路上互相鼓励。

比赛当天，街道会禁止车辆通行，以便跑者能够安全地穿过城市。当精疲力竭的运动员终于冲过终点线时，他们通常会露出灿烂的笑容，忘却最后几公里的痛苦。赛后，许多人会与朋友一起简单吃点东西再回家。市政官员表示，这项赛事也促进了公共福利，因为它鼓励了更健康的生活方式，并让社区变得更加团结。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The history of marathon racing in China
The growing popularity of marathon running on the mainland
How to train for a marathon safely
The economic benefits of sports events
questions.explain: 全文围绕马拉松在大陆的流行展开，第一段点明主旨，故选B。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, how long do many runners spend preparing for a marathon?
options: At least three months
A few days
Exactly one year
Only during race week
questions.explain: 第二段提到runners devote at least three months to serious training，故选A。

questions.q: It can be inferred from paragraph 3 that closing streets to cars is meant to
options: reduce traffic pollution
protect the safety of runners
punish local drivers
attract more tourists
questions.explain: 第三段明确写道streets are closed to cars so that runners can move safely，表明目的是保证安全，故选B。

questions.q: In paragraph 2, the word "quality" is closest in meaning to
options: amount
speed
standard
price
questions.explain: 此处quality指训练水平的高低，与standard意思最接近，故选C。

longSentences.sentence: What used to be a quiet hobby for a small group of fitness fans has turned into a public affair that draws crowds from all walks of life.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是主语从句作主语的结构：What used to be...has turned into a public affair
longSentences.modifiers: for a small group of fitness fans 修饰hobby；that draws crowds from all walks of life 是定语从句修饰affair
longSentences.translation: 这项曾经只是一小群健身爱好者的安静爱好，如今已变成吸引各行各业人群参与的公共活动。
longSentences.tip: 遇到What引导的主语从句，先抓住句子主干has turned into…，再分析从句内部结构。

longSentences.sentence: On long training days, their legs may tremble with tiredness, but most keep going with a bunch of friends who share the same goal, encouraging one another along the muddy or hilly roads.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列句：their legs may tremble..., but most keep going with...
longSentences.modifiers: who share the same goal 是定语从句修饰friends；encouraging one another... 是现在分词短语作伴随状语
longSentences.translation: 在漫长的训练日里，他们的双腿可能会因疲惫而颤抖，但大多数人仍与一群志同道合的朋友一起坚持，在泥泞或起伏的道路上互相鼓励。
longSentences.tip: 长句中出现现在分词短语时，通常是伴随状语而非谓语，先找准句子真正的谓语动词。

items.title: Why Teenagers Need More Sleep
items.passage:
Getting enough sleep has become a real challenge for many teenagers today. Doctors say most students between fourteen and eighteen need at least eight hours of sleep every night, yet surveys show that the actual minimum many teens get is closer to six. Homework, phone screens, and worry about exams keep them awake long after they should be resting.

The body's internal clock partly governs when we feel sleepy and when we wake up, and disrupting this rhythm night after night can cause real harm. Teenagers who suffer from chronic sleep loss often rush through breakfast, arrive at school half asleep, and struggle to concentrate in morning classes. Their facial expressions alone, tired eyes and pale skin, can reveal how poorly they slept.

Some schools have begun to permit a later start time, allowing students an extra half hour in bed each morning. Given the choice, most teenagers would rather sleep a little longer than rush out the door. Small changes like this, experts argue, could quietly improve the atmosphere of an entire school, leaving students calmer, healthier, and more ready to learn.
items.translation:
获得充足的睡眠，对如今许多青少年来说已成为一项真正的挑战。医生指出，大多数14到18岁的学生每晚至少需要8小时睡眠，然而调查显示，青少年实际获得的最低睡眠时间往往接近6小时。作业、手机屏幕以及对考试的担忧，让他们在本该休息的时候仍然保持清醒。

人体的生物钟在一定程度上支配着我们何时感到困倦、何时醒来，而日复一日地打乱这一节律会带来真正的危害。长期睡眠不足的青少年常常匆匆吃完早餐、睡眼惺忪地到校，并且难以在早晨的课堂上集中注意力。仅从他们的面部表情——疲惫的双眼和苍白的皮肤——就能看出他们睡得有多差。

一些学校已经开始允许推迟到校时间，让学生每天早晨能多在床上待半小时。如果可以选择，大多数青少年宁愿多睡一会儿，也不愿匆忙冲出家门。专家认为，这样微小的改变就能悄然改善整个学校的氛围，让学生变得更平静、更健康，也更能专注于学习。

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: How teenagers can improve their grades
Why teenagers often lack enough sleep and what can be done about it
The history of school starting times
How the body's internal clock works
questions.explain: 全文先分析青少年睡眠不足的原因，再提出应对办法，故选B。

questions.q: According to the passage, what is the recommended minimum amount of sleep for teenagers?
options: six hours
seven hours
eight hours
nine hours
questions.explain: 第一段提到医生建议at least eight hours of sleep every night，故选C；六小时是青少年实际获得的睡眠时间，属于干扰项。

questions.q: What can be inferred about schools that permit a later start time?
options: They expect students' academic performance to fall
They aim to help students get more sleep
They plan to shorten the school day
They want to reduce transportation costs
questions.explain: 第三段说明推迟到校是为了让学生能多睡半小时，目的是增加睡眠，故选B。

questions.q: In paragraph 2, the word "govern" is closest in meaning to
options: ignore
control
measure
copy
questions.explain: govern在此表示生物钟对睡眠节律的支配、调控，与control意思接近，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: Doctors say most students between fourteen and eighteen need at least eight hours of sleep every night, yet surveys show that the actual minimum many teens get is closer to six.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列句：Doctors say [that]..., yet surveys show that...
longSentences.modifiers: 两个that宾语从句分别作say和show的宾语；between fourteen and eighteen修饰students
longSentences.translation: 医生指出，大多数14到18岁的学生每晚至少需要8小时睡眠，然而调查显示，青少年实际获得的最低睡眠时间往往接近6小时。
longSentences.tip: 长句含并列连词yet时，先找连接词，再分别分析前后两个分句的主谓宾。

longSentences.sentence: Teenagers who suffer from chronic sleep loss often rush through breakfast, arrive at school half asleep, and struggle to concentrate in morning classes.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干含三个并列谓语：Teenagers ... rush through breakfast, arrive at school half asleep, and struggle to concentrate...
longSentences.modifiers: who suffer from chronic sleep loss 是定语从句修饰Teenagers
longSentences.translation: 长期睡眠不足的青少年常常匆匆吃完早餐、睡眼惺忪地到校，并且难以在早晨的课堂上集中注意力。
longSentences.tip: 遇到who引导的定语从句时，先跳过从句找到主句的并列谓语动词。

items.title: The Hidden Cost of Silence
items.passage:
Family relationships are often more complicated than they appear from the outside. A recent study offers a surprising insight into why some households stay close while others slowly drift apart: the key difference, researchers found, is not how much time family members spend together, but how honestly they speak to one another.

Many parents assume there is an implicit understanding between them and their children, a silent agreement that certain feelings do not need to be said aloud. To their dismay, however, psychologists discovered that this unspoken code often does more harm than good. When children constantly suppress their worries to avoid upsetting their parents, small misunderstandings can build up quietly until a single argument shatters years of carefully kept peace.

Respecting a teenager's privacy, the study suggests, does not mean avoiding difficult conversations altogether. Instead, families that check in regularly, even briefly, tend to handle conflict far better than those that wait for problems to disappear on their own. As one researcher put it, a five-minute talk today is worth more than a long lecture after the damage is done.
items.translation:
家庭关系往往比表面看起来要复杂得多。一项最新研究揭示了一个令人惊讶的洞见，解释了为什么有些家庭关系亲密无间，而另一些却渐渐疏远：研究人员发现，关键的差异并不在于家人相处时间的长短，而在于他们彼此交流的坦诚程度。

许多父母以为自己与孩子之间存在一种心照不宣的默契，即认为某些感受不必说出口。然而令他们沮丧的是，心理学家发现，这种未曾言明的默契往往弊大于利。当孩子一再压抑自己的担忧、以避免让父母不安时，微小的误解会不断累积，直到某一次争吵瞬间打破多年小心维系的平静。

该研究指出，尊重青少年的隐私，并不意味着完全回避那些艰难的对话。相反，那些即便只是简短地经常沟通的家庭，处理冲突的能力要远远好于那些一味等待问题自行消失的家庭。正如一位研究者所说，今天五分钟的谈话，胜过伤害已经造成之后的一场长篇说教。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A new method for resolving family arguments
Why family relationships are more complicated abroad
A study on what keeps families emotionally close
The importance of spending more time with family
questions.explain: 全文围绕一项研究展开，探讨什么因素使家庭关系保持亲密，故选C；D项在第一段已被明确否定。

questions.q: According to the study mentioned in paragraph 1, what mainly determines whether families stay close?
options: How much time they spend together
How honestly they communicate
How much money they have
How often they travel together
questions.explain: 第一段明确指出关键不在于相处时间，而在于how honestly they speak to one another，故选B。

questions.q: What does paragraph 2 suggest about children who constantly hide their worries from their parents?
options: They will grow up to be more independent
Their relationship with their parents may eventually suffer
They tend to have better academic performance
Their parents will trust them more
questions.explain: 第二段说明压抑担忧会导致误解累积，最终a single argument shatters years of carefully kept peace，故选B。

questions.q: In paragraph 3, the phrase "check in" most probably means
options: arrive at a hotel
briefly talk and share feelings
complete a form
leave the house
questions.explain: 结合上下文families that check in regularly...tend to handle conflict far better及后文a five-minute talk，可知check in指简短交流沟通，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: A recent study offers a surprising insight into why some households stay close while others slowly drift apart: the key difference, researchers found, is not how much time family members spend together, but how honestly they speak to one another.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：A recent study offers a surprising insight into why...；冒号后说明insight的内容，其主干为the key difference is not A, but B
longSentences.modifiers: why some households stay close while others slowly drift apart 是介词into的宾语从句，内含while对比状语从句；researchers found 是插入语
longSentences.translation: 一项最新研究揭示了一个令人惊讶的洞见，解释了为什么有些家庭关系亲密无间，而另一些却渐渐疏远：研究人员发现，关键的差异并不在于家人相处时间的长短，而在于他们彼此交流的坦诚程度。
longSentences.tip: 冒号后常是对前文的解释说明，遇到插入语（如researchers found）可先跳过，理清主句框架。

longSentences.sentence: Many parents assume there is an implicit understanding between them and their children, a silent agreement that certain feelings do not need to be said aloud.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Many parents assume [that] there is an implicit understanding...
longSentences.modifiers: a silent agreement that certain feelings do not need to be said aloud 是同位语，补充说明understanding，其中that引导定语从句修饰agreement
longSentences.translation: 许多父母以为自己与孩子之间存在一种心照不宣的默契，即认为某些感受不必说出口。
longSentences.tip: 逗号后若出现与前面名词同类的短语，多为同位语，用来进一步解释说明前文内容。

items.title: The Frenzy of Modern Shopping
items.passage:
Every November, frantic crowds line up outside stores long before dawn, waiting for doorbuster sales to begin. Social media has only amplified this rush, turning a single shopping day into a global spectacle watched and shared by millions. For many companies, such events are no longer just about selling goods; they have become a yearly test of brand popularity.

Critics, however, argue that this culture of endless buying raises serious questions of morality. When advertisements are designed specifically to elicit envy and manufacture new aspirations, is a shopper's decision to buy still truly free? Opponents of consumer culture claim that companies deliberately breach the trust between buyer and seller by exaggerating how much a product can improve one's life or status.

Not everyone shares this pessimistic view. Some economists remain cautiously optimistic, pointing out that consumer spending drives job creation and keeps small businesses alive. Whether this optimism is justified may depend on whether shoppers can learn to separate genuine need from a passing aspiration that fades as soon as the sale ends.
items.translation:
每年11月，疯狂的人群会在天亮之前就在商店外排起长队，等待抢购促销活动开始。社交媒体进一步放大了这股热潮，将原本一天的购物活动变成了数百万人围观和转发的全球性景观。对许多公司而言，这类活动已不再只是单纯卖货，而是成了检验品牌人气的年度考验。

然而，批评者认为，这种无休止购物的文化引发了严肃的道德问题。当广告被刻意设计来引发嫉妒、制造出新的欲望时，购物者做出购买决定的自由是否还真实存在？消费文化的反对者声称，企业通过夸大产品能在多大程度上改善人们的生活或社会地位，蓄意破坏了买卖双方之间的信任。

并非所有人都持这种悲观看法。一些经济学家依然保持谨慎乐观，指出消费支出能够创造就业机会、维持中小企业的生存。而这种乐观情绪是否站得住脚，或许取决于消费者能否学会区分真正的需求与那种促销一结束便随之消退的一时冲动。

questions.q: What is the main topic of the passage?
options: How online shopping has replaced physical stores
The debate over the culture of frantic, endless consumer buying
The history of Black Friday sales
Why small businesses struggle to survive
questions.explain: 全文围绕疯狂购物文化展开正反两方观点的讨论，故选B。

questions.q: According to paragraph 1, what has social media done to shopping events like doorbuster sales?
options: It has reduced their popularity
It has made them illegal in some countries
It has amplified them into global spectacles
It has replaced them completely
questions.explain: 第一段提到Social media has only amplified this rush, turning a single shopping day into a global spectacle，故选C。

questions.q: What can be inferred from paragraph 3 about the author's attitude toward frantic consumer culture?
options: The author completely condemns it
The author presents both benefits and concerns without taking a firm side
The author believes it should be banned by law
The author thinks it has no effect on the economy
questions.explain: 第三段既提到消费拉动经济的积极一面，又以Whether this optimism is justified may depend on...提出保留态度，可见作者持中立、辩证的看法，故选B。

questions.q: In paragraph 2, the word "elicit" is closest in meaning to
options: hide
provoke
reduce
forbid
questions.explain: elicit意为引出、诱发，与provoke（激起）意思接近，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: When advertisements are designed specifically to elicit envy and manufacture new aspirations, is a shopper's decision to buy still truly free?
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为一般疑问句：is a shopper's decision to buy still truly free?
longSentences.modifiers: When advertisements are designed specifically to elicit envy and manufacture new aspirations 是时间状语从句，修饰主句
longSentences.translation: 当广告被刻意设计来引发嫉妒、制造出新的欲望时，购物者做出购买决定的自由是否还真实存在？
longSentences.tip: 疑问句若以when引导的状语从句开头，先跳过从句，找到主句中的be动词构成疑问结构。

longSentences.sentence: Opponents of consumer culture claim that companies deliberately breach the trust between buyer and seller by exaggerating how much a product can improve one's life or status.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Opponents ... claim that companies deliberately breach the trust...
longSentences.modifiers: that companies deliberately breach the trust between buyer and seller 是claim的宾语从句；by exaggerating how much a product can improve one's life or status 是方式状语，其中how much引导的从句作exaggerating的宾语
longSentences.translation: 消费文化的反对者声称，企业通过夸大产品能在多大程度上改善人们的生活或社会地位，蓄意破坏了买卖双方之间的信任。
longSentences.tip: by + 动名词结构常表示手段或方式，翻译时可处理为通过……的方式。

items.title: A Staircase and an Inheritance
items.passage:
For thirty years, Grandfather Lin worked as a miner in the hills outside town, saving every coin to build the modest house where three generations still gathered every spring. He carved the narrow wooden staircase with his own hands, and long after his death in a fatal accident underground, its worn steps still creaked with the rhythm he had left behind.

When Grandfather Lin died without a clear will, the family held an informal forum to formulate a strategy for the house and the debts he had quietly accumulated. It began in a brisk, businesslike tone, but the questions of who was entitled to what, and who should repay the outstanding loans, proved more intricate than anyone had expected. There was even something queer about a few entries in his old ledger.

Beneath the polite discussion, old resentments began to lurk. One cousin, convinced an uncle had pocketed money meant for repairs, was ready to denounce him, and contempt flickered across more than one face before calmer relatives stepped in. In the end, it was not law but the memory of the man who had built that staircase that persuaded them to work things out together, repaying the debts in installments and dividing the house fairly among those who had once climbed its worn steps as children.
items.translation:
林爷爷在镇外的山里当了三十年矿工，省吃俭用攒钱盖起了这栋普通的房子，三代人如今仍每年春天在此团聚。他亲手打造了这道窄窄的木楼梯，而在他于井下遭遇致命事故去世多年后，那磨损的台阶仍以他留下的节奏咯吱作响。

林爷爷去世时没有留下明确的遗嘱，家人便召开了一场非正式的“论坛”，商量该如何为这栋房子以及他悄悄积欠下的债务制定一套应对策略。讨论一开始节奏轻快、公事公办，但“谁有权得到什么”“谁该偿还未清的欠款”这两个问题，却比任何人预想的都要复杂。老账本上的几笔记录甚至透着一股说不出的蹊跷。

在礼貌的交谈之下，旧日的怨气开始悄然潜伏。一位表亲坚信某位叔叔私吞了本该用于修缮的钱，几乎就要公开指责他，好几张脸上都闪过一丝轻蔑，直到较为冷静的亲戚出面劝阻。最终，说服他们携手解决问题的，不是法律，而是对那位建造了这道楼梯的老人的怀念——他们分期偿还债务，把房子公平地分给了当年一起爬过那磨损台阶的孩子们。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A family's efforts to work through an inheritance dispute after a grandfather's death
A biography of a coal miner's career
The history of staircase construction techniques
A legal guide to writing a will
questions.explain: 全文围绕林爷爷去世后家人如何处理房产与债务展开，第二、三段是核心内容，故答案为A。

questions.q: How did Grandfather Lin die?
options: In a fatal accident while working underground as a miner
Of old age at home
In a car accident downtown
The passage does not say
questions.explain: 依据第一段“long after his death in a fatal accident underground”，可知他死于井下事故。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the cousin's accusation against the uncle?
options: It was based on solid legal evidence presented in court
It arose mainly from suspicion and was never confirmed within the family meeting
It was proven false by a family lawyer
It led to the uncle being expelled from the family
questions.explain: 第三段只说表亲“convinced”并“ready to denounce”，随后被冷静的亲戚劝阻，全文未提供证据被证实或推翻，只能推断出于怀疑而非确凿证据。

questions.q: In paragraph 2, the word "intricate" is closest in meaning to
options: simple and clear
complicated and tangled
financially costly
legally binding
questions.explain: 上下文“proved more intricate than anyone had expected”与后文“老账本记录蹊跷”相呼应，intricate意为“复杂的，错综的”。

longSentences.sentence: He carved the narrow wooden staircase with his own hands, and long after his death in a fatal accident underground, its worn steps still creaked with the rhythm he had left behind.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列句：He carved the staircase...，and its worn steps still creaked with...
longSentences.modifiers: “with his own hands”方式状语；“long after his death in a fatal accident underground”时间状语，内嵌介词短语说明死因；“with the rhythm he had left behind”方式状语，其中he had left behind是省略关系代词修饰rhythm的定语从句
longSentences.translation: 他亲手打造了这道窄窄的木楼梯，而在他于井下遭遇致命事故去世多年后，那磨损的台阶仍以他留下的节奏咯吱作响。
longSentences.tip: 先找and连接的两个并列分句各自的主谓宾，再把中间的时间状语单独拎出来理解。

longSentences.sentence: It began in a brisk, businesslike tone, but the questions of who was entitled to what, and who should repay the outstanding loans, proved more intricate than anyone had expected.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列转折句：It began in...，but the questions...proved more intricate than...
longSentences.modifiers: “in a brisk, businesslike tone”方式状语；“of who was entitled to what, and who should repay the outstanding loans”同位语性质的介词短语，内含两个并列的宾语从句，说明questions的具体内容；“than anyone had expected”比较状语从句
longSentences.translation: 讨论一开始节奏轻快、公事公办，但“谁有权得到什么”“谁该偿还未清的欠款”这两个问题，却比任何人预想的都要复杂。
longSentences.tip: 先抓but后面的主干the questions proved more intricate，再把of后两个who从句当作questions的具体解释来读。

longSentences.sentence: In the end, it was not law but the memory of the man who had built that staircase that persuaded them to work things out together, repaying the debts in installments and dividing the house fairly among those who had once climbed its worn steps as children.
longSentences.skeleton: 强调句型：It was not A but B that persuaded them to...
longSentences.modifiers: “not law but the memory of the man”并列的被强调成分；“who had built that staircase”定语从句修饰the man；“to work things out together”不定式作宾语补足语；“repaying...and dividing...”现在分词短语并列作伴随状语；“among those who had once climbed its worn steps as children”介词短语内含定语从句修饰those
longSentences.translation: 最终，说服他们携手解决问题的，不是法律，而是对那位建造了这道楼梯的老人的怀念——他们分期偿还债务，把房子公平地分给了当年一起爬过那磨损台阶的孩子们。
longSentences.tip: 识别“It was not A but B that...”强调句结构，先确认被强调的是B（memory），再顺着that找谓语persuaded。

items.title: Small Changes That Saved His Health
items.passage:
Two years ago, Wei Ming was rushed to a downtown hospital after collapsing at his desk. Doctors told him plainly that, unlike most cases of simple exhaustion, his condition was close to becoming a matter of life and death. It was actually the outgoing head of his department, only weeks from retirement, who had convinced him to get a swift diagnosis instead of waiting for the symptoms to pass.

The changes that followed were not dramatic, but they gradually became part of his daily life. He cut his portions in half, gave up rich desserts, and started carrying a small handbag filled with fruit and water instead of snacks bought on the street. In the evenings, rather than sitting in front of a screen, he found a clever way to relax: painting outdoors, sometimes staying out until the moon had risen, and taking long walks through the park nearby.

A year later, colleagues who had known him before said he seemed like a different person, calmer and full of energy. He no longer felt he had nothing to look forward to; instead, he found real pleasure in small daily routines. His recovery, doctors agreed, was a reminder that even ordinary choices can matter just as much as any medicine.
items.translation:
两年前，魏明在办公桌前晕倒后被紧急送往市中心的一家医院。医生直言不讳地告诉他，与大多数单纯的疲劳过度不同，他的情况已经接近生死攸关的地步。其实，是他那位即将退休、只剩几周就要卸任的部门负责人，说服他去做一次快速诊断，而不是干等着症状自行消失。

接下来的这些改变并不剧烈，却渐渐成了他日常生活的一部分。他把饭量减半，戒掉了油腻的甜点，出门时开始带一个小手提包，里面装着水果和水，而不再买街边的零食。到了晚上，他不再对着屏幕枯坐，而是找到了一种聪明的放松方式：到户外画画，有时一直待到月亮升起，还会在附近的公园散很长时间的步。

一年后，认识他多年的同事都说他像换了一个人，变得更加沉稳、精力充沛。他不再觉得自己没有什么可期待的；相反，他从日常的小习惯中找到了真正的乐趣。医生们一致认为，他的康复提醒人们：哪怕是再普通不过的选择，也可能和任何药物一样重要。

keyWords.zh: ad./a. 市中心的，商业区的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: How a health scare led a man to adopt healthier daily habits
The medical procedures used to treat exhaustion
A comparison between city and rural hospitals
A retirement ceremony for a department head
questions.explain: 全文讲述魏明健康出问题后改变生活习惯并逐渐康复的过程，第一段起因，第二段具体改变，第三段结果，故答案为A。

questions.q: Who first convinced Wei Ming to get a swift diagnosis?
options: A doctor he met by chance downtown
His department head, who was about to retire
A colleague who had the same illness
His family members
questions.explain: 依据第一段“It was actually the outgoing head of his department, only weeks from retirement, who had convinced him...”，可知是即将退休的部门负责人。

questions.q: What can be inferred about Wei Ming's new evening activities?
options: They replaced screen time with more active, relaxing pursuits
They mainly involved working overtime at the office
They required him to stop seeing friends completely
They were recommended directly by his hospital
questions.explain: 第二段提到他不再对着屏幕，而是户外画画、散步，可推断新习惯以更主动、放松的活动取代了屏幕时间；文中并未提及是医院直接建议的。

questions.q: In paragraph 1, "outgoing" is closest in meaning to
options: sociable and friendly
about to leave a position
extremely strict
newly promoted
questions.explain: 上下文“only weeks from retirement”表明此处outgoing指“即将离任的”，而不是常见的“外向的”含义。

longSentences.sentence: Doctors told him plainly that, unlike most cases of simple exhaustion, his condition was close to becoming a matter of life and death.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Doctors told him that his condition was close to becoming...
longSentences.modifiers: “plainly”方式状语修饰told；“unlike most cases of simple exhaustion”插入的比较状语；that引导宾语从句
longSentences.translation: 医生直言不讳地告诉他，与大多数单纯的疲劳过度不同，他的情况已经接近生死攸关的地步。
longSentences.tip: 遇到逗号夹住的unlike插入语可先跳过，抓住that从句的主干his condition was close to...

longSentences.sentence: It was actually the outgoing head of his department, only weeks from retirement, who had convinced him to get a swift diagnosis instead of waiting for the symptoms to pass.
longSentences.skeleton: 强调句型：It was...who had convinced him to...
longSentences.modifiers: “actually”副词修饰强调结构；“only weeks from retirement”插入成分，补充说明department head；“instead of waiting for the symptoms to pass”介词短语作状语
longSentences.translation: 其实，是他那位即将退休、只剩几周就要卸任的部门负责人，说服他去做一次快速诊断，而不是干等着症状自行消失。
longSentences.tip: 先判断It was...who...是强调句型，被强调部分是the outgoing head of his department，再顺势找谓语convinced。

longSentences.sentence: In the evenings, rather than sitting in front of a screen, he found a clever way to relax: painting outdoors, sometimes staying out until the moon had risen, and taking long walks through the park nearby.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：he found a clever way to relax
longSentences.modifiers: “In the evenings”时间状语；“rather than sitting in front of a screen”对比状语；冒号后painting outdoors, staying out..., taking long walks三个并列现在分词短语具体说明“a clever way”
longSentences.translation: 到了晚上，他不再对着屏幕枯坐，而是找到了一种聪明的放松方式：到户外画画，有时一直待到月亮升起，还会在附近的公园散很长时间的步。
longSentences.tip: 冒号后的内容是对前面“a clever way to relax”的具体解释，抓住三个并列动名词短语理解细节。

items.title: Small Steps Toward Rural Change
items.passage:
Every few months, a group of urban volunteers travels to a small county in the mountains, arriving at regular intervals to support a project that has slowly changed the community. What began as one pioneer's idea five years ago is now held up as an example of how small, steady help can matter more than a single large donation.

The plan itself is simple: each willing family that wants to raise its own income receives a young goat, along with basic training on how to care for it. Volunteers also bring warm garments for children before the rainy season begins and arrange free medical checkups in the village square. In the early days, some volunteers were clumsy at handling the animals and unsure how to explain their plans in the local dialect, but they learned quickly from the villagers themselves.

Today, families that once depended entirely on a single crop have another source of income to fall back on, and many say daily life has become noticeably nicer. Word of the program has spread, and neighboring villages have asked to join. For the volunteers, the greatest reward is not gratitude but watching families become capable of managing their own future.
items.translation:
每隔几个月，一群城市志愿者就会前往山区的一个小县城，按固定的时间间隔去支持一个悄悄改变了当地社区的项目。这个项目最初只是五年前一位开拓者的一个想法，如今却被视为一个范例，证明持续不断的小小帮助，往往比一次性的大笔捐助更有意义。

这个方案本身很简单：每个愿意通过养殖增加收入的家庭，都会得到一只小山羊，以及饲养方面的基础培训。志愿者们还会在雨季来临之前给孩子们送去保暖的衣物，并在村口的广场上安排免费的医疗检查。项目刚开始时，一些志愿者在照料这些动物时显得笨手笨脚，也不太清楚该怎样用当地方言解释自己的计划，但他们很快就从村民那里学会了。

如今，那些曾经完全依赖单一作物的家庭，多了另一份可以依靠的收入来源，不少人说日常生活明显变得更好了。这个项目的口碑逐渐传开，邻近的村庄也纷纷要求加入。对志愿者来说，最大的回报不是感激，而是亲眼看着这些家庭逐渐有能力掌握自己的未来。

keyWords.zh: a. 自愿的，心甘情愿的

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: A volunteer program helps rural families raise income through steady, small-scale support
A history of large international charity organizations
The difficulties of raising goats in mountain regions
A comparison between urban and rural medical care
questions.explain: 全文讲述志愿者项目通过赠送山羊、送衣物、提供医疗检查等小规模持续帮助来改善乡村家庭收入，主旨在第一段末句已点明，故答案为A。

questions.q: What do volunteers give to families as the core part of the income project?
options: Money to start a small business
A young goat and basic training on raising it
Free housing in the county town
Farming machinery
questions.explain: 依据第二段第一句“each willing family...receives a young goat, along with basic training on how to care for it”。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the volunteers' early experience in the village?
options: They were experienced farmers before joining the project
They faced some initial difficulties but improved with the villagers' help
They refused to learn the local dialect
The villagers rejected their help at first
questions.explain: 第二段提到早期志愿者“were clumsy at handling the animals and unsure how to explain their plans”，但随后“learned quickly from the villagers themselves”，说明起初有困难但很快在村民帮助下有所改善。

questions.q: In paragraph 1, the word "pioneer" is closest in meaning to
options: someone who follows an established tradition
someone who starts something new before others
a government official
a professional farmer
questions.explain: 上下文“What began as one pioneer's idea five years ago”指该项目最初由一个开创性想法发起，pioneer意为“开拓者，先驱”。

longSentences.sentence: What began as one pioneer's idea five years ago is now held up as an example of how small, steady help can matter more than a single large donation.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：What began as...is now held up as an example of how...
longSentences.modifiers: “What began as one pioneer's idea five years ago”主语从句；“of how small, steady help can matter more than a single large donation”介词短语，内嵌how引导的宾语从句，修饰example
longSentences.translation: 这个项目最初只是五年前一位开拓者的一个想法，如今却被视为一个范例，证明持续不断的小小帮助，往往比一次性的大笔捐助更有意义。
longSentences.tip: What引导的主语从句作整句主语，先找到句子真正的谓语is held up，再处理后面how引导的从句。

longSentences.sentence: In the early days, some volunteers were clumsy at handling the animals and unsure how to explain their plans in the local dialect, but they learned quickly from the villagers themselves.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列句：some volunteers were clumsy...and unsure...，but they learned quickly...
longSentences.modifiers: “at handling the animals”介词短语修饰clumsy；“how to explain their plans in the local dialect”疑问式不定式短语作unsure的宾语；“from the villagers themselves”状语修饰learned
longSentences.translation: 项目刚开始时，一些志愿者在照料这些动物时显得笨手笨脚，也不太清楚该怎样用当地方言解释自己的计划，但他们很快就从村民那里学会了。
longSentences.tip: 先抓住but前后两个并列分句的主谓were clumsy/unsure和learned，再补充各自的修饰成分。

longSentences.sentence: Today, families that once depended entirely on a single crop have another source of income to fall back on, and many say daily life has become noticeably nicer.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列句：families...have another source of income...，and many say (that) daily life has become...
longSentences.modifiers: “that once depended entirely on a single crop”定语从句修饰families；“to fall back on”不定式作定语修饰source of income；宾语从句daily life has become noticeably nicer
longSentences.translation: 如今，那些曾经完全依赖单一作物的家庭，多了另一份可以依靠的收入来源，不少人说日常生活明显变得更好了。
longSentences.tip: 先剥离定语从句that once depended...，找到families的真正谓语have，再看第二个分句的宾语从句。

items.title: Confronting Climate Change Step by Step
items.passage:
Since the early days of industrialization, human activity has released ever larger amounts of carbon dioxide into the atmosphere, and scientists now broadly agree on the basic mechanism behind global warming. Documentaries and news reports often portray the issue through dramatic images of forests that blaze for weeks or glaciers that crack apart, yet the underlying science is far less sensational: it is simply the slow accumulation of gases that trap heat.

This explanation, once treated with suspicion in certain circles, has become part of the orthodox scientific consensus, and most governments now anticipate that stricter regulation will eventually follow. Progress remains slow, however, partly because proposals to cut emissions often disappear into layers of bureaucracy before they are ever put into practice. Meanwhile, ordinary citizens face the consequences directly, from sticky, unbearably humid summers to sudden storms and unpredictable harvests.

Some cities have tried to trigger change at a smaller scale, encouraging residents to pedal to work rather than drive and rewarding companies that reduce waste. Such measures alone will not resolve the crisis, but they show that action need not wait for perfect agreement among nations. What is needed, most researchers argue, is neither panic nor slack indifference, but a steady, sustained effort matched to the scale of the problem.
items.translation:
自工业化早期以来，人类活动向大气中排放了越来越多的二氧化碳，科学家们如今在全球变暖的基本机制上已基本达成共识。纪录片和新闻报道常常通过烈焰连烧数周的森林、或崩裂消融的冰川等震撼画面来描绘这一问题，然而其背后的科学原理其实远没有那么耸人听闻：这不过是能困住热量的气体在缓慢累积的过程。

这一解释曾在某些圈子里遭到怀疑，如今却已成为正统科学共识的一部分，大多数政府也都预计更严格的监管终将随之出台。然而进展依然缓慢，部分原因在于减排提案往往还没来得及付诸实施，就先被淹没在层层官僚程序之中。与此同时，普通民众正直接承受着后果：从闷热难耐的黏湿夏季，到突如其来的风暴，再到难以预料的歉收。

一些城市尝试在较小范围内触发改变，鼓励居民骑车上班而非开车，并奖励那些减少浪费的企业。这类举措单靠自身并不能解决这场危机，但它们表明，行动并不需要等到各国达成完美一致才能展开。大多数研究者认为，眼下需要的既不是恐慌，也不是松懈麻木，而是一种与问题规模相匹配、持续不断的努力。

keyWords.zh: n. 官僚机构，官僚主义
a. 黏的；（引申）闷热潮湿的
vi. 燃烧 n. 火焰

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: The science of climate change is disputed among most scientists today
Climate change is well understood scientifically, but political and social responses remain slow and small-scale
Only urban cycling programs can solve climate change
Bureaucracy is the sole cause of global warming
questions.explain: 第一段说明科学原理已获广泛认同，第二段说明政策推进因官僚程序而缓慢，第三段介绍小规模行动，整体主旨是科学共识已成但应对行动仍缓慢、局部，B项概括最准确。

questions.q: According to the passage, why does climate policy often move slowly?
options: Scientists still disagree about the basic mechanism of warming
Emission-cutting proposals often get stuck in bureaucratic processes
Citizens refuse to accept any government regulation
Cities lack the technology to reduce emissions
questions.explain: 依据第二段“proposals to cut emissions often disappear into layers of bureaucracy before they are ever put into practice”。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the cycling and waste-reduction programs mentioned in paragraph 3?
options: They are presented as complete solutions to climate change on their own
They are small-scale efforts that demonstrate action is possible without full international agreement
They have replaced the need for government regulation entirely
They were rejected by most city residents
questions.explain: 第三段明确说“Such measures alone will not resolve the crisis, but they show that action need not wait for perfect agreement among nations”，说明这些举措只是表明局部行动可行，并非完整解决方案。

questions.q: In paragraph 2, "sticky" is used to describe
options: surfaces that are covered in glue
uncomfortably hot and humid weather conditions
a type of bureaucratic paperwork
glaciers that are melting slowly
questions.explain: 上下文“from sticky, unbearably humid summers to sudden storms”，sticky与humid并列，用来形容闷热潮湿的夏季天气，而非字面“黏的”物体表面。

longSentences.sentence: Documentaries and news reports often portray the issue through dramatic images of forests that blaze for weeks or glaciers that crack apart, yet the underlying science is far less sensational: it is simply the slow accumulation of gases that trap heat.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列句：Documentaries...portray the issue...，yet the underlying science is far less sensational
longSentences.modifiers: “through dramatic images of forests that blaze for weeks or glaciers that crack apart”方式状语，内含两个that引导的定语从句分别修饰forests和glaciers；冒号后“it is simply...that trap heat”进一步解释science的本质，that trap heat为定语从句修饰gases
longSentences.translation: 纪录片和新闻报道常常通过烈焰连烧数周的森林、或崩裂消融的冰川等震撼画面来描绘这一问题，然而其背后的科学原理其实远没有那么耸人听闻：这不过是能困住热量的气体在缓慢累积的过程。
longSentences.tip: 先抓yet前后两个并列分句的主干，再把through引导的长状语和两个定语从句拆开单独理解。

longSentences.sentence: This explanation, once treated with suspicion in certain circles, has become part of the orthodox scientific consensus, and most governments now anticipate that stricter regulation will eventually follow.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列句：This explanation...has become part of...，and most governments...anticipate that...
longSentences.modifiers: “once treated with suspicion in certain circles”插入的过去分词短语作状语，修饰explanation；“that stricter regulation will eventually follow”宾语从句作anticipate的宾语
longSentences.translation: 这一解释曾在某些圈子里遭到怀疑，如今却已成为正统科学共识的一部分，大多数政府也都预计更严格的监管终将随之出台。
longSentences.tip: 逗号中间的过去分词短语是插入的背景信息，先跳过它抓主干This explanation has become part of...，再处理第二个分句的宾语从句。

longSentences.sentence: What is needed, most researchers argue, is neither panic nor slack indifference, but a steady, sustained effort matched to the scale of the problem.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：What is needed is neither...nor...，but...
longSentences.modifiers: “most researchers argue”插入语；“matched to the scale of the problem”过去分词短语作后置定语修饰effort
longSentences.translation: 大多数研究者认为，眼下需要的既不是恐慌，也不是松懈麻木，而是一种与问题规模相匹配、持续不断的努力。
longSentences.tip: What引导的主语从句作句子主语，中间most researchers argue是插入语可先忽略，抓住is neither...nor...but...的并列表语结构。

items.title: From Home Brew to Small Business
items.passage:
For Mia Chen, weekend mornings once followed a familiar ritual: measuring grains, boiling water, and watching the mixture bubble in a five-gallon pot on her kitchen stove. Brewing beer at home began purely as a leisure pursuit, something to unwind with after a demanding week at her graphic-design job. She rarely thought of it as anything more than a hobby that let her mind wander.

That changed when neighbors started asking to buy bottles instead of simply tasting them for free. Encouraged by the growing demand, Mia sought counsel from a friend who had already launched a small food business. The advice was practically the same every time: start small, keep records, and let the product speak for itself. Interestingly, her design skills and her new brewing venture began to overlap—she used her professional eye for color and layout to design labels that made her bottles instantly recognizable at local markets.

Within two years, what had begun almost by accident had grown into a modest but steady company primarily serving neighborhood restaurants and bars. Mia insists the growth is not perpetual; she expects slow seasons and plans for them. Still, she is proud that a quiet Saturday ritual once meant only for herself has become something dozens of customers now look forward to each week.
items.translation:
对米娅·陈来说，曾经的周末早晨总遵循着一套熟悉的惯例：称量麦芽、煮水，看着混合物在厨房炉灶上的五加仑大锅里咕嘟冒泡。在家酿啤酒起初纯粹是一种消遣，是她在平面设计工作忙碌一周后放松身心的方式。她很少把它当作爱好以外的事情，只是一种让思绪随意游荡的方式。

当邻居们开始主动提出想买瓶装啤酒，而不只是免费尝一尝时，情况发生了变化。面对日益增长的需求，米娅向一位已经开办过小型食品生意的朋友请教。对方给出的建议几乎每次都一样：从小做起，做好记录，让产品自己说话。有趣的是，她的设计技能和这项新的酿酒事业开始产生了交集——她运用自己在色彩与版式方面的专业眼光设计酒标，使她的瓶装啤酒在本地市集上一眼就能被认出。

两年之内，这个几乎是偶然开始的项目，已经发展成一家规模不大却稳步经营的公司，主要为周边的餐厅和酒吧供货。米娅坚持认为这种增长不会永远持续下去；她预料到会有清淡的季节，并为此做好准备。尽管如此，她仍为此感到自豪：曾经只属于自己的一个安静的周六惯例，如今已成为每周都有数十名顾客翘首以盼的事情。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: How a woman transformed a weekend hobby into a small company
The technical process of brewing craft beer at home
Why graphic designers often start side businesses
The history and culture of local beer markets
questions.explain: 文章通篇讲述米娅如何把周末酿酒的爱好逐步发展成一家为本地商家供货的小公司，属于全文主旨题，答案信息分布在三段之中。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, what first made Mia consider selling her beer?
options: A magazine article about craft breweries
Neighbors offering to pay for her bottles
Advice from a business school professor
A sudden loss of her design job
questions.explain: 第二段开头提到“neighbors started asking to buy bottles instead of simply tasting them for free”，说明是邻居主动购买促使她开始考虑经营，对应B项。

questions.q: What can be inferred about Mia's attitude toward her business's future?
options: She expects sales to increase every month without exception.
She plans to quit her design job as soon as possible.
She realistically anticipates ups and downs rather than nonstop growth.
She regrets that the business grew beyond a hobby.
questions.explain: 第三段提到“Mia insists the growth is not perpetual; she expects slow seasons and plans for them”，说明她对未来增长持理性、非盲目乐观的态度，对应C项。

questions.q: The word "overlap" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to...
options: conflict
coincide and intersect
disappear
compete
questions.explain: 此处overlap指她的设计技能与酿酒事业“产生交集”，即二者部分重合、相互促进，故选与“重合、交叉”含义相近的选项。

longSentences.sentence: Encouraged by the growing demand, Mia sought counsel from a friend who had already launched a small food business.
longSentences.skeleton: Mia sought counsel from a friend。
longSentences.modifiers: Encouraged by the growing demand作句首状语，说明原因；who had already launched a small food business是定语从句，修饰a friend。
longSentences.translation: 在日益增长的需求的鼓励下，米娅向一位已经开办过小型食品生意的朋友请教。
longSentences.tip: 遇到句首的过去分词短语，先找到其逻辑主语（通常是主句主语），再顺势理解主句主谓宾。

longSentences.sentence: Brewing beer at home began purely as a leisure pursuit, something to unwind with after a demanding week at her graphic-design job.
longSentences.skeleton: Brewing beer began as a leisure pursuit。
longSentences.modifiers: something to unwind with...是同位语，对leisure pursuit进一步说明；不定式to unwind with作后置定语修饰something。
longSentences.translation: 在家酿啤酒起初纯粹是一种消遣，是她在平面设计工作忙碌一周后放松身心的方式。
longSentences.tip: 同位语成分常用逗号隔开，起补充说明作用，可先跳过同位语抓主干，再回头理解补充内容。

longSentences.sentence: Still, she is proud that a quiet Saturday ritual once meant only for herself has become something dozens of customers now look forward to each week.
longSentences.skeleton: She is proud that a ritual has become something。
longSentences.modifiers: that引导宾语从句作proud的宾语；once meant only for herself是过去分词短语作定语修饰ritual；dozens of customers now look forward to each week是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰something。
longSentences.translation: 尽管如此，她仍为此感到自豪：曾经只属于自己的一个安静的周六惯例，如今已成为每周都有数十名顾客翘首以盼的事情。
longSentences.tip: 宾语从句中若又嵌套定语从句，要先理清主句结构，再逐层拆分嵌套的从句。

items.title: A Quiet Path to Career Success
items.passage:
Career counselors often note that success in the workplace is largely determined not just by technical skill but by a set of soft abilities that underlie almost every promotion decision. Among these, the willingness to socialize with colleagues across departments stands out as particularly valuable, since informal conversations frequently lead to opportunities that never appear in a formal job posting.

Take the case of a young accountant who, rather than waiting quietly for recognition, made an earnest effort to introduce herself to managers outside her own team. She attended company events, asked sincere questions about other people's work, and was quick to grab any project that let her learn something new. Colleagues later admitted that her enthusiasm gave them a glimpse of leadership qualities they had not expected from someone so junior.

Two years later, when the company began planning for the succession of a retiring director, her name came up almost immediately. It was a striking outcome for someone who had simply tried to steer her own career with curiosity rather than a fixed plan. Her story reminds younger employees that visible ambition matters less than consistent, genuine engagement with the people around them.
items.translation:
职业顾问常常指出，职场上的成功很大程度上不仅取决于专业技能，还取决于一系列构成几乎每一次晋升决定基础的软实力。其中，愿意与其他部门的同事交际尤其可贵，因为非正式的交谈常常能带来那些从不会出现在正式招聘信息里的机会。

以一位年轻会计师为例：她没有安静地等待被人注意，而是认真主动地向本部门以外的经理们做自我介绍。她参加公司活动，就别人的工作提出真诚的问题，并总是抓住任何能让自己学到新东西的项目机会。同事们后来承认，她的热情让他们看到了原本没想到会出现在一位如此资历尚浅的员工身上的领导潜质。

两年后，当公司开始为一位即将退休的总监规划继任人选时，她的名字几乎立刻被提了出来。对于一个只是凭着好奇心而非固定计划来把握自己职业方向的人来说，这是一个引人瞩目的结果。她的故事提醒年轻员工：外露的雄心其实不如与周围人持续、真诚的交往来得重要。

keyWords.zh: a. 显著的，引人注目的

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Technical skills are the only factor that determines promotion.
Genuine engagement with colleagues can quietly build a successful career.
Company events are mainly designed to reward senior staff.
Young accountants rarely have opportunities to meet company directors.
questions.explain: 全文借一位年轻会计师的经历说明：真诚待人、主动与同事交往等软实力，往往比技术能力或刻意规划更能推动职业发展，这是全文主旨。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, how did the young accountant behave toward colleagues outside her team?
options: She avoided them to focus on her own department's work.
She waited for managers to notice her achievements first.
She actively introduced herself and asked genuine questions about their work.
She only communicated with them through formal emails.
questions.explain: 第二段描述她“made an earnest effort to introduce herself to managers outside her own team”并“asked sincere questions about other people's work”，对应C项的主动、真诚交流。

questions.q: It can be inferred from paragraph 3 that the accountant was chosen for succession mainly because she...
options: had worked in the company the longest.
had built genuine relationships across the company over time.
had a formal plan to become director from day one.
was related to the retiring director.
questions.explain: 第三段指出她的成功是“someone who had simply tried to steer her own career with curiosity rather than a fixed plan”，结合全文她长期真诚地与人交往，可推知她被选中主要因为已建立起真实的人际关系，而非资历或刻意规划，对应B项。

questions.q: The word "underlie" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to...
options: replace
form the basis of
compete with
come after
questions.explain: underlie在此指软实力“构成几乎每一次晋升决定的基础”，与“form the basis of（成为……的基础）”含义最接近。

longSentences.sentence: Career counselors often note that success in the workplace is largely determined not just by technical skill but by a set of soft abilities that underlie almost every promotion decision.
longSentences.skeleton: Career counselors note that success is determined by soft abilities。
longSentences.modifiers: that引导宾语从句作note的宾语；not just...but...结构强调决定因素不止一个；that underlie almost every promotion decision是定语从句，修饰a set of soft abilities。
longSentences.translation: 职业顾问常常指出，职场上的成功很大程度上不仅取决于专业技能，还取决于一系列构成几乎每一次晋升决定基础的软实力。
longSentences.tip: 看到not just...but...结构时，要注意两个并列成分共同修饰宾语从句的核心内容，别只读到but前就停。

longSentences.sentence: Take the case of a young accountant who, rather than waiting quietly for recognition, made an earnest effort to introduce herself to managers outside her own team.
longSentences.skeleton: Take the case of a young accountant who made an effort to introduce herself。
longSentences.modifiers: who引导定语从句修饰a young accountant；rather than waiting quietly for recognition是插入的对比状语，说明她没有做什么。
longSentences.translation: 以一位年轻会计师为例：她没有安静地等待被人注意，而是认真主动地向本部门以外的经理们做自我介绍。
longSentences.tip: 插入语常用逗号隔开，可先跳过它抓住主干（who made an effort...），再回头理解插入部分的补充对比含义。

longSentences.sentence: Colleagues later admitted that her enthusiasm gave them a glimpse of leadership qualities they had not expected from someone so junior.
