longSentences.skeleton: Colleagues admitted that her enthusiasm gave them a glimpse of leadership qualities。
longSentences.modifiers: that引导宾语从句；they had not expected from someone so junior是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰leadership qualities。
longSentences.translation: 同事们后来承认，她的热情让他们看到了原本没想到会出现在一位如此资历尚浅的员工身上的领导潜质。
longSentences.tip: 定语从句省略关系代词时，从句紧跟在被修饰名词后面，读到主谓结构突然出现要反应过来这是省略了that/which的定语从句。

items.title: A Summer Visit to My Cousin's Farm
items.passage:
Last summer I visited my cousin, who lives on a small farm far from the city and has always loved agriculture. From the moment I arrived, life there felt completely opposite to my busy days at school. Instead of staring at a computer screen, I spent my mornings walking through green fields and helping with simple farm work.

One afternoon we joined the villagers in the fields to bring in the rice harvest. The sun was strong, and soon sweat covered my face; my shoes turned muddy after only a few steps. My cousin laughed and said that nothing else could teach me so much about hard work in such a short time. That evening the whole village held a small ceremony to celebrate the harvest, and an old poet who had lived there his whole life read a poem about the quiet deeds of farmers.

Before I left, my cousin told me that a scientist from the city had recently visited to study why some crops kept getting sick, almost like catching the flu. Villagers decided to donate part of their harvest to a nearby town that had suffered a poor season. Sitting there, I realized that here, far from any city, people still found real happiness in ordinary work.
items.translation:
去年夏天我去看望住在乡下小农场里的表哥，他一直很喜欢农业。从我到达的那一刻起，那里的生活似乎和我在学校忙碌的日子完全相反。我没有整天盯着电脑屏幕，而是每天早晨走在绿油油的田野里，帮忙做一些简单的农活。

一天下午，我们和村民们一起下地收割水稻。太阳很毒，没过多久我脸上就布满了汗水；才走几步，鞋子就沾满了泥。表哥笑着说，再没有什么别的事能在这么短的时间里让我明白劳动有多辛苦了。那天晚上，全村人举行了一个小小的庆典来庆祝丰收，一位在那里生活了一辈子的老诗人朗诵了一首关于农民默默耕耘的诗。

临走前，表哥告诉我，最近有一位来自城里的科学家到村里，研究为什么有些庄稼总是生病，就好像得了流感一样。村民们决定把一部分收成捐给附近一个收成不好的小镇。坐在那里，我忽然明白，即使远离城市，人们依然能从平凡的劳作中找到真正的快乐。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The writer's experience visiting a cousin's farm during summer vacation
A scientist's research on rice diseases
The history of farming ceremonies in China
Why city life is better than country life
questions.explain: 全文按时间顺序讲述作者暑假到表哥的农场做客、参与收割、感受乡村生活的经历，属于记叙文的主旨概括题。

questions.q: What happened during the harvest work described in paragraph 2?
options: The writer stayed inside and watched others work.
The writer got sweaty and muddy while helping in the fields.
The village cancelled the harvest ceremony that year.
The cousin refused to join the villagers.
questions.explain: 第二段明确写道“soon sweat covered my face; my shoes turned muddy after only a few steps”，说明作者在田间劳动时出汗、鞋子沾泥，对应B项。

questions.q: What can be inferred from paragraph 3?
options: The village had a poor harvest that year and needed help from others.
The village's harvest was good enough to help another town in need.
The scientist came to buy the village's entire harvest.
The writer decided to become a farmer after the visit.
questions.explain: 第三段提到村民“decided to donate part of their harvest to a nearby town that had suffered a poor season”，说明本村收成尚可，才有余力捐助收成不好的邻镇，可推知该村这一年收成不错，对应B项。

questions.q: The word "deeds" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to...
options: words
actions
dreams
tools
questions.explain: 此处deeds与诗歌所描写的“quiet deeds of farmers（农民默默无闻的行为）”相关，指人们做的事情、行为，与actions含义最接近。

longSentences.sentence: Last summer I visited my cousin, who lives on a small farm far from the city and has always loved agriculture.
longSentences.skeleton: I visited my cousin。
longSentences.modifiers: who引导非限制性定语从句，修饰my cousin，说明他住在哪里、喜欢什么；far from the city是地点状语修饰farm。
longSentences.translation: 去年夏天我去看望住在乡下小农场里的表哥，他一直很喜欢农业。
longSentences.tip: 遇到who/which引导的从句，先找到它修饰的名词，再把从句内容当作对这个名词的补充说明来理解。

longSentences.sentence: That evening the whole village held a small ceremony to celebrate the harvest, and an old poet who had lived there his whole life read a poem about the quiet deeds of farmers.
longSentences.skeleton: The village held a ceremony, and a poet read a poem。
longSentences.modifiers: and连接两个并列分句；who had lived there his whole life是定语从句修饰an old poet；to celebrate the harvest是不定式作目的状语。
longSentences.translation: 那天晚上，全村人举行了一个小小的庆典来庆祝丰收，一位在那里生活了一辈子的老诗人朗诵了一首关于农民默默耕耘的诗。
longSentences.tip: 长句中出现and连接两个完整分句时，可以把它们拆成两个独立的短句分别理解，再看两者之间的逻辑关系。

longSentences.sentence: Villagers decided to donate part of their harvest to a nearby town that had suffered a poor season.
longSentences.skeleton: Villagers decided to donate part of their harvest to a town。
longSentences.modifiers: that had suffered a poor season是定语从句，修饰a nearby town。
longSentences.translation: 村民们决定把一部分收成捐给附近一个收成不好的小镇。
longSentences.tip: that引导的定语从句紧跟在名词后面时，先把从句去掉读主干，再补充理解从句对名词的限定说明。

items.title: Scientists Refine a New Waterproof Material
items.passage:
In a small laboratory outside Boston, a team of materials scientists has spent nearly a decade trying to design a coating that can withstand some of the harshest conditions on Earth—deep-sea pressure, extreme cold, and constant exposure to salt water. Their goal was ambitious from the start: to create a waterproof material light enough for diving equipment yet strong enough to survive years underwater without cracking.

The project nearly ended twice. Funding cuts forced the team to cease testing in 2019, and many assumed the idea had quietly died. Then, three years later, a chance conversation with an ocean-engineering firm sparked a revival of interest. Encouraged by fresh investment, the researchers proposed an updated formula, replacing one key chemical with a cheaper, more stable compound, and spent months refining the mixture until it passed every pressure test they could devise.

What began as a narrow engineering problem has grown into something with far greater breadth than anyone expected, from ship hulls to outdoor clothing to underwater sensors. The team remains cautious about the material's long-term outlook, noting that laboratory success does not always translate into industrial use. Still, after years of setbacks, they say simply reaching this stage already feels like a genuine achievement.
items.translation:
在波士顿郊外一间小型实验室里，一组材料科学家花费了近十年时间，试图设计出一种能够抵御地球上最恶劣环境的涂层材料——深海压力、极端寒冷，以及持续接触盐水的腐蚀。他们从一开始的目标就很有野心：研制出一种既足够轻、能用于潜水装备，又足够坚固、能在水下经年累月而不开裂的防水材料。

这个项目曾两度濒临夭折。2019年，经费削减迫使团队停止了测试，许多人以为这个想法已经悄然夭折。三年后，一次与海洋工程公司的偶然交谈，重新点燃了人们对这一项目的兴趣。得益于新一轮投资的支持，研究人员提出了一套改良配方，用一种更便宜、更稳定的化合物替换了其中一种关键成分，并花了数月时间不断调配、精炼这种混合物，直到它通过了他们能设计出的每一项压力测试。

这个最初只是一个狭窄的工程问题，如今其应用范围之广已远远超出了所有人的预期，从船体到户外服装再到水下传感器都能用到它。团队对这种材料的长期前景仍保持谨慎，指出实验室里的成功并不总能转化为工业上的应用。尽管如此，历经多年的挫折，他们说，仅仅走到这一步，就已经是一项真正的成就。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A team's long struggle to develop and improve a durable waterproof material
The history of ocean-engineering companies in the United States
Why funding for scientific research has decreased in recent years
A new type of diving equipment sold to the public
questions.explain: 全文以时间顺序讲述某材料科学团队历经十年、几度中断，最终不断改良出一种耐用防水材料的过程，属于全文主旨概括题。

questions.q: Why did the team stop testing in 2019, according to paragraph 2?
options: They had already achieved their research goal.
A chance conversation with an ocean-engineering firm ended the project.
A cut in funding forced them to halt the work.
Government regulations banned the chemical they were using.
questions.explain: 第二段明确指出“Funding cuts forced the team to cease testing in 2019”，即经费削减迫使团队停止测试，对应C项。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the researchers' attitude toward the material's future in paragraph 3?
options: They are certain it will soon be used in every industry.
They are cautiously optimistic, aware that lab success may not guarantee industrial use.
They believe the project has already failed completely.
They plan to abandon further testing immediately.
questions.explain: 第三段提到团队“remains cautious about the material's long-term outlook, noting that laboratory success does not always translate into industrial use”，同时又称这已是“a genuine achievement”，说明他们的态度是谨慎中带着肯定，对应B项。

questions.q: The word "revival" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to...
options: a sudden failure
a renewed interest or activity
a permanent end
a formal complaint
questions.explain: 此处revival指项目在沉寂之后因一次偶然交谈而“重新引发兴趣”，即兴趣或活动的复苏、重燃，与“a renewed interest or activity”含义最接近。

longSentences.sentence: In a small laboratory outside Boston, a team of materials scientists has spent nearly a decade trying to design a coating that can withstand some of the harshest conditions on Earth—deep-sea pressure, extreme cold, and constant exposure to salt water.
longSentences.skeleton: A team of scientists has spent a decade trying to design a coating。
longSentences.modifiers: In a small laboratory outside Boston是地点状语；that can withstand...是定语从句修饰a coating；破折号后deep-sea pressure, extreme cold, and constant exposure to salt water是对harshest conditions的具体列举说明。
longSentences.translation: 在波士顿郊外一间小型实验室里，一组材料科学家花费了近十年时间，试图设计出一种能够抵御地球上最恶劣环境的涂层材料——深海压力、极端寒冷，以及持续接触盐水的腐蚀。
longSentences.tip: 破折号后面常是对前文名词或短语的具体解释或举例，可以将其看作插入的补充说明，不影响主干理解。

longSentences.sentence: Encouraged by fresh investment, the researchers proposed an updated formula, replacing one key chemical with a cheaper, more stable compound, and spent months refining the mixture until it passed every pressure test they could devise.
longSentences.skeleton: The researchers proposed a formula and spent months refining the mixture。
longSentences.modifiers: Encouraged by fresh investment是句首过去分词短语作原因状语；replacing one key chemical with...是现在分词短语作伴随状语；until引导时间状语从句，they could devise是省略关系代词修饰every pressure test的定语从句。
longSentences.translation: 得益于新一轮投资的支持，研究人员提出了一套改良配方，用一种更便宜、更稳定的化合物替换了其中一种关键成分，并花了数月时间不断调配、精炼这种混合物，直到它通过了他们能设计出的每一项压力测试。
longSentences.tip: 长句中若出现多个分词短语，可先抓住并列谓语proposed...and spent...这条主干，再分别理解各分词短语的补充作用。

longSentences.sentence: What began as a narrow engineering problem has grown into something with far greater breadth than anyone expected, from ship hulls to outdoor clothing to underwater sensors.
longSentences.skeleton: What began as a problem has grown into something with greater breadth。
longSentences.modifiers: What began as a narrow engineering problem是主语从句；than anyone expected是比较状语从句；from...to...to...是介词短语作举例说明。
longSentences.translation: 这个最初只是一个狭窄的工程问题，如今其应用范围之广已远远超出了所有人的预期，从船体到户外服装再到水下传感器都能用到它。
longSentences.tip: 句子以what引导的主语从句开头时，要先把整个what从句当作一个大的“名词”，再看它后面的谓语动词。

items.title: Managing Stress at Work
items.passage:
Many companies now recognize that an employee's emotional state directly affects productivity, so human resources teams have started offering psychological training sessions for their staff. A moderate amount of stress can actually prompt people to work harder and stay alert, but too much pressure often has the opposite effect, leaving employees anxious and unfocused.

To help staff conquer these negative feelings, a well known consulting firm recently designed a short training script that managers could read aloud during team meetings. The script contains several excerpts from popular psychology books, along with practical tips such as keeping a daily task list up to date and setting realistic goals. Employees are also encouraged to entertain positive thoughts rather than dwell on small mistakes, since a calmer mind usually leads to clearer judgment.

According to the trainers, workers who follow this simple routine presently report feeling far less overwhelmed after only a few weeks. The ultimate goal is not to remove pressure altogether but to maximize the benefits of moderate stress while keeping its harmful side effects under control. In this way, both employees and their companies can build a healthier, more balanced working environment.
items.translation:
许多公司如今认识到，员工的情绪状态直接影响工作效率，因此人力资源部门开始为员工提供心理培训课程。适度的压力实际上可以促使人们更加努力、保持警觉，但压力过大往往适得其反，会让员工感到焦虑、难以集中注意力。

为了帮助员工克服这些负面情绪，一家知名咨询公司最近设计了一份简短的培训讲稿，供管理者在团队会议上朗读。讲稿中摘录了几段颇受欢迎的心理学书籍内容，并附有一些实用建议，例如保持每日任务清单的更新，以及设定切实可行的目标。员工们还被鼓励多想些积极的事情，而不是纠结于小失误，因为平静的心态通常会带来更清晰的判断。

培训师表示，坚持这一简单做法的员工，仅仅几周后就表示自己不再那么不堪重负。最终目标并不是要彻底消除压力，而是在把压力的负面影响控制住的同时，最大限度地发挥适度压力的益处。这样一来，员工和公司都能够建立起更健康、更平衡的工作环境。

keyWords.zh: a. 最新的，跟上时代的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: How companies use scripted movies to entertain employees during breaks.
How moderate stress can be managed to benefit both employees and companies.
Why psychological training sessions should be canceled to reduce costs.
The history of stress management research in the workplace.
questions.explain: 文章围绕企业如何帮助员工管理压力、发挥适度压力的积极作用展开，第一段提出问题，第二、三段给出具体做法与结果，主旨为B；A、C、D均与原文不符或未提及，依据全文。

questions.q: According to the passage, what does the training script include?
options: Excerpts from psychology books and practical tips like updating task lists.
A list of employees who suffer from severe anxiety.
Instructions on how to write formal business reports.
A record of company profits over the past five years.
questions.explain: 第二段明确提到讲稿中摘录了心理学书籍内容，并附有保持任务清单更新等实用建议，依据在第二段。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the passage that the trainers believe ______.
options: all workplace stress should be completely eliminated.
a certain amount of stress is not necessarily harmful.
employees should avoid setting daily goals.
reading psychology books has no real effect on stress.
questions.explain: 第三段提到最终目标不是彻底消除压力，而是发挥适度压力的益处，说明适度压力并非全然有害，可推知B正确，依据第三段；A与原文相悖，C、D原文未提及或相反。

questions.q: In the sentence "Employees are also encouraged to entertain positive thoughts rather than dwell on small mistakes," the word "entertain" is closest in meaning to ______.
options: invite guests
hold in mind
perform on stage
ignore completely
questions.explain: 此处entertain并非“招待”或“表演”之意，而是“心里想着、抱有（某种想法）”，与hold in mind同义，依据第二段句意判断。

longSentences.sentence: Many companies now recognize that an employee's emotional state directly affects productivity, so human resources teams have started offering psychological training sessions for their staff.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“companies recognize that... so teams have started offering...”
longSentences.modifiers: that引导宾语从句作recognize的宾语；so引导结果状语从句
longSentences.translation: 许多公司如今认识到，员工的情绪状态直接影响工作效率，因此人力资源部门开始为员工提供心理培训课程。
longSentences.tip: 遇到so连接的结果状语从句，可先理解前半句原因，再看后半句结果，把握因果逻辑。

longSentences.sentence: To help staff conquer these negative feelings, a well known consulting firm recently designed a short training script that managers could read aloud during team meetings.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“a consulting firm designed a training script”
longSentences.modifiers: To help...为目的状语；that引导定语从句修饰script
longSentences.translation: 为了帮助员工克服这些负面情绪，一家知名咨询公司最近设计了一份简短的培训讲稿，供管理者在团队会议上朗读。
longSentences.tip: 不定式短语作目的状语时通常置于句首，先看清主句主谓宾再理解修饰部分。

longSentences.sentence: The ultimate goal is not to remove pressure altogether but to maximize the benefits of moderate stress while keeping its harmful side effects under control.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“The goal is not... but to maximize...”
longSentences.modifiers: not...but...并列结构表对比；while引导伴随状语从句
longSentences.translation: 最终目标并不是要彻底消除压力，而是在把压力的负面影响控制住的同时，最大限度地发挥适度压力的益处。
longSentences.tip: not...but...结构强调后半部分才是重点，翻译时应突出转折后的内容。

items.title: Guarding Against Online Scams
items.passage:
As more daily business is carried out online, companies are placing new emphasis on protecting sensitive information. Employees are now required to change their password on a regular basis, and prior approval from the IT department is needed before installing any new software. Such rules may seem inconvenient at first, but they form the foundation of a strong security system within the establishment.

One of the most common threats employees face is a phishing email that appears trustworthy but is designed to steal personal information. Experts say certain cues can help people spot a fake message: an odd sender address, urgent language, or a request to click an unfamiliar link. Paying attention to these details can significantly lower the risk of online fraud. Consistency also matters; checking every email with the same caution, rather than only when something looks suspicious, is the safest habit to develop.

Companies that build a culture of awareness tend to suffer far fewer security incidents than those that treat training as a one-time event. Regular reminders, short quizzes, and open discussion about recent scams all help employees stay alert. In the end, protecting a company's data depends less on complicated technology and more on the steady, careful habits of every person who uses a computer at work.
items.translation:
随着越来越多的日常业务在网上开展，企业开始更加重视保护敏感信息。员工现在被要求定期更换密码，在安装任何新软件之前，也需要事先获得信息技术部门的批准。这些规定起初可能显得有些不便，但它们正是整个机构建立强大安全体系的基础。

员工面临的最常见威胁之一，就是那些看似可信、实则意在窃取个人信息的钓鱼邮件。专家表示，一些线索可以帮助人们识破虚假邮件：例如发件人地址异常、语气过于紧急，或要求点击陌生链接。留意这些细节能够显著降低遭遇网络诈骗的风险。保持一致同样重要——用同样的谨慎态度对待每一封邮件，而不是只在觉得可疑时才多留个心眼，才是最安全的习惯。

培养安全意识文化的企业，其安全事故的发生率要远低于那些把培训视为一次性任务的企业。定期提醒、简短测验以及围绕近期诈骗案例展开的公开讨论，都有助于让员工保持警惕。归根结底，保护企业数据靠的不是多么复杂的技术，而更多依赖每一位使用电脑办公的员工日积月累、细致谨慎的习惯。

keyWords.zh: vt. 降低 a. 较低的

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Companies should stop using email entirely to avoid fraud.
Employees can help protect company data by staying alert and consistent.
IT departments are responsible for all security problems.
Passwords are no longer necessary in modern offices.
questions.explain: 全文围绕企业如何通过员工的警惕性与一致的谨慎习惯来防范网络诈骗、保护信息安全展开，第三段总结保护数据更多依赖员工的习惯，主旨为B；A、C、D均与原文不符。

questions.q: According to the passage, which of the following is NOT mentioned as a sign of a phishing email?
options: An odd sender address
Urgent language
A request to click an unfamiliar link
A message written in a foreign language
questions.explain: 第二段列举的钓鱼邮件线索包括发件人地址异常、语气紧急、要求点击陌生链接，并未提及“用外语写的邮件”，故答案为D，依据第二段。

questions.q: It can be inferred that companies which treat security training as a one-time event are likely to ______.
options: save more money than other companies
experience more security incidents
have more satisfied employees
need fewer IT staff members
questions.explain: 第三段指出培养安全意识文化的企业事故发生率远低于那些把培训视为一次性任务的企业，可推知后者事故更多，故选B，依据第三段。

questions.q: In the sentence "Experts say certain cues can help people spot a fake message," the word "cues" most likely means ______.
options: mistakes
hints or signals
passwords
software programs
questions.explain: 结合上下文，cues帮助人们识别虚假邮件，指的是可供辨别的“线索、提示”，与hints or signals同义，依据第二段。

longSentences.sentence: Employees are now required to change their password on a regular basis, and prior approval from the IT department is needed before installing any new software.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列句，主干为“Employees are required to change their password”和“prior approval...is needed”
longSentences.modifiers: and连接两个并列分句；before installing...为时间状语
longSentences.translation: 员工现在被要求定期更换密码，在安装任何新软件之前，也需要事先获得信息技术部门的批准。
longSentences.tip: 遇到and连接的并列句，先分别找出两个分句各自的主谓结构，再理解整体逻辑关系。

longSentences.sentence: Experts say certain cues can help people spot a fake message: an odd sender address, urgent language, or a request to click an unfamiliar link.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“Experts say (that) certain cues can help people spot a fake message”
longSentences.modifiers: say后省略that引导宾语从句；冒号后为并列的三个具体例子，对cues进行解释说明
longSentences.translation: 专家表示，一些线索可以帮助人们识破虚假邮件：例如发件人地址异常、语气过于紧急，或要求点击陌生链接。
longSentences.tip: 冒号后的内容通常是对前文的举例或说明，可将其看作对前面名词的补充解释。

longSentences.sentence: In the end, protecting a company's data depends less on complicated technology and more on the steady, careful habits of every person who uses a computer at work.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“protecting...depends less on A and more on B”
longSentences.modifiers: 动名词短语protecting a company's data作主语；who引导定语从句修饰person
longSentences.translation: 归根结底，保护企业数据靠的不是多么复杂的技术，而更多依赖每一位使用电脑办公的员工日积月累、细致谨慎的习惯。
longSentences.tip: less...and more...结构表示对比重点，翻译时应突出more后面的内容为强调重点。

items.title: A Library Brings Reading Home
items.passage:
Every summer holiday, a local public library invites well-known speakers to talk with young readers about the joy of books. This year the theme is simple: reading should belong to everyone, not just to a small group of academic scholars. Organizers hope more people, especially children who rarely visit a library, will discover that a good book can be as relaxing as a family picnic in the park.

To reach children who live far from the city center, the library started mailing a small parcel of picture books and comic-style cartoons to rural schools twice a month. Teachers say the plain, easy-to-follow stories help students build confidence before they move on to longer novels. Many children who once thought reading was boring have now committed to finishing at least one book every week.

The library also encourages a sense of ownership among young readers by letting them choose which titles to order next. According to the head librarian, giving children real choices, rather than simply handing them a required reading list, makes them far more willing to open a book on their own. As the program enters its third year, more and more families are treating reading not as a duty but as a favorite way to spend a holiday afternoon.
items.translation:
每年暑假，当地公共图书馆都会邀请知名演讲者与年轻读者畅谈阅读的乐趣。今年的主题很简单：阅读应当属于每一个人，而不仅仅属于一小部分学术学者。组织者希望有更多人，尤其是那些很少去图书馆的孩子，能够发现一本好书带来的放松，不亚于一家人在公园里的一次野餐。

为了让居住在远离市中心地区的孩子们也能读到书，图书馆开始每月两次向乡村学校寄送一小包图画书和漫画风格的读物。老师们表示，这些语言浅显、易于理解的故事能帮助学生在进入更长篇幅的小说之前建立起阅读的信心。许多曾经觉得阅读枯燥的孩子，如今已经承诺每周至少读完一本书。

图书馆还通过让小读者们自己选择接下来订购哪些书目，来培养他们的一种主人翁意识。据图书馆馆长介绍，让孩子们拥有真正的选择权，而不是仅仅发给他们一份规定的阅读书单，会让他们更愿意主动打开一本书阅读。随着该项目进入第三个年头，越来越多的家庭开始把阅读视为一种度过假日午后的心爱方式，而不是一项任务。

keyWords.zh: n. 所有权，主人翁意识

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A library's efforts to make reading more accessible and enjoyable for children.
The reasons why academic books are more valuable than cartoons.
A complaint from rural schools about a lack of reading materials.
A comparison between reading indoors and having a picnic outdoors.
questions.explain: 全文围绕图书馆通过邀请演讲者、寄送包裹读物、给予孩子选书权等方式，让阅读变得更普及、更有趣展开，主旨是A；B、C、D均与原文重点不符，依据全文。

questions.q: According to the second paragraph, how does the library help children who live far from the city center?
options: By inviting them to attend speeches given by famous authors.
By mailing them picture books and cartoons twice a month.
By giving them free tickets to a family picnic.
By requiring them to finish an academic reading list.
questions.explain: 第二段明确提到图书馆开始每月两次向乡村学校寄送一小包图画书和漫画风格的读物，依据在第二段。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the third paragraph that giving children a choice in what they read ______.
options: makes the library's job more difficult
increases their willingness to read on their own
has little effect on their reading habits
is less effective than a required reading list
questions.explain: 第三段指出让孩子拥有真正的选择权会让他们更愿意主动打开一本书阅读，可推知给予选择权能提高其阅读的主动性，故选B，依据第三段。

questions.q: In the sentence "Teachers say the plain, easy-to-follow stories help students build confidence," the word "plain" is closest in meaning to ______.
options: beautiful and colorful
simple and clear
long and complicated
expensive and rare
questions.explain: 结合语境，plain与easy-to-follow并列，修饰故事的语言风格，意为“简单明了的”，与simple and clear同义，依据第二段。

longSentences.sentence: Every summer holiday, a local public library invites well-known speakers to talk with young readers about the joy of books.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“a local public library invites speakers to talk with readers”
longSentences.modifiers: Every summer holiday为时间状语；to talk with...about...为不定式短语作目的状语
longSentences.translation: 每年暑假，当地公共图书馆都会邀请知名演讲者与年轻读者畅谈阅读的乐趣。
longSentences.tip: 先抓住句子主谓宾（图书馆邀请演讲者），再看不定式短语补充说明邀请的目的。

longSentences.sentence: Teachers say the plain, easy-to-follow stories help students build confidence before they move on to longer novels.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“Teachers say (that) the stories help students build confidence”
longSentences.modifiers: say后省略that引导宾语从句；before引导时间状语从句
longSentences.translation: 老师们表示，这些语言浅显、易于理解的故事能帮助学生在进入更长篇幅的小说之前建立起阅读的信心。
longSentences.tip: 遇到help sb. do sth.结构，直接翻译为“帮助某人做某事”即可，不要遗漏宾补部分。

longSentences.sentence: According to the head librarian, giving children real choices, rather than simply handing them a required reading list, makes them far more willing to open a book on their own.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“giving children real choices...makes them...willing to open a book”
longSentences.modifiers: giving children real choices为动名词短语作主语；rather than...为插入的对比成分
longSentences.translation: 据图书馆馆长介绍，让孩子们拥有真正的选择权，而不是仅仅发给他们一份规定的阅读书单，会让他们更愿意主动打开一本书阅读。
longSentences.tip: rather than插入语打断了主谓结构，阅读时可先跳过它，抓住giving...makes them willing to...的主干。

items.title: A New Lens for Field Research
items.passage:
Deep in a virgin rainforest that few humans have ever entered, a team of biologists is using a newly developed lens system to photograph insects that are almost invisible to the naked eye. The device, no larger than a coin, can be attached to an ordinary smartphone, allowing researchers to capture images with extraordinary detail. Every specimen they find receives a formal Latin name before it is recorded in the international database.

Traditional field research in such remote areas is neither quick nor economical; expeditions can take months and require expensive equipment that is not always readily available. The new lens changes this by letting scientists manipulate the focus digitally rather than adjusting fragile glass parts by hand. This intelligent design also allows the device to prolong its battery life by automatically switching off between shots, so a single trip can last far longer than before.

Back at the laboratory, researchers spend weeks pondering the images collected in the field, comparing subtle differences in color and shape that might indicate a new species. Some findings are dramatic, revealing insects never described before; others simply confirm what scientists already suspected. Either way, the team insists that this quiet, careful work is exactly what real scientific progress requires.
items.translation:
在几乎无人涉足的原始雨林深处，一支生物学家团队正使用一套新研发的镜头系统，为肉眼几乎无法看清的昆虫拍照。这个装置比一枚硬币还小，可以安装在普通智能手机上，让研究人员拍出细节极为丰富的照片。他们发现的每一个标本，在被记录进国际数据库之前，都会先获得一个正式的拉丁文名称。

在这样偏远的地区开展传统的野外研究，既不快捷也不经济：一次考察可能耗时数月，还需要用到并非总能轻易获得的昂贵设备。新型镜头改变了这一切，它让科学家可以通过数字方式操控对焦，而不必用手去调整脆弱的玻璃部件。这种智能设计还能让设备在两次拍摄之间自动关闭，从而延长电池续航时间，使一次考察能够比以往持续更久。

回到实验室后，研究人员要花上数周时间反复推敲野外采集到的图像，比对颜色和形状上的细微差异，判断是否可能是一个新物种。有些发现令人振奋，揭示出前所未见的昆虫；另一些则只是印证了科学家原本的猜测。无论如何，研究团队坚称，这种安静而细致的工作，正是真正的科学进步所需要的。

keyWords.zh: a. 原始的，未经开发的

questions.q: What is the main purpose of the passage?
options: To criticize traditional field research methods used by biologists.
To describe how a new lens technology is improving research in remote areas.
To argue that virgin rainforests should be closed to all scientists.
To explain how insects are officially named in Latin.
questions.explain: 全文介绍了一款新型镜头设备如何帮助生物学家在偏远雨林中开展研究，提高效率、延长续航、便于对焦，主旨为B；A、C、D均非文章重点或与原文不符，依据全文。

questions.q: According to the second paragraph, why is traditional field research in remote areas difficult?
options: Because local governments do not allow scientists to enter virgin forests.
Because expeditions take a long time and require equipment that is not always easy to get.
Because insects in these areas are too dangerous to photograph.
Because researchers cannot agree on Latin names for new species.
questions.explain: 第二段指出传统野外研究既不快捷也不经济，一次考察可能耗时数月，还需要并非总能轻易获得的昂贵设备，依据第二段。

questions.q: It can be inferred that the new lens system is considered an improvement mainly because it ______.
options: is more expensive than older equipment
makes it easier and more efficient to conduct research in the field
eliminates the need for any laboratory analysis
allows scientists to avoid naming new species
questions.explain: 第二、三段说明该镜头体积小、可数字对焦、延长续航，使野外研究更容易、更高效，可推知B正确；A、C、D均与原文不符或未提及。

questions.q: In the sentence "researchers spend weeks pondering the images collected in the field," the word "pondering" is closest in meaning to ______.
options: ignoring
printing
carefully thinking about
quickly deleting
questions.explain: 结合上下文，研究人员花数周仔细比对、判断图像，pondering意为仔细思考、琢磨，与carefully thinking about同义，依据第三段。

longSentences.sentence: Deep in a virgin rainforest that few humans have ever entered, a team of biologists is using a newly developed lens system to photograph insects that are almost invisible to the naked eye.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“a team of biologists is using a lens system to photograph insects”
longSentences.modifiers: Deep in a virgin rainforest为地点状语；that引导两个定语从句，分别修饰rainforest和insects
longSentences.translation: 在几乎无人涉足的原始雨林深处，一支生物学家团队正使用一套新研发的镜头系统，为肉眼几乎无法看清的昆虫拍照。
longSentences.tip: 句首较长的地点状语可以先跳过，找到主句“团队正在使用...拍照”后再回头理解修饰成分。

longSentences.sentence: Traditional field research in such remote areas is neither quick nor economical; expeditions can take months and require expensive equipment that is not always readily available.
longSentences.skeleton: 分号连接两个并列分句，前句主干“research is neither quick nor economical”，后句主干“expeditions can take months and require equipment”
longSentences.modifiers: neither...nor...并列否定结构；that引导定语从句修饰equipment
longSentences.translation: 在这样偏远的地区开展传统的野外研究，既不快捷也不经济：一次考察可能耗时数月，还需要用到并非总能轻易获得的昂贵设备。
longSentences.tip: 分号相当于逗号加连接词，前后两句是并列或解释关系，可分别拆解理解再合并。

longSentences.sentence: This intelligent design also allows the device to prolong its battery life by automatically switching off between shots, so a single trip can last far longer than before.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“This design allows the device to prolong its battery life”
longSentences.modifiers: by doing结构作方式状语；so引导结果状语从句
longSentences.translation: 这种智能设计还能让设备在两次拍摄之间自动关闭，从而延长电池续航时间，使一次考察能够比以往持续更久。
longSentences.tip: allow sb./sth. to do结构表示“使…得以做…”，抓住这一固定搭配有助于快速理解句意。

items.title: The Meaning Behind Clothes
items.passage:
Around the world, clothes do far more than cover the body. In many cultures, what people wear can indicate their age, their social status, or even their religion. A simple piece of cloth wrapped around the body might look ordinary to an outsider, but to those who wear it, every fold and colour carries meaning passed down from their ancestors.

Some dress codes may seem arbitrary to visitors, yet they usually have a long history behind them. In certain regions, guests are expected to cover their arms and legs before entering a temple; elsewhere, bright colours are worn only at weddings, never at funerals. Ignoring these conventional customs, even without meaning to cause offence, can make local people feel that a foreigner has failed to respect their traditions, and a visitor may not be as welcome as before.

Anthropologists have long recorded such customs, noting how clothing choices reflect a community's values and beliefs. Before travelling abroad, therefore, it is wise to learn a little about local dress. A traveller who takes the trouble to dress appropriately will often find that villagers greet them with warmer smiles and are more willing to share stories about their history and their ancestors' way of life.
items.translation:
在世界各地，衣服的作用远不止遮盖身体。在许多文化中，人们的穿着可以显示出他们的年龄、社会地位，甚至宗教信仰。一块裹在身上的简单布料，在外人看来也许平平无奇，但对穿着它的人来说，每一处褶皱和每一种颜色都承载着祖先流传下来的意义。

在游客看来，有些着装规定或许显得随意武断，但它们背后通常都有悠久的历史。在某些地区，客人进入寺庙前须遮住手臂和腿部；而在另一些地方，鲜艳的颜色只在婚礼上穿着，绝不会出现在葬礼上。忽视这些约定俗成的习俗，哪怕并非有意冒犯，也会让当地人觉得外来者未能尊重他们的传统，游客受欢迎的程度也可能因此打折扣。

人类学家长期以来一直在记录这类习俗，指出服饰的选择如何反映出一个群体的价值观与信仰。因此，出国旅行之前，稍微了解一下当地的着装习惯是明智之举。一个不惜费心得体着装的旅行者，往往会发现村民们会以更热情的笑容迎接自己，也更愿意分享有关本地历史和祖先生活方式的故事。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The history of anthropology as an academic subject
Why clothing customs around the world carry cultural meaning
How to design fashionable clothes for different climates
The economic cost of traditional costumes
questions.explain: 文章主旨在于说明世界各地的服饰习俗承载着文化意义，游客应了解并尊重当地的着装传统，B项最全面地概括了全文主旨，依据全文，尤其第一段和第三段。

questions.q: According to the passage, what are guests expected to do before entering a temple in certain regions?
options: Wear bright colours to show respect
Bring a gift for local ancestors
Cover their arms and legs
Consult an anthropologist first
questions.explain: 第二段明确提到「guests are expected to cover their arms and legs before entering a temple」，故选C，依据第二段。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the passage that a traveller who ignores local dress customs is likely to ______.
options: be arrested by local police
receive a less warm welcome from local people
be asked to leave the country immediately
have to pay a fine
questions.explain: 第二段指出忽视这些习俗会让当地人觉得外来者不尊重传统，游客「may not be as welcome as before」，由此可推断会受到较冷淡的对待，而非被逮捕或罚款，故选B，依据第二段。

questions.q: The word “conventional” in the second paragraph is closest in meaning to ______.
options: modern and fashionable
traditional and generally accepted
rare and unusual
expensive and luxurious
questions.explain: 根据上下文，conventional customs指约定俗成、被广泛认可的习俗，与「modern新潮」「rare罕见」「expensive昂贵」等含义无关，故选B，依据第二段语境。

longSentences.sentence: A simple piece of cloth wrapped around the body might look ordinary to an outsider, but to those who wear it, every fold and colour carries meaning passed down from their ancestors.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“A simple piece of cloth ... might look ordinary ..., but ... every fold and colour carries meaning ...”，即“一块布可能看起来平常，但每一处褶皱和颜色都承载着意义”
longSentences.modifiers: “wrapped around the body”是过去分词短语作后置定语，修饰cloth；“to an outsider”与“to those who wear it”是介词短语作状语，形成对比；“passed down from their ancestors”是过去分词短语修饰meaning。
longSentences.translation: 一块裹在身上的简单布料，在外人看来也许平平无奇，但对穿着它的人来说，每一处褶皱和每一种颜色都承载着祖先流传下来的意义。
longSentences.tip: 遇到but转折句，先分别抓住两个分句的主谓宾，再处理分词短语等修饰成分。

longSentences.sentence: Ignoring these conventional customs, even without meaning to cause offence, can make local people feel that a foreigner has failed to respect their traditions, and a visitor may not be as welcome as before.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“Ignoring these conventional customs ... can make local people feel that ..., and a visitor may not be as welcome as before.”，即“忽视这些习俗会让当地人有某种感受，游客也可能不再受欢迎”
longSentences.modifiers: “Ignoring these conventional customs”是动名词短语作主语；“even without meaning to cause offence”是插入的让步状语；“that a foreigner has failed to respect their traditions”是feel的宾语从句。
longSentences.translation: 忽视这些约定俗成的习俗，哪怕并非有意冒犯，也会让当地人觉得外来者未能尊重他们的传统，游客受欢迎的程度也可能因此打折扣。
longSentences.tip: 动名词做主语的长句，先找谓语can make，再拆分插入语和宾语从句。

items.title: Advances in Organ Transplant Technology
items.passage:
For decades, organ transplant surgery has given desperately ill patients a second chance at life, yet a shortage of donor organs remains one of the greatest obstacles the field faces. Surgeons and scientists have shown a keen interest in finding new ways to reduce this gap, and recent progress suggests that the wait for a suitable organ may not always be so long.

One promising factor behind this progress is the synthesis of artificial tissue in the laboratory. Researchers can now grow simple structures, such as blood vessels, from a patient's own cells, which helps to eliminate the risk that the body will reject the new material. At the same time, virtual reality tools allow surgeons to practise complicated transplant procedures on a digital model before ever touching a real patient, reinforcing their skills and reducing errors in the operating room.

Despite these advances, experts warn that laboratory-grown organs are still years away from replacing human donors on a large scale. What technology can do, for now, is support existing methods rather than eliminate the need for them entirely. Even so, each small step brings hope to thousands of patients who are waiting, sometimes for years, for the transplant that could save their lives.
items.translation:
几十年来，器官移植手术让病情危重的患者获得了重生的机会，然而供体器官短缺依然是这一领域面临的最大障碍之一。外科医生和科学家们对寻找缩小这一缺口的新方法表现出浓厚的兴趣，而最近的进展表明，等待合适器官的时间或许不会总是那么漫长。

推动这一进展的一个有希望的因素是实验室中人造组织的合成。研究人员如今可以利用患者自身的细胞培育出诸如血管之类的简单结构，这有助于消除人体排斥新材料的风险。与此同时，虚拟现实技术让外科医生能够在真正接触病人之前，先在数字模型上练习复杂的移植手术，从而磨练技能、减少手术室中的失误。

尽管取得了这些进展，专家警告说，实验室培育的器官距离大规模取代人体捐献者还需要好些年。就目前而言，科技所能做的是支持现有方法，而不是彻底消除对它们的需求。即便如此，每一小步进展都给成千上万等待移植手术、有时甚至等待数年的患者带来了希望——这项手术可能挽救他们的生命。

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Laboratory-grown organs have already replaced human donors
New technologies are helping to address the shortage of donor organs
Virtual reality is mainly used to train medical students in anatomy
Organ transplant surgery is becoming less necessary
questions.explain: 全文围绕器官移植领域如何借助新科技（组织合成、虚拟现实等）缓解供体短缺问题展开论述，B项概括了全文主旨，依据全文，尤其首段和第三段。

questions.q: According to the second paragraph, how do researchers reduce the risk of organ rejection?
options: By using organs donated by family members only
By growing tissue from the patient's own cells
By treating patients with new medication before surgery
By practising surgery through virtual reality alone
questions.explain: 第二段提到「grow simple structures... from a patient's own cells, which helps to eliminate the risk that the body will reject the new material」，故选B，依据第二段。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the last paragraph that current technology ______.
options: has completely solved the organ shortage problem
is not yet able to fully replace human organ donors
is too expensive to be used in most hospitals
will soon be banned due to safety concerns
questions.explain: 末段指出实验室培育器官距离大规模取代人体捐献者“还需要好些年”，说明当前技术尚不能完全取代人体器官捐献，故选B，依据第三段。

questions.q: The word “eliminate” in the passage is closest in meaning to ______.
options: increase
remove
explain
delay
questions.explain: eliminate意为“消除，排除”，与remove同义；increase（增加）、explain（解释）、delay（推迟）均不符合语境，故选B，依据第二段「helps to eliminate the risk」。

longSentences.sentence: For decades, organ transplant surgery has given desperately ill patients a second chance at life, yet a shortage of donor organs remains one of the greatest obstacles the field faces.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“organ transplant surgery has given ... patients a second chance..., yet a shortage of donor organs remains one of the greatest obstacles...”，即“器官移植手术给患者带来了机会，然而器官短缺仍是最大障碍之一”
longSentences.modifiers: “desperately ill”作定语修饰patients；“the field faces”是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰obstacles。
longSentences.translation: 几十年来，器官移植手术让病情危重的患者获得了重生的机会，然而供体器官短缺依然是这一领域面临的最大障碍之一。
longSentences.tip: yet连接的转折句，先分别抓住两个分句的主谓宾，再看定语从句修饰的是哪个名词。

longSentences.sentence: At the same time, virtual reality tools allow surgeons to practise complicated transplant procedures on a digital model before ever touching a real patient, reinforcing their skills and reducing errors in the operating room.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“virtual reality tools allow surgeons to practise ... procedures on a digital model”，即“虚拟现实技术让外科医生在数字模型上练习手术”
longSentences.modifiers: “before ever touching a real patient”是时间状语；“reinforcing their skills and reducing errors...”是现在分词短语作伴随状语，说明结果。
longSentences.translation: 与此同时，虚拟现实技术让外科医生能够在真正接触病人之前，先在数字模型上练习复杂的移植手术，从而磨练技能、减少手术室中的失误。
longSentences.tip: allow sb. to do结构先抓住谓语allow，再关注分词短语作伴随状语表结果。

items.title: A City After the Quake
items.passage:
Early one morning, a powerful quake hit a city in the east of the country, shaking buildings and knocking out electricity across several neighbourhoods. The initial reports suggested that damage was limited, but as rescue workers arrived, it became clear that whole streets had been destroyed and that many families urgently needed help.

In the days that followed, local officials made the difficult decision to postpone the opening of schools and to close roads for repair. Volunteers arrived from similar disasters in other regions, bringing food, water and medical supplies. Survivors later said that the mercy shown by strangers, who gave up their own time and money to help, meant as much to them as the physical aid itself.

Engineers studying the ruins noted an important distinction between older buildings, many of which collapsed, and newer ones built to modern safety standards, which mostly remained standing. This finding has encouraged the government to invest more heavily in stronger construction, so that the next quake, whenever it strikes, will hopefully cause far less loss of life.
items.translation:
一天清晨，一场强烈地震袭击了该国东部的一座城市，导致建筑物摇晃，多个社区停电。最初的报道称损失有限，但随着救援人员的到来，情况逐渐清楚：整条整条街道被摧毁，许多家庭亟需帮助。

在接下来的日子里，当地官员做出艰难决定，推迟学校开学并封闭道路以便维修。曾经历过类似灾难的其他地区的志愿者纷纷赶来，带来了食物、水和医疗用品。幸存者后来说，那些牺牲自己时间和金钱前来帮助的陌生人所展现的仁慈，对他们而言和物质援助本身同样重要。

研究废墟的工程师们注意到，许多倒塌的老旧建筑，与按照现代安全标准建造、大多屹立未倒的新建筑之间，存在着重要区别。这一发现促使政府加大投入建造更坚固的建筑，以便下一次地震，无论何时发生，都能将人员伤亡降到最低。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The economic cost of natural disasters
How a city responded to and learned from an earthquake
The daily life of rescue workers
The geography of the eastern region
questions.explain: 文章主要讲述了一座城市在地震中的经历、救援过程以及震后从新旧建筑差异中获得的经验教训，B项最全面地概括了全文，依据全文。

questions.q: What did local officials decide to do after the earthquake?
options: Open more schools immediately
Postpone school opening and close roads for repair
Ask volunteers to leave the affected area
Cancel all construction projects
questions.explain: 第二段明确提到「local officials made the difficult decision to postpone the opening of schools and to close roads for repair」，故选B，依据第二段。

questions.q: What can be inferred about newer buildings from the last paragraph?
options: They were more likely to collapse than older buildings
They were built without following safety standards
They generally survived the earthquake better than older ones
They were more expensive but equally unsafe
questions.explain: 末段提到新建筑“大多屹立未倒”，而许多老建筑倒塌，由此可推知新建筑在抗震方面表现更好，故选C，依据第三段。

questions.q: The word “distinction” in the last paragraph is closest in meaning to ______.
options: similarity
difference
connection
agreement
questions.explain: distinction意为“区别，差异”，与difference同义；similarity（相似）、connection（联系）、agreement（一致）均不符合上下文强调新旧建筑对比的语境，故选B，依据第三段。

longSentences.sentence: Early one morning, a powerful quake hit a city in the east of the country, shaking buildings and knocking out electricity across several neighbourhoods.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“a powerful quake hit a city in the east of the country”，即“一场强烈地震袭击了该国东部的一座城市”
longSentences.modifiers: “Early one morning”是时间状语置于句首；“shaking buildings and knocking out electricity...”是现在分词短语作伴随状语，说明地震造成的结果。
longSentences.translation: 一天清晨，一场强烈地震袭击了该国东部的一座城市，导致建筑物摇晃，多个社区停电。
longSentences.tip: 主句提炼出主谓宾后，分词短语通常表伴随结果，可单独译成汉语的后续动作。

longSentences.sentence: Survivors later said that the mercy shown by strangers, who gave up their own time and money to help, meant as much to them as the physical aid itself.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“Survivors said that the mercy ... meant as much to them as the physical aid itself.”，即“幸存者说，仁慈和物质援助同样重要”
longSentences.modifiers: “shown by strangers”是过去分词短语作后置定语修饰mercy；“who gave up their own time and money to help”是非限定性定语从句修饰strangers，补充说明。
longSentences.translation: 幸存者后来说，那些牺牲自己时间和金钱前来帮助的陌生人所展现的仁慈，对他们而言和物质援助本身同样重要。
longSentences.tip: 遇到mean as much...as的比较结构，先抓住主句谓语meant，再处理中间的定语成分。

items.title: The Trouble With Drug Shortages
items.passage:
In recent years, patients across many countries have found that certain medicines have become scarce, with pharmacy shelves sometimes standing empty for weeks at a time. For people who depend on daily medication to heal from chronic illness, this shortage is more than an inconvenience; it can be genuinely frightening.

Economists point to several reasons behind the problem. At its core, drug manufacturing depends on a small number of factories, so if one plant slows production, the effect can spread quickly through the whole supply chain. Workers in these factories, often paid an hourly wage, may also go on strike when conditions feel shaky, further delaying the delivery of essential drugs.

For ordinary customers, the experience of standing in a pharmacy only to be told a medicine is unavailable is deeply frustrating. Many perceive the shortage as a sign that the healthcare system is not working as it should. Public health experts now argue that governments must invest in more reliable supply chains, so that patients no longer need to worry whether the medicine they rely on will be there when they need it.
items.translation:
近年来，许多国家的患者发现，某些药物变得稀缺，药房的货架有时会连续数周空空如也。对于那些依靠每日服药来治疗慢性疾病的人来说，这种短缺不仅仅是不便，甚至会让人感到真正的恐惧。

经济学家指出了这一问题背后的几个原因。从根本上说，药品生产依赖少数几家工厂，因此一旦某家工厂放缓生产，其影响便会迅速波及整个供应链。这些工厂里按小时计薪的工人，在感到工作条件不稳定时也可能罢工，从而进一步延误基本药品的供应。

对普通顾客来说，站在药房里却被告知某种药无货，这种经历令人十分沮丧。许多人认为，这种短缺表明医疗体系并未如其应有的方式运转。公共卫生专家目前主张，政府必须投资建设更可靠的供应链，让患者不再需要担心自己所依赖的药物在需要时是否能够买到。

questions.q: What is the main topic of the passage?
options: How pharmacies are run in different countries
The causes and effects of drug shortages
How chronic illnesses are treated
The daily wages of factory workers
questions.explain: 全文围绕药品短缺现象的成因及其对患者和医疗体系的影响展开论述，B项概括了全文主旨，依据全文。

questions.q: According to the second paragraph, why can a shortage spread quickly through the supply chain?
options: Because too many factories produce the same drug
Because drug manufacturing relies on only a small number of factories
Because hospitals order too much medicine at once
Because pharmacies refuse to restock their shelves
questions.explain: 第二段指出「drug manufacturing depends on a small number of factories, so if one plant slows production, the effect can spread quickly」，故选B，依据第二段。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the passage that patients with chronic illness are especially affected by drug shortages because ______.
options: they usually cannot afford medicine at all
they need daily medication to manage their condition
they refuse to use pharmacies
their illnesses cannot be treated by modern medicine
questions.explain: 第一段提到依靠“daily medication”治疗慢性病的人受此影响尤为严重，说明他们需要每日用药来控制病情，故选B，依据第一段。

questions.q: The word “perceive” in the last paragraph is closest in meaning to ______.
options: ignore
regard
deny
forget
questions.explain: perceive在此意为“认为，将……视为”，与regard（把……视为）意思相近；ignore（忽视）、deny（否认）、forget（忘记）均不符合语境，故选B，依据第三段「perceive the shortage as a sign...」。

longSentences.sentence: In recent years, patients across many countries have found that certain medicines have become scarce, with pharmacy shelves sometimes standing empty for weeks at a time.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“patients ... have found that certain medicines have become scarce”，即“患者们发现某些药物变得稀缺”
longSentences.modifiers: “across many countries”介词短语修饰patients；“with pharmacy shelves sometimes standing empty for weeks at a time”是with复合结构作伴随状语，补充说明具体情况。
longSentences.translation: 近年来，许多国家的患者发现，某些药物变得稀缺，药房的货架有时会连续数周空空如也。
longSentences.tip: with+名词+分词的独立结构常表伴随状态，可单独译成一个分句。

longSentences.sentence: Workers in these factories, often paid an hourly wage, may also go on strike when conditions feel shaky, further delaying the delivery of essential drugs.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“Workers ... may also go on strike ...”，即“工人们也可能罢工”
longSentences.modifiers: “in these factories”介词短语修饰Workers；“often paid an hourly wage”是插入的过去分词短语，补充说明工人身份；“when conditions feel shaky”是时间状语从句；“further delaying...”是现在分词短语作结果状语。
longSentences.translation: 这些工厂里按小时计薪的工人，在感到工作条件不稳定时也可能罢工，从而进一步延误基本药品的供应。
longSentences.tip: 句中插入语容易打断主谓，先划掉逗号间的插入成分找到主干，再补充回去理解。

items.title: Volunteering at a Food Bank
items.passage:
Each December, community food banks across the country report a familiar shortage: not enough hands to sort, pack, and dispense donations before the holidays arrive. To fill the gap, local newspapers publish notices urging residents to opt for even a single afternoon shift, and many people leap at the chance once they realize how little time is actually required.

On a typical Saturday, dozens gather outside a downtown place of worship, where a hand-painted banner announces the food drive to everyone passing by. Inside, teams wrap canned goods into family-sized parcels while others loosen the rules that once limited assistance to a fixed number of visits per month, allowing anyone in need to receive help. Off-duty sailors from a nearby naval station often join the effort, moving boxes with practiced efficiency.

Because the food bank operates with limited funding, it depends on a loan of refrigerated trucks from a local grocery chain to keep produce fresh. Over the past year, organizers have managed to amass enough supplies to serve twice as many families as before, despite early speculation that donations would fall. Their success suggests that, when a community is asked directly, generosity is rarely in short supply.
items.translation:
每年12月，全国各地的食品银行都会面临一个熟悉的问题：没有足够的人手来分拣、打包和分发捐赠物资，而假期已迫在眉睫。为填补这一缺口，当地报纸纷纷刊登通知，呼吁居民哪怕只抽出一个下午也来做志愿者；许多人一旦意识到所需时间其实很少，便欣然抓住了这个机会。

在典型的周六，数十人聚集在市中心一处礼拜场所外，一条手绘的横幅向路过的每个人宣告着这场食物募捐活动。在里面，志愿者团队将罐头食品打包成适合一家人食用的小包，另一些人则放宽了曾经将援助限制在每月固定次数以内的规定，让任何有需要的人都能获得帮助。附近海军基地的休班水兵也常常加入进来，熟练地搬运着箱子。

由于资金有限，食品银行依靠从当地一家连锁超市借来的冷藏卡车来保持农产品的新鲜。过去一年里，尽管早先有人猜测捐赠会减少，组织者们还是设法积攒了足够的物资，为比以往多一倍的家庭提供了服务。他们的成功表明，只要直接向社区求助，善意往往并不稀缺。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: How community food banks recruit volunteers and manage resources during the holiday season
How naval sailors changed their base's community service policy
Why supermarkets are lending trucks to charities to reduce costs
The methods food banks use to prevent donation fraud
questions.explain: 全文围绕社区食品银行在节日期间招募志愿者、组织募捐并合理分配资源展开，主旨是A；其余选项只是文中提到的局部细节，依据第一、二段。

questions.q: According to the passage, who helps move boxes at the food bank?
options: Local newspaper reporters
Off-duty sailors from a nearby naval station
Refrigerated truck drivers
City government officials
questions.explain: 第二段提到附近海军基地的休班水兵常来帮忙搬运箱子，故答案为B，其余选项文中未提及。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the passage that the food bank's leaders
options: were confident donations would increase before the drive began
had expected the volunteer shortage to persist all year
were surprised that the community responded generously despite early doubts
planned to reduce the number of families served this year
questions.explain: 第三段指出尽管早先有人猜测捐赠会减少，组织者最终还是积攒了充足物资，说明结果好于预期的担忧，故选C；依据第三段。

questions.q: In paragraph 2, the word "loosen" is closest in meaning to
options: tighten
relax
ignore
invent
questions.explain: loosen在文中指“放宽（规定）”，与relax意思相近，故选B；tighten、ignore、invent均不符合语境，依据第二段。

longSentences.sentence: Each December, community food banks across the country report a familiar shortage: not enough hands to sort, pack, and dispense donations before the holidays arrive.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：食品银行报告一种短缺（food banks report a shortage）
longSentences.modifiers: Each December作时间状语；across the country作后置定语修饰food banks；冒号后的内容是shortage的同位语说明，其中to sort, pack, and dispense donations是不定式短语作hands的后置定语，before the holidays arrive是时间状语从句
longSentences.translation: 每年12月，全国各地的食品银行都会面临一个熟悉的问题：没有足够的人手来分拣、打包和分发捐赠物资，而假期已迫在眉睫。
longSentences.tip: 遇到冒号先找主干，再把冒号后的内容当作对主干名词的补充说明来理解。

longSentences.sentence: Inside, teams wrap canned goods into family-sized parcels while others loosen the rules that once limited assistance to a fixed number of visits per month, allowing anyone in need to receive help.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：两个并列分句——teams wrap canned goods... / others loosen the rules...（由while连接）
longSentences.modifiers: while引导的分句中，that once limited assistance...是定语从句修饰rules；allowing anyone in need to receive help是现在分词短语作结果状语
longSentences.translation: 在里面，志愿者团队将罐头食品打包成适合一家人食用的小包，另一些人则放宽了曾经将援助限制在每月固定次数以内的规定，让任何有需要的人都能获得帮助。
longSentences.tip: 先划出while前后两个并列主谓结构，再处理定语从句和分词状语这两层修饰。

longSentences.sentence: Because the food bank operates with limited funding, it depends on a loan of refrigerated trucks from a local grocery chain to keep produce fresh.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：it depends on a loan of refrigerated trucks（它依靠借来的冷藏卡车）
longSentences.modifiers: Because引导原因状语从句；to keep produce fresh是不定式短语作目的状语
longSentences.translation: 由于资金有限，食品银行依靠从当地一家连锁超市借来的冷藏卡车来保持农产品的新鲜。
longSentences.tip: 先剥离Because状语从句，锁定主句主谓宾，再看句末的不定式目的状语。

items.title: From Poetry to a New Career
items.passage:
Five years ago, Li Ming was a recent graduate who loved sitting under the fragrant trees near his university, writing short poems about daily life. He hoped his talent would shine on the page, but he knew that a poet's income was rarely enough to live on, and he did not want to miss the chance to help his family.

Following the advice in a career guide his teacher gave him, Li Ming decided to join the police force instead. The application process was not easy: he had to pass written tests, go through several rounds of interviews, and complete tough physical training. Whenever he felt tired, he reminded himself that success rarely comes to those who give up early.

Today, Li Ming works in a busy city district. His salary is stable, and his new circle of colleagues has become like a second family. He still writes a poem from time to time, but now it is only a hobby. Looking back, he believes that changing his plan was the wisest decision he ever made.
items.translation:
五年前，李明是一名刚毕业的大学生，喜欢在大学附近的芬芳树下坐着，写一些关于日常生活的小诗。他希望自己的才华能够闪光，但他明白诗人的收入很难维持生计，而他不想错过帮助家人的机会。

按照老师给他的一本职业指南中的建议，李明决定改去报考警察。申请过程并不轻松：他要通过笔试，经历好几轮面试，还要完成艰苦的体能训练。每当感到疲惫时，他都提醒自己，成功很少会眷顾轻易放弃的人。

如今，李明在一个繁忙的城区工作。他的薪水稳定，新结识的同事圈子也如同第二个家庭。他偶尔仍会写一首诗，但现在这只是一种爱好。回想起来，他相信改变计划是自己做过的最明智的决定。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A student who became a professional poet
A young man who chose a career in the police force over writing poetry
A teacher who wrote a career guide book
The training methods used by the police force
questions.explain: 全文讲述李明放弃成为诗人的梦想、选择报考警察并最终获得稳定生活的故事，主旨为B，依据全文。

questions.q: What convinced Li Ming to give up his plan to become a full-time poet?
options: His teacher forced him to change his mind
He failed the university entrance exam
He worried that a poet's income could not support his family
He was rejected by several magazines
questions.explain: 第一段提到他明白诗人的收入很难维持生计，不想错过帮助家人的机会，故选C，依据第一段。

questions.q: It can be inferred that Li Ming
options: regrets giving up his dream of becoming a poet
no longer enjoys writing poems at all
still feels connected to poetry even after starting his new career
plans to quit the police force soon
questions.explain: 第三段提到他偶尔仍会写诗，只是现在当作爱好，说明他并未完全放弃对诗歌的感情，故选C，A、B、D均与原文不符。

questions.q: In paragraph 2, the phrase "go through" is closest in meaning to
options: avoid
experience
design
cancel
questions.explain: go through在文中意为“经历”，与experience意思相近，故选B，依据第二段。

longSentences.sentence: Five years ago, Li Ming was a recent graduate who loved sitting under the fragrant trees near his university, writing short poems about daily life.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：李明是一名毕业生（Li Ming was a graduate）
longSentences.modifiers: Five years ago作时间状语；who loved sitting...是定语从句修饰graduate；writing short poems about daily life是现在分词短语作伴随状语
longSentences.translation: 五年前，李明是一名刚毕业的大学生，喜欢在大学附近的芬芳树下坐着，写一些关于日常生活的小诗。
longSentences.tip: 先找主句的主谓表，再依次处理who引导的定语从句和现在分词伴随状语。

longSentences.sentence: He hoped his talent would shine on the page, but he knew that a poet's income was rarely enough to live on, and he did not want to miss the chance to help his family.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：三个并列分句——He hoped..., but he knew..., and he did not want...
longSentences.modifiers: that a poet's income was rarely enough to live on是knew的宾语从句，其中to live on是不定式修饰enough；to help his family是不定式作目的状语修饰miss the chance
longSentences.translation: 他希望自己的才华能够闪光，但他明白诗人的收入很难维持生计，而他不想错过帮助家人的机会。
longSentences.tip: 遇到but、and连接的长句，先按并列连词切分出三个分句再逐一分析。

longSentences.sentence: Whenever he felt tired, he reminded himself that success rarely comes to those who give up early.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：he reminded himself（他提醒自己）
longSentences.modifiers: Whenever he felt tired是时间状语从句；that success rarely comes to those...是宾语从句；who give up early是定语从句修饰those
longSentences.translation: 每当感到疲惫时，他都提醒自己，成功很少会眷顾轻易放弃的人。
longSentences.tip: 先剥离Whenever状语从句，再看主句宾语从句中还嵌套着一层定语从句。

items.title: Protecting an Ancient Heritage Site
items.passage:
In recent years, many governments have adopted increasingly stern rules to protect ancient monuments from careless tourists and unauthorized construction. A parliamentary cabinet committee recently reviewed a bill that would give heritage sites stronger legal protection, arguing that historic buildings are an invaluable part of national identity that no generation has the right to destroy.

One vigorous public campaign centers on a stone altar dating back more than a thousand years, its surface carved with fragments of ancient poetry that scholars still study today. Local authorities recently sent workers to gently scrub centuries of grime from the stone, while officials launched an investigation into a nearby construction project that residents describe, only half joking, as an architectural monster rising beside the sacred site.

Officials insist that all future development must adhere to strict guidelines that highlight, rather than hide, a city's history. The protection plan will unfold in three phases over the next decade, though officials admit that occasional exceptions may be granted for public infrastructure. Critics counter that such promises resemble little more than propaganda unless they are backed by real enforcement and steady funding.
items.translation:
近年来，许多国家政府都出台了越来越严格的规定，以保护古代遗迹免受粗心游客和未经批准的建设活动的破坏。议会内阁委员会最近审议了一项法案，拟为文化遗产地提供更强有力的法律保护，理由是历史建筑是民族身份中无价的一部分，任何一代人都无权将其毁掉。

一场声势浩大的公众运动的焦点，是一座有一千多年历史的石制祭坛，其表面雕刻着古代诗歌的残篇，至今仍有学者在研究。当地政府最近派工人小心地擦洗掉石头上多年积存的污垢，同时官员们对附近一个建设项目展开调查——居民们半开玩笑地称，那是一座矗立在圣地旁边的“建筑怪物”。

官员们坚持认为，今后一切开发都必须遵守严格的准则，这些准则应突出而非掩盖一座城市的历史。保护计划将在未来十年分三个阶段推进，不过官员们承认，公共基础设施建设可能会偶尔获得例外许可。批评者则反驳说，若没有切实的执法和持续的资金支持，这些承诺不过是宣传口号罢了。

keyWords.zh: a. 无价的，非常贵重的
a. 有力的，精力充沛的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A government campaign to remove ancient monuments that block new construction
Efforts to protect a historic site through stricter policy and public debate
A dispute between scholars over the meaning of ancient poetry
A construction company's plan to modernize a historic district
questions.explain: 全文讲述政府通过更严格的政策及公众运动来保护一处历史遗址，主旨为B，依据全文。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, what have local authorities recently done to the stone altar?
options: Moved it to a museum
Carved new poetry onto its surface
Cleaned away years of accumulated grime
Sealed it off from all visitors
questions.explain: 第二段提到当地政府派工人擦洗掉石头上多年积存的污垢，故选C，依据第二段。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the nearby construction project mentioned in paragraph 2?
options: It has been praised by residents for its modern design
It is viewed by some residents as unsuitable given its location
It was built before the stone altar was discovered
It was funded entirely by the cabinet committee
questions.explain: 第二段提到居民半开玩笑地把附近新建的建筑称为“建筑怪物”，说明部分居民认为该建筑与遗址所在环境不协调，故选B，依据第二段。

questions.q: In paragraph 3, the word "adhere" is closest in meaning to
options: comply
object
contribute
respond
questions.explain: adhere to在文中意为“遵守”，与comply意思相近，故选A，依据第三段。

longSentences.sentence: A parliamentary cabinet committee recently reviewed a bill that would give heritage sites stronger legal protection, arguing that historic buildings are an invaluable part of national identity that no generation has the right to destroy.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：内阁委员会审议了一项法案（A cabinet committee reviewed a bill）
longSentences.modifiers: that would give heritage sites stronger legal protection是定语从句修饰bill；arguing that...是现在分词短语作伴随状语，其中that historic buildings are...是arguing的宾语从句；that no generation has the right to destroy是定语从句修饰national identity
longSentences.translation: 议会内阁委员会最近审议了一项法案，拟为文化遗产地提供更强有力的法律保护，理由是历史建筑是民族身份中无价的一部分，任何一代人都无权将其毁掉。
longSentences.tip: 长句中出现多个that时，先判断每个that引导的是定语从句还是宾语从句，再逐层拆解。

longSentences.sentence: Local authorities recently sent workers to gently scrub centuries of grime from the stone, while officials launched an investigation into a nearby construction project that residents describe, only half joking, as an architectural monster rising beside the sacred site.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：两个并列分句——authorities sent workers... / officials launched an investigation...（由while连接）
longSentences.modifiers: to gently scrub centuries of grime from the stone是不定式作目的状语；that residents describe...as an architectural monster是定语从句修饰project，其中only half joking是插入语；rising beside the sacred site是现在分词短语作后置定语修饰monster
longSentences.translation: 当地政府最近派工人小心地擦洗掉石头上多年积存的污垢，同时官员们对附近一个建设项目展开调查——居民们半开玩笑地称，那是一座矗立在圣地旁边的“建筑怪物”。
longSentences.tip: 先抓住while连接的两个并列主谓结构，插入语可以先跳过，最后再补充理解。

longSentences.sentence: The protection plan will unfold in three phases over the next decade, though officials admit that occasional exceptions may be granted for public infrastructure.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：The protection plan will unfold（保护计划将展开）
longSentences.modifiers: though officials admit that...是让步状语从句；that occasional exceptions may be granted for public infrastructure是admit的宾语从句
longSentences.translation: 保护计划将在未来十年分三个阶段推进，不过官员们承认，公共基础设施建设可能会偶尔获得例外许可。
longSentences.tip: though引导的让步状语从句里还嵌套一个宾语从句，先看清让步关系再逐层展开。

items.title: An Undergraduate's Startup Journey
items.passage:
As an undergraduate, Zhang Wei spent more time writing code than attending lectures. Friends jokingly called him a hacker because he could fix almost any computer problem within minutes. In his second year, he sketched a rough blueprint for a mobile app that would help students find secondhand textbooks, never imagining it would one day become his full-time job.

Turning the idea into a real business was not easy. Zhang Wei had to give persuasive presentations to convince investors that his small team could overtake far more experienced competitors. He worked with relentless energy, often coding through the night, even as headlines warned that the technology market was heading toward another bubble.

When a period of financial turbulence hit the industry two years later, many similar startups quietly closed. Zhang Wei's company survived because it had built a loyal user base from the very first month. Even small setbacks, such as a server crash at midnight, felt like a nuisance rather than a real threat. Looking back, he believes that stubborn persistence, more than luck, carried his small idea through its most difficult years.
items.translation:
作为一名大学生，张伟花在写代码上的时间比花在听课上的时间还要多。朋友们开玩笑地叫他“黑客”，因为他几乎能在几分钟内解决任何电脑问题。大二那年，他为一款帮助学生寻找二手教材的手机应用画出了一份粗略的蓝图，当时完全没有想到，这有一天会成为他的全职工作。

把这个想法变成真正的企业并不容易。张伟必须做出有说服力的展示，才能让投资人相信自己这支小团队能够超越经验丰富得多的竞争对手。他以不知疲倦的干劲投入工作，常常通宵编写代码，即便当时不断有新闻警告说科技行业正走向又一场泡沫。

两年后，一场金融动荡冲击了整个行业，许多类似的初创企业悄然倒闭。张伟的公司之所以能够存活下来，是因为它从最初的第一个月起就积累了一批忠实用户。即使是像午夜服务器崩溃这样的小挫折，在他看来也不过是个小麻烦，而非真正的威胁。回想起来，他相信，比起运气，顽强的坚持才是让他这个小小的想法挺过最艰难岁月的关键。

keyWords.zh: n. 大学生，大学肄业生
n. 麻烦事，讨厌的人/事

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A college student who became a professional textbook seller
A young entrepreneur's journey from a student idea to a surviving business
A study comparing successful and failed technology companies
The dangers of working through the night as a programmer
questions.explain: 全文讲述张伟从大学生程序员到创业、最终带领公司挺过市场动荡的经历，主旨为B，依据全文。

questions.q: According to the passage, what was Zhang Wei's original app designed to do?
options: Help students find secondhand textbooks
Connect investors with new startups
Track server crashes automatically
Teach students basic computer programming
questions.explain: 第一段提到他的应用旨在帮助学生寻找二手教材，故选A，依据第一段。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the startups mentioned in paragraph 3 that "quietly closed"?
options: They failed mainly because of a lack of persuasive presentations
They were unable to survive the period of market turbulence
They were shut down by government regulations
They merged with Zhang Wei's company
questions.explain: 第三段提到金融动荡冲击行业后许多同类初创企业悄然倒闭，说明这些企业未能挺过市场动荡，故选B，依据第三段。

questions.q: In paragraph 2, the word "relentless" is closest in meaning to
options: occasional
persistent
careless
uncertain
questions.explain: relentless在文中意为“不懈的、持续不断的”，与persistent意思相近，故选B，依据第二段。

longSentences.sentence: In his second year, he sketched a rough blueprint for a mobile app that would help students find secondhand textbooks, never imagining it would one day become his full-time job.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：he sketched a blueprint（他画出了一份蓝图）
longSentences.modifiers: In his second year作时间状语；that would help students find secondhand textbooks是定语从句修饰app；never imagining...是现在分词短语作伴随状语，表转折
longSentences.translation: 大二那年，他为一款帮助学生寻找二手教材的手机应用画出了一份粗略的蓝图，当时完全没有想到，这有一天会成为他的全职工作。
longSentences.tip: 先找出主句主谓宾，定语从句和分词状语可以分别单独理解再拼接。

longSentences.sentence: Zhang Wei had to give persuasive presentations to convince investors that his small team could overtake far more experienced competitors.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Zhang Wei had to give presentations（张伟必须做展示）
longSentences.modifiers: persuasive是形容词修饰presentations；to convince investors是不定式作目的状语；that his small team could overtake far more experienced competitors是convince的宾语从句
longSentences.translation: 张伟必须做出有说服力的展示，才能让投资人相信自己这支小团队能够超越经验丰富得多的竞争对手。
longSentences.tip: 遇到“to do…that…”结构，先看不定式的目的状语功能，再处理其后的宾语从句。

longSentences.sentence: He worked with relentless energy, often coding through the night, even as headlines warned that the technology market was heading toward another bubble.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：He worked with relentless energy（他不懈地工作）
longSentences.modifiers: often coding through the night是现在分词短语作伴随状语；even as headlines warned that...是让步状语从句，其中包含warned的宾语从句
longSentences.translation: 他以不知疲倦的干劲投入工作，常常通宵编写代码，即便当时不断有新闻警告说科技行业正走向又一场泡沫。
longSentences.tip: 先抓主句主谓，再依次处理分词伴随状语和even as引导的让步状语从句。

items.title: From Architect To Hardware Entrepreneur
items.passage:
Ten years ago, Mia Chen was a licensed architect designing office buildings. Watching small manufacturers struggle to find affordable tools, she decided to halt her career in architecture and build something new: a startup that produced affordable diagnostic hardware for rural clinics. Colleagues were stunned, and even Mia admitted to a momentary flash of doubt before she signed the resignation letter.

The competitive landscape she entered was unforgiving. Established medical-device firms dominated the market, and investors were reluctant to fund a founder without an engineering background. To win their trust, Mia had to narrate her transition convincingly, drafting a detailed memorandum that laid out manufacturing costs, supply chains, and expected returns. The exposure she gained from a regional business competition, where her prototype was displayed alongside dozens of rivals, eventually attracted a small but committed group of backers.

Five years later, her company ships portable devices to clinics across three provinces. Growth has not been steady; a factory fire once forced production to halt for two months. Yet Mia insists that the discipline she brought from architecture, planning every structure down to the smallest joint, taught her to treat setbacks as temporary rather than fatal, and to keep building toward a design that finally works.
items.translation:
十年前，米娅·陈（Mia Chen）还是一名执业建筑师，专门设计写字楼。看到许多小型制造商买不起合适的工具，她决定停下建筑行业的职业生涯，转而创办一家企业，为乡村诊所生产价格低廉的诊断类硬件设备。同事们对此深感震惊，就连米娅自己在签下辞职信之前，也承认心里闪过一丝短暂的犹豫。

她所进入的行业格局竞争激烈，毫不留情。成熟的医疗器械公司占据着市场，而投资人也不愿资助一个没有工程背景的创业者。为了赢得他们的信任，米娅不得不有说服力地讲述自己的转型故事，还起草了一份详细的备忘录，列明生产成本、供应链和预期回报。在一次区域商业大赛上，她的原型产品与数十个竞争对手的产品同台展出，由此获得的曝光最终吸引了一小群但十分坚定的支持者。

五年后，她的公司已向三个省份的诊所出货便携式设备。发展并非一帆风顺：一场工厂火灾曾迫使生产停摆两个月。但米娅坚持认为，建筑行业培养出的那种严谨——将每一处结构精确到最小的接口——教会了她把挫折看作暂时的而非致命的，并继续朝着最终行之有效的设计不断打磨。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: How architects design office buildings efficiently.
How a former architect built a hardware startup for rural clinics.
The history of diagnostic hardware in medicine.
Why investors avoid funding new companies.
questions.explain: 第一段引出米娅·陈从建筑师转型创办医疗硬件初创公司，第二、三段介绍其融资与发展过程，全文围绕这一经历展开，故主旨为B。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, why did investors hesitate to fund Mia's company?
options: Her prototype failed a safety test.
She lacked an engineering background.
The market was already saturated with clinics.
She refused to write a business memorandum.
questions.explain: 依据第二段“investors were reluctant to fund a founder without an engineering background”可知，投资人犹豫的原因是米娅缺乏工程背景，故选B。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the last paragraph that Mia believes her setbacks were
options: permanent failures that ended her business.
proof that hardware startups cannot survive.
temporary obstacles rather than fatal ones.
unrelated to her training as an architect.
questions.explain: 末段提到建筑训练教会她“把挫折看作暂时的而非致命的”，可推知她认为挫折只是暂时性的障碍，故选C。

questions.q: In the sentence "even Mia admitted to a momentary flash of doubt", the word "momentary" is closest in meaning to
options: permanent
brief
serious
hidden
questions.explain: momentary意为“短暂的，瞬间的”，与brief意思最接近，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: Watching small manufacturers struggle to find affordable tools, she decided to halt her career in architecture and build something new: a startup that produced affordable diagnostic hardware for rural clinics.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为 she decided to halt her career...and build something new
longSentences.modifiers: 句首 Watching small manufacturers struggle to find affordable tools 是现在分词短语作状语，表示原因/伴随；冒号后 a startup that produced affordable diagnostic hardware for rural clinics 是同位语，补充说明 something new，其中 that 引导定语从句修饰 startup。
longSentences.translation: 看到许多小型制造商买不起合适的工具，她决定停下建筑行业的职业生涯，转而创办一家企业——一家为乡村诊所生产价格低廉的诊断类硬件设备的初创公司。
longSentences.tip: 遇到句首分词短语，先跳过它抓主句主谓宾，再回头看分词表达的逻辑关系（原因或伴随）。

longSentences.sentence: To win their trust, Mia had to narrate her transition convincingly, drafting a detailed memorandum that laid out manufacturing costs, supply chains, and expected returns.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为 Mia had to narrate her transition convincingly
longSentences.modifiers: To win their trust 是不定式作目的状语；drafting a detailed memorandum... 是现在分词短语作伴随状语；that laid out... 是定语从句修饰 memorandum。
longSentences.translation: 为了赢得他们的信任，米娅不得不有说服力地讲述自己的转型故事，同时起草一份详细的备忘录，列明生产成本、供应链和预期回报。
longSentences.tip: 句中含有目的状语、分词状语和定语从句三层修饰，逐层剥离即可看清主干。

longSentences.sentence: Growth has not been steady; a factory fire once forced production to halt for two months.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为两个由分号连接的并列分句：Growth has not been steady 和 a factory fire...forced production to halt
longSentences.modifiers: once 为时间状语；for two months 为时间状语，说明停产持续的时长；to halt 是不定式，在 force sth to do 结构中作宾语补足语。
longSentences.translation: 发展并非一帆风顺；一场工厂火灾曾迫使生产停摆两个月。
longSentences.tip: 分号连接两个独立分句，注意 force sb/sth to do 结构中不定式作宾语补足语的用法。

items.title: Tracking Shifting Climate Zones
items.passage:
Climate scientists recently achieved a breakthrough that helps explain why plants and animals are shifting into new areas. Using huge amounts of satellite information collected over the past forty years, researchers built software that maps how the boundaries of each climate zone are slowly moving toward the poles. A single glance at the resulting maps shows changes that would once have taken months of manual work to notice.

The team studied hundreds of species, from mountain birds to desert camels, and found that many are already living outside the regions where they were once common. In parts of North Africa, camels once suited only to the hottest zones now wander into areas that used to be too cool for them. Such findings give governments clearer information for planning farmland, water supplies, and nature reserves.

Turning this research into real protection, however, still needs money. International agencies have promised additional funding, but scientists warn that without continued support, the software and the monitoring networks behind it could fall behind the pace of change. For now, at least, the new tools offer a rare, hopeful glance at how fast, and how far, climate zones are moving.
items.translation:
气候科学家最近取得一项突破，有助于解释植物和动物为何正迁往新的区域。借助过去四十年间收集到的大量卫星信息，研究人员开发出一款软件，用来描绘各个气候带的边界正在如何缓慢地向两极移动。只需看一眼由此绘制出的地图，就能发现过去需要数月人工才能察觉到的变化。

研究团队考察了数百个物种，从高山鸟类到沙漠骆驼，发现许多物种如今已生活在它们曾经常见的区域之外。在北非部分地区，原本只适应最炎热地带的骆驼，如今也漫游到了过去对它们而言过于凉爽的区域。这些发现为各国政府规划农田、水资源和自然保护区提供了更清晰的信息。

然而，要把这些研究成果转化为切实的保护措施，仍然离不开资金。国际机构已承诺提供更多资助，但科学家警告说，如果得不到持续支持，这套软件及其背后的监测网络可能会跟不上变化的速度。至少就目前而言，这些新工具让人们得以难得而充满希望地一窥气候带正在以怎样的速度、朝着多远的方向移动。

questions.q: What is the main topic of the passage?
options: A new species of camel discovered in North Africa.
New software mapping how climate zones are shifting.
A shortage of research funding for wildlife study.
How satellite technology was invented forty years ago.
questions.explain: 全文围绕科学家利用新软件绘制气候带边界移动的研究展开，第一段提出突破，第二段举例说明，第三段讨论资金问题，故主旨为B。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, what has happened to some camels in North Africa?
options: They have moved to zones that used to be too cool for them.
They have disappeared from their original habitat completely.
They have adapted to survive without water.
They have been moved to nature reserves by scientists.
questions.explain: 第二段提到camels...now wander into areas that used to be too cool for them，说明部分骆驼已进入过去过于凉爽而不适宜其生存的区域，故选A。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the future of the mapping software?
options: It will definitely be replaced by satellite technology.
Its usefulness may be limited without continued funding.
It has already solved the funding problem completely.
It is no longer needed once nature reserves are set up.
questions.explain: 第三段指出without continued support, the software...could fall behind the pace of change，暗示若得不到持续资助，软件效用将受限，故选B。

questions.q: In the passage, the phrase "at a glance" is closest in meaning to
options: after careful study
with a quick look
in complete detail
without any evidence
questions.explain: at a glance意为“看一眼，一瞥之间”，强调迅速一看即可发现变化，与with a quick look意思相近，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: Using huge amounts of satellite information collected over the past forty years, researchers built software that maps how the boundaries of each climate zone are slowly moving toward the poles.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为 researchers built software
longSentences.modifiers: 句首 Using huge amounts of satellite information collected over the past forty years 是现在分词短语作状语，其中 collected over the past forty years 是过去分词短语修饰 information；that maps how... 是定语从句修饰 software，从句中又嵌套 how 引导的宾语从句作 maps 的宾语。
longSentences.translation: 借助过去四十年间收集到的大量卫星信息，研究人员开发出一款软件，用来描绘各个气候带的边界正在如何缓慢地向两极移动。
longSentences.tip: 句中嵌套多层修饰（分词+定语从句+宾语从句），先找出 researchers built software 这一主干，再逐层拆解修饰成分。

longSentences.sentence: The team studied hundreds of species, from mountain birds to desert camels, and found that many are already living outside the regions where they were once common.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为并列谓语 The team studied hundreds of species...and found that...
longSentences.modifiers: from mountain birds to desert camels 是插入语，举例说明物种范围；that many are already living... 是 found 的宾语从句；where they were once common 是定语从句修饰 the regions。
longSentences.translation: 研究团队考察了数百个物种，从高山鸟类到沙漠骆驼，并发现许多物种如今已生活在它们曾经常见的区域之外。
longSentences.tip: 插入语用逗号隔开，可先跳过阅读主干，再回头理解其举例说明的作用。

items.title: The Economics Of Ethical Consumption
items.passage:
For a growing number of consumers, shopping decisions are no longer guided by price alone but by a personal philosophy about how goods should be produced. Surveys suggest that shoppers who buy according to strong ethical conviction, choosing products because they align with their values, are held in growing esteem by retailers eager to attract loyal customers.

The dairy industry offers a clear example. Because cattle farming is a notable source of greenhouse gas emissions, some consumers have cut back on milk and cheese, pushing supermarkets to add a sustainability clause to their supplier contracts, requiring proof of reduced emissions before goods reach the shelf. Meanwhile, several dairy brands now donate a share of profits to environmental charity, hoping the gesture will strengthen customer loyalty.

Even so, analysts caution against overstating the trend. Total spending on dairy and other staple foods has remained largely static over the past two years, and price, not principle, still determines most household budgets, respectively, for high- and low-income families alike. Ethical conviction may be reshaping the edges of the market, but for now it has not replaced the basic economics of everyday consumption.
items.translation:
对越来越多的消费者而言，购物决策不再仅仅由价格决定，而是由个人对于商品应如何被生产的理念所主导。调查显示，那些依据坚定的伦理信念购物——因为某件商品符合自己价值观而选择它——的消费者，正日益受到零售商的看重，因为零售商渴望留住这类忠诚顾客。

乳制品行业就是一个典型例子。由于养牛业是温室气体排放的重要来源，一些消费者减少了牛奶和奶酪的消费，这促使超市在供应商合同中加入可持续发展条款，要求供应商在商品上架前证明其已降低排放。与此同时，一些乳制品品牌如今将部分利润捐给环保慈善机构，希望这一举动能增强顾客的忠诚度。

尽管如此，分析人士提醒不要过度夸大这一趋势。过去两年间，乳制品及其他日常食品的总体消费支出基本保持不变，无论是高收入家庭还是低收入家庭，价格而非原则，仍分别是决定大多数家庭预算的主要因素。伦理信念或许正在重塑市场的边缘地带，但目前尚未取代日常消费背后最基本的经济规律。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The rising price of dairy products worldwide.
How ethical conviction influences, but has not replaced, price in consumer spending.
The environmental damage caused solely by cattle farming.
Why charities depend on donations from dairy brands.
questions.explain: 首段提出消费者理念影响购物决策，第二段以乳制品行业为例说明，末段指出价格仍是决定消费的主因，说明伦理信念对消费有影响但未取代价格因素，故选B。

questions.q: Why have some supermarkets added a sustainability clause to supplier contracts?
options: To reduce the price of dairy products.
To require proof of reduced greenhouse gas emissions.
To increase donations to environmental charities.
To ban the sale of dairy products entirely.
questions.explain: 第二段提到超市要求供应商在商品上架前证明已降低排放，requiring proof of reduced emissions before goods reach the shelf，故选B。

questions.q: What does the last paragraph suggest about ethical consumption?
options: It has completely replaced price as the main factor in shopping.
It affects only high-income families' spending habits.
It is a growing but still limited part of overall consumption.
It has caused total spending on dairy to fall sharply.
questions.explain: 末段指出总体消费支出基本保持不变，伦理信念“可能正在重塑市场的边缘地带，但尚未取代日常消费背后最基本的经济规律”，说明其影响仍然有限但在增长，故选C。

questions.q: In the passage, "static" is closest in meaning to
options: increasing rapidly
largely unchanged
completely random
steadily declining
questions.explain: static意为“静态的，不变的”，文中指乳制品消费支出largely static，即基本保持不变，与largely unchanged意思相近，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: Surveys suggest that shoppers who buy according to strong ethical conviction, choosing products because they align with their values, are held in growing esteem by retailers eager to attract loyal customers.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为 Surveys suggest that shoppers...are held in growing esteem by retailers...
longSentences.modifiers: who buy according to strong ethical conviction 是定语从句修饰 shoppers；choosing products because they align with their values 是现在分词短语作伴随状语；eager to attract loyal customers 是形容词短语作后置定语修饰 retailers。
longSentences.translation: 调查显示，那些依据坚定的伦理信念购物——选择商品是因为其符合自身价值观——的消费者，正日益受到渴望留住忠诚顾客的零售商的看重。
longSentences.tip: 长句中多重定语和分词状语层层修饰主语和宾语，先抓住 suggest that 后的主谓宾主干，再逐一还原每层修饰。

longSentences.sentence: Because cattle farming is a notable source of greenhouse gas emissions, some consumers have cut back on milk and cheese, pushing supermarkets to add a sustainability clause to their supplier contracts, requiring proof of reduced emissions before goods reach the shelf.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为 some consumers have cut back on milk and cheese
longSentences.modifiers: Because cattle farming is a notable source of greenhouse gas emissions 是原因状语从句；pushing supermarkets to add... 和 requiring proof of... 均为现在分词短语作结果状语，说明消费者行为带来的连锁后果。
longSentences.translation: 由于养牛业是温室气体排放的重要来源，一些消费者减少了牛奶和奶酪的消费，这促使超市在供应商合同中加入可持续发展条款，要求在商品上架前证明其已降低排放。
longSentences.tip: 句尾连续出现两个分词短语作结果状语，表示一系列连锁反应，翻译时可译为“从而”“促使”等表因果关系的词。

items.title: Remote Work: Costs And Benefits
items.passage:
Since the pandemic, extensive adoption of remote work has forced companies to rethink how offices function. Employers once notorious for resisting flexible schedules now rely on digital platforms to facilitate daily communication between scattered teams, while employees tackle a new set of challenges: distinguishing work hours from personal time, staying motivated without direct supervision, and managing a household that has suddenly become a workplace.

Setting up a functional home office is rarely free. Many workers incur unexpected costs, from faster internet connections to an inventory of basic equipment, chairs, monitors, and printers, that a traditional office would have supplied. Not everyone has a spacious room to dedicate to work either, and cramped apartments can make the boundary between job and rest even harder to maintain.
