questions.q: According to the passage, why are farm-stay bookings rising, especially among young families?
options: Because farms are cheaper than city apartments
Because parents want their children to see where food actually comes from
Because travel agents no longer offer spa vacations
Because seminars are compulsory for all families
questions.explain: 第一段明确提到，农场民宿受到“渴望让孩子亲眼看看食物真正来源”的年轻家庭青睐，故B正确；其余选项原文均未提及。（第1段）

questions.q: What can be inferred from the last paragraph about the value of farm experiences?
options: They are considered less valuable than classroom education
They can teach qualities such as patience that formal classrooms may not easily provide
They are mainly designed for professional farmers
They will completely replace the need for convenience in modern life
questions.explain: 末段指出农业能传授“关于耐心和季节节律的道理，而这些是任何课堂都无法复制的”，可推知B正确；A与原文相悖，C、D原文未提及或与原文“无法完全取代”相矛盾。（第3段）

questions.q: The word 'absorb' in the second paragraph is closest in meaning to ___.
options: ignore
take in fully
photograph
avoid
questions.explain: 此处absorb意为“吸收，专心感受”，与take in fully（充分领会/吸收）含义最接近；ignore（忽视）、photograph（拍照）、avoid（避免）均不符合语境。（第2段）

longSentences.sentence: As cities become more crowded, an interesting social trend has emerged: growing numbers of urban residents are trading the convenience of downtown apartments, even if only for a weekend, for a few days on a working farm.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“an interesting social trend has emerged”，冒号后具体说明是什么趋势：urban residents are trading A for B。
longSentences.modifiers: “As cities become more crowded”是原因状语从句；“even if only for a weekend”是插入的让步状语，强调即便只是短暂的周末。
longSentences.translation: 随着城市变得日益拥挤，一种有趣的社会潮流逐渐显现：越来越多的城市居民愿意放弃市中心公寓的便利——哪怕只是一个周末——换取在一座正常运营的农场里度过的几天时光。
longSentences.tip: 先抓住冒号前的主句，再看冒号后trading A for B的结构，插入的让步状语可先跳过以理清主干。

longSentences.sentence: Guides encourage visitors to touch the soil, listen to birds sing their unhurried song, and slow down enough to actually absorb their surroundings, rather than rushing past with a camera.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“Guides encourage visitors to do A, B, and C”，to touch/listen/slow down三个并列不定式作宾语补足语。
longSentences.modifiers: “rather than rushing past with a camera”是对比状语，与前面并列的三个动作形成对照。
longSentences.translation: 向导会鼓励游客亲手触摸泥土，聆听鸟儿从容不迫地鸣唱，并放慢脚步，真正用心感受周围的一切，而不是举着相机匆匆走过。
longSentences.tip: encourage sb. to do sth.结构中，留意do后面并列的三个不定式短语，以及rather than引出的对比部分。

items.title: A Study Trip to an Old Castle
items.passage:
Last month, a group of pupils from a middle school set off early one morning for a three-day study trip. Their teacher had planned a route that combined history with hands-on learning, and the first stop was an old castle standing on a hill above the town, its stone walls looking out over the surrounding fields.

Before boarding the bus, the pupils checked their luggage twice, afraid they might forget something relevant to the trip, such as notebooks and cameras. Inside the castle, a guide showed them a wooden beam carved with the signature of a king who had stayed there centuries earlier, and the students were surprised that such an old mark could still be read so clearly.

In the afternoon, a local craftsman taught the group how to shape clay into simple bowls. At first, many pupils were confused about which steps came first, and some pieces collapsed before they were finished. With patience, though, their hands grew steadier, and by evening most students had made something to take home, too.
items.translation:
上个月，一所中学的一群学生一大早就出发，开始了为期三天的研学旅行。老师安排的路线将历史知识与动手实践结合在一起，第一站是一座古老的城堡，它矗立在小镇上方的山丘上，石墙俯瞰着周围的田野。

上车前，学生们把行李检查了两遍，生怕落下与本次旅行相关的东西，比如笔记本和相机。在城堡内，导游给他们看了一根木梁，上面刻着一位国王的签名，那是几个世纪前留下的，学生们惊讶于这么古老的印记至今依然清晰可辨。

下午，一位当地的手艺人教大家如何用黏土捏成简单的碗。起初，许多学生对先做哪一步感到困惑，有些作品在完成前就塌了。不过凭着耐心，他们的手渐渐稳了下来，到傍晚时，大多数学生也都做出了可以带回家的作品。

keyWords.zh: a. 周围的 n. （pl.）周围事物，环境

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A school's plan to build a new castle
A three-day study trip that combined history and craft
A craftsman's career making clay bowls
The history of an old European castle
questions.explain: 全文讲述一次为期三天的研学旅行，涵盖参观城堡与体验黏土手工，主旨为B；A、C、D都只是文中局部细节或原文未展开的内容，第一段总起、全文印证。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, what did the guide show the pupils inside the castle?
options: A list of the king's belongings
A map of the surrounding fields
A wooden beam with a king's signature
A collection of ancient clay bowls
questions.explain: 第二段明确提到导游给学生看了刻有国王签名的木梁（a wooden beam carved with the signature of a king），依据在第二段。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the pupils' clay-making experience?
options: It was easy for everyone from the start.
Nobody managed to finish a bowl.
They found it challenging at first but improved with practice.
The craftsman refused to help them.
questions.explain: 第三段提到学生一开始困惑、作品塌陷，但凭耐心练习后手逐渐稳了，说明先难后易，依据在第三段。

questions.q: In paragraph 3, the word "confused" is closest in meaning to which word?
options: excited
uncertain
confident
relaxed
questions.explain: 语境中学生对先做哪一步感到confused，表示“不确定、迷惑”，故选B，依据第三段上下文（步骤混乱、作品塌陷）。

longSentences.sentence: Their teacher had planned a route that combined history with hands-on learning, and the first stop was an old castle standing on a hill above the town, its stone walls looking out over the surrounding fields.
longSentences.skeleton: 老师安排了一条路线，第一站是一座城堡
longSentences.modifiers: that combined history with hands-on learning 是定语从句修饰route；standing on a hill above the town 是现在分词短语修饰castle；its stone walls looking out...是独立主格结构补充说明
longSentences.translation: 老师安排的路线将历史知识与动手实践结合在一起，第一站是一座矗立在小镇上方山丘上的古老城堡，石墙俯瞰着周围的田野。
longSentences.tip: 遇到并列句加现在分词加独立主格时，先抓两个主谓（老师安排路线；第一站是城堡），再把附加成分当作补充信息处理。

longSentences.sentence: Inside the castle, a guide showed them a wooden beam carved with the signature of a king who had stayed there centuries earlier, and the students were surprised that such an old mark could still be read so clearly.
longSentences.skeleton: 导游给他们看了一根木梁，学生们感到惊讶
longSentences.modifiers: carved with the signature of a king 是过去分词短语修饰beam；who had stayed there centuries earlier 是定语从句修饰king；that such an old mark...是宾语从句说明surprised的内容
longSentences.translation: 在城堡内，导游给他们看了一根刻有国王签名的木梁——那位国王几个世纪前曾在此居住，学生们惊讶于这么古老的印记至今仍能被清晰辨认。
longSentences.tip: 先找and连接的两个并列分句，再分别处理各自的从句和分词修饰成分。

items.title: Volunteers Dig Up Ancient History
items.passage:
Every summer, a group of volunteers gathers at a classical Roman site on the edge of the city to help archaeologists uncover an ancient burial ground. Over the past decade, the project has become something of a local fixture, drawing students, retirees and travelers who want to spend their holidays with a trowel in hand rather than on a beach.

The dig is organized by a nonprofit entity that relies heavily on a wealthy patron whose donations cover the bulk of the equipment costs. A commission of local historians affirms every discovery before it is announced to the public, making sure that excitement does not blur the line between fact and guesswork. Volunteers often joke that their leader manages the team like a shepherd, gently guiding newcomers away from fragile stones and toward safer tasks.

For many participants, the work is a welcome diversion from office routines. They spend hours carefully chipping away layers of soil, in sharp contrast to the speed of daily city life. By the end of each season, the team has usually cleared enough ground to reveal a handful of new artifacts, proof that patience still pays off.
items.translation:
每年夏天，一群志愿者都会来到城郊的一处古典罗马遗址，帮助考古学家发掘一座古墓地。在过去十年里，这个项目已经成为当地的一项固定活动，吸引着学生、退休人员和游客——他们宁愿假期里手持小铲，也不愿躺在海滩上。

这项发掘工作由一家非营利机构组织，该机构在很大程度上依赖一位富有赞助人的捐款来支付大部分设备费用。当地历史学家组成的委员会会在每一项新发现公之于众前予以确认，以确保兴奋之情不会使事实与推测之间的界限变得模糊。志愿者们常开玩笑说，他们的领队管理团队的方式就像牧羊人一样，会轻声引导新人远离易碎的石块，转向更安全的任务。

对许多参与者来说，这项工作是从办公室日常事务中获得的一种令人愉快的消遣。他们花上数小时仔细地一点点铲去土层，这与城市日常生活的快节奏形成了鲜明的对比。每个季度结束时，团队通常已清理出足够的地面，能够发现少量新文物，证明耐心终究是值得的。

keyWords.zh: n. 固定装置；固定项目
vt. 使模糊不清 n. 模糊

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A city's plan to build a new museum
A summer volunteer project at an ancient archaeological site
A dispute between historians and a patron
The daily life of a professional shepherd
questions.explain: 全文围绕志愿者夏季在古罗马遗址参与考古发掘展开，主旨为B；其余选项要么原文未提，要么只是局部细节。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, what does the commission of historians do?
options: They dig up the burial ground themselves.
They confirm each discovery before it is made public.
They provide most of the project's funding.
They train volunteers to use tools safely.
questions.explain: 第二段明确说委员会会在每一项发现公之于众前予以确认（affirms every discovery before it is announced to the public），故选B。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the volunteers mentioned in the passage?
options: They are mostly professional archaeologists.
They are paid a high salary for their work.
They value the experience more than a typical beach holiday.
They dislike working under the project leader.
questions.explain: 第一段提到志愿者宁愿手持小铲度假也不愿去海滩，说明他们更看重这种经历，依据在第一段；第二段又说领队像牧羊人一样温和引导新人，并无“讨厌领队”的信息。

questions.q: In paragraph 2, the phrase "blur the line" most likely means to make the boundary ______.
options: completely disappear
unclear or hard to distinguish
more official
financially costly
questions.explain: blur本义为“使模糊”，此处指兴奋情绪可能使事实与推测之间的界限变得难以区分，故选B，依据第二段上下文。

longSentences.sentence: Over the past decade, the project has become something of a local fixture, drawing students, retirees and travelers who want to spend their holidays with a trowel in hand rather than on a beach.
longSentences.skeleton: 这个项目已成为当地的一项固定活动
longSentences.modifiers: drawing students, retirees and travelers 是现在分词短语作结果状语；who want to spend...是定语从句修饰前面提到的人群；rather than on a beach 是比较状语
longSentences.translation: 在过去十年里，这个项目已经成为当地的一项固定活动，吸引了许多学生、退休人员和游客，他们宁愿假期里手持小铲，也不愿去海滩。
longSentences.tip: 现在分词短语作伴随或结果状语时，可先处理主干，再把分词部分当作补充说明。

longSentences.sentence: The dig is organized by a nonprofit entity that relies heavily on a wealthy patron whose donations cover the bulk of the equipment costs.
longSentences.skeleton: 这项发掘工作由一家非营利机构组织
longSentences.modifiers: that relies heavily on a wealthy patron 是定语从句修饰entity；whose donations cover the bulk of the equipment costs 是定语从句修饰patron，构成从句嵌套
longSentences.translation: 这项发掘工作由一家非营利机构组织，该机构在很大程度上依赖一位富有赞助人，其捐款支付了大部分设备费用。
longSentences.tip: 出现从句套从句时，从后往前逐层翻译，先处理最内层的whose从句，再回到外层。

longSentences.sentence: A commission of local historians affirms every discovery before it is announced to the public, making sure that excitement does not blur the line between fact and guesswork.
longSentences.skeleton: 委员会会确认每一项发现
longSentences.modifiers: before it is announced to the public 是时间状语从句；making sure that...是现在分词短语作目的状语，内嵌that引导的宾语从句
longSentences.translation: 当地历史学家组成的委员会会在每一项发现公之于众前予以确认，以确保兴奋之情不会使事实与推测之间的界限变得模糊。
longSentences.tip: 遇到分词短语加that从句的组合，先抓主句主谓宾，再把分词部分当作补充目的处理。

items.title: City Life and Mental Exhaustion
items.passage:
Life in a large metropolitan area offers many conveniences: an abundance of restaurants, entertainment and job opportunities within a short ride. Yet psychologists have long warned that this same environment can quietly wear down mental health. The noise of traffic and the pressure to keep pace with colleagues can, over time, produce a sheer sense of exhaustion that no vacation seems to cure.

One office worker described her days as an endless drilling routine: wake, commute, work, repeat, with barely any intimate conversation to break the monotony. Researchers who study how people interact with their surroundings note that constant novelty, such as new apps and notifications, can keep the brain in a state of low-level alert. Ironically, this alertness does not raise productivity; it often does the opposite, leaving workers too tired to concentrate.

Doctors marshal a simple message when treating burnout: rest is not optional, it is necessary. Some patients half joke that the fatigue feels almost deadly, though experts prefer gentler words. What matters, they say, is that people persist in setting small boundaries, whether that means switching off phones after dinner or treating a busy schedule the way a surgeon treats an operation, cutting away tasks that no longer serve a purpose.
items.translation:
生活在大都市地区能带来诸多便利：短短车程内就有丰富的餐厅、娱乐场所和工作机会。然而，心理学家早就警告，同样的环境也可能在不知不觉中损害人们的心理健康。交通噪音和与同事保持步调一致的压力，久而久之会产生一种彻底的疲惫感，连度假似乎都无法治愈。

一位上班族形容自己的日子就像一场没完没了的操练：起床、通勤、工作、再重复，几乎没有任何亲密的交流来打破这种单调。研究人们如何与周围环境互动的学者指出，诸如新应用和消息提醒之类持续不断的新鲜刺激，会让大脑始终处于低水平警觉状态。讽刺的是，这种警觉并不会提高生产力，反而往往适得其反，让员工疲惫得难以集中注意力。

医生们在治疗职业倦怠时会传达一个简单的信息：休息不是可选项，而是必需品。一些患者半开玩笑地说，这种疲惫感几乎是致命的，尽管专家更愿意使用温和一些的说法。他们说，重要的是人们要坚持设立一些小小的界限，不论是晚饭后关掉手机，还是像外科医生对待一场手术那样对待繁忙的日程，把不再有意义的任务一一切除。

keyWords.zh: a. 大都市的 n. 大城市人
vt. 整理；引领 n. 元帅

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The advantages of living in a big city
How urban pressure affects mental health and ways to cope
A study comparing doctors and office workers
The history of burnout as a medical term
questions.explain: 全文讲述大都市生活压力对心理健康的损害以及应对方式（设立界限、重视休息），主旨为B；A只是第一段的引子，C、D原文均未展开。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, what is the ironic effect of constant novelty such as new apps and notifications?
options: It helps people relax more easily.
It lowers productivity instead of raising it.
It improves the quality of sleep.
It reduces the number of daily notifications.
questions.explain: 第二段明确说这种警觉状态“does not raise productivity; it often does the opposite”，即降低而非提高生产力，故选B。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the doctors' advice mentioned in paragraph 3?
options: People should completely avoid using phones.
Rest should be treated as a necessary part of life, not an extra option.
Surgery is the only effective treatment for burnout.
Setting boundaries is unnecessary once vacation is taken.
questions.explain: 第三段指出“rest is not optional, it is necessary”，并建议人们坚持设立小界限，可推知医生认为休息应被视为必需而非可有可无，故选B。

questions.q: In paragraph 1, the word "sheer" is closest in meaning to ______.
options: slight
pure and complete
temporary
hidden
questions.explain: sheer在此修饰exhaustion（疲惫），表示“彻底的、纯粹的”疲惫感，故选B，依据第一段上下文（连度假都无法治愈的疲惫）。

longSentences.sentence: The noise of traffic and the pressure to keep pace with colleagues can, over time, produce a sheer sense of exhaustion that no vacation seems to cure.
longSentences.skeleton: 交通噪音和压力会产生一种彻底的疲惫感
longSentences.modifiers: to keep pace with colleagues 是不定式作定语修饰pressure；over time 是插入语作时间状语；that no vacation seems to cure 是定语从句修饰exhaustion
longSentences.translation: 交通噪音和与同事保持步调一致的压力，久而久之会产生一种彻底的疲惫感，连度假似乎都无法治愈。
longSentences.tip: 先跳过插入语over time抓主谓宾，再补上定语从句的修饰内容。

longSentences.sentence: Researchers who study how people interact with their surroundings note that constant novelty, such as new apps and notifications, can keep the brain in a state of low-level alert.
longSentences.skeleton: 研究人员指出，持续的新鲜感会让大脑处于低水平警觉状态
longSentences.modifiers: who study how people interact with their surroundings 是定语从句修饰Researchers，内嵌how引导的宾语从句；such as new apps and notifications 举例说明novelty；that引导note的宾语从句
longSentences.translation: 研究人们如何与周围环境互动的学者指出，诸如新应用和消息提醒之类持续不断的新鲜刺激，会让大脑始终处于低水平警觉状态。
longSentences.tip: 主语很长时先划出真正的谓语note，再逐层还原修饰成分。

longSentences.sentence: What matters, they say, is that people persist in setting small boundaries, whether that means switching off phones after dinner or treating a busy schedule the way a surgeon treats an operation, cutting away tasks that no longer serve a purpose.
longSentences.skeleton: 重要的是人们要坚持设立小界限
longSentences.modifiers: What matters 是主语从句；they say 是插入语；that people...boundaries 是表语从句；whether...or...是让步状语从句举例说明；cutting away...是现在分词短语作伴随状语
longSentences.translation: 他们说，重要的是人们要坚持设立一些小小的界限，不论是晚饭后关掉手机，还是像外科医生对待手术那样对待繁忙的日程，把不再有意义的任务一一切除。
longSentences.tip: what从句作主语时，先找系动词is，再分别处理插入语和表语从句。

items.title: A Football Club's Financial Reform
items.passage:
A century-old football club recently announced a major shift in how it plans to survive the next decade. After years of heavy investment in star players, the club came close to going bankrupt last season, and its owners openly admitted that the old business model was no longer sustainable, especially as broadcasting income continued to fall.

Instead of relying only on ticket sales, the club now hopes to build a stronger secondary income stream, namely sponsorship deals with electronics companies eager to place their gadgets in front of young fans. Coaches have also begun to trim training budgets, cutting less essential travel and equipment costs without touching youth programs, though the first team's roster remains untouched for now.

The club's new director sketched out a five-year plan that includes a humanitarian project supporting children in the surrounding neighborhood, arguing that community trust matters as much as trophies and that fans deserve honest communication. Critics, however, have alleged that some of these changes are simply public relations moves rather than genuine reform. Only time will tell whether the club's finances, and its reputation, truly recover.
items.translation:
一家有百年历史的足球俱乐部近日宣布，将对未来十年的生存方式做出重大转变。多年来在明星球员身上投入巨资后，该俱乐部上赛季一度濒临破产，俱乐部所有者公开承认，旧的商业模式已难以为继，尤其是在转播收入持续下滑的情况下。

俱乐部现在希望不再仅仅依靠门票收入，而是打造更强劲的第二收入来源，也就是与电子产品公司签订赞助协议，这些公司渴望让自己的产品出现在年轻球迷面前。教练组也已开始削减训练预算，削减了一些不太必要的出行和装备开支，但青训项目并未受到影响，一线队的名单目前也维持不变。

俱乐部新任总监拟定了一份五年计划，其中包括一项面向周边社区儿童的人道主义项目，他认为社区信任与奖杯同等重要，球迷也理应获得诚实的沟通。然而，批评者则声称，这些变化中有一些不过是公关手段，而非真正的改革。俱乐部的财务状况和声誉能否真正好转，只有时间能给出答案。

keyWords.zh: a. 破产的 n. 破产者

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A football club's plan to sign more star players
A football club's financial reform after nearly going bankrupt
A dispute between a club and its sponsors
The history of a century-old football club
questions.explain: 全文围绕俱乐部濒临破产后进行的财务与形象改革展开，主旨为B；A与原文相反（俱乐部正减少此类投资），C、D原文均未展开。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, how does the club plan to increase its secondary income?
options: By raising ticket prices significantly
By signing sponsorship deals with electronics companies
By selling its youth training programs
By reducing the size of the first team
questions.explain: 第二段明确提到俱乐部希望通过“sponsorship deals with electronics companies”增加第二收入来源，故选B。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the critics' attitude in paragraph 3?
options: They fully trust the club's new plan.
They doubt whether the reform is genuine.
They believe the humanitarian project is unnecessary.
They think the club should raise ticket prices instead.
questions.explain: 第三段提到批评者声称部分变化“simply public relations moves rather than genuine reform”，说明他们怀疑改革的真实性，故选B，依据在第三段。

questions.q: In paragraph 1, the word "sustainable" most likely means ______.
options: profitable in the short term only
able to continue without major problems over time
popular among fans
approved by the government
questions.explain: 语境中旧商业模式“no longer sustainable”，结合前文濒临破产、转播收入下滑，可推知sustainable意为“能够长期维持、不出大问题”，故选B，依据第一段。

longSentences.sentence: After years of heavy investment in star players, the club came close to going bankrupt last season, and its owners openly admitted that the old business model was no longer sustainable, especially as broadcasting income continued to fall.
longSentences.skeleton: 俱乐部上赛季一度濒临破产，所有者公开承认旧模式已难以为继
longSentences.modifiers: After years of heavy investment in star players 是时间状语；that the old business model...是宾语从句；especially as broadcasting income continued to fall 是原因状语从句
longSentences.translation: 多年来在明星球员身上投入巨资后，该俱乐部上赛季一度濒临破产，俱乐部所有者公开承认，旧的商业模式已难以为继，尤其是在转播收入持续下滑的情况下。
longSentences.tip: and连接两个并列分句，先各自找主谓宾，再补充状语和从句信息。

longSentences.sentence: The club's new director sketched out a five-year plan that includes a humanitarian project supporting children in the surrounding neighborhood, arguing that community trust matters as much as trophies and that fans deserve honest communication.
longSentences.skeleton: 俱乐部新任总监拟定了一份五年计划
longSentences.modifiers: that includes a humanitarian project...是定语从句修饰plan；supporting children...是现在分词短语修饰project；arguing that...是现在分词短语作伴随状语，内嵌两个并列的that宾语从句
longSentences.translation: 俱乐部新任总监拟定了一份五年计划，其中包括一项面向周边社区儿童的人道主义项目，他认为社区信任与奖杯同等重要，球迷也理应获得诚实的沟通。
longSentences.tip: 分词短语作伴随状语时可看作补充说明，先抓“总监拟定计划”这一主干。

items.title: A Graduation Ceremony by the Lake
items.passage:
Every June, our school holds its graduation ceremony beside the small lake on campus. This year the event began early in the morning, and by eight o'clock the sidewalk leading to the lawn was already crowded with students in their gowns. Parents walked slowly behind them, trying to find good seats before the music started. By the time the ceremony began, only a few seats near the front were still vacant.

The school had hired a photographer to get a group shot of every class before the speeches began. Teachers then read out the names of students whose grades had risen the most over the past year, and each one walked up to receive a small prize. The atmosphere was warm, and many students said it was the proudest morning of their school life.

Just as the principal stood up to give the closing speech, however, a strong wind began to blow across the lake, scattering papers everywhere. Rather than fight against the noise, she shortened her remarks and brought the ceremony to an abrupt ending. Even so, no one seemed to mind; students said the sudden finish only made the morning feel more memorable.
items.translation:
每年六月，我们学校都会在校园里那片小湖畔举行毕业典礼。今年活动一大早就开始了，到八点钟，通往草坪的人行道上已经挤满了身穿学位袍的学生。家长们跟在他们身后慢慢走着，想在典礼开始前找到好座位。等到典礼开始时，前排只剩下几个座位还空着。

学校请了一位摄影师，在演讲开始前为每个班级拍一张合影。接着老师宣读了过去一年里成绩进步最大的学生名单，每个人都走上台领取一份小奖品。现场气氛很温馨，许多学生说这是他们求学生涯中最自豪的一个早晨。

就在校长起身致闭幕演讲时，一阵大风突然从湖面吹来，把纸张吹得四处飞散。她没有硬和噪音较劲，而是缩短了讲话内容，让典礼以一种略显突然的方式结束了。尽管如此，似乎没有人介意；学生们说，这个突如其来的收尾反而让这个早晨更加令人难忘。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A high school's plan to build a new lake on campus
A graduation ceremony that ended earlier than planned because of wind
A photographer's career at a local school
The reasons why students' grades improved sharply this year
questions.explain: 全文主要讲述一场因大风而提前、仓促结束的毕业典礼，依据全文尤其第三段，故选B。

questions.q: According to the passage, why were only a few seats vacant when the ceremony began?
options: The school had removed most of the chairs
Many parents and students had already arrived early to find seats
The ceremony started later than expected
A storm kept most people away
questions.explain: 依据第一段，家长和学生早早到场寻找座位，所以典礼开始时前排只剩少数空位，选B。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the passage that the principal ________.
options: refused to give a speech at all
was unprepared for the ceremony
adapted quickly when the weather turned bad
was upset that the ceremony had to end
questions.explain: 第三段写校长面对大风从容缩短讲话，可推断她能灵活应变，而非拒绝演讲或慌乱、生气，选C。

questions.q: In the last paragraph, the word "abrupt" most nearly means ________.
options: careful and well-planned
sudden and unexpected
long and detailed
quiet and peaceful
questions.explain: 结合语境“大风突然吹来，校长缩短讲话”，abrupt ending指“突然、仓促的结束”，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: This year the event began early in the morning, and by eight o'clock the sidewalk leading to the lawn was already crowded with students in their gowns.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列句：the event began early in the morning，and the sidewalk was already crowded with students
longSentences.modifiers: “leading to the lawn”是现在分词短语作定语修饰sidewalk；“in their gowns”是介词短语作后置定语修饰students
longSentences.translation: 今年，活动一大早就开始了，到八点钟，通往草坪的人行道上已经挤满了身穿学位袍的学生。
longSentences.tip: 遇到and连接的两个分句，先分别找出各自主谓宾，再看分词短语和介词短语补充的细节信息。

longSentences.sentence: Just as the principal stood up to give the closing speech, however, a strong wind began to blow across the lake, scattering papers everywhere.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：a strong wind began to blow across the lake
longSentences.modifiers: “Just as the principal stood up to give the closing speech”是时间状语从句，位于句首；“however”是插入语表转折；“scattering papers everywhere”是现在分词短语作伴随状语
longSentences.translation: 就在校长起身致闭幕演讲时，一阵大风突然从湖面吹来，把纸张吹得四处飞散。
longSentences.tip: 先找主句的主谓（wind began to blow），时间状语从句和分词状语都是补充说明，理解主干后再回头看细节。

longSentences.sentence: Rather than fight against the noise, she shortened her remarks and brought the ceremony to an abrupt ending.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：she shortened her remarks and brought the ceremony to an abrupt ending（并列谓语）
longSentences.modifiers: “Rather than fight against the noise”是介词短语作方式状语，表示“没有……而是……”
longSentences.translation: 她没有硬和噪音较劲，而是缩短了讲话内容，让典礼以一种略显突然的方式结束了。
longSentences.tip: “rather than+动词原形”结构常表示“没有做前者，而是做后者”，注意与后面的谓语动词保持逻辑对比。

items.title: How One Shop Fixed Its Carbon Reports
items.passage:
In recent years, many online shops have begun publishing yearly reports on how much carbon dioxide their deliveries produce. At first, such reports were often vague, offering rough estimates rather than exact numbers, and customers found it hard to trust figures that seemed to shift from one year to the next.

One well-known retailer faced this problem after an incident in which journalists questioned the accuracy of its report. Instead of defending the old numbers, the company announced a pause in publishing new data so that its team could digest customer complaints and rebuild the system step by step. Rather than blaming shoppers for the mistake, managers took a gentle approach, asking staff for patience while the new method was tested.

The report released the following year proved far more careful: each copy included a clear, step-by-step account of emissions from factory to shop shelf, and the numbers matched independent checks. The latter version earned back much of the public's trust. Whether such honesty will spread to shops across the globe is still unclear, but this company's experience suggests that admitting a mistake can, in time, be less costly than hiding one.
items.translation:
近年来，许多网络商店开始发布年度报告，说明其配送业务产生了多少二氧化碳。起初，这类报告往往含糊不清，只给出粗略的估计而非确切数字，顾客们发现很难相信那些逐年变化不定的数据。

一家知名零售商在一起事件之后正面遭遇了这一问题：记者们质疑该公司报告的准确性。公司没有为旧数据辩护，而是宣布暂停发布新数据，以便团队消化顾客的投诉，并一步步重建整个体系。管理层没有责怪购物者的过失，而是采取了温和的做法，请员工耐心等待新方法接受测试。

次年发布的报告要严谨得多：每一份报告副本都包含了从工厂到店铺货架各环节排放情况的详细说明，且数据与独立核查结果相符。后一版报告重新赢得了公众的大部分信任。这种诚实做法能否推广到全球各地的商店，目前仍不确定，但这家公司的经历表明，承认错误从长远看往往比隐瞒错误代价更小。

questions.q: What does the passage mainly discuss?
options: Why online shopping harms the environment
How a retailer improved the accuracy of its carbon dioxide reports after criticism
The history of carbon dioxide research
A new law requiring shops to publish sales figures
questions.explain: 全文围绕一家零售商因数据不准确遭质疑，进而改进碳排放报告的过程展开，依据第二、三段，选B。

questions.q: Why did the company announce a pause in publishing new data?
options: Government regulators forced it to stop
It wanted to save money on reporting
It needed time to address complaints and rebuild its method
Customers stopped buying its products
questions.explain: 第二段提到公司宣布暂停是为了让团队消化投诉并一步步重建体系，故选C。

questions.q: It can be inferred that the new report published a year later ________.
options: contained even less detail than before
was checked and found to be more reliable
was ignored by the public
blamed customers for the earlier mistake
questions.explain: 第三段说明新报告更严谨，且数据与独立核查结果相符，重新赢得公众信任，说明它更可靠，故选B。

questions.q: In the first paragraph, "vague" is closest in meaning to ________.
options: exact and detailed
unclear and imprecise
exciting and new
expensive and rare
questions.explain: 上下文提到报告“只给出粗略估计而非确切数字”，说明vague意为“含糊不清的”，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: One well-known retailer faced this problem after an incident in which journalists questioned the accuracy of its report.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：One well-known retailer faced this problem
longSentences.modifiers: “after an incident”是介词短语作时间状语；“in which journalists questioned the accuracy of its report”是定语从句修饰incident
longSentences.translation: 一家知名零售商在一起事件之后正面遭遇了这一问题，当时记者们质疑该公司报告的准确性。
longSentences.tip: “in which”引导的定语从句常见于书面语，可把从句还原为完整句子理解：journalists questioned the accuracy of its report in that incident。

longSentences.sentence: Instead of defending the old numbers, the company announced a pause in publishing new data so that its team could digest customer complaints and rebuild the system step by step.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：the company announced a pause in publishing new data
longSentences.modifiers: “Instead of defending the old numbers”是介词短语作方式状语；“so that its team could digest customer complaints and rebuild the system step by step”是目的状语从句
longSentences.translation: 公司没有为旧数据辩护，而是宣布暂停发布新数据，以便团队消化顾客投诉，一步步重建整个体系。
longSentences.tip: “so that”引导目的状语从句时，先找主句动作，再看从句说明“为了什么目的”。

longSentences.sentence: Whether such honesty will spread to shops across the globe is still unclear, but this company's experience suggests that admitting a mistake can, in time, be less costly than hiding one.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列句：Whether...is still unclear，but experience suggests that...
longSentences.modifiers: “Whether such honesty will spread to shops across the globe”是主语从句；“that admitting a mistake can...be less costly than hiding one”是宾语从句；“in time”是插入的时间状语
longSentences.translation: 这种诚实做法能否推广到全球各地的商店，目前仍不确定，但这家公司的经历表明，承认错误从长远看往往比隐瞒错误代价更小。
longSentences.tip: 主语从句常以whether/that开头占据主语位置，先跳过从句内部细节，抓住“从句整体作主语”这一结构。

items.title: Choosing a University Major Wisely
items.passage:
Every spring, thousands of grown-up teenagers face one of the biggest decisions of their young lives: which subject to study at university. Guidance counselors often ask students to list a personal objective before browsing course catalogues, but many eighteen-year-olds still feel they lack a clear one.

Enrollment data from several universities show that interest in some fields, such as certain branches of sociology, has fallen quite sharply over the past decade, while demand for majors linked to data and design keeps rising. Researchers warn that making a premature choice, before a student has had any real experience of the field, is one of the most common reasons for switching majors later. A short internship or even a single volunteer engagement with a local project can offer a valuable clue about whether a subject truly fits a student's interests.

In the end, no single element of a decision, whether salary, family opinion, or personal passion, should be allowed to outweigh all the others. Universities that build real-world experience into the first year, rather than leaving it until graduation, tend to see students commit to their majors with far more confidence, and far less regret.
items.translation:
每年春天，成千上万即将成年的青少年都要面对他们年轻生命中最重大的决定之一：上大学该学什么专业。升学指导老师常常要求学生在浏览课程目录之前先列出一个个人目标，但许多十八岁的学生仍然觉得自己并没有明确的目标。

多所大学的招生数据显示，某些领域的兴趣——比如社会学的某些分支——在过去十年里下降得相当厉害，而与数据和设计相关的专业需求却在不断上升。研究者提醒，在学生对该领域尚无任何真实体验之前就仓促做出选择，是日后转专业最常见的原因之一。哪怕只是一次短期实习，或是一次参与本地项目的志愿活动，都能提供有价值的线索，帮助判断某个专业是否真正适合自己的兴趣。

归根结底，无论是薪资、家人的意见，还是个人的热情，都不应该成为压倒其他因素的唯一要素。那些把真实世界的实践经验安排在大一而非拖到毕业前的大学，往往能让学生带着更多的信心投入所选专业，也更少事后后悔。

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Sociology is losing popularity among university students
Students should choose a major based only on salary
Choosing a university major wisely requires real experience, not a rushed decision
Universities should stop offering guidance counseling
questions.explain: 全文主旨是选专业应基于真实体验、避免仓促决定，第二、三段均支持此观点，故选C。

questions.q: According to the second paragraph, what has fallen sharply over the past decade?
options: Demand for majors related to data and design
Interest in certain branches of sociology
The number of guidance counselors
University tuition fees
questions.explain: 第二段明确指出某些社会学分支的兴趣在过去十年下降得相当厉害，而数据和设计类专业需求是上升的，故选B。

questions.q: What can be inferred about students who choose a major without any real experience of the field?
options: They are more likely to switch majors later
They always achieve higher grades
They tend to become guidance counselors
They save more money on tuition
questions.explain: 第二段提到仓促选择常是日后转专业最常见的原因，可推断缺乏真实体验容易导致后续转专业，故选A。

questions.q: In the last paragraph, "outweigh" most likely means ________.
options: to weigh less than something else
to be more important than something else
to be measured accurately
to be completely ignored
questions.explain: 语境中“no single element…should be allowed to outweigh all the others”意为任何单一因素都不应压倒其他因素，故outweigh意为“比……更重要”，选B。

longSentences.sentence: Guidance counselors often ask students to list a personal objective before browsing course catalogues, but many eighteen-year-olds still feel they lack a clear one.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列句：counselors ask students to list a personal objective，but eighteen-year-olds feel they lack a clear one
longSentences.modifiers: “before browsing course catalogues”是时间状语；“they lack a clear one”是feel的宾语从句（省略that）
longSentences.translation: 升学指导老师常常要求学生在浏览课程目录之前先列出一个个人目标，但许多十八岁的学生仍然觉得自己并没有明确的目标。
longSentences.tip: 遇到but连接的两个分句，先分别理清各自主谓宾，再体会前后语义的对比转折。

longSentences.sentence: Researchers warn that making a premature choice, before a student has had any real experience of the field, is one of the most common reasons for switching majors later.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Researchers warn that [making a premature choice] is one of the most common reasons for switching majors later
longSentences.modifiers: “that...”是宾语从句，从句主语是动名词短语making a premature choice；“before a student has had any real experience of the field”是插入的时间状语从句，打断了从句的主谓
longSentences.translation: 研究者提醒，在学生对该领域尚无任何真实体验之前就仓促做出选择，是日后转专业最常见的原因之一。
longSentences.tip: 遇到插入语打断主谓时，先把逗号之间的插入部分暂时去掉，找到真正的主谓结构，再补回插入信息。

longSentences.sentence: Universities that build real-world experience into the first year, rather than leaving it until graduation, tend to see students commit to their majors with far more confidence, and far less regret.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Universities...tend to see students commit to their majors with far more confidence, and far less regret
longSentences.modifiers: “that build real-world experience into the first year”是定语从句修饰Universities；“rather than leaving it until graduation”是插入的对比状语
longSentences.translation: 那些把真实世界的实践经验安排在大一而非拖到毕业前的大学，往往能让学生带着更多的信心投入所选专业，也更少事后后悔。
longSentences.tip: 主语Universities后面紧跟较长的定语从句，注意先跳过从句找谓语tend to see，避免被中间成分绕晕。

items.title: Bridging the Gap at Sunday Lunch
items.passage:
Every Sunday, my grandmother insists that the whole family gather for a proper luncheon, even though most of us would rather grab something quick and get back to our own rooms. She still remembers years of hardship before the family achieved any real prosperity, and for her, a shared meal is proof that things have finally gotten better.

Yet the gap between her generation and ours often shows itself at the table. When my younger cousin quietly opens a browser on his phone to check messages, my grandmother frowns and mutters that conversation these days feels stale compared with the lively talk of her childhood. My father, who has long been the sole person managing the household bills, tries to keep the peace, gently reminding everyone that the heating and the air conditioner alone cost more each month than her entire salary once did.

Still, as a precaution against real conflict, we have learned a few small rules: phones stay in pockets until dessert, and no one is allowed to undo grandmother's seating plan. It is not always comfortable, but nobody wants to lose the sense of belonging that only these old-fashioned lunches still provide.
items.translation:
每逢星期天，祖母都坚持要全家人聚在一起吃一顿正式的午宴，尽管我们大多数人宁愿随便吃点东西就回自己房间。她仍然记得家里富裕起来之前那些艰难的岁月，对她而言，一顿团聚的饭是家境终于好转的证明。

然而，她那一代人与我们之间的代沟常常在餐桌上显现出来。当年幼的表弟悄悄打开手机浏览器查看消息时，祖母会皱起眉头，嘟囔说如今的谈话比起她小时候热闹的交谈显得索然无味。父亲一直是负责操持家里账单的唯一一人，他总是努力维持和气，温和地提醒大家，光是取暖和空调这两项，每个月的花费就比祖母当年的全部工资还要高。

不过，作为避免真正冲突的一种预防措施，我们也定下了几条小规矩：手机要放进口袋，直到上甜点才能拿出来；而且任何人都不许打乱祖母安排好的座位表。这样的聚会并不总是让人自在，但没有人愿意失去这种只有这些老式的家庭聚餐才能给予的归属感。

keyWords.zh: n. 午宴；正式的午餐会

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A family's disagreement over household expenses
How a family manages the generation gap during Sunday lunches
The history of a family recipe
Why grandmothers should not use smartphones
questions.explain: 全文围绕周日家庭午宴中显现的代沟以及一家人如何应对展开，依据第二、三段，故选B。

questions.q: According to the passage, what does the grandmother do when her grandson uses his phone at the table?
options: She takes the phone away immediately
She frowns and complains that conversation feels stale
She leaves the table without finishing her meal
She asks him to teach her how to use it
questions.explain: 第二段写道祖母皱起眉头，嘟囔说如今的谈话显得索然无味，对应B项。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the passage that the family's small rules at the table are meant to ________.
options: punish the younger cousin for using his phone
save money on the household bills
prevent real conflict while keeping the family together
impress guests who visit on Sundays
questions.explain: 第三段开头明确说这些规矩是“as a precaution against real conflict”，目的是避免真正冲突并维系家庭团聚，故选C。

questions.q: In the second paragraph, the word "sole" is closest in meaning to ________.
options: shared by everyone
only one, with no others
temporary and changing
unimportant
questions.explain: 上下文“the sole person managing the household bills”指父亲是唯一负责账单的人，故sole意为“唯一的”，选B。

longSentences.sentence: Every Sunday, my grandmother insists that the whole family gather for a proper luncheon, even though most of us would rather grab something quick and get back to our own rooms.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：my grandmother insists that the whole family gather for a proper luncheon
longSentences.modifiers: “that the whole family gather for a proper luncheon”是insist后接的宾语从句（虚拟语气，从句谓语用动词原形gather）；“even though most of us would rather grab something quick and get back to our own rooms”是让步状语从句
longSentences.translation: 每逢星期天，祖母都坚持要全家人聚在一起吃一顿正式的午宴，尽管我们大多数人宁愿随便吃点东西就回自己房间。
longSentences.tip: insist that后接的宾语从句若表示“坚持要求”，从句谓语用动词原形（如gather），这是虚拟语气的固定用法，要特别留意。

longSentences.sentence: My father, who has long been the sole person managing the household bills, tries to keep the peace, gently reminding everyone that the heating and the air conditioner alone cost more each month than her entire salary once did.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：My father...tries to keep the peace
longSentences.modifiers: “who has long been the sole person managing the household bills”是非限制性定语从句，插入说明父亲身份；“gently reminding everyone that...”是现在分词短语作伴随状语；其中的that从句是reminding的宾语从句，内含比较结构
longSentences.translation: 父亲一直是负责操持家里账单的唯一一人，他总是努力维持和气，温和地提醒大家，光是取暖和空调这两项，每个月的花费就比祖母当年的全部工资还要高。
longSentences.tip: 主语和谓语之间插入了较长的非限制性定语从句（逗号隔开），阅读时可先跳过从句抓主干：My father tries to keep the peace，再回头理解插入信息。

longSentences.sentence: It is not always comfortable, but nobody wants to lose the sense of belonging that only these old-fashioned lunches still provide.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列句：It is not always comfortable，but nobody wants to lose the sense of belonging
longSentences.modifiers: “that only these old-fashioned lunches still provide”是定语从句修饰the sense of belonging
longSentences.translation: 这样的聚会并不总是让人自在，但没有人愿意失去这种只有这些老式的家庭聚餐才能给予的归属感。
longSentences.tip: 定语从句中的only修饰整个从句谓语部分，翻译时可处理为“只有……才”的强调结构，更符合中文表达习惯。

items.title: A Family of Different Paths
items.passage:
In the Chen family, three generations share one house, and each member has found a different path in life. Grandfather, a retired doctor, still keeps his old medicine cabinet by the door, ready to help neighbors with minor complaints. His son, David, teaches mathematics at the local middle school, while David's wife works part-time as a volunteer at a community garden, spending her afternoons on projects related to environmental conservation.

Their daughter, Amy, is a lively teenager with a mind of her own. Rather than following the latest fashion trend, she prefers a simple blouse and an old overcoat passed down from her mother, insisting that casual clothes suit her personality better than any expensive purchase. She carries a portable speaker everywhere, filling the house with music while she studies, and her parents have learned not to complain, since her grades remain excellent.

Every weekend, the family takes part in voluntary clean-up work along the river near their home. Grandfather says these shared activities keep everyone's brain active and their bond strong, reminding the younger generation that family life is not only about individual success but also about caring for the community and the world around them.
items.translation:
陈家三代同堂，家中每个成员都走出了不同的人生道路。祖父曾是一名医生，如今退休后仍把旧药箱放在门口，随时准备帮邻居处理些小毛病。他的儿子戴维在当地一所中学教数学，戴维的妻子则每天下午在社区花园做志愿者，参与与环境保护相关的项目。

他们的女儿艾米是个活泼有主见的少女。她不追逐最新的流行款式，而是喜欢穿简单的衬衫，套上母亲传下来的旧大衣，坚持认为随意的穿着比昂贵的购物更适合自己的性格。她走到哪儿都带着一个便携式音箱，学习时家里满是音乐，父母也渐渐不再抱怨，因为她的成绩始终名列前茅。

每逢周末，全家都会参加在家附近河边举行的志愿清洁活动。祖父说，这些共同参与的活动能让每个人的大脑保持活跃，也让家人之间的感情更加牢固，提醒年轻一代：家庭生活不仅关乎个人成就，也在于关心社区与周围的世界。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: How different family members lead varied yet connected lives while staying close
How to become a doctor in modern China
The disadvantages of buying expensive clothes
The history of environmental conservation movements
questions.explain: 文章通过陈家三代人的经历，展现家庭成员虽走上不同道路，却依然保持紧密联系，这是全文（第一、二、三段）共同的主旨，故选A。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, why does Amy prefer simple clothes?
options: She cannot afford expensive brands
She believes casual clothes suit her personality better than expensive purchases
Her parents forbid her from buying new clothes
She lost her favorite blouse
questions.explain: 第二段明确指出艾米“坚持认为随意的穿着比昂贵的购物更适合自己的性格”，故B正确，其余选项文中均未提及。

questions.q: What can be inferred about grandfather's attitude toward the family's weekend activities?
options: He believes they only waste time
He sees them as meaningless once the children are grown
He values them for keeping the family bonded and active
He prefers doing volunteer work alone
questions.explain: 第三段祖父强调这些集体活动能让大脑保持活跃、让家人感情更牢固，可推知他重视这些活动对家庭凝聚力的作用，故C正确。

questions.q: In paragraph 1, the word "voluntary" is closest in meaning to which of the following?
options: forced
paid
done willingly, without being forced
accidental
questions.explain: voluntary意为“自愿的，非强制进行的”，第一段中戴维的妻子做志愿者正是自愿参与的行为，故C正确。

longSentences.sentence: Rather than following the latest fashion trend, she prefers a simple blouse and an old overcoat passed down from her mother, insisting that casual clothes suit her personality better than any expensive purchase.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：she prefers a simple blouse and an old overcoat（她更喜欢简单的衬衫和一件旧大衣）
longSentences.modifiers: Rather than...为方式状语，与追逐潮流形成对比；passed down from her mother为过去分词短语作后置定语修饰overcoat；insisting that...为现在分词短语作伴随状语，内含that引导的宾语从句。
longSentences.translation: 她不追逐最新的流行款式，而是喜欢穿简单的衬衫，套上母亲传下来的旧大衣，坚持认为随意的穿着比昂贵的购物更适合自己的性格。
longSentences.tip: 遇到分词短语作定语或状语时，先划出逗号锁定主干动词prefers，再逐一还原每个分词短语的修饰对象。

longSentences.sentence: Grandfather says these shared activities keep everyone's brain active and their bond strong, reminding the younger generation that family life is not only about individual success but also about caring for the community and the world around them.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Grandfather says (that) these shared activities keep...active and...strong（祖父说这些活动让……保持活跃/牢固）
longSentences.modifiers: says后省略that引导宾语从句；keep+宾语+形容词结构中两组宾补并列（brain active / bond strong）；reminding...为现在分词短语作结果状语，内含that宾语从句及not only...but also并列结构。
longSentences.translation: 祖父说，这些共同参与的活动能让每个人的大脑保持活跃，也让家人之间的感情更加牢固，提醒年轻一代：家庭生活不仅关乎个人成就，也在于关心社区与周围的世界。
longSentences.tip: 看到keep+宾语+形容词结构时先确认并列的两组宾补，再处理句末分词状语引出的从句。

items.title: The Science Behind Regular Exercise
items.passage:
The Olympic Games trace their roots back to ancient Greece, where Greek athletes gathered every four years to test their strength and speed. At its peak, the ancient festival was so significant that wars were sometimes paused so competitors could travel safely. Records also mention how the organizers made careful provision for food, water, and shelter, since a sudden outbreak of illness among the crowded spectators could ruin the entire event.

Centuries later, a British research team studying university athletes found that regular exercise brings tremendous benefits beyond physical strength. Running, swimming, and even a simple yoga pose held for a few minutes were shown to relieve stress and sharpen cognitive skills such as memory and attention. The scientists noted that people who trained consistently, rather than making hasty, unplanned attempts, were far more likely to reach their personal peak performance.

I once overheard my coach telling a new teammate that success in sport rarely comes from a single tremendous effort; it comes from small, steady habits. Ever since, I have kept my running shoes and towel in a separate compartment of my bag, packed and ready before every session, treating each workout as a quiet way to purify both body and mind.
items.translation:
奥运会的历史可以追溯到古希腊，那时希腊的运动员们每四年聚集一次，比拼力量与速度。在鼎盛时期，这项古老的盛会意义重大，以至于战争有时会暂停，好让参赛者能够安全前往赛场。史料还记载，组织者会为食物、饮水和住所做周密的准备，因为拥挤的观众中一旦突然爆发疾病，整个盛会都可能因此毁于一旦。

几个世纪后，一支英国研究团队在对大学生运动员的研究中发现，规律运动带来的益处是巨大的，远不止强健体魄本身。跑步、游泳，甚至只是保持一个简单的瑜伽姿势几分钟，都被证实能够缓解压力，提升记忆力和注意力等认知能力。科学家指出，那些坚持系统训练、而非仓促随意尝试的人，更有可能达到自己的巅峰状态。

我曾无意中听到教练对一位新队员说，运动中的成功很少来自一次巨大的努力，而是源于日积月累的小习惯。从那以后，我总把跑鞋和毛巾放进包里单独的一个隔层，训练前提前收拾好，把每一次训练都当作净化身心的一种宁静方式。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The history and modern scientific benefits of regular exercise
How to organize a gym bag efficiently
The rules of ancient Greek Olympic wrestling
Why British athletes are the fastest runners
questions.explain: 全文先介绍古希腊奥运会的起源，再讲述现代科学证实运动对认知和情绪的益处，最后以个人经历总结坚持锻炼的意义，主旨对应全文结构，故选A。

questions.q: According to paragraph 1, why did ancient organizers make provision for food, water, and shelter?
options: To increase ticket sales
Because a disease outbreak among the crowded spectators could ruin the event
To attract more foreign kings
Because Greek law required it
questions.explain: 第一段指出，拥挤观众中一旦突发疾病（outbreak of illness），整个盛会都可能因此毁于一旦，这正是组织者周密准备的原因，故B正确。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the coach's advice mentioned in paragraph 3?
options: Long-term consistency matters more than one great effort
New teammates should avoid competing
Yoga poses are more effective than running
Ancient training methods are outdated
questions.explain: 第三段中教练所说“成功很少来自一次巨大的努力，而是源于日积月累的小习惯”，说明长期坚持比一时的努力更重要，故A正确。

questions.q: In paragraph 2, "hasty" is closest in meaning to:
options: careful and well-planned
rushed and poorly considered
confident and experienced
slow and steady
questions.explain: hasty意为“仓促的、草率的”，文中与“坚持系统训练”形成对比，故B（匆忙且欠考虑）正确。

longSentences.sentence: At its peak, the ancient festival was so significant that wars were sometimes paused so competitors could travel safely.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：the ancient festival was so significant that wars were sometimes paused（这个古老节日意义重大，以至于战争会暂停）
longSentences.modifiers: At its peak为时间状语；so...that引导结果状语从句；so competitors could travel safely为so that引导的目的状语从句，说明暂停战争的目的。
longSentences.translation: 在鼎盛时期，这项古老的盛会意义重大，以至于战争有时会暂停，好让参赛者能够安全前往赛场。
longSentences.tip: 遇到so...that结构先找出程度形容词，再看清后面是结果从句还是紧跟的目的状语，两者可连用但功能不同。

longSentences.sentence: Records also mention how the organizers made careful provision for food, water, and shelter, since a sudden outbreak of illness among the crowded spectators could ruin the entire event.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Records mention how the organizers made careful provision（史料记载组织者如何做了周密准备）
longSentences.modifiers: how引导宾语从句作mention的宾语；since引导原因状语从句，说明做准备的原因。
longSentences.translation: 史料还记载，组织者会为食物、饮水和住所做周密的准备，因为拥挤的观众中一旦突然爆发疾病，整个盛会都可能因此毁于一旦。
longSentences.tip: 含疑问词引导的宾语从句常与since/because等原因状语从句连用，先分清宾语从句与状语从句的边界。

longSentences.sentence: The scientists noted that people who trained consistently, rather than making hasty, unplanned attempts, were far more likely to reach their personal peak performance.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：The scientists noted that people were far more likely to reach their peak performance（科学家指出人们更有可能达到巅峰状态）
longSentences.modifiers: that引导宾语从句；who trained consistently为定语从句修饰people；rather than making...为插入的介词短语作对比状语。
longSentences.translation: 科学家指出，那些坚持系统训练、而非仓促随意尝试的人，更有可能达到自己的巅峰状态。
longSentences.tip: 定语从句和插入语常打断主谓之间的连贯，先跳过插入成分抓住主句主谓，再回头理解修饰内容。

items.title: The Rise of Aerial Delivery
items.passage:
Aerial delivery drones, once dismissed as a science-fiction fantasy, now precede traditional trucks in delivering small packages across several cities. In pilot programs launched last year, delivery times using drones exceeded even the most optimistic engineering forecasts, cutting average waiting periods from hours to minutes.

Building a reliable delivery drone, however, is far from a trivial task. Engineers must constantly add and subtract variables — wind speed, battery life, air traffic — while the aircraft is still in flight, adjusting its route hundreds of times per minute. When the technology was first proposed, many transportation officials remained dubious, worried that drones sharing airspace with regular flights might create unnecessary danger. Strict safety regulations were therefore introduced before any company received permission to operate.

Today, though, aerial delivery is steadily becoming the norm rather than the exception. Customers order everything from medicine to a favorite paperback novel and expect it to arrive within the hour. What seemed an unrealistic idea only a decade ago has quietly reshaped how goods move through modern cities, showing how quickly innovation can turn a dubious experiment into everyday convenience.
items.translation:
航空配送无人机曾一度被视为科幻幻想，如今却在多个城市率先于传统卡车完成小件包裹的配送。在去年启动的试点项目中，无人机的配送速度超出了工程师最乐观的预测，把原本数小时的等待时间缩短到几分钟。

然而，打造一款可靠的配送无人机绝非一件小事。工程师必须在飞行器仍处于飞行状态时，不断增减风速、电池续航、空中交通等各项变量，每分钟调整航线数百次。这项技术最初被提出时，许多交通部门官员仍心存疑虑，担心无人机与普通航班共用空域会带来不必要的危险。因此，在任何公司获准运营之前，都先出台了严格的安全规定。

如今，航空配送正逐渐从例外变成常态。顾客可以订购从药品到心爱的平装小说等各种物品，并期待一小时内送达。十年前还显得不切实际的想法，如今已悄然改变了现代城市中货物流转的方式，这也说明创新可以多么迅速地把一场令人怀疑的实验，变成日常生活中的便利。

keyWords.zh: a. 半信半疑的，可疑的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: How aerial delivery drones evolved from a doubtful idea into common practice
Why traditional trucks are safer than drones
The history of paperback publishing
New government regulations banning drone flights
questions.explain: 全文围绕航空配送无人机从被质疑到逐渐成为行业常态展开，主旨为技术从存疑走向普及的过程，对应全文结构，故选A。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, why did officials introduce strict safety regulations?
options: Customers complained about late deliveries
They worried drones sharing airspace with regular flights could be dangerous
Battery technology was too expensive
Paperback deliveries were often damaged
questions.explain: 第二段说明官员担心无人机与普通航班共用空域会带来危险，因此才出台严格安全规定，故B正确。

questions.q: What can be inferred about public attitudes toward delivery drones over time?
options: They have remained completely doubtful throughout
They shifted from skepticism to acceptance as the technology proved reliable
They were positive from the very beginning
They have not changed at all
questions.explain: 从第二段的“心存疑虑”到第三段“逐渐成为常态”，可推知公众态度经历了从怀疑到接受的转变，故B正确。

questions.q: In paragraph 2, "dubious" most nearly means:
options: enthusiastic and supportive
doubtful and uncertain
confident and certain
indifferent and careless
questions.explain: dubious意为“半信半疑的，心存疑虑的”，第二段官员对新技术安全性表示担忧，与B项（doubtful and uncertain）含义一致。

longSentences.sentence: Aerial delivery drones, once dismissed as a science-fiction fantasy, now precede traditional trucks in delivering small packages across several cities.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Aerial delivery drones now precede traditional trucks（航空配送无人机如今领先于传统卡车）
longSentences.modifiers: once dismissed as a science-fiction fantasy为过去分词短语作插入语，补充说明主语曾经的处境；in delivering...为介词短语作状语说明领先的方面。
longSentences.translation: 航空配送无人机曾一度被视为科幻幻想，如今却在多个城市率先于传统卡车完成小件包裹的配送。
longSentences.tip: 两个逗号之间的分词短语通常是插入的补充说明，可先跳过找主谓，再回头理解其含义。

longSentences.sentence: Engineers must constantly add and subtract variables — wind speed, battery life, air traffic — while the aircraft is still in flight, adjusting its route hundreds of times per minute.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Engineers must constantly add and subtract variables（工程师必须不断增减各项变量）
longSentences.modifiers: 破折号之间的内容为variables的同位语，列举具体变量；while引导时间状语从句；adjusting...为现在分词短语作伴随状语。
longSentences.translation: 工程师必须在飞行器仍处于飞行状态时，不断增减风速、电池续航、空中交通等各项变量，每分钟调整航线数百次。
longSentences.tip: 破折号中的内容通常是对前面名词的举例说明，可先略过快速定位句子主干。

longSentences.sentence: When the technology was first proposed, many transportation officials remained dubious, worried that drones sharing airspace with regular flights might create unnecessary danger.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：many transportation officials remained dubious（许多交通官员仍心存疑虑）
longSentences.modifiers: When引导时间状语从句；worried that...为过去分词短语作伴随状语，内含that引导的宾语从句，从句主语drones后接现在分词短语sharing...作后置定语。
longSentences.translation: 这项技术最初被提出时，许多交通部门官员仍心存疑虑，担心无人机与普通航班共用空域会带来不必要的危险。
longSentences.tip: 分词短语作状语时先看清逻辑主语是否与主句主语一致，再逐层拆解其内部从句结构。

items.title: How Culture Crosses Borders
items.passage:
Eastern culture has spread across the world in ways few people could have imagined a generation ago. Traditional music, ancient astronomy, and even rural farm festivals that once stayed within a single village are now shared instantly online, reaching audiences on the other side of the planet within seconds. Decades ago, when families still used a rotary dial telephone to reach relatives in the next town, such rapid exchange of culture would have seemed impossible.

One clear example is a young actress from an eastern country who recently gained international fame through a streaming series. Fans abroad, eager to understand her culture, began trying to pronounce local phrases from her films, occasionally making a small slip but rarely feeling discouraged. Streaming platforms, aggressive in promoting original content, helped the show spread far beyond its home market, thus introducing millions of new viewers to customs and stories they had never encountered before.

Cultural exchange, of course, is never one-directional. As eastern traditions travel outward, local communities must decide how much of their heritage to share and how much to protect. Whatever they choose, one thing is certain: with each passing year, technology continues to shrink the time it takes for a single song, festival, or story to circle the globe.
items.translation:
东方文化在全球的传播方式，是一代人之前几乎难以想象的。曾经只留在一个村庄里的传统音乐、古老的天文学知识，甚至乡村农耕节庆，如今都能在网上瞬间被分享，几秒钟内就能传到地球另一端的观众面前。几十年前，当人们还要用老式的拨号电话联系邻镇亲戚时，如此迅速的文化交流是难以想象的。

一个典型的例子是，一位来自东方国家的年轻女演员最近凭借一部网络剧在国际上走红。海外粉丝渴望了解她的文化，开始尝试念出她影片中的当地词语，偶尔会读错，却很少因此气馁。各大流媒体平台大力推广原创内容，帮助这部剧的影响力远远超出本土市场，从而让数百万从未接触过这些习俗与故事的新观众认识了它们。

当然，文化交流从来都不是单向的。随着东方传统向外传播，当地社区也必须决定要分享多少、又要保留多少自己的文化遗产。无论他们做出怎样的选择，有一点是确定的：随着时间流逝，科技仍在不断缩短一首歌、一场节庆或一个故事传遍全球所需的时间。

keyWords.zh: a. 有进取心的，大力的
n. 拨号盘 v. 拨号

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: How technology has helped eastern culture spread more quickly around the world
The biography of a famous actress
The history of rotary telephones
Why farming festivals are disappearing
questions.explain: 全文讨论科技如何帮助东方文化（音乐、天文学、影视作品等）更快地传播到世界各地，主旨对应全文各段内容，故选A。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, how did streaming platforms contribute to the spread of the show?
options: By translating it into every language
By promoting the content aggressively
By hiring only local actors
By focusing solely on domestic audiences
questions.explain: 第二段提到流媒体平台“大力推广”（aggressive in promoting）原创内容，帮助该剧走出本土市场，故B正确。

questions.q: What can be inferred from paragraph 3 about local communities facing cultural exchange?
options: They have no choice but to share everything
They face a decision about how much heritage to share versus protect
They refuse to participate in cultural exchange
They no longer value their traditions
questions.explain: 第三段指出当地社区必须决定分享多少、保留多少文化遗产，说明他们面临取舍的抉择，故B正确。

questions.q: In paragraph 1, "spread" is closest in meaning to:
options: concentrated within one place
distributed or shared widely
hidden from public view
reduced in size
questions.explain: spread在文中表示文化“被广泛传播、分享”，与B项（distributed or shared widely）意思相符。

longSentences.sentence: Traditional music, ancient astronomy, and even rural farm festivals that once stayed within a single village are now shared instantly online, reaching audiences on the other side of the planet within seconds.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Traditional music, ancient astronomy, and...festivals are now shared instantly online（传统音乐、古老天文学和乡村节庆如今都在网上被即时分享）
longSentences.modifiers: that once stayed within a single village为定语从句修饰前面的并列主语；reaching audiences...为现在分词短语作结果状语。
longSentences.translation: 曾经只留在一个村庄里的传统音乐、古老的天文学知识，甚至乡村农耕节庆，如今都能在网上瞬间被分享，几秒钟内就能传到地球另一端的观众面前。
longSentences.tip: 并列主语后接定语从句时，先看从句动词的语义来判断修饰的是哪个成分，再确定句子主干。

longSentences.sentence: Fans abroad, eager to understand her culture, began trying to pronounce local phrases from her films, occasionally making a small slip but rarely feeling discouraged.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Fans abroad began trying to pronounce local phrases（海外粉丝开始尝试念出当地词语）
longSentences.modifiers: eager to understand her culture为插入的形容词短语作原因状语；occasionally making...but rarely feeling...为并列的现在分词短语作伴随状语。
longSentences.translation: 海外粉丝渴望了解她的文化，开始尝试念出她影片中的当地词语，偶尔会读错，却很少因此气馁。
longSentences.tip: 两个逗号间的形容词短语一般说明主语的状态或原因，可将其还原成一个原因状语从句来理解。

longSentences.sentence: Streaming platforms, aggressive in promoting original content, helped the show spread far beyond its home market, thus introducing millions of new viewers to customs and stories they had never encountered before.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：Streaming platforms helped the show spread far beyond its home market（流媒体平台帮助该剧的影响力远超本土市场）
longSentences.modifiers: aggressive in promoting original content为插入的形容词短语作状语，说明平台的方式；thus introducing...为现在分词短语作结果状语；they had never encountered before为省略关系代词that的定语从句修饰stories。
longSentences.translation: 各大流媒体平台大力推广原创内容，帮助这部剧的影响力远远超出本土市场，从而让数百万从未接触过这些习俗与故事的新观众认识了它们。
longSentences.tip: 省略关系代词的定语从句常紧跟在名词后面，遇到“名词+主语+谓语”结构要留意是否为省略了that/which的定语从句。

items.title: From Barn to Modern Family Home
items.passage:
On a quiet farm in the English countryside, an abandoned barn that had stood empty for decades is getting a second life. Instead of tearing it down, a young couple decided to turn the old stone building into their family home, keeping much of its original character while adding modern comforts.

The project was far from simple. Converting a barn built for animals and hay into a livable house required careful engineering, since the old timber frame had never been designed to carry insulated walls or a modern roof. Every delivery of stone and steel had to be planned weeks ahead, as narrow country lanes ruled out large trucks. The architect's interpretation of the structure guided each decision, exposing the rough beams instead of hiding them.

What kept the family going through delays and rising costs was sheer determination. Local planning officials worried at first that such an unusual design would not be acceptable, but once the work was finished, neighbors praised it as one of the most thoughtful conversions in the area. The barn's weathered exterior looks almost unchanged from the road, yet inside, the space has been wholly transformed into a bright, comfortable home.
items.translation:
在英格兰乡间一处僻静的农场上，一座荒废了数十年的谷仓正迎来新生。一对年轻夫妇没有选择将其拆除，而是决定把这座老旧的石头建筑改造成自己的家，在增添现代化设施的同时，保留了它原有的风貌。

这个项目远非易事。把一座原本用来饲养牲畜、堆放干草的谷仓改造成宜居住宅，需要精细的工程设计，因为老旧的木质框架从未被设计用来承受保温墙体或现代屋顶的重量。每一批石材和钢材的运送都必须提前数周安排，因为狭窄的乡间小路无法通行大型卡车。建筑师对原有结构的解读贯穿了每一个决定：外露的粗糙横梁没有被遮掩，反而成为设计的一部分。

支撑这家人熬过工期延误和成本上涨的，是他们坚定的决心。当地规划部门起初担心这种不同寻常的设计难以被接受，但工程完工后，邻居们纷纷称赞它是当地最用心的改造项目之一。从路边望去，谷仓风化的外墙几乎没有变化，但内部空间已经彻底改头换面，成为一处明亮舒适的家。

keyWords.zh: n. 外部；外表 a. 外部的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: An old barn being turned into a modern family home
The history of barns in the English countryside
Problems with rural transportation
How local officials regulate architecture
questions.explain: 全文（尤其第一段）讲述一对夫妇把废弃谷仓改造成家庭住宅的过程，主旨是A；B、C、D均只是文中局部细节，不能概括全文。

questions.q: Why was delivering materials difficult, according to the passage?
options: The couple could not afford enough materials
Narrow country lanes prevented large trucks from being used
The barn was too far from any road
Steel and stone were in short supply
questions.explain: 第二段明确指出“narrow country lanes ruled out large trucks”，即狭窄的乡间小路使大型卡车无法通行，对应B；其余选项文中未提及。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the architect's approach to the old barn?
options: The architect wanted to hide all signs of the barn's past
The architect preferred to demolish the timber frame
The architect chose to keep the barn's original features visible
The architect ignored the original structure entirely
questions.explain: 第二段说建筑师的解读“guided each decision, exposing the rough beams instead of hiding them”，说明其做法是保留而非掩盖原有结构，故选C。

questions.q: In the passage, the word "acceptable" most nearly means
options: profitable
satisfactory or approved
illegal
expensive
questions.explain: 第三段中官员担心设计“would not be acceptable”，结合上下文（后来邻居称赞该设计）可知acceptable意为“被认可、令人满意的”，对应B。

longSentences.sentence: Converting a barn built for animals and hay into a livable house required careful engineering, since the old timber frame had never been designed to carry insulated walls or a modern roof.
longSentences.skeleton: 动名词短语作主语（Converting...house）+ required + 宾语（careful engineering），since引导原因状语从句
longSentences.modifiers: “built for animals and hay”为过去分词短语，修饰barn；since从句中含被动语态“had never been designed to carry...”
longSentences.translation: 把一座原本用来饲养牲畜、堆放干草的谷仓改造成宜居住宅，需要精细的工程设计，因为老旧的木质框架从未被设计用来承受保温墙体或现代屋顶的重量。
longSentences.tip: 遇到动名词做主语的长句，先抓“Converting...house required engineering”这条主干，再看since从句补充的原因。

longSentences.sentence: Every delivery of stone and steel had to be planned weeks ahead, as narrow country lanes ruled out large trucks.
longSentences.skeleton: 主语every delivery + had to be planned（被动语态），as引导原因状语从句
longSentences.modifiers: “of stone and steel”介词短语修饰delivery；“weeks ahead”作时间状语；as从句补充原因
longSentences.translation: 每一批石材和钢材的运送都必须提前数周安排，因为狭窄的乡间小路无法通行大型卡车。
longSentences.tip: 先找出被动语态主干“delivery had to be planned”，再看as从句给出的具体原因。

longSentences.sentence: Local planning officials worried at first that such an unusual design would not be acceptable, but once the work was finished, neighbors praised it as one of the most thoughtful conversions in the area.
longSentences.skeleton: 分句一：officials worried + that宾语从句；but连接分句二：once时间状语从句 + neighbors praised it
longSentences.modifiers: “that such an unusual design would not be acceptable”为that宾语从句；“once the work was finished”为时间状语从句
longSentences.translation: 当地规划部门起初担心这种不同寻常的设计难以被接受，但工程完工后，邻居们纷纷称赞它是当地最用心的改造项目之一。
longSentences.tip: 抓住but前后两个分句的对比关系，分别处理that宾语从句和once时间状语从句即可理清全句。

items.title: Debating How to Regulate AI
items.passage:
Artificial intelligence is often described as the driving force behind a fourth industrial revolution, one that is reshaping how people work, communicate, and make decisions. At the outset, most conversations about the technology focused on its benefits: faster diagnoses in medicine, smarter traffic systems, more efficient factories. Few imagined how turbulent the public debate over AI would eventually become.

As the technology has advanced, however, new hazards have started to emerge. Automated systems can make biased decisions, spread false information, or threaten jobs that once seemed safe from machines. Much of the most powerful AI is controlled by a small number of companies, and critics warn that this gives a handful of firms a virtual monopoly over data and computing power, along with too much influence over the future. These companies have often proven resistant to outside oversight, arguing that strict rules would slow down useful innovation.

In response, a growing coalition of governments, researchers, and civil-society groups is pushing for international rules on how AI should be built and used. Reaching agreement, however, has been difficult: lawmakers are far from unanimous on how strict such rules should be, and different countries favor very different approaches. What is clear is that AI is no longer a narrow technical issue; it has become a central question for society as a whole.
items.translation:
人工智能常被认为是推动第四次工业革命的核心力量，正在重塑人们工作、沟通和决策的方式。在这项技术发展之初，人们讨论的大多是它带来的好处：更快的医学诊断、更智能的交通系统、更高效的工厂。当时很少有人能想到，围绕人工智能的公众讨论后来会变得如此动荡不安。

然而，随着技术不断进步，新的隐患开始显现。自动化系统可能做出带有偏见的决策，传播虚假信息，或威胁那些曾被认为不会被机器取代的工作岗位。目前最强大的人工智能大多掌握在少数几家公司手中，批评者警告说，这使得这些企业在数据和算力上形成了近乎垄断的地位，并对未来拥有过大的影响力。这些公司往往对外部监管持抵制态度，辩称严格的规则会拖慢有益的创新。

为此，越来越多的政府、研究人员和民间团体组成联盟，推动制定人工智能开发和使用的国际规则。然而，要达成共识并不容易：立法者在规则应有多严格这一问题上远未取得一致意见，不同国家也倾向于采取截然不同的做法。可以肯定的是，人工智能已不再只是一个狭窄的技术问题，而已成为整个社会需要面对的核心议题。

keyWords.zh: a. 骚乱的，混乱的；狂暴的
n. 垄断；垄断者；专卖权
a. 无异议的；全体一致的

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: AI development has been smooth and free of controversy
AI brings benefits but also raises hazards that are hard to regulate
Governments have already reached full agreement on AI rules
AI is only relevant to the medical field
questions.explain: 全文（尤其第二、三段）说明AI在带来便利的同时也产生了新的风险，而各方在监管上仍难达成一致，故B概括最全面。

questions.q: According to the passage, why are critics concerned about a small number of companies controlling AI?
options: These companies refuse to hire new employees
It gives them excessive influence over data, computing power, and the future
They have stopped investing in new technology
They are located in only one country
questions.explain: 第二段指出少数公司掌握AI会形成“a virtual monopoly over data and computing power, along with too much influence over the future”，对应B。

questions.q: What can be inferred about international efforts to regulate AI?
options: All countries fully agree on how strict the rules should be
No government has shown any interest in regulating AI
Reaching a common approach to regulation remains difficult
Regulation efforts have already been abandoned
questions.explain: 第三段提到“lawmakers are far from unanimous”“different countries favor very different approaches”，说明各国在监管上仍难达成共识，对应C。

questions.q: In the passage, "resistant to outside oversight" most likely means the companies
options: welcome external supervision
are unwilling to accept external supervision
have no connection to supervision
actively seek government control
questions.explain: 第二段中该短语紧接“arguing that strict rules would slow down useful innovation”，说明公司抵触外部监管，对应B。

longSentences.sentence: Artificial intelligence is often described as the driving force behind a fourth industrial revolution, one that is reshaping how people work, communicate, and make decisions.
longSentences.skeleton: 主语AI + is described as + 表语（the driving force...），“one that...”为非限制性定语从句补充说明revolution
longSentences.modifiers: “behind a fourth industrial revolution”介词短语修饰driving force；从句内部含how引导的宾语从句
longSentences.translation: 人工智能常被认为是推动第四次工业革命的核心力量，这场革命正在重塑人们工作、沟通和决策的方式。
longSentences.tip: 遇到“one that...”结构时，把它当作对前面名词的补充说明，先理清主句再看从句。

longSentences.sentence: Much of the most powerful AI is controlled by a small number of companies, and critics warn that this gives a handful of firms a virtual monopoly over data and computing power, along with too much influence over the future.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列句：分句一Much...is controlled by...；分句二critics warn + that宾语从句
longSentences.modifiers: “of the most powerful AI”修饰Much；that从句中“along with...”为并列成分
longSentences.translation: 目前最强大的人工智能大多掌握在少数几家公司手中，批评者警告说，这使得这些企业在数据和算力上形成了近乎垄断的地位，并对未来拥有过大的影响力。
longSentences.tip: and连接两个独立分句，先分别抓住各自主干，再理解critics warn后面的that宾语从句。

longSentences.sentence: Reaching agreement, however, has been difficult: lawmakers are far from unanimous on how strict such rules should be, and different countries favor very different approaches.
longSentences.skeleton: 动名词短语作主语（Reaching agreement）+ has been difficult，冒号后进行具体说明，and连接两个分句
longSentences.modifiers: “however”为插入语；“on how strict such rules should be”介词短语中嵌套how引导的从句
longSentences.translation: 然而，要达成共识并不容易：立法者在规则应有多严格这一问题上远未取得一致意见，不同国家也倾向于采取截然不同的做法。
longSentences.tip: 冒号后的内容是对“difficult”的具体解释，抓住两个由and并列的分句分别理解即可。

items.title: Caring for an Aging Population
items.passage:
Many countries are facing a rapidly aging population, and government expenditure on healthcare, pensions, and elderly care is rising faster than at any point in recent history. Policymakers are now under pressure to make decisive choices about how to support a growing number of older citizens without placing an unbearable burden on younger taxpayers.

In many communities, free shuttle buses now carry elderly residents to hospitals, markets, and social centers, helping those who can no longer drive stay connected to daily life. Some seniors also choose to lease smaller, easier-to-manage apartments. Traditional expectations that children would always live nearby and provide care are increasingly incompatible with modern life, since many adult children now work in distant cities. As a result, it has become unlikely that families alone can meet every need, and professional caregivers are stepping in to complement family support. For elderly people without close relatives, courts sometimes appoint a legal guardian to manage their affairs.

Experts agree that today's decisions will shape how well societies cope with aging in the coming decades. Without decisive investment in elderly services now, later expenditure could rise even further, straining public budgets already stretched thin. Building a system that combines family care, community support, and professional services may be the most realistic way forward.
items.translation:
许多国家正面临人口迅速老龄化的问题，政府在医疗、养老金和老年照护方面的支出增长速度已超过近年来任何时期。政策制定者如今承受着压力，必须就如何在不给年轻纳税人带来过重负担的前提下，为日益增多的老年人口提供支持做出果断的抉择。

在许多社区，免费的班车如今接送老年居民前往医院、市场和社区活动中心，帮助那些不再开车的人保持与日常生活的联系。一些老人也选择租住面积更小、更便于打理的公寓。传统观念认为子女总会住在附近并照顾老人，但这种观念如今与现代生活越来越不相容，因为许多成年子女如今在遥远的城市工作。因此，仅靠家庭很难满足所有需求，专业护理人员开始介入，作为家庭照护的补充。对于没有近亲的老年人，法院有时会指定一名法定监护人来管理其事务。

专家们一致认为，今天的决策将决定社会未来数十年应对老龄化问题的成效。如果现在不在养老服务上果断加大投入，日后的支出可能进一步攀升，给本已捉襟见肘的公共财政带来更大压力。将家庭照护、社区支持与专业服务结合起来，或许是现实可行的前进方向。

keyWords.zh: n. 往返汽车/列车/飞机；穿梭
n. 租借，租约 vt. 出租，租出
vt. 补足，补助 n. 补足物
n. 监护人，保护人；守护者

questions.q: What is the passage mainly discussing?
options: How countries are responding to the challenges of an aging population
Why young people refuse to work in distant cities
The history of public transportation systems
How courts handle property disputes
questions.explain: 全文围绕老龄化带来的支出压力、社区应对措施及专业照护的重要性展开，主旨是A；其余选项均只是文中提到的局部细节。

questions.q: According to the passage, why has family care alone become insufficient for many elderly people?
options: Elderly people no longer trust their family members
Many adult children now work far from home
Shuttle buses have replaced family visits
Legal guardians are required by law for everyone
questions.explain: 第二段指出“many adult children now work in distant cities”，导致传统的子女照护方式与现代生活不相容，故家庭照护难以满足需求，对应B。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the passage that professional caregivers are increasingly important because
options: family support alone often cannot meet elderly people's needs
governments have banned family members from providing care
elderly people prefer strangers to their own relatives
shuttle services have made caregiving unnecessary
questions.explain: 第二段说“it has become unlikely that families alone can meet every need, and professional caregivers are stepping in to complement family support”，可推知A正确。

questions.q: In the passage, "incompatible with modern life" most nearly means
options: well suited to modern life
unable to fit with modern life
required by modern life
unrelated to modern life
questions.explain: 第二段上下文说明传统的子女照护期待与子女在外地工作的现实相矛盾，incompatible意为“不相容的，无法适应的”，对应B。

longSentences.sentence: Policymakers are now under pressure to make decisive choices about how to support a growing number of older citizens without placing an unbearable burden on younger taxpayers.
longSentences.skeleton: 主语Policymakers + are under pressure + 不定式to make decisive choices作补充说明
longSentences.modifiers: “about how to support...”介词短语中嵌套how引导的不定式结构；“without placing...”为伴随状语
longSentences.translation: 政策制定者如今承受着压力，必须就如何在不给年轻纳税人带来过重负担的前提下，为日益增多的老年人口提供支持做出果断的抉择。
longSentences.tip: 先抓主干“policymakers are under pressure to make choices”，再依次剥离about和without引导的修饰成分。

longSentences.sentence: Traditional expectations that children would always live nearby and provide care are increasingly incompatible with modern life, since many adult children now work in distant cities.
longSentences.skeleton: 主语Traditional expectations + are incompatible with...，that引导同位语从句修饰expectations，since引导原因状语从句
longSentences.modifiers: “that children would always live nearby and provide care”为同位语从句；“since many adult children now work in distant cities”为原因状语从句
longSentences.translation: 传统观念认为子女总会住在附近并照顾老人，但这种观念如今与现代生活越来越不相容，因为许多成年子女如今在遥远的城市工作。
longSentences.tip: that从句是对expectations内容的具体说明，属于同位语从句，翻译时可提前处理成“认为……的传统观念”。

longSentences.sentence: As a result, it has become unlikely that families alone can meet every need, and professional caregivers are stepping in to complement family support.
longSentences.skeleton: it作形式主语 + has become unlikely + that引导真正的主语从句，and连接后一分句
longSentences.modifiers: “that families alone can meet every need”为主语从句；“to complement family support”为不定式作目的状语
longSentences.translation: 因此，仅靠家庭很难满足所有需求，专业护理人员开始介入，作为家庭照护的补充。
longSentences.tip: 看到“it is + adj. + that...”结构，先判断it是形式主语，再把that从句还原为真正的主语来理解。

items.title: In Search of Hidden Crystal
items.passage:
Every summer, a small group of hobbyists travels to an abandoned mining region to search for crystal. They rent a simple wooden hut near an old pit that miners dug decades ago, and each morning they proceed down narrow paths with hammers, buckets, and small chisels, hoping to crack open rocks that might hide something valuable inside.

The search is rarely easy. Faulty tools break, sudden rain turns paths to mud, and after hours of digging many diggers find nothing but plain stone. Before starting, each group must also stake out a small area to search, since digging outside the marked zone is not allowed. Whether a particular pit will produce anything worth keeping is always doubtful, and even experienced hunters admit they can never be certain what a cracked rock will reveal.

Yet most hobbyists say the uncertainty is part of the appeal. They return home each evening tired, holding a mug of hot tea by the hut's small stove, talking about the day's finds no matter how small. Researchers who study outdoor hobbies note that such simple, focused activities can certainly improve mental well-being, offering a break from screens, calculators, and daily routines. For these amateur hunters, the joy lies not just in finding crystal, but in the patient search itself.
items.translation:
每年夏天，一小群业余爱好者都会前往一处废弃的采矿区寻找水晶。他们在一座数十年前矿工挖出的旧矿坑附近，租下一间简朴的木屋，每天清晨便沿着狭窄的小路出发，带着锤子、水桶和小凿子，希望能敲开岩石，看看里面是否藏着什么珍贵的东西。

寻找的过程很少一帆风顺。有缺陷的工具会损坏，突如其来的降雨会把小路变成泥泞，许多人辛苦挖掘数小时后却只找到普通的石头。开始搜寻之前，每个小组还必须先划定一小片区域，因为在划定区域之外挖掘是不被允许的。某个矿坑是否能有所收获，始终难以确定，即便是经验丰富的寻宝者也承认，谁也无法确定一块被敲开的岩石里究竟藏着什么。

然而，大多数爱好者表示，这种不确定性正是这项活动的魅力所在。每天傍晚，他们拖着疲惫的身子回到木屋，围着小炉子捧一杯热茶，谈论当天的收获，哪怕收获很小。研究户外爱好的学者指出，这类简单而专注的活动确实能提升人的心理健康水平，让人暂时远离屏幕、计算器和日常琐事。对这些业余寻宝者来说，快乐不仅在于找到水晶本身，更在于耐心寻找的过程。

keyWords.zh: v. 敲开，使破裂 n. 裂缝
a. 有错误的，有缺点的
a. 怀疑的；难以确定的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A group of hobbyists searching for crystal in an old mining area
Professional miners competing for mining rights
The history of a famous crystal museum
How to repair faulty digging tools
questions.explain: 全文围绕业余爱好者在废弃矿区寻找水晶的经历展开，主旨是A；B、C、D均未在文中作为核心内容出现。

questions.q: According to the passage, why must each group stake out an area before searching?
options: To avoid digging outside the area allowed for their group
To sell the land to other hunters later
To measure how deep the pit is
To prevent rain from damaging the site
questions.explain: 第二段说明“each group must also stake out a small area to search, since digging outside the marked zone is not allowed”，即划定区域是为了不越界挖掘，对应A。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the hobbyists' attitude toward not finding crystal?
options: They are so upset that they usually give up the hobby
They accept the uncertainty as part of the enjoyment
They blame the researchers for their failure
They believe finding nothing means the tools were faulty
questions.explain: 第三段开头说“most hobbyists say the uncertainty is part of the appeal”，说明他们把不确定性视为活动乐趣的一部分，对应B。

questions.q: In the passage, "doubtful" is closest in meaning to
options: certain
uncertain
exciting
forbidden
questions.explain: 第二段中“Whether a particular pit will produce anything worth keeping is always doubtful”，结合后文“can never be certain”可知doubtful意为“不确定的”，对应B。

longSentences.sentence: They rent a simple wooden hut near an old pit that miners dug decades ago, and each morning they proceed down narrow paths with hammers, buckets, and small chisels, hoping to crack open rocks that might hide something valuable inside.
longSentences.skeleton: 并列句：分句一They rent a hut...；分句二they proceed down paths...，现在分词短语hoping...作伴随状语
longSentences.modifiers: “that miners dug decades ago”为定语从句修饰pit；“with hammers, buckets, and small chisels”为伴随状语；“that might hide something valuable inside”为定语从句修饰rocks
longSentences.translation: 他们在一座数十年前矿工挖出的旧矿坑附近，租下一间简朴的木屋，每天清晨便沿着狭窄的小路出发，带着锤子、水桶和小凿子，希望能敲开岩石，看看里面是否藏着什么珍贵的东西。
longSentences.tip: 长句中出现多个定语从句和分词结构时，先找到and连接的两个并列主干，再逐一处理修饰成分。

longSentences.sentence: Whether a particular pit will produce anything worth keeping is always doubtful, and even experienced hunters admit they can never be certain what a cracked rock will reveal.
longSentences.skeleton: Whether引导的主语从句 + is always doubtful，and连接第二分句，admit后接省略that的宾语从句，其中嵌套what引导的从句
longSentences.modifiers: “worth keeping”为形容词短语修饰anything；“what a cracked rock will reveal”为宾语从句
longSentences.translation: 某个矿坑是否能有所收获，始终难以确定，即便是经验丰富的寻宝者也承认，谁也无法确定一块被敲开的岩石里究竟藏着什么。
longSentences.tip: whether引导的从句作主语时，可以先把它当作一个整体名词来理解句子主干。

longSentences.sentence: Researchers who study outdoor hobbies note that such simple, focused activities can certainly improve mental well-being, offering a break from screens, calculators, and daily routines.
longSentences.skeleton: 主语Researchers + note + that宾语从句，现在分词短语offering...作伴随状语
longSentences.modifiers: “who study outdoor hobbies”为定语从句修饰researchers；“that such simple, focused activities can certainly improve mental well-being”为that宾语从句
longSentences.translation: 研究户外爱好的学者指出，这类简单而专注的活动确实能提升人的心理健康水平，让人暂时远离屏幕、计算器和日常琐事。
longSentences.tip: 先剥离who引导的定语从句，找到主句“Researchers note that...”，再处理that从句内部结构。

items.title: Teenagers Learn to Climb Safely
items.passage:
Each July, a coastal town welcomes a fresh series of teenage climbers to its outdoor adventure camp. Under the watch of professional instructors, beginners scramble up gentle rock faces before attempting steeper routes. For many participants, the greatest reward is not reaching the top but the notable change in confidence they gain along the way, evident in the wide grin that spreads across a nervous face once the first climb is complete.

Safety depends heavily on gear that meets strict specification standards. Anchors are embedded deep into solid rock, and temporary platforms are erected at rest points so climbers can pause without strain. Interestingly, some older equipment still lists measurements in imperial units, a reminder of the sport's British origins, though modern gauges now favor the metric system. Instructors insist that even a marginal flaw in a harness or rope can turn a routine climb into a genuine emergency.

Parents often voice a seeming contradiction: they want their children to take risks yet remain perfectly safe. Camp organizers try to alleviate this concern by explaining that every route is assessed beforehand and that supervision never lapses. Over the years, the program has shown that carefully managed adventure, rather than blind caution, best prepares teenagers for challenges beyond the climbing wall.
items.translation:
每年七月，一座海滨小镇都会迎来新一批参加户外探险营的青少年攀岩者。在专业教练的看护下，初学者们先在坡度平缓的岩壁上攀爬，然后再尝试更陡峭的路线。对许多参与者来说，最大的收获并非登顶本身，而是自信心方面显著的变化——当他们完成第一次攀爬后，紧张的脸上会绽放出灿烂的笑容，这一点显而易见。

安全在很大程度上取决于符合严格规格标准的装备。锚点被深深嵌入坚硬的岩石中，休息点还会临时搭建供攀爬者停留而不感到吃力的平台。有趣的是，一些旧装备的计量单位仍然是英制单位，这是该运动源自英国的印记，尽管如今的现代仪器更多采用公制单位。教练们坚持认为，哪怕安全带或绳索出现一个微小的缺陷，也可能让一次普通的攀爬变成真正的险情。

家长们常常表达出一种看似矛盾的心理：他们既希望孩子敢于冒险，又希望孩子绝对安全。营地组织者试图通过说明每条路线都经过事先评估、且监护从不间断，来缓解这种担忧。多年来的实践表明，精心管理的冒险活动，而非一味的谨慎回避，才最能让青少年为攀岩墙之外的种种挑战做好准备。

keyWords.zh: a. 专业的；n. 专业人员

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The history of rock climbing as a British sport
A summer program that teaches teenagers to climb safely
The reasons why parents refuse to let children climb
A comparison between imperial and metric measurement systems
questions.explain: 文章主旨是讲述一个教青少年安全攀岩的夏令营项目（见第一段），其余选项要么是次要细节（A、D），要么与原文态度相反（C，家长并非拒绝而是担忧）。
