questions.q: According to the passage, what happens to some older climbing equipment?
options: It has been completely replaced by metric equipment
Its measurements are still listed in imperial units
It is considered too dangerous to use
It was originally designed for professional climbers only
questions.explain: 第二段明确提到“some older equipment still lists measurements in imperial units”，可直接得出答案；其余选项原文未提及或与原文冲突。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the camp organizers' attitude toward parents' worries?
options: They think parents worry too much and ignore the concerns
They take safety concerns seriously and try to address them
They believe risk should be eliminated completely
They avoid discussing safety with parents
questions.explain: 第三段提到组织者“try to alleviate this concern by explaining...”，说明他们认真对待家长的担忧并主动解释缓解，故选B；其余选项与原文态度相反或原文未提及。

questions.q: In the sentence "even a marginal flaw in a harness or rope can turn a routine climb into a genuine emergency," the word "marginal" most likely means ____.
options: extremely serious
very small
completely invisible
financially costly
questions.explain: 根据上下文，哪怕是“微小的”缺陷都能引发严重后果，说明marginal意为“微小的，边缘性的”，故选B；其余选项曲解词义。

longSentences.sentence: For many participants, the greatest reward is not reaching the top but the notable change in confidence they gain along the way, evident in the wide grin that spreads across a nervous face once the first climb is complete.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“not A but B”结构：the greatest reward is not reaching the top but the notable change in confidence
longSentences.modifiers: “they gain along the way”为省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰confidence；“evident in...”为分词短语作伴随状语；“once the first climb is complete”为时间状语从句。
longSentences.translation: 对许多参与者来说，最大的收获并非登顶本身，而是自信心方面显著的变化——这一点在他们完成第一次攀爬后紧张脸上绽放的笑容中显而易见。
longSentences.tip: 遇到“not A but B”结构时，先抓住B才是强调重点，再逐层剥离后置修饰成分。

longSentences.sentence: Interestingly, some older equipment still lists measurements in imperial units, a reminder of the sport's British origins, though modern gauges now favor the metric system.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“some older equipment still lists measurements in imperial units”
longSentences.modifiers: “a reminder of...”为同位语，补充说明前面整个事实；“though modern gauges...”为让步状语从句。
longSentences.translation: 有趣的是，一些旧装备的计量单位仍是英制单位，这也是该运动源自英国的一个印记，尽管现代仪器如今更倾向于使用公制单位。
longSentences.tip: 逗号后接名词短语作同位语时，可将其理解为对前句内容的补充说明，不影响主干判断。

longSentences.sentence: Camp organizers try to alleviate this concern by explaining that every route is assessed beforehand and that supervision never lapses.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“Camp organizers try to alleviate this concern”
longSentences.modifiers: “by explaining that...”为方式状语，其中包含两个由and并列的that宾语从句。
longSentences.translation: 营地组织者试图通过说明每条路线都经过事先评估、且监护从不间断，来缓解这种担忧。
longSentences.tip: by doing结构常表示方式，其后的宾语从句需要按并列结构逐一拆解理解。

items.title: The Hidden System Behind Online Shopping
items.passage:
When shoppers open an app today, they are greeted by rows of gadgets, clothing, and groceries neatly arranged for their convenience. Long gone are the days when customers wandered aisles pushing a real cart; now a virtual cart holds every item until checkout. Before finishing a purchase, users are usually asked to specify size, color, or delivery date, details that once required a face-to-face conversation with a shop assistant.

Behind every screen lies a system built to keep customers engaged. A recommendation feature studies past purchases and browsing habits, then suggests products a shopper never knew existed. Warehouses use a handheld scanner to track each parcel from shelf to doorstep, while product pages are constantly populated with fresh reviews. Most customer-service chatbots are programmed to sound respectful and patient, even when faced with confusing or repetitive questions.

Yet not every message reaching a shopper's phone is useful; some notifications amount to little more than nonsense, promoting items no one ever asked for. Consumers who value their time have learned to ignore such clutter and rely instead on trusted recommendations, treating online platforms not as endless distractions but as efficient tools for daily life.
items.translation:
如今，当购物者打开一个应用程序时，映入眼帘的是为方便他们而整齐排列的各类小玩意、服装和食品杂货。顾客推着真实购物车在货架间穿梭的日子早已一去不返；如今，一个虚拟购物车会保存每一件商品，直到结账为止。在完成购买之前，用户通常需要指定尺寸、颜色或送货日期，而这些细节以前往往需要与店员当面交流才能确定。

每一块屏幕背后，都有一套旨在留住顾客的系统在运作。推荐功能会研究用户过去的购买记录和浏览习惯，进而推荐一些购物者从未想到过的商品。仓库使用手持扫描仪追踪每一件包裹从货架到家门口的全过程，而商品页面也在不断被新的评论填充。大多数客服聊天机器人被设定为语气恭敬而有耐心，即便面对令人困惑或反复出现的问题也是如此。

然而，并非每一条推送到购物者手机上的消息都有用；有些通知不过是些无意义的废话，推销着根本没人要的商品。珍惜时间的消费者已经学会忽略这类干扰信息，转而依赖值得信任的推荐，把网络购物平台当作日常生活中高效的工具，而不是无休止的干扰源。

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Online shopping apps rely on hidden systems and features to serve and engage customers
Customers prefer traditional shopping carts to virtual ones
Recommendation systems always waste customers' time
Warehouses no longer need any technology to track parcels
questions.explain: 全文围绕网购App如何通过虚拟购物车、推荐系统、扫描仪等技术为顾客提供便利并留住顾客展开，A概括最全面；B与原文相反，C、D均与原文内容不符。

questions.q: According to the passage, what do warehouses use to track parcels?
options: A virtual cart
A handheld scanner
A chatbot
A recommendation feature
questions.explain: 第二段明确提到“Warehouses use a handheld scanner to track each parcel from shelf to doorstep”，故选B。

questions.q: What can be inferred about some notifications sent to shoppers?
options: They are always based on careful analysis of the shopper's needs
They can be irrelevant and unwanted by the shopper
They are sent only by customer-service chatbots
They have completely replaced product recommendations
questions.explain: 第三段提到部分通知只是“nonsense, promoting items no one ever asked for”，说明这些消息与消费者需求无关甚至令人反感，故选B。

questions.q: In the sentence "some notifications amount to little more than nonsense," the phrase "amount to" most likely means ____.
options: are worth financially
essentially are, equal to
are measured in numbers
are completely different from
questions.explain: “amount to”在此语境中意为“等同于，实质上是”，即这些通知实质上就是无意义的信息，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: Long gone are the days when customers wandered aisles pushing a real cart; now a virtual cart holds every item until checkout.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为主谓倒装强调结构：Long gone are the days（那些日子已经一去不返了）
longSentences.modifiers: “when customers wandered aisles pushing a real cart”为定语从句修饰the days，其中“pushing a real cart”为伴随状语。
longSentences.translation: 顾客推着真实购物车在货架间穿梭的日子早已一去不返；如今，一个虚拟购物车会保存每一件商品，直到结账为止。
longSentences.tip: “Long gone are...”是主谓倒装的强调结构，阅读时可先还原为“The days...are long gone”再分析从句。

longSentences.sentence: A recommendation feature studies past purchases and browsing habits, then suggests products a shopper never knew existed.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为并列谓语结构：A recommendation feature studies... then suggests...
longSentences.modifiers: “products a shopper never knew existed”中省略了关系代词that/which，是定语从句修饰products。
longSentences.translation: 推荐功能会研究用户过去的购买记录和浏览习惯，进而推荐一些购物者从未想到过的商品。
longSentences.tip: 名词后紧跟主谓结构且无引导词时，往往是省略关系代词的定语从句，需要补出关系词理解。

items.title: Reviving an Old Town District
items.passage:
In many cities, an old town sits like the spine of a much larger urban body, connecting long-standing inhabitant communities to newer suburbs. Decades of expansion have left the district as little more than a remainder of what it once was, its narrow streets overshadowed by taller developments nearby. City officials recently posted a bulletin outlining an ambitious renewal project intended to breathe fresh life into these historic blocks without erasing their character.

Investors have shown surprising confidence in the plan, even though renovating century-old buildings is widely regarded as something of a gamble. Property prices in the area already carry a premium compared with surrounding neighborhoods, reflecting expectations that the district will soon flourish. Officials concede that the schedule is tight and that a fully realistic timeline may require another two years, but they insist the project remains on track.

A bronze plaque near the main square already commemorates the neighborhood's founding families, and planners hope future visitors will pause there before wandering the reopened lanes. Whether the renewal succeeds or not, residents say the attempt itself has already restored something valuable: a sense of pride in a place many had begun to overlook.
items.translation:
在许多城市里，老城区就像一座更大的城市躯体中的“脊梁”，将历史悠久的居民社区与新建的郊区连接起来。数十年的扩张使这片区域沦为往日辉煌的残余，狭窄的街道也被附近更高的新建筑所遮蔽。市政官员最近发布了一份公告，概述了一项雄心勃勃的更新计划，旨在为这些历史街区注入新的活力，同时又不抹去它们原有的特色。

投资者对这一计划表现出令人意外的信心，尽管翻新百年老建筑普遍被视为一场赌博。该地区的房价已经比周边社区高出一截溢价，这反映出人们对这片区域即将复兴的预期。官员们承认，时间安排相当紧张，一个完全现实的时间表可能还需要再等两年，但他们坚称项目仍在按计划推进。

主广场附近的一块青铜牌匾已经在纪念这个社区的建立者家族，规划者希望未来的游客在漫步于重新开放的巷道之前，会在此驻足片刻。无论这次更新最终是否成功，居民们表示，这次尝试本身已经恢复了一种可贵的东西：一种对一个曾被许多人忽视的地方的自豪感。

keyWords.zh: a. 现实的，切实可行的
n. 青铜；a. 青铜色的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly discussing?
options: Why old towns should be demolished to make room for suburbs
A renewal project aiming to revive an old town district
A decline in property prices in historic neighborhoods
The history of bronze statues in city squares
questions.explain: 全文围绕老城更新计划的信心、风险与成效展开，B概括最准确；A与原文相反，C与原文（房价含溢价、更高）矛盾，D只是细节，并非主旨。

questions.q: According to the passage, how do property prices in the old district compare with surrounding neighborhoods?
options: They are noticeably lower
They carry a premium, meaning they are higher
They are exactly the same
They have not been affected by the renewal plan
questions.explain: 第二段提到“Property prices in the area already carry a premium compared with surrounding neighborhoods”，说明价格更高，故选B。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the officials' attitude toward the renewal project's timeline?
options: They are certain the project will finish exactly as originally planned
They admit the schedule is difficult but remain committed to the project
They have already abandoned the original plan
They refuse to discuss the timeline publicly
questions.explain: 第二段中官员“concede that the schedule is tight...but they insist the project remains on track”，表明他们承认困难但仍坚持推进，故选B。

questions.q: In the sentence "renovating century-old buildings is widely regarded as something of a gamble," the word "gamble" most likely means ____.
options: a guaranteed success
a risky undertaking with an uncertain outcome
a routine, low-cost task
a legal requirement
questions.explain: gamble本义为“赌博”，此处引申为“结果不确定的冒险行为”，与前后文“翻新百年建筑存在风险”一致，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: In many cities, an old town sits like the spine of a much larger urban body, connecting long-standing inhabitant communities to newer suburbs.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“an old town sits like the spine of a much larger urban body”
longSentences.modifiers: “connecting long-standing inhabitant communities to newer suburbs”为现在分词短语作伴随状语，补充说明老城的作用。
longSentences.translation: 在许多城市里，老城区就像一座更大的城市躯体中的“脊梁”，将历史悠久的居民社区与新建的郊区连接起来。
longSentences.tip: 分词短语作状语时通常紧跟在主句之后，可先理解主句意思，再把分词部分当作补充信息添加进去。

longSentences.sentence: Decades of expansion have left the district as little more than a remainder of what it once was, its narrow streets overshadowed by taller developments nearby.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“leave sth. as sth.”结构：Decades of expansion have left the district as little more than a remainder
longSentences.modifiers: “its narrow streets overshadowed by taller developments nearby”为独立主格结构，补充说明伴随状态。
longSentences.translation: 数十年的扩张使这片区域沦为往日辉煌的残余，狭窄的街道也被附近更高的新建筑所遮蔽。
longSentences.tip: 名词+过去分词构成的独立主格结构常放在句末补充背景信息，可单独翻译成一个分句。

longSentences.sentence: Officials concede that the schedule is tight and that a fully realistic timeline may require another two years, but they insist the project remains on track.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为并列句：Officials concede that... but they insist that...
longSentences.modifiers: 两个由and并列的that从句作concede的宾语。
longSentences.translation: 官员们承认，时间安排相当紧张，一个完全现实的时间表可能还需要再等两年，但他们坚称项目仍在按计划推进。
longSentences.tip: 遇到“concede...but insist...”这种让步转折结构时，要重点关注but之后作者真正想强调的内容。

items.title: White-Collar Workers and Lost Sleep
items.passage:
For many white-collar employees, a full night's rest has quietly become a luxury rather than a routine. Workdays are often saturated with meetings and messages that overflow into the evening, leaving little space for sufficient sleep. Some workers evidently believe that shrugging off fatigue is simply part of professional life, while others feel a slow descent into constant exhaustion long before they recognize the pattern as a problem.

Doctors argue that this schedule is not in accord with how the human body actually functions, since directly disrupting normal rest cycles can quietly damage memory, mood, and immune health. Rather than treating sleep as a small, adjustable thread in the fabric of daily life, specialists say it should be treated as a fixed requirement, much like food or water, that no amount of ambition can safely replace.

Recently, several companies have inaugurated new policies limiting after-hours messages, hoping to prevent employees from feeling isolated in their exhaustion. Early results appear encouraging: teams report enormous improvements in focus, and fewer workers describe themselves as constantly tired. Whether such measures spread widely may determine how the next generation of offices treats rest.
items.translation:
对许多白领员工来说，一夜安睡已在不知不觉间从常态变成了一种奢侈。工作日常常被会议和消息填得满满当当，甚至溢出到夜晚，几乎不给充足的睡眠留下空间。有些员工显然认为，耸耸肩不把疲惫当回事只是职业生活的一部分，而另一些人则在还未意识到问题所在之前，就已经缓慢滑向持续疲惫的状态。

医生们指出，这种作息安排与人体实际的运行方式并不一致，因为直接打乱正常的休息周期，会在不知不觉中损害记忆力、情绪和免疫健康。专家们表示，不应把睡眠当作日常生活这块织物中一根可有可无、可随意调整的细线，而应将其视为一项固定不变的需求，就像食物和水一样，无论多大的雄心壮志都不能安全地取而代之。

最近，几家公司已经开始施行限制下班后消息的新政策，希望以此防止员工在疲惫中感到孤立无援。初步结果令人鼓舞：多个团队反映专注力有了巨大的提升，自称长期疲惫的员工也减少了。这类举措能否广泛推行，或许将决定下一代办公室对待休息的态度。

keyWords.zh: v./n. 耸肩（此处指不予理会）

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: White-collar workers no longer need sleep to function well
Overwork is damaging sleep among white-collar workers, and some companies are responding
Doctors recommend that people work through the night to finish tasks
Companies have completely solved the problem of employee fatigue
questions.explain: 全文先描述白领睡眠不足的问题及其危害，再介绍部分公司采取的应对措施，B最贴合主旨；A与原文相反，C原文未提及，D夸大了原文结果（原文只说“early results appear encouraging”）。

questions.q: According to doctors mentioned in the passage, what can disrupting normal rest cycles damage?
options: Only physical strength
Memory, mood, and immune health
Only professional reputation
Only work efficiency
questions.explain: 第二段明确提到“directly disrupting normal rest cycles can quietly damage memory, mood, and immune health”，故选B。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the companies that limited after-hours messages?
options: Their new policies have shown some positive effects on employees' focus
Their policies have failed completely
They plan to cancel the new policies soon
They introduced the policies without any hope of improvement
questions.explain: 第三段提到“Early results appear encouraging: teams report enormous improvements in focus”，说明新政策带来了积极效果，故选A。

questions.q: In the sentence "Rather than treating sleep as a small, adjustable thread in the fabric of daily life," the word "fabric" most likely means ____.
options: a piece of clothing
the underlying structure or texture of something
a type of sleep disorder
a company policy document
questions.explain: “fabric”本义为“织物”，此处比喻日常生活的“结构、组织”，把睡眠比作织物中的一根线，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: Some workers evidently believe that shrugging off fatigue is simply part of professional life, while others feel a slow descent into constant exhaustion long before they recognize the pattern as a problem.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为并列句：Some workers believe that..., while others feel...
longSentences.modifiers: “shrugging off fatigue”为动名词短语作believe从句的主语；“long before they recognize the pattern as a problem”为时间状语从句。
longSentences.translation: 有些员工显然认为，耸耸肩不把疲惫当回事只是职业生活的一部分，而另一些人则在还未意识到问题所在之前，就已经缓慢滑向持续疲惫的状态。
longSentences.tip: while连接的并列句常表对比，读到while时要留意前后两个分句在态度或情况上的差异。

longSentences.sentence: Doctors argue that this schedule is not in accord with how the human body actually functions, since directly disrupting normal rest cycles can quietly damage memory, mood, and immune health.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“Doctors argue that this schedule is not in accord with...”
longSentences.modifiers: “how the human body actually functions”为介词with的宾语从句；“since...”引导原因状语从句。
longSentences.translation: 医生们指出，这种作息安排与人体实际的运行方式并不一致，因为直接打乱正常的休息周期，会在不知不觉中损害记忆力、情绪和免疫健康。
longSentences.tip: 从句中嵌套从句时，先抓住最外层的argue that主干，再逐层解析内部的with宾语从句和since原因状语从句。

longSentences.sentence: Rather than treating sleep as a small, adjustable thread in the fabric of daily life, specialists say it should be treated as a fixed requirement, much like food or water, that no amount of ambition can safely replace.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干为“specialists say it should be treated as a fixed requirement”
longSentences.modifiers: “Rather than treating sleep as...”为对比状语置于句首；“much like food or water”为插入的比较状语；“that no amount of ambition can safely replace”为定语从句修饰requirement。
longSentences.translation: 专家们表示，不应把睡眠当作日常生活这块织物中一根可有可无、可随意调整的细线，而应将其视为一项固定不变的需求，就像食物和水一样，无论多大的雄心壮志都不能安全地取而代之。
longSentences.tip: “Rather than doing..., ...should...”是常见的对比结构，先跳过rather than部分抓住主句，再回头理解对比内容。

items.title: Reviving a Forgotten Village Trail
items.passage:
In a quiet valley in the American Midwest, a group of local residents established a small nonprofit organization to restore an abandoned railway trail. Their goal was to commemorate the pioneers who once relied on the line to reach distant markets, and to pursue a broader vision of sustainable rural tourism.

The restoration project quickly grew into something more elaborate than anyone had expected. Volunteers repaired the old station house, planted trees along the path, and installed small audio devices at points, which visitors could use to hear a recording about the local history. To fund the work, the group organized bake sales, sought private donations, and eventually received recognition from the state tourism board, which offered a modest grant.

Word of the project has begun to spread. Several rival towns nearby, hoping to draw the same kind of attention, are exploring similar restoration ideas. But most observers agree that the true merit of the project lies in its focus: it is not commercial, and it never intended to compete with theme parks or shopping centers. As spring approaches, the newly reopened trail will unfold before its first wave of visitors, offering not entertainment, but a quiet path into the past.
items.translation:
在美国中西部一处僻静的山谷里，一群当地居民成立了一个小型非营利组织，致力于修复一条废弃的铁路步道。他们的目标是纪念曾经依赖这条铁路线抵达远方市场的拓荒者，并追求一个更宏大的愿景——发展可持续的乡村旅游。

修复项目很快变得比任何人预想的都更为精细复杂。志愿者们修缮了老旧的车站小屋，沿步道两侧栽种了树木，并在若干地点安装了小型音频设备，游客可以借此聆听一段关于当地历史的讲解录音。为了给工程筹措资金，该组织举办了义卖活动，寻求私人捐款，并最终获得了州旅游局的认可，得到了一笔不多的资助。

关于这个项目的消息开始传开。附近的几个竞争对手小镇也希望吸引同样的关注，正在探索类似的修复方案。但大多数观察者都认为，这个项目真正的价值在于它的定位：它不带商业色彩，也从未打算与主题公园或购物中心一较高下。随着春天临近，这条重新开放的步道即将在第一批游客面前展现，带给他们的不是娱乐，而是一条通向过去的静谧小径。

keyWords.zh: a. 精心制作的，复杂详尽的
n. 竞争对手 a. 竞争的
n. 资金 vt. 资助

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A nonprofit's effort to restore an old railway trail for meaningful tourism
A comparison between rural and urban tourism development
The economic decline of towns in the American Midwest
A dispute between rival towns over tourism funding
questions.explain: 文章第一段即点明主旨：一个非营利组织修复废弃铁路步道，用于纪念历史、发展可持续旅游，全文围绕此展开，故选A；B、C、D均非文章重点。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, how did the group raise money for the project?
options: By selling naming rights to local businesses
By organizing bake sales and seeking private donations
By charging an entrance fee to the trail
By taking out a bank loan
questions.explain: 第二段提到“organized bake sales, sought private donations”，明确说明筹款方式为义卖和寻求私人捐款，故选B。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the project's relationship with theme parks and shopping centers, per paragraph 3?
options: It plans to merge with a nearby theme park
It aims to attract the same visitors as theme parks
It deliberately avoids competing with commercial attractions
It failed because it could not compete with them
questions.explain: 第三段说“it never intended to compete with theme parks or shopping centers”，说明该项目刻意不与商业景点竞争，故选C。

questions.q: In paragraph 3, the word "merit" most likely means ______.
options: financial profit
worth or value
legal right
popular reputation
questions.explain: 根据语境“the true merit of the project lies in its focus”，指项目真正的价值/优点所在，而非金钱或名声，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: Their goal was to commemorate the pioneers who once relied on the line to reach distant markets, and to pursue a broader vision of sustainable rural tourism.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：他们的目标是……并且……(Their goal was to commemorate...and to pursue...)
longSentences.modifiers: who引导的定语从句修饰pioneers，说明拓荒者曾经依赖这条铁路线抵达远方市场；to reach为不定式作目的状语
longSentences.translation: 他们的目标是纪念曾经依赖这条铁路线抵达远方市场的拓荒者，并追求一个更宏大的愿景——发展可持续的乡村旅游。
longSentences.tip: 遇到who/which引导的定语从句，先抓住主干goal was to do...and to do...，再把从句内容作为附加信息补充理解。

longSentences.sentence: Volunteers repaired the old station house, planted trees along the path, and installed small audio devices at points, which visitors could use to hear a recording about the local history.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：志愿者做了三件事——修缮车站小屋、栽种树木、安装音频设备(Volunteers repaired..., planted..., and installed...)
longSentences.modifiers: which引导非限制性定语从句修饰devices，补充说明设备的用途
longSentences.translation: 志愿者们修缮了老旧的车站小屋，沿步道两侧栽种了树木，并安装了小型音频设备，游客可以借此聆听一段关于当地历史的讲解录音。
longSentences.tip: 并列谓语句要先找出几个并列动词，再处理后面的定语从句作补充说明。

longSentences.sentence: But most observers agree that the true merit of the project lies in its focus: it is not commercial, and it never intended to compete with theme parks or shopping centers.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：大多数观察者认为……(most observers agree that...)
longSentences.modifiers: that引导宾语从句作agree的宾语；冒号后内容进一步解释merit所在，用两个并列分句说明
longSentences.translation: 但大多数观察者都认为，这个项目真正的价值在于它的定位：它不带商业色彩，也从未打算与主题公园或购物中心一较高下。
longSentences.tip: 冒号后的内容通常是对前面观点的具体解释，阅读时要将其与主句内容对应起来理解。

items.title: Thanksgiving Shopping Season Heats Up
items.passage:
Preparations for the biggest shopping weekend of the year are already underway across the United States. In the weeks before Thanksgiving, factories work around the clock producing every kind of commodity, from kitchen appliances to children's toys, in anticipation of the holiday rush. Retailers plan an intensive marketing campaign, hoping that this year's sales will outshine the last.

The retail market has become highly competitive in the days before Thanksgiving. To attract customers, chains release new items at sharply reduced prices, notwithstanding warnings from some economists that early discounts merely shift spending forward rather than creating real growth. Analysts often draw an analogy between this annual rush and a sporting contest, in which each competitor pushes harder simply because rivals are doing the same. Meanwhile, consumer groups have filed a plea with regulators, urging clearer rules on how discounts are advertised, after receiving a wave of complaints from shoppers who felt misled by inflated original prices.

Despite these concerns, most economists still describe the season as a sign of a prosperous economy. Shoppers, some muttering about crowded parking lots, continue to line up before dawn. Even industries far removed from retail, such as aerospace manufacturing, benefit indirectly, as a busy holiday season tends to lift confidence across the wider economy.
items.translation:
美国各地为一年中最大的购物周末所做的准备工作已经启动。在感恩节前的几周里，工厂昼夜不停地生产各类日用商品，从厨房电器到儿童玩具，为即将到来的假日消费高峰做准备。零售商们策划了密集的营销活动，希望今年的销售业绩能超过去年。

感恩节前夕，零售市场的竞争变得异常激烈。为了吸引顾客，各连锁商店纷纷以大幅降价的方式推出新商品，尽管一些经济学家警告说，提前打折只会把消费需求提前透支，而并不能真正拉动增长。分析人士常把这种一年一度的抢购热潮比作一场体育比赛，其中每个参赛者都因为对手在拼命而不得不更拼命。与此同时，消费者团体已向监管部门提出诉求，呼吁就折扣广告的宣传方式制定更清晰的规则，原因是他们收到了大量消费者投诉，称自己被虚高的“原价”所误导。

尽管存在这些担忧，大多数经济学家仍然认为，这个购物季是经济繁荣的一个标志。一些购物者一边抱怨着拥挤的停车场，一边仍在天亮前就排起了长队。就连远离零售业的一些产业，比如航空航天制造业，也间接从中受益，因为一个红火的假日购物季往往能提振整个经济的信心。

keyWords.zh: a. 竞争的，有竞争力的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The environmental impact of holiday shopping
Intense retail competition and economic effects around the Thanksgiving shopping season
A government ban on early holiday discounts
The decline of the aerospace industry
questions.explain: 全文围绕感恩节购物季零售商的激烈竞争、消费者投诉及其对整体经济的影响展开，三段内容均服务于此主旨，故选B。

questions.q: Why did consumer groups file a plea with regulators?
options: They wanted holiday sales banned entirely
They were unhappy about factory working conditions
They wanted clearer rules on how discounts are advertised
They demanded a delay of the shopping season
questions.explain: 第二段明确说明消费者团体向监管部门提出诉求是为了“urging clearer rules on how discounts are advertised”，即要求明确折扣广告规则，故选C。

questions.q: What can be inferred from the analogy drawn by analysts in paragraph 2?
options: Retailers discount prices mainly because their rivals are doing so
Sporting events and shopping seasons share the same regulations
Analysts believe shopping should be treated as a competitive sport
The comparison shows retailers dislike competing with each other
questions.explain: 第二段将购物季比作体育比赛，“each competitor pushes harder simply because rivals are doing the same”，说明商家降价很大程度上是因为对手也在这样做，故选A。

questions.q: In paragraph 3, "muttering" most nearly means ______.
options: shouting loudly in anger
complaining in a low voice
laughing quietly
singing softly
questions.explain: 语境中购物者“muttering about crowded parking lots”指一边低声抱怨拥挤的停车场一边继续排队，符合“低声抱怨”之意，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: Analysts often draw an analogy between this annual rush and a sporting contest, in which each competitor pushes harder simply because rivals are doing the same.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：分析人士常常把……比作……(Analysts...draw an analogy between A and B)
longSentences.modifiers: in which引导的定语从句修饰a sporting contest，解释比赛中的动态：因为对手在努力所以自己更努力
longSentences.translation: 分析人士常把这种一年一度的抢购热潮比作一场体育比赛，其中每个参赛者都因为对手在拼命而不得不更拼命。
longSentences.tip: draw an analogy between A and B是常见搭配，表示把A和B作比较、类比，抓住这个结构即可理解句意。

longSentences.sentence: Meanwhile, consumer groups have filed a plea with regulators, urging clearer rules on how discounts are advertised, after receiving a wave of complaints from shoppers who felt misled by inflated original prices.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：消费者团体向监管部门提出了诉求(consumer groups have filed a plea with regulators)
longSentences.modifiers: urging...为现在分词短语作伴随状语，说明诉求内容；after receiving...为介词短语作时间状语，说明原因；who felt misled...为定语从句修饰shoppers
longSentences.translation: 与此同时，消费者团体已向监管部门提出诉求，呼吁就折扣广告的宣传方式制定更清晰的规则，原因是他们收到了大量消费者投诉，称自己被虚高的原价所误导。
longSentences.tip: 遇到分词短语和介词短语连续出现时，先抓主句filed a plea with regulators，其余部分都是补充说明。

longSentences.sentence: Even industries far removed from retail, such as aerospace manufacturing, benefit indirectly, as a busy holiday season tends to lift confidence across the wider economy.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：就连一些产业也间接受益(Even industries...benefit indirectly)
longSentences.modifiers: far removed from retail为过去分词短语作后置定语修饰industries；such as aerospace manufacturing为举例插入语；as引导原因状语从句说明受益的原因
longSentences.translation: 就连远离零售业的一些产业，比如航空航天制造业，也间接从中受益，因为一个红火的假日购物季往往能提振整个经济的信心。
longSentences.tip: 插入语和后置定语不影响主干判断，先找主谓宾industries benefit indirectly，再补充理解修饰部分。

items.title: A Family's Yearly Golf Tradition
items.passage:
Every summer, my family arranges a small gathering at my grandfather's house, a tradition that has stayed consistent for almost twenty years. The highlight is always a round of golf on the course behind his garden. My niece, who was just a shy teenager when it all began, has since grown into the family's unofficial golf star.

This year, before we headed out, my grandfather pulled out an old photo album. He wanted to enlarge one picture in particular: a blurry shot of my niece, aged twelve, swinging a club nearly as tall as she was. Everyone burst into laughter looking at it, and my niece laughed the loudest of all, though she pretended to be embarrassed.

No one can guarantee a perfect game, of course, and this year was no exception. My niece's first shot flew wide and landed in the pond, its tail of ripples spreading across the water. Yet she did not lose heart. By the final hole, she had pumped her fist twice and earned a warm hug from my grandfather, who poured everyone a toast of juice rather than alcohol, insisting that some traditions are worth keeping simple.
items.translation:
每年夏天，我家都会在祖父家举办一场小型聚会，这一传统已经持续了将近二十年，始终如一。聚会的重头戏永远是在他家花园后面的球场上打一轮高尔夫球。我的侄女在这一切开始时还只是个害羞的少年，如今却已成长为家里公认的高尔夫明星。

今年，在我们出发之前，祖父翻出了一本旧相册。他特意想把其中一张照片放大：那是一张有些模糊的照片，照片里十二岁的侄女正挥动着一根几乎和她一样高的球杆。大家看着照片都忍不住大笑起来，侄女自己笑得最响，尽管她装作有些不好意思。

当然，没有人能保证每一场比赛都完美无缺，今年也不例外。侄女的第一杆打偏了，球落进了池塘，激起的水纹一圈圈荡开。然而她并没有因此气馁。打到最后一洞时，她已经握拳庆祝了两次，还得到了祖父一个温暖的拥抱；祖父给每个人倒了一杯果汁而不是酒，坚持说有些传统就该保持简单。

keyWords.zh: a. 始终如一的，一致的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A family's yearly golf gathering and a tradition passed down through generations
The rules and history of golf as a sport
A niece's decision to become a professional golfer
A disagreement between family members about drinking alcohol
questions.explain: 全文讲述作者一家每年夏天在祖父家聚会打高尔夫球的家庭传统，故选A；B、C、D均非文章重点。

questions.q: What did the grandfather want to do with the old photo?
options: Sell it to a local newspaper
Frame it and hang it in his house
Enlarge it
Give it to the niece as a gift
questions.explain: 第二段明确提到“He wanted to enlarge one picture in particular”，即祖父想把那张照片放大，故选C。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the niece's attitude after her first shot landed in the pond?
options: She gave up playing for the rest of the day
She remained determined and kept playing
She blamed her grandfather for the mistake
She refused to continue the family tradition
questions.explain: 第三段提到“Yet she did not lose heart”，随后又在最后一洞握拳庆祝，说明她并未气馁，而是坚持打完，故选B。

questions.q: In paragraph 3, the phrase "lose heart" most nearly means ______.
options: feel physically tired
become discouraged
forget the rules
get injured
questions.explain: 结合上下文，侄女第一杆失误后“did not lose heart”，随后依然振作发挥，说明lose heart表示灰心、气馁，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: My niece, who was just a shy teenager when it all began, has since grown into the family's unofficial golf star.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：我的侄女如今已经成长为家里公认的高尔夫明星(My niece...has since grown into...)
longSentences.modifiers: who引导的非限制性定语从句插入主语和谓语之间，补充说明侄女最初的样子
longSentences.translation: 我的侄女在这一切开始时还只是个害羞的少年，如今却已成长为家里公认的高尔夫明星。
longSentences.tip: 定语从句插在主谓之间时，先跳过从句抓住主干（My niece has grown into...），再回头理解插入的补充信息。

longSentences.sentence: My niece's first shot flew wide and landed in the pond, its tail of ripples spreading across the water.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：侄女的第一杆打偏了，落进了池塘(My niece's first shot flew wide and landed in the pond)
longSentences.modifiers: its tail of ripples spreading across the water为独立主格结构，作伴随状语，补充描述水面泛起涟漪的画面
longSentences.translation: 侄女的第一杆打偏了，球落进了池塘，激起的水纹一圈圈荡开。
longSentences.tip: 名词+现在分词构成的独立主格结构常用来补充描写伴随的画面，不必强行找主谓关系。

longSentences.sentence: By the final hole, she had pumped her fist twice and earned a warm hug from my grandfather, who poured everyone a toast of juice rather than alcohol, insisting that some traditions are worth keeping simple.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：到最后一洞时，她已经握拳庆祝两次，并得到了祖父的一个拥抱(she had pumped her fist twice and earned a warm hug)
longSentences.modifiers: who引导的非限制性定语从句修饰grandfather，补充说明他倒饮料的举动；insisting...为现在分词短语作伴随状语，说明祖父的态度
longSentences.translation: 打到最后一洞时，她已经握拳庆祝了两次，还得到了祖父一个温暖的拥抱；祖父给每个人倒了一杯果汁而不是酒，坚持说有些传统就该保持简单。
longSentences.tip: 长句中出现多个从句和分词结构时，先划出and连接的并列谓语（pumped...and earned...），再依次处理后面的修饰成分。

items.title: Missionaries Who Served a Marsh Community
items.passage:
In the nineteenth century, a small group of missionaries embarked on a journey into a remote marsh region, drawn by reports of a community struggling with disease and poverty. Their principal aim was not conversion alone but the delivery of basic medical care to a population that had long been neglected.

Conditions in the marsh were harsh, and disease was common; several of the missionaries themselves perished within the first two years, victims of the same fevers they had come to treat. Yet the survivors pressed on with remarkable enthusiasm, building a small clinic and training local residents in basic nursing. They assured worried families that the clinic's doors would remain open regardless of the risks involved.

Historians now regard their work as comparable, in its long-term impact, to that of larger public health campaigns launched decades later. The population they served, once desperately vulnerable to seasonal outbreaks, gradually gained access to consistent care. Few records of the mission survive today, but local memory of their quiet persistence has not faded.
items.translation:
十九世纪，一小群传教士出发前往一处偏远的沼泽地区，促使他们此行的是关于当地社区深受疾病与贫困困扰的报告。他们此行的主要目的并非只是传教，而是为长期被忽视的居民提供基本的医疗救助。

沼泽地区的条件十分艰苦，疾病也很常见；在最初的两年里，几名传教士自己也因感染了他们本要救治的同一种热病而离世。然而幸存下来的人依然满怀热情地坚持下去，建起了一间小诊所，并培训当地居民掌握基本的护理知识。他们向忧心忡忡的家庭保证，无论风险多大，诊所的大门都会一直敞开。

历史学家如今认为，从长远影响来看，他们的工作可以与几十年后开展的大规模公共卫生运动相媲美。他们所服务的居民，曾经在季节性疫情面前极为脆弱，如今逐渐获得了持续稳定的医疗照顾。这次传教活动的记录留存至今的已经不多，但当地人对他们默默坚守的记忆并未褪色。

keyWords.zh: vi. 着手，从事；上船（或飞机）
a. 主要的，首要的 n. 负责人
a. 可比较的，比得上的
a. 脆弱的，易受伤害的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A modern public health campaign in a marsh region
A group of missionaries who provided medical care to a neglected community
The religious conversion methods used by missionaries
A scientific study of marsh diseases
questions.explain: 文章主要讲述一群传教士前往沼泽地区，为被忽视的社区提供医疗救助的故事，故选B；A是后来历史学家的评价而非文章主线，C、D文中未涉及。

questions.q: What happened to some of the missionaries during the first two years?
options: They returned home due to lack of funding
They died from the same fevers they were treating
They were replaced by government health workers
They converted the local population to their religion
questions.explain: 第二段明确提到“several of the missionaries themselves perished within the first two years, victims of the same fevers they had come to treat”，说明部分传教士死于他们本要救治的热病，故选B。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the local population before the missionaries' clinic was built?
options: They had reliable access to modern medical facilities
They were largely indifferent to disease outbreaks
They had little consistent protection against seasonal disease outbreaks
They had already built their own hospital
questions.explain: 第三段提到当地居民“once desperately vulnerable to seasonal outbreaks”，之后才“gradually gained access to consistent care”，可推知诊所建立之前他们缺乏持续稳定的医疗保障，故选C。

questions.q: In paragraph 3, the word "comparable" most nearly means ______.
options: completely identical
similar in significance
financially cheaper
historically older
questions.explain: 语境中“their work as comparable...to that of larger public health campaigns”指他们的工作在长远影响上与后来的大型公共卫生运动具有可比性、相似分量，即“相似、可媲美”，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: In the nineteenth century, a small group of missionaries embarked on a journey into a remote marsh region, drawn by reports of a community struggling with disease and poverty.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：一小群传教士出发前往一处偏远的沼泽地区(a small group of missionaries embarked on a journey into a remote marsh region)
longSentences.modifiers: drawn by reports...为过去分词短语作原因状语，说明他们出发的原因；struggling with disease and poverty为现在分词短语修饰community
longSentences.translation: 十九世纪，一小群传教士出发前往一处偏远的沼泽地区，促使他们此行的是关于当地社区深受疾病与贫困困扰的报告。
longSentences.tip: 句首时间状语后先找主谓宾（missionaries embarked on a journey），分词短语drawn by...是补充说明原因，不是主干。

longSentences.sentence: Historians now regard their work as comparable, in its long-term impact, to that of larger public health campaigns launched decades later.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干：历史学家现在认为他们的工作可以与……相媲美(Historians now regard their work as comparable...to that of...)
longSentences.modifiers: in its long-term impact为插入语，补充说明比较的角度；launched decades later为过去分词短语修饰campaigns
longSentences.translation: 历史学家如今认为，从长远影响来看，他们的工作可以与几十年后开展的大规模公共卫生运动相媲美。
longSentences.tip: regard A as comparable to B是固定结构，中间插入语可以先跳过，理清主干后再补充理解。

items.title: The Changing Face of Retail
items.passage:
For decades, the retail industry occupied the heart of every town center, but the rise of online shopping has literally transformed how people shop. Department stores that once employed thousands have announced repeated rounds of layoffs, as customers increasingly complete purchases with a few clicks rather than a trip downtown.

Retail analysts say the shift is not simply about convenience. The ratio of online sales to in-store sales has climbed steadily for a decade, forcing traditional chains to rethink their business models. Some governments have moved to regulate the sector more closely, requiring large online platforms to disclose pricing algorithms and protect smaller competitors from unfair practices. Meanwhile, a handful of visionary executives have tried to reinvent the shopping experience itself, turning stores into showrooms where customers try products before ordering them for home delivery.

Loyalty badges, once a minor marketing gimmick, have become central to this new strategy, rewarding shoppers who blend browsing online with visiting a physical store. Hence, the future of retail may not be a simple contest between online and offline, but a careful balance between the two, in which stores that adapt survive and those that do not eventually disappear.
items.translation:
几十年来，零售业一直是每个市镇中心的核心，但网络购物的兴起真的改变了人们的购物方式。曾经雇佣成千上万员工的百货商店已经宣布了多轮裁员，因为顾客越来越多地只需点击几下鼠标完成购物，而不必再去市中心购物。

零售业分析人士表示，这一转变并不仅仅是为了方便。过去十年间，线上销售与线下销售的比率持续攀升，迫使传统连锁企业重新思考自己的商业模式。一些政府已经开始对该行业实施更严格的监管，要求大型在线平台公开定价算法，并保护规模较小的竞争者免受不公平行为的侵害。与此同时，一批有远见的企业高管正尝试重新打造购物体验本身，把门店变成体验展厅，让顾客先试用产品，再下单送货到家。

会员徽章曾经只是一种小小的营销噱头，如今已成为这一新策略的核心，用来奖励那些既在网上浏览又光顾实体店的顾客。因此，零售业的未来或许不是线上与线下之间的简单对抗，而是二者之间的谨慎平衡：能够适应变化的商店得以生存，不能适应的商店最终将会消失。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: How online shopping has changed the retail industry and how it is adapting
A history of department store architecture
A guide to launching an online retail startup
The reasons behind government layoffs in general
questions.explain: 全文围绕零售业受网络购物冲击而转型展开：第一段引出现象，第二、三段说明监管与应对策略，故正确答案为A，依据全文主旨，尤其第一、二段。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, what have some governments required large online platforms to do?
options: Close down their physical branches
Disclose pricing algorithms and protect smaller competitors
Stop all online sales activity
Increase the number of layoffs
questions.explain: 依据第二段“requiring large online platforms to disclose pricing algorithms and protect smaller competitors from unfair practices”可知，答案为B。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the "visionary executives" mentioned in paragraph 2?
options: They want physical stores to disappear entirely
They are trying to combine physical and online shopping experiences
They oppose all forms of government regulation
They believe stores should return only to their traditional role
questions.explain: 第二段提到有远见的高管把门店变成体验展厅，让顾客先试用产品再线上下单送货，说明他们在尝试融合线上线下购物体验，故选B；其余选项原文均无依据。

questions.q: In the last paragraph, the word "Hence" is closest in meaning to
options: However
Therefore
Meanwhile
Otherwise
questions.explain: “Hence”意为“因此”，用于引出结论，与Therefore同义，依据末段句子的逻辑关系可判断答案为B。

longSentences.sentence: Department stores that once employed thousands have announced repeated rounds of layoffs, as customers increasingly complete purchases with a few clicks rather than a trip downtown.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“百货商店已经宣布了多轮裁员”
longSentences.modifiers: “that once employed thousands”是定语从句，修饰主语Department stores；“as customers...downtown”是as引导的原因状语从句
longSentences.translation: 曾经雇佣成千上万员工的百货商店已经宣布了多轮裁员，因为顾客越来越多地只需点击几下鼠标完成购物，而不必再去市中心购物。
longSentences.tip: 先抓住主干“商店已宣布裁员”，再分辨定语从句和as状语从句的范围，避免把从句内容误当成句子主干成分。

longSentences.sentence: Some governments have moved to regulate the sector more closely, requiring large online platforms to disclose pricing algorithms and protect smaller competitors from unfair practices.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“一些政府已经开始更严格地监管该行业”
longSentences.modifiers: “requiring large online platforms to disclose...and protect...”是现在分词短语作伴随状语，补充说明监管的具体做法
longSentences.translation: 一些政府已经开始对该行业实施更严格的监管，要求大型在线平台公开定价算法，并保护规模较小的竞争者免受不公平行为的侵害。
longSentences.tip: 逗号后接doing分词结构时，通常是伴随状语，用来补充说明前面动作的具体方式，翻译时可处理成并列的动作。

items.title: Five Years Without City-Centre Cars
items.passage:
This month marks the fifth anniversary of a rule that bans private vehicles from the old town centre between eight in the morning and eight at night. When the ban first began, many drivers complained that it would cause chaos on the roads outside the centre, as cars looked for other routes.

Five years later, the picture looks different. The city government carried out further studies and found that traffic outside the centre did not become worse. Instead, more people found it suitable to walk, cycle, or take the bus into town. Small shops report that customers now spend more time in the quiet streets, since there is no noise from engines and no long queues of vehicles waiting at traffic lights.

Of course, not every conversation about the ban has been positive. Parents who used to drive their children to school nearby say the walk takes longer, especially on rainy days. Even so, most residents agree that the ban has made the centre a calmer, cleaner place to spend time, and few would like to see private cars return.
items.translation:
本月是市中心禁止私家车通行规定实施五周年。该规定禁止私家车在早上八点至晚上八点之间进入老城中心区域。禁令刚开始实施时，许多司机曾抱怨说，由于汽车会另寻他路，这会在中心区以外的道路上造成混乱。

五年后的今天，情况已大不相同。市政府进行了进一步的研究，发现中心区以外的交通状况并没有变差。相反，越来越多的人发现步行、骑自行车或乘公交车进城更为合适。小商铺反映，如今顾客在安静的街道上停留的时间更长了，因为再也没有了发动机的噪音，也不再有车辆在交通灯前排起长队。

当然，关于这项禁令的讨论并非全是正面的。那些曾经开车送孩子去附近学校的家长说，如今步行的时间变长了，尤其是在下雨天。尽管如此，大多数居民仍然认为，这项禁令让市中心变得更加安静整洁、更适合驻足停留，很少有人希望私家车重新回到这里。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The history of vehicle production in one city
The effects of a five-year-old ban on cars in a town centre
A plan to build new roads outside a city
Complaints from shop owners about a lack of customers
questions.explain: 全文围绕市中心禁止私家车通行政策实施五年后的效果展开，故答案为B，依据首段及全文脉络。

questions.q: According to the passage, what did the city government find after further study?
options: Traffic outside the centre became much worse
Traffic outside the centre did not get worse
Most shops near the centre closed down
Parents stopped taking children to school
questions.explain: 第二段明确指出“found that traffic outside the centre did not become worse”，对应选项B。

questions.q: It can be inferred from the last paragraph that
options: all residents strongly oppose the ban
the ban has caused parents to stop walking their children to school
most residents support keeping the ban despite some inconvenience
cars will soon be allowed back into the centre
questions.explain: 末段提到虽有不便（如接送孩子步行时间变长），但“most residents agree that the ban has made the centre a calmer, cleaner place...few would like to see private cars return”，说明大多数居民支持保留该政策，故选C。

questions.q: In paragraph 1, the word "chaos" most nearly means
options: improvement
disorder
silence
safety
questions.explain: “chaos”意为“混乱”，与disorder同义；第一段中司机担心禁令会在中心区外的道路上造成混乱，故答案为B。

longSentences.sentence: When the ban first began, many drivers complained that it would cause chaos on the roads outside the centre, as cars looked for other routes.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“许多司机曾抱怨（禁令）会造成混乱”
longSentences.modifiers: “When the ban first began”是时间状语从句；“that it would cause chaos...”是complained的宾语从句；“as cars looked for other routes”是as引导的原因状语从句
longSentences.translation: 禁令刚开始实施时，许多司机曾抱怨说，由于汽车会另寻他路，这会在中心区以外的道路上造成混乱。
longSentences.tip: 句中嵌套了时间状语从句、宾语从句和原因状语从句，先找到主句“drivers complained”，再逐层剥离修饰成分。

longSentences.sentence: Parents who used to drive their children to school nearby say the walk takes longer, especially on rainy days.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“家长们说步行时间变长了”
longSentences.modifiers: “who used to drive their children to school nearby”是定语从句修饰Parents；“the walk takes longer”是say省略that的宾语从句；“especially on rainy days”是补充说明
longSentences.translation: 那些曾经开车送孩子去附近学校的家长说，如今步行上学花费的时间更长了，尤其是在下雨天。
longSentences.tip: 主语后紧跟较长定语从句时，需先跳过从句找到真正的谓语动词（say），避免遗漏句子主干。

items.title: Keeping Old Festivals Alive
items.passage:
Across many societies, families have found creative ways to incorporate traditional festivals into modern, fast-paced lives. Rather than abandoning old customs, progressive households often blend them with new routines, keeping the spirit of a celebration alive even when time is short.

This shift has not been free from critical commentary. Some observers argue that certain festivals have become little more than an excuse to indulge in shopping and eating, losing the meaning that once ushered in a new season or honoured ancestors. Others take a more hopeful view, pointing out that young people who grew up abroad often exemplify a genuine effort to nurture ties with their heritage, learning recipes or songs from grandparents precisely because such traditions feel unfamiliar and worth preserving.

What both sides tend to agree on is that traditions survive only when each generation chooses, in some overt or quiet way, to carry them forward. The stability of a community's identity, in this sense, depends less on strict rules than on the willingness of individuals to keep old stories and habits meaningfully connected to present-day life.
items.translation:
在许多社会中，家庭都在想方设法把传统节日融入快节奏的现代生活。与其说是抛弃旧习俗，不如说，观念进步的家庭常常把旧习俗与新的生活方式结合起来，即使时间紧张，也让庆祝活动的精神得以延续。

这一转变并非没有引来批评性的评论。一些观察者认为，某些节日已经沦为购物和大吃大喝的借口，失去了当初用来迎接新季节或纪念祖先的意义。另一些人则持更乐观的看法，指出在国外长大的年轻人常常切实地努力培养与祖辈文化的联系，向祖父母学习食谱或歌曲，正是因为这些传统让他们感到陌生，却又觉得值得保留。

双方大体都认同的一点是：只有当每一代人都以某种或公开或悄然的方式选择将其传承下去，传统才能延续下去。从这个意义上说，一个社群身份认同的稳定性，与其说取决于严格的规则，不如说取决于个体是否愿意让旧的故事与习俗与当下的生活保持有意义的联系。

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Traditional festivals should be abolished in modern society
Families and communities are finding new ways to keep traditions meaningful in modern life
Only young people who grew up abroad care about their heritage
Cultural critics agree that festivals have completely lost their meaning
questions.explain: 全文讨论家庭与社区如何在现代生活中融合并延续传统节日的意义，故B为主旨，依据首段与末段的总结句。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, what do some observers argue?
options: Festivals have become mainly about shopping and eating, losing their original meaning
Festivals should be celebrated more frequently every year
Young people living abroad have no interest in tradition at all
Grandparents should stop teaching traditional recipes
questions.explain: 第二段提到部分观察者认为节日已沦为购物和吃喝的借口，失去了原有的意义，对应选项A。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the young people who grew up abroad, as mentioned in paragraph 2?
options: They reject their cultural heritage completely
They often make deliberate efforts to learn about and preserve their heritage
They are generally indifferent to festival traditions
They believe traditions should never change at all
questions.explain: 第二段指出他们“exemplify a genuine effort to nurture ties with their heritage”，即用心努力去学习并维系与传统文化的联系，故答案为B。

questions.q: In the last paragraph, the word "overt" is closest in meaning to
options: hidden
obvious
accidental
forbidden
questions.explain: “overt”意为“公开的，明显的”，与obvious同义；末段将“overt or quiet way”并列对比，指人们以公开或悄然的方式延续传统，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: Rather than abandoning old customs, progressive households often blend them with new routines, keeping the spirit of a celebration alive even when time is short.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“观念进步的家庭常常把旧习俗与新的生活方式结合起来”
longSentences.modifiers: “Rather than abandoning old customs”是介词短语作状语，表比较/让步；“keeping the spirit...alive”是现在分词短语作伴随状语；“even when time is short”是让步状语从句
longSentences.translation: 与其说是抛弃旧习俗，不如说，观念进步的家庭常常把它们与新的生活方式结合起来，即使时间紧张，也让庆祝活动的精神得以延续。
longSentences.tip: “Rather than doing”结构常置于句首表比较或让步，可译作“与其……不如……”，注意其与主句动作的对比关系。

longSentences.sentence: Others take a more hopeful view, pointing out that young people who grew up abroad often exemplify a genuine effort to nurture ties with their heritage, learning recipes or songs from grandparents precisely because such traditions feel unfamiliar and worth preserving.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“另一些人持更乐观的看法”
longSentences.modifiers: “pointing out that...”是现在分词短语作伴随状语，that后接宾语从句；从句中“who grew up abroad”是定语从句修饰young people；“learning recipes or songs from grandparents”是分词短语作伴随状语；“precisely because...”是原因状语从句
longSentences.translation: 另一些人则持更乐观的看法，指出在国外长大的年轻人常常切实地努力培养与祖辈文化的联系，向祖父母学习食谱或歌曲，正是因为这些传统让他们感到陌生却又值得保留。
longSentences.tip: 长句中多层从句和分词结构层层嵌套，建议先找主句“Others take a view”，再依次拆解pointing out、that从句、who从句和because从句。

items.title: The Heroine of the Valley
items.passage:
In a small mountain village, local folklore tells of a young woman who saved her community from a flood centuries ago. According to the story, she noticed the rising river at night and ran from house to house waking her neighbours, refusing to save herself until everyone else was safe. Villagers still consider her the heroine of the valley, and every spring they hold a colorful festival with lanterns, music, and dancing in her memory.

The tale has grown more romantic over time, with each generation adding small details: a sudden storm, a mysterious light guiding her path, a final word she supposedly whispered before the waters reached her. Older residents admit, with a shiver, that they still feel a chill telling the story on cold nights, even though most agree it is at least partly invented. When asked directly, a few frank historians point out that no written record from that period actually mentions her by name.

Even so, the village council has continued to endorse the festival, arguing that its value lies less in historical accuracy than in bringing the community together. Local schools have proven remarkably responsive to this spirit, inviting children to write their own versions of the legend and keeping the folklore alive for yet another generation.
items.translation:
在一个山区小村庄里，当地的民间传说讲述了一位年轻女子在几个世纪前拯救全村免于洪水之灾的故事。据传说，她在夜里注意到河水上涨，便挨家挨户地跑去叫醒邻居，直到确保其他所有人都安全后才肯自己逃生。村民们至今仍将她视为山谷的女英雄，每年春天都会举办一场丰富多彩的节日，点起灯笼、奏乐起舞，以纪念她。

随着时间推移，这个传说变得愈发浪漫：每一代人都会添加一些新的细节——一场突如其来的暴风雨、一道指引她前行的神秘光亮、她在洪水淹没自己之前据说留下的最后一句话。年长的居民带着一丝颤栗承认，在寒冷的夜晚讲这个故事时，他们仍会感到一阵寒意，尽管大多数人也承认这个故事至少有一部分是虚构的。当被直接问及时，几位坦率的历史学家指出，那个时期并没有任何文字记录提到过她的名字。

尽管如此，村委会依然认可并支持这一节日，认为它的价值不在于历史的准确性，而在于能够把整个社区凝聚在一起。当地学校对这种精神表现得格外积极响应，鼓励孩子们写出属于自己的传说版本，让这则民间故事得以在新一代人中继续流传。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A scientific study of how river floods form
A local legend about a heroine and how the community keeps it alive
A comparison between two neighbouring villages
A history of traditional lantern-making techniques
questions.explain: 全文讲述一则关于女英雄的地方传说，以及社区如何通过节日等方式延续这一传说，故答案为B。

questions.q: According to the passage, what do a few frank historians point out?
options: The heroine's name appears in many official records
No written record from that period mentions her by name
The flood described in the story never actually happened
The festival is against local regulations
questions.explain: 第二段提到“a few frank historians point out that no written record from that period actually mentions her by name”，对应选项B。

questions.q: It can be inferred that the village council continues to support the festival mainly because
options: it wants to prove the legend is historically accurate
it values the way the festival brings the community together
it hopes to attract scientific researchers to study the flood
it is required to do so by regional law
questions.explain: 末段提到村委会认可该节日，“arguing that its value lies less in historical accuracy than in bringing the community together”，说明主要原因是促进社区凝聚力，故选B。

questions.q: In paragraph 2, the phrase "with a shiver" suggests that the older residents feel
options: bored while telling the story
a slight fear or chill when telling the story
proud of their scientific knowledge
annoyed by children's questions
questions.explain: “shiver”意为“颤抖，战栗”，常表达寒冷或恐惧感；文中老年人讲述故事时“with a shiver”并提到“still feel a chill”，说明他们讲述时仍感到一丝寒意或恐惧，故答案为B。

longSentences.sentence: According to the story, she noticed the rising river at night and ran from house to house waking her neighbours, refusing to save herself until everyone else was safe.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“她注意到河水上涨，并挨家挨户跑去叫醒邻居”
longSentences.modifiers: “waking her neighbours”是现在分词短语作伴随状语；“refusing to save herself until everyone else was safe”是现在分词短语作伴随状语，其中“until everyone else was safe”是时间状语从句
longSentences.translation: 据传说，她在夜里注意到河水上涨，便挨家挨户地跑去叫醒邻居，直到确保其他所有人都安全后才肯自己逃生。
longSentences.tip: 句子由并列谓语（noticed and ran）加两个分词短语构成，分词短语只是补充说明动作方式，翻译时可处理为伴随的动作。

longSentences.sentence: Older residents admit, with a shiver, that they still feel a chill telling the story on cold nights, even though most agree it is at least partly invented.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“年长的居民承认，他们仍会感到一丝寒意”
longSentences.modifiers: “with a shiver”是插入语，补充说明承认时的状态；“that they still feel...”是admit的宾语从句；“telling the story on cold nights”是分词短语作状语；“even though...invented”是让步状语从句
longSentences.translation: 年长的居民带着一丝颤栗承认，在寒冷的夜晚讲这个故事时，他们仍会感到一阵寒意，尽管大多数人也承认这故事至少有一部分是虚构的。
longSentences.tip: 插入语“with a shiver”打断了主谓结构，阅读时可先跳过插入成分抓住主干“residents admit that...”，再补充插入语的含义。

items.title: A Town Built on Stairs
items.passage:
In parts of southern Europe, some old towns were built on steep hillsides centuries ago. Streets there are often too steep for buses or taxis, so getting around can be difficult. Instead of wide roads, the towns are connected mainly by narrow stone stairs that climb up and down the hills.

To make daily life easier without damaging the old streets, local governments have added public lifts around town. These lifts work as a substitute for short bus routes: instead of driving around the hill, people simply step into a lift and reach the top floor in seconds. At the same time, the stairs themselves have been carefully preserved, since they are a key part of the town's history and appearance. Visitors are still encouraged to choose the right combination of lifts and stairs to explore the town on foot.

For native residents, this mix of stairs and lifts remains the primary way of getting around, and most people would not trade it for a modern road system. For travelers, walking up hundreds of steps and then riding a lift back down offers a memorable experience that few flat, ordinary cities can provide. In this sense, the town's stairs are not simply an old inconvenience; they are its most distinctive feature.
items.translation:
在南欧的一些地方，古老的城镇几个世纪前就建在陡峭的山坡上。那里的街道往往太陡，公交车或出租车无法通行，因此出行常常十分不便。这些城镇主要不是靠宽阔的道路相连，而是靠沿山坡上下延伸的狭窄石阶。

为了在不破坏古老街道的前提下方便日常生活，当地政府在镇上加装了公共电梯。这些电梯充当了短途公交线路的替代品：人们不必绕山驾车，只需走进电梯，几秒钟就能到达高处。与此同时，石阶本身也被精心保留下来，因为它们是这座小镇历史与风貌的重要组成部分。游客仍然被鼓励把乘电梯和爬台阶恰当地结合起来，徒步探索这座小镇。

对当地土生土长的居民来说，台阶与电梯相结合的方式仍是他们出行的主要方式，大多数人并不愿意用现代化的道路系统来替换它。对游客而言，爬上几百级台阶再乘电梯下山，是大多数平坦、普通的城市无法提供的难忘体验。从这个意义上说，这座小镇的台阶不仅仅是一种陈旧的不便，更是它最与众不同的特色。

keyWords.zh: n. 替代物 vt. 代替
a. 本地的，土生土长的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The history of bus systems in southern Europe
How an old hillside town combines stairs and lifts for transportation
Why old towns should replace stairs with roads
The dangers of steep streets in mountain towns
questions.explain: 全文围绕一座建在陡坡上的古镇如何用台阶和电梯结合的方式解决出行问题展开，第一至三段均在说明这一点，故选B；A、C、D均与原文不符或未提及。

questions.q: According to the passage, why did local governments add public lifts?
options: To replace all the old stone stairs completely
To attract more tourists to the town
To help people move around without damaging the old streets
To reduce the number of visitors climbing stairs
questions.explain: 第二段首句明确说明加装电梯是为了在“不破坏古老街道”的前提下方便日常生活，故选C；A项与后文“台阶被精心保留”相矛盾，B、D原文均未提及。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the native residents mentioned in paragraph 3?
options: They want the stairs to be replaced by roads as soon as possible
They generally accept and even prefer their current way of getting around
They rarely use the public lifts installed by the government
They find the stone stairs too dangerous to use daily
questions.explain: 第三段提到“大多数人并不愿意用现代化的道路系统来替换它”，说明当地居民普遍接受甚至偏爱现有的出行方式，故选B；A、C、D均与原文相悖或缺乏依据。

questions.q: In the last sentence, the word "inconvenience" most likely refers to
options: an attractive feature that draws tourists
a modern lift that saves time
something troublesome or not easy to deal with
a type of stone used to build the stairs
questions.explain: 根据上下文“不仅仅是一种陈旧的inconvenience，更是……特色”，通过转折结构可推知inconvenience指“麻烦、不便之处”，故选C。

longSentences.sentence: In parts of southern Europe, some old towns were built on steep hillsides centuries ago.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“一些古镇建在陡峭的山坡上”
longSentences.modifiers: “In parts of southern Europe”是地点状语；“on steep hillsides”是地点状语；“centuries ago”是时间状语
longSentences.translation: 在南欧的一些地方，古老的城镇几个世纪前就建在陡峭的山坡上。
longSentences.tip: 抓住被动语态“were built”确定句子主干，其余成分都是补充说明地点和时间的状语。

longSentences.sentence: These lifts work as a substitute for short bus routes: instead of driving around the hill, people simply step into a lift and reach the top floor in seconds.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是冒号前后两个并列分句：“这些电梯充当替代品”和“人们走进电梯、到达高处”
longSentences.modifiers: “for short bus routes”修饰substitute；“instead of driving around the hill”是介词短语作状语，表对比；“in seconds”是时间状语
longSentences.translation: 这些电梯充当了短途公交线路的替代品：人们不必绕山驾车，只需走进电梯，几秒钟就能到达高处。
longSentences.tip: 冒号后是对前半句的具体解释，抓住instead of引导的对比结构可以更快理解句意。

longSentences.sentence: For travelers, walking up hundreds of steps and then riding a lift back down offers a memorable experience that few flat, ordinary cities can provide.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“爬台阶并乘电梯下山这件事提供了一种难忘的体验”，两个动名词短语并列作主语
longSentences.modifiers: “For travelers”是状语；“up hundreds of steps”修饰walking；“back down”修饰riding；“that few flat, ordinary cities can provide”是定语从句，修饰experience
longSentences.translation: 对游客而言，爬上几百级台阶再乘电梯下山，是大多数平坦、普通的城市无法提供的难忘体验。
longSentences.tip: 两个动名词短语构成并列主语，谓语动词用第三人称单数offers；that引导的从句是定语从句，修饰experience。

items.title: Farm Tourism in the Countryside
items.passage:
In recent years, farm tourism has become increasingly popular among city residents who want a break from busy urban life. Many travel to fertile counties in the countryside, where wide fields of hay and fruit trees offer a scene that is almost impossible to find in the city. For a weekend, visitors can pick vegetables, feed animals, and sleep in a converted barn.

Before opening a farm to the public, however, owners usually need to study the feasibility of the plan carefully. They must coordinate transportation, food, and lodging, and think about the prevention of accidents, since farm equipment and large animals can be dangerous to people who have never been near them before. Some first-time visitors even feel a little uneasy walking past a cow or a tractor, though this feeling usually disappears within a day.

By the time visitors say farewell to the farm and head home, most agree that the trip was worth the effort. Virtually every guest reports feeling more relaxed, and many actually plan to return the following year. What stays in their memory is not a five-star hotel, but days when modern comforts were missing, replaced by fresh air, quiet fields, and a simpler way of life.
items.translation:
近年来，越来越多渴望暂时逃离忙碌都市生活的城市居民开始青睐农场旅游。许多人前往乡间那些土地肥沃的县区，那里大片的干草地和果树呈现出一种在城市里几乎看不到的景象。周末期间，游客可以采摘蔬菜、给动物喂食，还能在改造过的谷仓里过夜。

然而，在向公众开放农场之前，经营者通常需要仔细研究方案的可行性。他们必须协调交通、餐饮和住宿等安排，还要考虑事故的预防，因为农用设备和大型动物对从未接触过它们的人来说可能存在危险。有些初次到访的游客走过奶牛或拖拉机旁时甚至会感到有点不安，不过这种感觉通常一天之内就会消失。

等到游客向农场道别、启程回家时，大多数人都认为这趟旅程不虚此行。几乎每一位客人都表示感觉更加放松，还有不少人确实打算明年再来。留在他们记忆中的不是五星级酒店，而是那些缺少现代生活便利的日子——取而代之的是新鲜的空气、宁静的田野和更简单的生活方式。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The dangers of visiting fertile farmland
How farm owners plan and manage tourism for city visitors
Why city life is more comfortable than rural life
A comparison between five-star hotels and rural homestays
questions.explain: 全文讲述农场旅游兴起后，经营者如何做可行性研究、协调安排并预防事故，以及游客的体验和感受，主旨是农场旅游的运作与体验，故选B。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, why do some first-time visitors feel uneasy?
options: Because the farm is too far from the city
Because they are not used to being near farm animals and equipment
Because the food and lodging are poorly arranged
Because they are afraid of getting lost on the farm
questions.explain: 第二段提到农用设备和大型动物对从未接触过它们的人来说可能存在危险，紧接着说初次到访者走过奶牛或拖拉机旁会感到不安，说明不安源于不熟悉农具和大型动物，故选B。

questions.q: What can be inferred about most visitors' overall experience of farm tourism?
options: They regret spending money on the trip
They find the trip disappointing compared to city life
They generally feel it is a worthwhile and relaxing experience
They prefer staying in modern hotels instead
questions.explain: 第三段提到大多数人认为这趟旅程不虚此行，几乎每位客人都感觉更加放松，可推断游客总体认为这次旅程值得且令人放松，故选C。

questions.q: In paragraph 1, the word "converted" in "sleep in a converted barn" most nearly means
options: newly built
changed for a different use
extremely old
temporarily closed
questions.explain: 谷仓本用于存放干草或饲养牲畜，“converted barn”指经过改造、变为其他用途（供游客留宿）的谷仓，故converted意为“改造过的”，选B。

longSentences.sentence: Many travel to fertile counties in the countryside, where wide fields of hay and fruit trees offer a scene that is almost impossible to find in the city.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“许多人前往乡间土地肥沃的县区”
longSentences.modifiers: “in the countryside”是地点状语；“where wide fields of hay and fruit trees offer a scene...”是定语从句修饰counties；“that is almost impossible to find in the city”是定语从句修饰scene
longSentences.translation: 许多人前往乡间那些土地肥沃的县区，那里大片的干草地和果树呈现出一种在城市里几乎看不到的景象。
longSentences.tip: 注意两层定语从句嵌套：where引导的从句修饰counties，that引导的从句修饰scene，需分层理解。

longSentences.sentence: They must coordinate transportation, food, and lodging, and think about the prevention of accidents, since farm equipment and large animals can be dangerous to people who have never been near them before.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是并列谓语“他们必须协调……并考虑……”
longSentences.modifiers: “since farm equipment and large animals can be dangerous to people...”是原因状语从句；“who have never been near them before”是定语从句修饰people
longSentences.translation: 他们必须协调交通、餐饮和住宿等安排，还要考虑事故的预防，因为农用设备和大型动物对从未接触过它们的人来说可能存在危险。
longSentences.tip: since引导原因状语从句，从句中又嵌套who引导的定语从句，逐层拆分即可理清句意。

longSentences.sentence: What stays in their memory is not a five-star hotel, but days when modern comforts were missing, replaced by fresh air, quiet fields, and a simpler way of life.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“What stays in their memory is not A, but B”，What引导主语从句
longSentences.modifiers: “What stays in their memory”是主语从句；“when modern comforts were missing”是定语从句修饰days；“replaced by...”是过去分词短语作后置修饰
longSentences.translation: 留在他们记忆中的不是五星级酒店，而是那些缺少现代生活便利的日子——取而代之的是新鲜的空气、宁静的田野和更简单的生活方式。
longSentences.tip: What引导主语从句作句子主语，抓住not...but...并列结构可知语义重心落在but后面的内容上。

items.title: From Zero to a Local Favorite
items.passage:
After her husband passed away, Mrs. Chen, a widow in her fifties, found herself with plenty of free time but very little business experience, virtually zero, in fact. She decided to turn her family's old grape jam recipe into a small business, selling jars of jam from a stall near her home. Friends thought the idea was a little crazy, since she had never worked in a shop or a factory before.

Instead of rushing in, Mrs. Chen did her homework first. She visited nearby markets to study prices, spoke with a business teacher, and drew a simple chart to compare her possible costs and profits. Only after she felt confident that the numbers made sense did she ask herself whether she should spend her small savings on jars, sugar, and fresh grapes.

Within a year, her stall had become one of the most popular spots on the block, and her sudden success even made her the subject of a short article in the local newspaper. Customers praised the taste of her jam, and Mrs. Chen said the greatest satisfaction came not from the money, but from proving that it is never too late to start something new.
items.translation:
在丈夫去世后，五十多岁的陈女士忽然有了大把空闲时间，但几乎没有任何经商经验——实际上等于零。她决定把家里祖传的葡萄酱配方变成一门小生意，在自家附近摆摊卖果酱。朋友们都觉得这个想法有点疯狂，因为她从未在商店或工厂工作过。

陈女士没有贸然行动，而是先做足了功课。她走访附近的市场调查价格，请教一位商业老师，还画了一张简单的图表来比较可能的成本和利润。直到她确信这些数字合理之后，她才问自己是否应该拿出不多的积蓄去买罐子、糖和新鲜葡萄。

不到一年，她的摊位就成了这条街区最受欢迎的地方之一，她的突然成功甚至让她成了当地报纸一篇短文的报道对象。顾客们纷纷称赞她果酱的味道，陈女士说，最大的满足感并非来自金钱，而是来自证明了开始一件新事情永远不会太晚。

keyWords.zh: n. 家庭作业；(引申)准备工作

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: A widow's difficulties in finding a new job after her husband's death
How careful preparation helped a widow succeed in a small jam business
The history of grape jam making in a small town
Why local newspapers often report on small businesses
questions.explain: 全文讲述陈女士在丈夫去世后，通过认真调研、做足准备，最终把葡萄酱生意做成功的过程，主旨是准备工作帮助她创业成功，故选B。

questions.q: Before starting her business, what did Mrs. Chen do to prepare?
options: She borrowed money from a bank
She hired an experienced shop manager
She studied prices and drew a chart comparing costs and profits
She traveled abroad to learn jam-making skills
questions.explain: 第二段明确提到她走访市场调查价格，并画图表比较可能的成本和利润，故选C；其余选项原文均未提及。

questions.q: What can be inferred about Mrs. Chen's friends' initial reaction to her plan?
options: They fully supported her decision from the start
They doubted the idea because she lacked business experience
They refused to buy her jam even after she succeeded
They convinced her not to spend any of her savings
questions.explain: 第一段提到朋友们觉得这个想法有点疯狂，是因为她从未在商店或工厂工作过，说明朋友们最初对此持怀疑态度，故选B。

questions.q: In the last paragraph, the word "subject" is closest in meaning to
options: a school course
a topic or person being discussed
someone who obeys rules
a citizen of a country
questions.explain: 短语“the subject of a short article”意为“(成为)一篇短文报道的对象”，此处subject指“报道所涉及的对象、话题”，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: After her husband passed away, Mrs. Chen, a widow in her fifties, found herself with plenty of free time but very little business experience, virtually zero, in fact.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“陈女士发现自己有大把空闲时间，但几乎没有经商经验”
longSentences.modifiers: “After her husband passed away”是时间状语；“a widow in her fifties”是同位语修饰Mrs. Chen；“virtually zero, in fact”是插入语，补充强调前文
longSentences.translation: 在丈夫去世后，五十多岁的陈女士忽然有了大把空闲时间，但几乎没有任何经商经验——实际上等于零。
longSentences.tip: 抓住“found herself with A but B”的对比结构，插入语是对前面内容的强调补充，可先跳过再回读理解。

longSentences.sentence: Only after she felt confident that the numbers made sense did she ask herself whether she should spend her small savings on jars, sugar, and fresh grapes.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“did she ask herself whether...”，因Only位于句首触发部分倒装
longSentences.modifiers: “Only after she felt confident that the numbers made sense”是时间状语，内含that宾语从句；“whether she should spend...”是ask的宾语从句
longSentences.translation: 直到她确信这些数字合理之后，她才问自己是否应该拿出不多的积蓄去买罐子、糖和新鲜葡萄。
longSentences.tip: “Only+状语”位于句首时，主句要部分倒装（did she ask），这是阅读中常见的倒装结构，需先识别倒装再理解语序。

longSentences.sentence: Customers praised the taste of her jam, and Mrs. Chen said the greatest satisfaction came not from the money, but from proving that it is never too late to start something new.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是两个并列分句：“顾客称赞果酱味道”和“陈女士说最大的满足感来自……”
longSentences.modifiers: “not from the money, but from proving that...”是not...but...并列结构，作came from的状语；“that it is never too late to start something new”是that从句作proving的宾语
longSentences.translation: 顾客们纷纷称赞她果酱的味道，陈女士说，最大的满足感并非来自金钱，而是来自证明了开始一件新事情永远不会太晚。
longSentences.tip: not...but...结构表强调对比，proving后接that宾语从句，注意层层嵌套的从句关系。

items.title: A Hidden Census Inside Us
items.passage:
The human body is not occupied by humans alone. Trillions of microbes live on the skin, in the mouth, and especially in the gut, and in recent decades scientists have carried out something close to a full census of these tiny residents. The results were surprising: microbial cells are roughly as numerous as our own, and together they form an invisible community that shapes much of our daily health.

This discovery has helped establish a new way of thinking about disease. A balanced, well-tamed population of gut microbes appears to support digestion, strengthen the immune system, and may even influence intellectual development in early childhood through what researchers call the gut-brain connection. When the balance is lost, the effects are far from petty; an unhealthy microbiome has been linked to conditions ranging from allergies to mood disorders, giving the field of medicine a noticeable bump in research funding over the past ten years.

Some scientists now argue that this research sets a precedent for the future of medicine, forming part of the basic skeleton of personalized treatment. Instead of treating only symptoms, doctors may one day adjust a patient's microbes directly, much as farmers manage the natural balance of a healthy field.
items.translation:
人体并非只由人类细胞构成。数以万亿计的微生物生活在皮肤、口腔，尤其是肠道之中，近几十年来，科学家们几乎对这些微小的“居民”做了一次全面的普查。结果令人惊讶：微生物细胞的数量与人体自身细胞大致相当，它们共同构成了一个看不见的群落，在很大程度上影响着我们日常的健康状况。

这一发现有助于建立一种认识疾病的新思路。一个平衡的、被良好“驯化”的肠道微生物群落，似乎有助于促进消化、增强免疫系统，甚至可能通过研究者所称的“肠-脑连接”影响儿童早期的智力发育。而一旦这种平衡被打破，其影响绝非细枝末节：不健康的微生物群已被发现与从过敏到情绪障碍等多种病症相关，这也使医学领域在过去十年间的研究经费出现了明显增长。

一些科学家现在认为，这项研究为未来医学的发展开创了先例，构成了个性化治疗基本框架的一部分。医生或许有朝一日不再只是治疗症状，而是像农民管理健康农田的自然平衡那样，直接调节患者体内的微生物。

keyWords.zh: vt. 驯养 a. 驯服的

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The dangers of microbes living inside the human body
A recent study of microbes inside the human body and its implications for medicine
A comparison between human cells and microbial cells
New methods for removing harmful gut bacteria
questions.explain: 全文先介绍科学家对人体内微生物进行了一次“普查”，再说明其对健康和疾病的影响，最后指出这项研究对未来医学的意义，主旨是对人体微生物的研究及其医学意义，故选B。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, a balanced population of gut microbes may help with all of the following EXCEPT
options: supporting digestion
strengthening the immune system
influencing intellectual development in early childhood
curing allergies that have already developed
questions.explain: 第二段提到平衡的肠道微生物有助于消化、增强免疫系统、影响儿童智力发育，但并未提到能治愈已经发生的过敏，反而说不健康的微生物群与过敏等病症相关，故D项原文未提及，选D。

questions.q: What can be inferred from the passage about future medical treatment?
options: It may focus more on adjusting patients' gut microbes rather than only treating symptoms
It will completely stop using traditional medicine
It will no longer require any scientific research
It will replace doctors with farmers
questions.explain: 第三段提到医生或许有朝一日不再只是治疗症状，而是直接调节患者体内的微生物，可推知未来医学可能更注重调节微生物而非仅治疗症状，故选A。

questions.q: In paragraph 2, the phrase "far from petty" most nearly means
options: very minor and unimportant
quite significant and serious
completely unrelated to health
extremely rare to happen
questions.explain: “far from”表示“绝非，一点也不”，因此“far from petty”意为“绝非微不足道的”，即“相当重要、严重的”，故选B。

longSentences.sentence: Trillions of microbes live on the skin, in the mouth, and especially in the gut, and in recent decades scientists have carried out something close to a full census of these tiny residents.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是两个并列分句：“数以万亿计的微生物生活在……”和“科学家们做了一次普查”
longSentences.modifiers: “on the skin, in the mouth, and especially in the gut”是三个并列地点状语；“in recent decades”是时间状语；“close to a full census of these tiny residents”修饰something，说明普查的程度
longSentences.translation: 数以万亿计的微生物生活在皮肤、口腔，尤其是肠道之中，近几十年来，科学家们几乎对这些微小的“居民”做了一次全面的普查。
longSentences.tip: and连接两个并列分句；注意“something close to...”表示“接近于……的程度”，是英语中常见的委婉表达方式。

longSentences.sentence: A balanced, well-tamed population of gut microbes appears to support digestion, strengthen the immune system, and may even influence intellectual development in early childhood through what researchers call the gut-brain connection.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“一个……微生物群落似乎支持消化、增强免疫系统，甚至可能影响智力发育”，appears to与may even构成并列谓语
longSentences.modifiers: “balanced, well-tamed”是形容词修饰population；“in early childhood”是时间状语修饰influence；“through what researchers call the gut-brain connection”是方式状语，内含what引导的宾语从句
longSentences.translation: 一个平衡的、被良好“驯化”的肠道微生物群落，似乎有助于促进消化、增强免疫系统，甚至可能通过研究者所称的“肠-脑连接”影响儿童早期的智力发育。
longSentences.tip: appears to和may even两个结构并列作谓语，through后的what从句作介词宾语，需整体把握。

longSentences.sentence: Some scientists now argue that this research sets a precedent for the future of medicine, forming part of the basic skeleton of personalized treatment.
longSentences.skeleton: 主干是“一些科学家认为……”，that引导宾语从句
longSentences.modifiers: “now”是时间状语；“forming part of the basic skeleton of personalized treatment”是现在分词短语作伴随状语，补充说明结果
longSentences.translation: 一些科学家现在认为，这项研究为未来医学的发展开创了先例，构成了个性化治疗基本框架的一部分。
longSentences.tip: that引导宾语从句作argue的宾语，句末的现在分词短语是伴随状语，说明动作的自然结果。
